## Apps to port (no TAE confirmed)

Likely candidates per matrix:
- **Pro Blogger** (5 snippets: `pro-blogger.snippet.related-products.liquid`, `pro-blogger.snippet.related-product-articles.liquid`, `pro-blogger.snippet.related-articles.liquid`, `pro-blogger-section-wrapper.liquid`, `pro-blogger.shortcode.liquid`) — IF Pro Blogger is being kept (verify against bd `hairmnl-theme-01vl` epic — if that epic is replacing Pro Blogger with Vertex, DROP all snippets instead)

## Port pattern

For each app that stays as a snippet:
1. Convert `{% include %}` → `{% render %}` syntax (Pipeline 8 requires this).
2. Wrap in a dedicated Pipeline 8 section file (`sections/<app>.liquid`) so the snippet becomes placeable via theme editor.
3. Strip Pipeline 6 conditionals that no longer apply.

## Constraints

- Output files file-disjoint with M5 (which writes `assets/theme.js`). M5 + M6 can swarm in parallel.

- DO NOT create intermediate working-notes files.

PR with per-app status table:

Each ported app's snippet/section verified rendering on dev theme. `git show HEAD --stat` in close notes.

title: Phase 4.M5: Bulk port custom-theme.js to Pipeline 8 assets/theme.js
description: OFFLOAD to OC after spike validates scope. Eliminate dead code (sticky-js if P8 native, P6 Flickity overrides, jQuery deps). Port survivors. Target ~5000 LoC final (45% reduction from 9118).
design:
## Output path (explicit)

- `assets/theme.js` on the Phase 4 working branch (Pipeline 8 working tree, top-level `assets/`)
- **NO `docs/os2-migration/` paths.**

## Pre-requisite

The spike (bd `hairmnl-theme-2i8b.spike`) MUST be closed first with a documented decision to dispatch this ticket OR pull it back to CC. If the spike's extrapolation predicts >10 working days, this ticket does NOT dispatch — operator pulls the work back to CC and re-plans.

## What to port

Per `os2-migration/customization-audit.md` § 7.1 — current `assets/custom-theme.js` is 9,118 LoC bundled/compiled.

**Identify dead code (drop 30-40% of file):**
- `sticky-js` library (`Sticky` class) — if Pipeline 8 has built-in sticky OR Intersection Observer pattern works
- Pipeline-6-specific Flickity overrides (Pipeline 8 may use different media gallery library)
- jQuery dependencies (Pipeline 8 doesn't ship jQuery)
- Any `theme.legacy.*` or Pipeline 6 versioning conditionals

**Port survivors:**
- Cart drawer behaviors (Pipeline 8's cart drawer uses different event model — adapt)
- Drawer interaction patterns
- Custom behaviors mixed in (find via grep against Pipeline 6 stock theme.js diff)
- Section-scoped JS where Pipeline 8's architecture supports it

**Target:** ~5,000 LoC final after dead-code removal (45% reduction from 9,118).

## Side-by-side comparison test plan

The PR must include a test plan listing each behavior verified working on Pipeline 8 dev theme:
- ☐ Cart drawer opens / closes
- ☐ A/B test framework cookie set + dataLayer push
- ☐ JS error tracking (`hairmnl_js_error`) → dataLayer
- ☐ Cart pre-init dispatcher (`theme:cart:init` on pageshow)
- ☐ Klaviyo form events (open/submit/close → gtag)
- ☐ Backbar account registration DOM reorder
- ☐ Sticky ATC bar on product page (if STKY's TAE doesn't fully cover)
- ☐ Mobile drawer-toggle interceptor (no tap-through)

## Constraints

- Single file: `assets/theme.js`. ~5K LoC target.
- DO NOT add jQuery.
- DO NOT create intermediate working-notes files.
- CC merge-gates with high scrutiny — this is the highest-risk port in the migration.

## Acceptance

Pipeline 8 `assets/theme.js` PR'd. All test plan items checked. No jQuery dependency. `git show HEAD --stat` in close notes. CC explicitly approves the diff before bd close.

title: Phase 4.M4: Consolidate 31 brand-collection sections into 1 parameterized
description: OFFLOAD to OC. Largest single porting payoff. Take sections/collection-branded.liquid (1568 LoC) as base. Variable schema becomes section settings (brand colors, logo, hero, collection handle, description). Produce 1 new section + 30 JSON templates.
design:
## Output paths (explicit)

- `sections/brand-collection.liquid` (NEW — Pipeline 8 working tree, top-level `sections/`)
- `templates/collection.<handle>.json` × 30 (NEW or updated — Pipeline 8 working tree, top-level `templates/`)
- **NO `docs/os2-migration/` paths anywhere.**

## What to consolidate

Per `os2-migration/customization-audit.md` § 2.1 — 31 brand-collection sections (~52,500 LoC total) are near-identical. Take `sections/collection-branded.liquid` (1,568 LoC) as canonical base.

**Variable schema (becomes section settings):**
- `brand_logo` (image_picker)
- `brand_hero_image` (image_picker)
- `brand_primary_color` (color)
- `brand_accent_color` (color)
- `brand_description_html` (richtext)
- `collection_handle` (collection_picker)
- `hero_layout_variant` (select: standard / split / video-bg)
- Per-brand product-grid settings (per-row count, sort default, etc.)

## The 30 templates

Generate one `templates/collection.<handle>.json` per brand. Each references `sections/brand-collection.liquid` with brand-specific settings only — no Liquid logic in the JSON.

Handles to cover (from audit):
- absolutrepair, blondabsolu, chronologiste, densifique, discipline, elixir, specifique, nutritive, resistance
- lorealprofessionnel, krsymbiose, krchromaabsolu, krcurlmanifesto, krgenesis, krspecifique-divalent
- lp-vitamino, lp-serioxyladvanced, lp-metaldetox, lp-styling, lp-prolonger, lp-mythicoil, lp-liss, lp-inforcer, lp-silver, lp-scalpadvanced, lp-instantclear, lp-antidandruff
- lp-serioxyl (minimal redirect)
- branded (base)
- branded-subcollection

## Constraints

- Target: ~1,200 LoC for the consolidated section (down from 1,568 base).
- Templates: ~5 LoC each, ~150 LoC total.
- Single PR, single coherent ticket — NOT splittable into per-brand sub-tickets (that would defeat consolidation).
- Read Pipeline 6's `collection-branded.liquid` and per-brand variants to extract the variable schema.
- DO NOT touch `sections/collection-*.liquid` of the OLD Pipeline 6 paths — those are still on live theme and will be deleted on cutover.
- DO NOT create intermediate working-notes files.

## Acceptance

PR opened. All 30 brand collection pages render correctly on dev theme preview with brand-specific identity (logo, colors, hero image, products). No visual regression vs Pipeline 6 brand pages. `git show HEAD --stat` in close notes.

---

## ADDENDUM (2026-05-17, after CC overnight session)

CC examined the 31 brand variants overnight and found the consolidation is **more complex than "same code + different settings"**:

1. **Structural diffs exist between variants.** Spot-diff between `collection-absolutrepair.liquid` and `collection-branded.liquid` showed:
   - Class-naming evolution: P6 grid classes `large--*` vs newer `large-up--*` (e.g., `large--one-half` vs `large-up--one-half`). Different variants are on different vintages of the same naming scheme.
   - Liquid logic differences: e.g., one variant has a `{% comment %}`-wrapped brand-logo block that the base renders directly.
   - These are NOT just `{% schema %}` setting differences — they're actual code drift.

2. **Recommended consolidation approach (revised):**
   - **Step 1: Pick the canonical structural version.** Read 3–5 of the largest variants (`collection-branded.liquid` 1568, `collection-lp-vitamino.liquid` 1810, `collection-krsymbiose.liquid` 1810, `collection-absolutrepair.liquid` 1740) and determine which class-naming scheme + Liquid structure is most consistent / forward-looking. Pick that as the base.
   - **Step 2: Build the schema for what truly varies.** Brand identity (colors, logo, hero image, description, handle) clearly varies. Class-naming variants do NOT need to vary — pick one and stick with it.
   - **Step 3: Build the consolidated section.** Use the canonical base + the new schema. Drop the variants' minor structural drift.
   - **Step 4: Build the 30 JSON templates.** Each carries only the brand-specific settings.
   - **Step 5: Reconcile any per-variant unique features.** During Steps 1–3, note any feature in a specific brand variant that doesn't fit the canonical base (e.g., a unique hero layout, a custom product-grid behavior). Document each as a known deviation in the PR description — they become Phase 5 visual-QA items.

3. **Pipeline 8 schema compatibility:**
   - Verify the section schema uses Pipeline 8 settings_schema.json conventions (P8 uses different setting types than P6 in places).
   - Use Pipeline 8's `{% schema %}` capabilities including `presets` so merchants can drag-drop the section onto new collection pages.

4. **NEW file ONLY — do not delete P6 sections:**
   - Create `sections/brand-collection.liquid` as a NEW file.
   - DO NOT delete the existing `sections/collection-{brand}.liquid` files (they're still serving live Pipeline 6 traffic).
   - Phase 6 cutover handles the deletion.

5. **Phase 5 visual QA is essential.** Without seeing each of the 30 brand pages render on the dev theme, we can't know if the consolidation lost any per-brand unique feature. The PR description should include a Phase 5 checklist: 30 brand handle URLs to visually verify.

6. **Estimated effort (revised after CC's analysis):**
   - Step 1 (read 3–5 variants): 1 hour
   - Step 2 (schema design): 30 min
   - Step 3 (consolidated section): 2 hours
   - Step 4 (30 templates): 1 hour
   - Step 5 (deviation documentation): 30 min
   - **Total: ~5 hours of focused OC work.** Within paid-tier scope.

7. **If the consolidation surfaces a per-variant feature that genuinely doesn't fit the canonical base**, OC should flag in the PR description and **leave that variant out** of the consolidation. Don't force-fit; partial consolidation (e.g., 28 of 30 brands consolidated, 2 kept as standalone sections) is acceptable.

## Pre-flight gotchas seen overnight

OC failed twice during the overnight session (one swarm, one single orchestrate) — both exited exit-0 without producing port commits. Possible mitigations for this dispatch:

- Make sure no other worktree has `claude/p4-migration-kickoff` or similar Phase 4 branch checked out — OC will create its own branch when starting.
- OC may try to read `/tmp/p8-pull-<date>/` paths (date-suffixed). The actual reference theme files are at `/tmp/p8-pull/` (no date suffix). If OC can't find P8 reference files, it should `shopify theme pull --theme=141168312419 --store=creations-gdc.myshopify.com --path=/tmp/p8-pull-fresh --nodelete` first.
- If OC's lead-architect can't read `/Users/y9378348c/.config/opencode/agents-paid/*` to load role definitions, that may be the silent-exit cause. The dispatch should still proceed since the role files are loaded by OC's own runtime, not the agents themselves.

title: Product accordion tabs render raw HTML markup — richtext schema rejects <ol>/<li>/<div>
description:
On product pages, the How To Use / Description / Benefits / Ingredients accordion shows raw HTML (
  1. Dispense and emulsify ...
    2. ) as plain text. Root cause: sections/product.liquid and sections/section-product.liquid declare raw_content_1 through raw_content_5 as type:richtext in section schema. Shopify's richtext type rejects
      /
        /
      • /
        and HTML-escapes the entire input when those tags are present. Fix: change schema type from "richtext" to "html" for all raw_content_* fields. Caveat: existing escaped content remains escaped — merchant must re-save affected products in theme editor. If many products affected, follow-up with unescape filter chain as separate fix.

title: Missing blog body images — malformed in article.content
description: Several blog posts (e.g. /blogs/hair-education/2026-best-in-beauty-hair-protocols-essentials — 10 of 11 broken) render tags whose parent tag is missing its closing > before the . Browsers refuse to render images inside malformed anchor wraps. Source HTML in article.content is already malformed (rich-text editor or vendor app produced bad HTML). The article-content-with-img-dims.liquid post-processor preserves the malformation. Fix: add defensive replace chain at top of post-processor to repair target="_blank"

title: Liquid syntax error on branded collection pages — quote-style mismatch in title-strip replace chain
description: Live-site Liquid syntax error (Unexpected character é) renders verbatim on /collections/kerastase, /collections/kerastase-genesis, /collections/kerastase-chroma-absolu. Root cause: replace: filter chain in snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid:187 and snippets/product-grid-item.liquid:294 mixes single + double quotes; entries 3 and 4 use single quotes around strings containing apostrophe (L'Oréal) which terminates the Liquid string literal early. Fix: standardize all 14 entries to double quotes. Bug provenance: introduced 2026-04-29 commit 0614791 (ujh) and copy-pasted into branded variant on 2026-05-03 commit cb485b9 (aoe consolidation).

title: AUDIT-H1: theme:cart:change dispatched without detail — drawer opens empty/stale, header count/price dead after quick-buy + sticky-bar adds
description: Phase4 closeout audit, CONFIRMED x2. hairmnl-common.js:613 and product-sticky-bar.liquid:256 dispatch theme:cart:change (+popdown) with no detail; theme.js listeners read e.detail.cart (drawer 2696, header count/price 1711, free-shipping 1111) and throw — drawer opens empty after add-to-empty-cart, stale on 2nd+ adds, header price/free-shipping never update, 2-3 uncaught TypeErrors per add. Server cart stays correct. FIX: dispatch with detail:{cart:cart} at both sites (fresh /cart.js response already in scope), defensive detail on popdown, AND fix first-paint ordering (dispatch theme:cart:reload first or defer section-7 theme:drawer:open via setTimeout 0 — section-7 registers before theme.js drawer controller so even native ATC paints stale on 2nd add). See audit-reports/phase4 report.
close_reason: FIXED+VERIFIED-STATIC (J30). detail:{cart:cart} at both dispatch sites (fresh /cart.js JSON in scope, shape-identical to theme.js native dispatch, all three listener consumers covered); section-7 drawer-open deferred one tick (registration-order traced: popdown suppression intact, drawer controller now processes fresh cart before paint; no double-open race). Repo-wide sweep: NO remaining detail-less dispatchers. RUNTIME confirmation blocked on preview domain (third-party fetch wrappers kill fetch in the test browser; team phones unaffected) — folded into the a7av.7 cutover smoke: add via quick-buy on live origin, drawer must open WITH the added item, header count/price update, zero console TypeErrors. LATENT (recorded): popdown consumers read detail.variant — inert while section-7 suppresses popdown; if section-7 is ever removed, pass the /cart/add.js response as detail.variant too.

title: Rec-rail/slider cards misaligned (web-team June 23) — follow-up to 2i8b.79. Product images zoomed/cropped inconsistently: the img uses inline object-fit:cover inside the uniform 4:5 box, so non-4:5 products look zoomed/cropped + different sizes; titles not on same row. Affects Vertex sliders (PDP recs, cart drawer, cart page) + Recently Viewed. Fix J25: object-fit:contain + white box + 2-line title clamp, scoped to .vrec-card--standard + .recent__content.
notes:
J25 (commit f429efd) pushed to Working Demo 141168312419. Desktop smoke-test: .vrec-card--standard rec-card img objectFit=contain, box bg=white — confirms the inline object-fit:cover crop is overridden. Pending team verify across all sliders (PDP recs, Recently Viewed, cart drawer, cart page).
J26 FIXED + VERIFIED on Working Demo 141168312419. (a) object-fit:contain + uniform 4:5 white box BROADENED beyond Vertex/RecentlyViewed to the home curated sliders: .swiper.collection, .collection-grid__box, .flickity-grid[data-slider], .homepage-collection .js-grid (NEVER bare .js-grid = collection page). (b) Removed the 2-line title clamp → FULL titles (team ask). (c) Root cause of 'cards misaligned / 2nd card lower': .product__badge computed position:STATIC on the rails (STKY's satcb_quick_buy_container wrapper) → rendered inline below the image, pushing titles down. Forced position:absolute top-left overlay on all rail wrappers. (d) Badge label parse fixed: remove_first+strip (clean '10% Off'). Browser-verified: badge absolute top:8/left:8, all 5 vrec titles start at identical 224px offset (aligned), home sliders contain+absolute-badge. Self-heals at cutover (STKY gone). Pending team re-confirm across PDP/cart/home.
J27 (iOS, web-team June 25 deck 26/27): Vertex rec-card badge was rendering as bare lowercase 'free gifts' (NOT the grey pill). Root cause (live-DOM confirmed, NOT BOGOS): it IS the theme .product__badge--custom but the Vertex context stripped its grey-pill styling — background transparent, padding 0, text-transform none (J26's overlay positioning held, but the pill look was lost). Fix: force background var(---color-badge,#e0e1e0)+padding 5px 10px+text-transform:uppercase on .vrec-card--standard .product__badge. Browser-verified: bg rgb(224,225,224), uppercase, padding 5px 10px, absolute top-left, looksLikePill=true. Scoped to Vertex so the working non-Vertex sliders are untouched.
Android June-25 video (6963 'For Colored Hair' slider) + 'Desktop badge': same slider-alignment + badge issues J25/J26/J27 fixed; video predates the fixes. Verified on current preview (desktop): .swiper.collection + .homepage-collection sliders = object-fit:contain, uniform 4:5 box, titles aligned, badge position:absolute bg rgb(224,225,224) text-transform:uppercase (grey 'FREE GIFT' pill overlay). No remaining gap found.
J28 (June 25 'Desktop badge' + deck 26/27 ROOT CAUSE): the base .product__badge rule (theme.css:12157) only made it into the HOME/COLLECTION/SEARCH per-template chunks — ujg6.42 Chrome-Coverage split never saw a badge on product/cart templates, so theme-product.css + theme-cart.css ship WITHOUT it → any badge on PDP/cart rails rendered as bare lowercase 'free gifts' (zero styling). This also explains slide 27 'only on Vertex AI sliders' (those live on PDP/cart) and 'showed correctly on mobile cart drawer' (drawer opened from home/collection has the chunk). Fix: mirror the base rule unscoped in css-overrides.liquid (renders inline on EVERY template, after the chunks; identical declarations are a no-op where the chunk already applies). VERIFIED on Working Demo Genesis PDP: all badges pill:true (bg rgb(224,225,224), uppercase, padding 5px 10px, absolute, z400) — including the exact slide-26 Kerastase Genesis card. NOTE: live P6 has the same chunk-gap symptom (observed during a preview bounce); fix ships at cutover per P8-only constraint.
J29 (June 29, Jessica: 'last minor fix on the size'): badge was a FIXED 12px/~95px pill on every screen — small on desktop rail cards (216px, 44% of card), half a narrow mobile card (~170px). Now scales WITH the card via container queries: container-type:inline-size on the rail .lazy-image (the badge's positioning parent) + font-size clamp(10px, 6.5cqw, 14px) + proportional padding, scoped to the six rail wrappers only. VERIFIED on Working Demo: vertex rail badge now 14px (up from 12) with container active + still grey pill; collection grid UNCHANGED (12px, container-type:normal — approved surface untouched). Mobile computes 11px + tighter padding by the same clamp (card-width-driven, deterministic). kt0-reviewed: .lazy-image's abs children (img/badge/STKY leftovers) already anchor to it; open QB popover portals to body — no containing-block trap; lint clean. Jessica confirmed items 1-3 (cart-page slider, trash bin, picker) fixed — badge size was the last open item.
close_reason: Team-confirmed FIXED (Jessica, June 29: 'Badge - confirmed showing properly' + size fix J29 landed + verified: card-proportional container-query badge, desktop rails 14px / mobile ~11px, grid untouched). Full chain J25-J29 documented in-ticket. Slider alignment items 1-3 confirmed by Jessica's same message.

title: Quick Buy popover clipped by rec-rail/cart-drawer overflow — variant names cut on leftmost card. Fix J24: portal popover to <body> + position:fixed at toggle rect (card/grid ancestors establish containing blocks; plain fixed was offset ~1050px). notes:
J25 (commit d5ec337): scroll/resize handler now REPOSITIONS the open popover to follow its toggle (rAF-throttled), closing ONLY if the toggle scrolls out of the viewport — instead of J24's blanket auto-close. Root cause of 'disappearing on its own': on iOS the address bar shows/hides on the slightest scroll (and on tap) firing resize, which J24's listener treated as a close. Desktop smoke-test: popover opens + stays. Pending team iOS re-verify.
J27 (iOS, web-team June 25 deck 29/30/31 + videos): variant picker now SCROLLS WITH the card instead of floating. position:absolute in DOCUMENT coords on page-scroll surfaces (z-index 100, below header 5000-6000 / search / filter [bumped .collection__nav 5->200] / popups 9995-9999, above cards); position:fixed + reposition at z 20100 in the cart drawer (which already worked). Closes ONLY on outside-tap / page-switch / add — NEVER on scroll (J24/J25 closed-on-scroll caused the iOS 'disappearing'/'floating'). Hides-not-closes on horizontal rail scroll. Browser-verified on Working Demo: home toggle pos=absolute z=100, popover scrolls with card (popMoved=togMoved=400px), stays open. inDrawer detection (.cart__drawer) correct.
Android June-25 videos (6966 home, 6970 cart drawer): same variant-picker float/close-on-scroll issue J27 fixed. These videos PREDATE J27 (recorded before today's push). Verified the J27 behavior on the current preview (Working Demo): popover position:absolute z=100, scrolls with card (popMoved=togMoved), stays open. Android Chrome = same Blink engine as the verified desktop Chrome, so the absolute-doc fix applies identically. Cart-drawer popover (v70) uses the fixed+reposition path (inDrawer) which I could not browser-verify (preview cart can't be populated — fetch blocked + /cart bounces to live), but it's the same JS. Recommend a FRESH Android capture to confirm post-J27.
J27 re-verified on the PDP 'Complete Your Routine' rail (the iOS video surface) via real mouse click on Working Demo: popover opens on the ABSOLUTE path (pos=absolute z=100, inDrawerPath=false) — same path measured riding-with-card on home (popMoved=togMoved, stays open through scroll). iOS/Android videos 6966/6970 predate J27. Deck 25 (cart-page slider) + 28 (trash bin) confirmed live-bounce artifacts — P8 cart already renders vertex-recommendations cart_above_checkout before the summary, has NO Buy-It-With block, and pins the mobile trash bin top-right.
close_reason: Team-confirmed (Jessica June 29: 'Variant picker behavior and order - fixed'; badge item was the last open piece, now J29-closed). Full fix chain J23-J27 documented in-ticket; J31 added the re-tap close (2i8b.91).

The .collection__nav position:sticky pin (<=600px) has regressed to float-away-on-scroll, the exact bug 2i8b.52 fixed. Must pin cleanly below the header again.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS (could not reproduce — browser locked at innerWidth 2188, cannot trigger the <=600px media query). Ruled OUT every desktop-observable cause on preview 141655801955: (1) @media (max-width:600px) .collection__nav{position:sticky;top:0} IS deployed (document.styleSheets scan: mediaQueryStickyRulePresent=true); (2) hairmnl-collection.js IS loaded and setStickyTop ran (navInlineTop empty at desktop where mql=false, correct); (3) --menu-height=70px both inline+computed, so JS sets top=69px on mobile; (4) .collection__nav ancestor chain (.wrapper > .collection > div > .shopify-section > main.main-content) has NO transform/contain/filter/overflow/will-change/perspective at desktop; (5) parent .wrapper=3520px tall, sticky has room; (6) no mobile-gated containment on those ancestors in css-overrides or theme.css. Code + deploy correct. NOTE: J23 this session re-pushed css-overrides to both P8 themes, so the sticky rule is freshly deployed — team report may predate it. NEXT: user re-verify on FRESH incognito collection load; if still floating, need 3s scroll recording to distinguish scrolls-away-entirely vs pins-with-gap (mobile header not sticky) vs pins-then-detaches (mobile-gated containing-block break).
RESOLVED on the live-bound theme. Web-team screenshots (2 share links, same store 14124580): filter bar PINS correctly on "Pipeline 8 Working Demo" = theme 141168312419 (the cutover/review draft that becomes live), and FLOATS only on "P8 STKY-free preview" = theme 141655801955 (the temporary STKY-hidden preview, deleted at cutover). This is why my earlier diagnosis looked correct yet it floated — I was inspecting 141655801955. No live impact: the float is isolated to the throwaway preview. OPEN: decide whether to re-sync/fix the temp preview (if team keeps reviewing there) OR retire it and standardize the team on 141168312419. Recommending retire — one review theme avoids the which-theme confusion.
close_reason: RESOLVED via theme retirement. Filter bar PINS correctly on the cutover theme (Pipeline 8 Working Demo 141168312419) per web-team screenshots — confirmed working on what actually ships. The float existed ONLY on the temp P8 STKY-free preview (141655801955), a CSS-hidden-STKY duplicate; user retired it (deleted 2026-06-23). No live impact, no code fix needed. Team standardized on 141168312419.

title: J20 (Samsung/Android Chrome): mobile sticky bar wraps to 2 rows — select balloons, CTA drops below
description: Screen recording (Samsung phone, Chrome, shopifypreview.com share link, 2026-06-18): the PDP mobile sticky add-to-cart bar renders as TWO rows — the variant <select> ("250ml") balloons to ~full width with qty crammed at the edge, forcing "ADD TO CART • ₱4,200" onto a 2nd row below. iPhone showed one tidy row. J19 colours confirmed working on Samsung (dark ATC + grey selected pill). Markup: .vrec-product-sticky-bar (flex) > __price + <form .vrec-product-sticky-bar__form>(flex) containing __variant <select> + __qty + __cta <button>. My S12 max-width:92px on __variant was NOT honoured on Android Chrome (select went full-width). FIX (J20): hide redundant standalone __price on mobile (CTA shows price), pin __variant to fixed 84px (flex:0 0 84px/width/max-width !important), make __form flex:1 1 auto + flex-wrap:nowrap + min-width:0 so it fills one row, __cta min-width:0 so it shrinks not wraps. Forces one row by construction. UNVERIFIED on Samsung (tooling can't render mobile). Needs hard-refresh re-test.
close_reason: Sticky bar CONFIRMED working desktop+mobile (user verified) as ONE clean row — J20 layout fix now visible since J22 enabled the bar to render. 2-row wrap resolved.

title: J18: variant-selector reported not-working on clean preview — touch hardening + needs device repro
description:
Web team (June 17) re-tested on the STKY-free preview (#141655801955) and reports the Quick Buy VARIANT SELECTOR not working. Investigation:

VERIFIED CORRECT IN CODE: the popover handler (hairmnl-common.js:716-749) is a delegated click listener with preventDefault + stopPropagation on [data-qb-toggle] and click-outside in the same handler (no race). Synthetic-click testing on the visible collection grid (STKY-free theme) shows the popover opens correctly: is-open added, display:block, visibility:visible, sized 84-122px, 3 options, all ancestors visible. The only hidden popovers are in the CLOSED cart drawer (expected). Rec-rail overflow does NOT clip it (popover sits within .vrec-card__media box). No code bug found.

COULD NOT REPRODUCE the failure: the browser tooling is stuck at desktop width (can't simulate mobile touch) and real-clicks/JS-exec kept poisoning the tabs this session.

LIKELY EXPLANATIONS (unconfirmed): (a) team tapped SINGLE-variant products — those have no picker by design (they add directly), and the add is blocked on preview → looks like "nothing happens"; (b) a mobile-touch-specific issue desktop synthetic testing masks.

ACTION TAKEN: added pointer-events:none on .quick-buy__icon svg so a real tap's e.target is always the button (not an inner <path>) — bulletproofs closest('[data-qb-toggle]') across browsers. Pushed to main P8 draft + the STKY-free test theme.

STILL NEEDED: which page (collection/product/cart drawer), which product, and what device/browser the team used — ideally a screen recording. OR confirm at cutover on the live origin where real testing works.
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED (J21, commit on origin/main 2b331c8). NOT STKY (earlier notes blaming the STKY ScriptTag were WRONG — confirmed: clean env STKY-disabled+incognito still failed). REAL cause = z-index stacking inversion: .product-grid-item__slide ([data-grid-slide]) is position:absolute inset:0 z-index:5, rendered INSIDE the product <a class=lazy-image> (line ~258). That <a> is not a stacking context, so the slide's z-index:5 resolved against .quick-buy (z-index:3) in .product-grid-item__media and painted OVER the icon. Real finger taps hit the invisible slide → ancestor <a href=product.url> → PDP. Synthetic dispatchEvent('click') worked (bypasses hit-testing); popover showed (z-index:6 > 5). On-device probe confirmed SYNTH-CLICK is-open=true while real tap navigated. FIX: .quick-buy z-index 3→7, .quick-buy__popover 6→8, +position:relative/z-index:7 on .quick-buy__icon (css-overrides). Slide still covers the rest of the card → image taps still open the PDP. Fixes desktop mouse clicks too. kt0 clean (z-index only). Pushed to both P8 themes; debug probe removed. NEEDS device re-test: real tap on the icon opens the picker, not the PDP. NOTE: J18b capture-phase change (from the wrong STKY theory) left in place — harmless.
close_reason: DEVICE-CONFIRMED working: user verified on real mobile (STKY uninstalled) — tapping the Quick Buy icon opens the variant picker and selecting a size adds to cart. Root cause was the z-index stacking inversion (slide overlay z5 over icon z3); fixed by J21 (origin/main 2b331c8: .quick-buy z3->7, popover 6->8, +position:relative on the icon). Cookie 2-bar issue also resolved (Nova uninstalled, Shopify-native banner on).

title: J17 S8: Quick Buy function + STKY-free test preview
description: J17 S8: Quick Buy "not working" on cart-page rail. (1) Single-variant: tapping should ADD (currently appears to lead to PDP — the /cart/add.js AJAX fails on the myshopify PREVIEW domain due to app fetch-wrappers; works on the live origin). (2) Multi-variant: must show the variant selector popover (built J11 — verify it opens). (3) TEAM ASK: provide a preview WITHOUT the old STKY app so the native button can be tested cleanly on home/collection/PDP/drawer/blog. Action: verify popover + single-add path on draft; set up a STKY-disabled duplicate test theme (or document the operator toggle) so they can test in isolation.
notes: STKY-FREE PREVIEW DELIVERED + Quick Buy fully diagnosed. New theme #141655801955 'P8 STKY-free preview'. KEY FINDING: STKY loads via an app-level ScriptTag (satcb.min.js?shop=) injecting on EVERY page — CANNOT be removed by theme edits; disabling the app embed + deleting the theme.liquid script was necessary but NOT sufficient. Worked around by CSS-hiding STKY's injected UI on the new theme only (.satcb_quick_buy is a separate DIV child of the image link, so display:none is safe). VERIFIED /collections/all: 44 STKY buttons hidden, 38 native icons visible, images intact. Quick Buy CODE works: popover opens (3 variants), single-click intercepted (no PDP nav). ADD on preview = env limit NOT code: even bare fetch('/cart.js') throws 'Failed to fetch' (other apps' wrappers); works on live. Content on origin/main via the J17 rebase (was bbf9c16).
close_reason: STKY-free preview delivered (#141655801955) + Quick Buy verified working. Add is preview-env-limited (bare fetch fails on preview, works on live), not a bug. Content on origin/main @ 53fd182.

title: J17 S12: PDP mobile bar ADD TO CART gets cut off (long variant)

Fix: mobile .vrec-product-sticky-bar__variant { min-width:0; max-width ~90px } so the select yields and the CTA (flex:1) always fits fully. CTA must never clip.
notes: FIXED on draft (bbf9c16): mobile variant <select> min-width:0 + max-width 92 + ellipsis → select yields, CTA never clipped. Viewport-locked tooling can't render mobile → DEVICE-VERIFY a long-variant product (e.g. the L'Oreal w/ 'FREE Absolut Rep').

title: J16 S14: mobile banner gaps STILL present (P8 + live)
description: J16 S14 (RECURRING — both P8 + LIVE): mobile banner STILL has side margins. Prior fixes (T3 css-overrides, J11 section inline-style) did not land on the real render. title: P6 PDP mobile LCP residual 19-21s: Flickity init late re-paint re-emits the LCP candidate (~20s) — geometry-stable / early gallery init
description:
SUCCESSOR to zlwx (the lazy-attr fix, shipped 2026-06-10). After the eager fix, P6 pdp mobile LCP improved only 22-24s -> 18.8-20.9s (PSI n=3 on the fresh-render ?variant= URL; LCP element now eager, lazy=False). ROOT CAUSE #2: late re-paint. The LCP breakdown phases sum to ~3.8s (TTFB~0 + loadDelay ~200ms + loadDuration ~50ms + renderDelay ~3.5s) while the LCP timestamp is 19-21s — the gallery image paints near FCP (~4.5s) and is RE-painted/re-sized when Flickity initializes (theme.js executes ~20s on the 4x-throttled mobile CPU), re-emitting a larger/late LCP candidate.

CORROBORATION: desktop same page = 1.0-1.3s LCP (no CPU throttle -> early init); P8's gallery pipeline = 7.0-7.5s mobile. FIELD caveat: CrUX mobile LCP p75 ~3.8s — real users interact early (LCP observation stops on input) and have faster CPUs, so the field impact is smaller than lab; this is partly a lab-amplified pattern.

FIX DIRECTIONS (scoped work, gallery is CLS-sensitive — see e4a-beta + T27 history): (a) geometry-stable init — pre-size slides so Flickity init does not change the selected slide's painted size (flickity-lock-height extension); (b) init the PDP gallery early/standalone instead of inside the main theme.js execution; (c) CSS-first initial state where the selected (variant-featured) slide is correctly positioned pre-init. Validate each against CLS (pdp cells currently 0.010-0.029) and the e4a/T27 fixes.
notes: FIXED + VERIFIED 2026-06-10. Root cause refined: not a re-paint of a visible slide — pre-init CSS (html.js .product__slides:not(.flickity-enabled) > .product__media:not(:first-child){display:none}) hides everything but DOM-first; Flickity inits at index 0 then JUMPS to the variant-featured media when theme.js runs -> the jump painted the LCP at ~20s AND showed the wrong variant image pre-JS (filmstrip evidence). FIX: featured-media-first DOM order (lead_media) + eager + fetchpriority + decoding=sync; selection is data-media-id based so order-agnostic. EVIDENCE: pre 21.9-24.5s (6/6 runs); post observed 0.95-3.58s, simulated 5.0-6.0s good-mode (P8 parity), high mode only when obs FCP ~2.3s (shared P6 render-start variance, all templates — the critical-CSS/render-blocking program, not pdp-specific). Smoke green: linters, 7 key pages, schemas, gallery structure, desktop 1.3s, CLS <=0.020. GOTCHA: Shopify normalizes unknown query params into ONE page-cache entry per known-URL — PSI acceptance must use never-seen ?variant= URLs or fresh products.

title: P6 PDP mobile LCP 22-24s: initially-visible (variant-featured) gallery media is lazy — is_first eager only covers DOM-first media
description:
EVIDENCE (2026-06-10 PSI matrix raw JSONs, 3/3 P6 mobile pdp runs): LCP element = product gallery img data-image-id=22744548147299 with loading="lazy" -> LCP 21.9/23.9/24.5s. Live HTML check: product has 9 media; DOM-first img (id 22744548737123) IS eager+fetchpriority (the is_first logic in snippets/media.liquid:44 works) but the initially-VISIBLE slide = the variant-featured media (2nd in DOM, id ...147299) stays lazy. P8 contrast: same selector renders loading="eager" -> LCP 7.0-7.5s. The ~17s P6-vs-P8 pdp gap is this one attribute. NOT an app/main-thread problem (BSS B2B uninstalled; bootup top = theme.js/GTM/web-pixels, all modest).

DESIGN: in sections/product.liquid:80 + sections/section-product.liquid:74, extend the flag passed to media.liquid so the eager path covers the variant-featured media, e.g. is_first: forloop.first OR media.id == product.selected_or_first_available_variant.featured_media.id (cap eager at 2 media). Also check snippets/media.liquid:7 recursion which re-renders without passing is_first. Pre-flight pull-and-diff vs live before any live push (MIGRATION-CONTRACT); push P6 live + P8 dev (shared snippet).

TEST URL: /products/kerastase-genesis-anti-hair-fall-fortifying-serum (the PSI matrix pdp cell).
acceptance_criteria: Rendered PDP HTML: visible/variant-featured media img has loading=eager + fetchpriority=high (max 2 eager media). PSI pdp mobile cell (n=3 median) LCP < 10s on P6. No CLS regression on pdp cells. Pushed to live 131664707683 + P8 141168312419; git committed.
notes:
FIX SHIPPED 2026-06-10: eager flag now covers the variant-featured media in sections/product.liquid + section-product.liquid (max 2 eager). Draft-verified, then LIVE-verified via UNCACHED preview-of-live render (target 147299 eager+fetchpriority, eager-total=2, sticky-cart=0). Smoke: snippet-wiring PASS, kt0 PASS, home/collection/cart/azta/listicle render clean, default-template PDP clean. PSI mobile x3 re-test queued (waits for public edge rollover; edge cache much stickier today than the usual 30-90s). LESSON: ?preview_theme_id=<LIVE-id> + cookie jar = uncached ground-truth render; plain curl can serve long-cached pages and false-fail verification.
OUTCOME 2026-06-10: fix shipped+verified serving (LCP element eager, lazy=False in PSI). Lab LCP 22-24s -> 18.8-20.9s (n=3, fresh-render variant URL) — the lazy attr was real but SECONDARY; dominant residual = Flickity-init late re-paint (phases sum ~3.8s vs LCP ~20s), successor hairmnl-theme-y79d. SMOKE CLEAN: CLS stable 0.010, TBT unchanged, wiring+kt0 linters PASS, all key templates render, schemas intact, no a7av leak. ALSO fixed during smoke: 0kv3.3 ItemList gate (article.handle is blog-qualified). NOTE: bare PDP URL page-cache entry still pinned serving pre-fix markup (fresh renders + ?variant= URLs carry the fix; entry will age out). git: see commit 'fix(zlwx)...'.

title: Desktop CLS >0.1 regression — 327 URLs (blog article template + homepage), GSC field data
design:

{{ cart_content }}
` (825×70 column). The BULK of remaining blog+homepage desktop CLS = HEADER CASCADE (article-grid/MainContent/sections pushed by LoyaltyLion points -> column-balancer), covered by wscl (deployed 06-06/07, browser-validated, pending field propagation). REC: let wscl propagate (~1-2wk GA4 RUM), re-measure; CLS program essentially complete at theme level.

title: STKY parity: sticky ATC bar — desktop-top + in-bar variant/qty
description:
Web-team review (STKY Add to Cart - May 18, decisions 1 & 4). Extend the native sticky ATC bar (snippets/product-sticky-bar.liquid + css-overrides.liquid) to match the STKY app:
1. POSITION: mobile stays bottom-fixed (a7av.9). DESKTOP moves to TOP — pinned just below the sticky header (slides DOWN from the top: hidden transform translateY(-100%), .is-visible translateY(0); top offset = header height). Mobile keeps the bottom slide-up.
2. IN-BAR VARIANT + QTY (parity with app): STKY bar shows variant dropdown(s) + a qty input + price + ADD TO CART (see image16). The native bar currently shows a variant LABEL + syncs from the main selector + qty=1. Add real in-bar variant selector(s) + qty stepper that update the hidden id/price and post the chosen variant+qty.
Acceptance: desktop bar appears at the top below the header on scroll-past-ATC; mobile bar at bottom; variant change in the bar updates price + the add payload; qty respected; ATC adds via the theme cart flow; kt0-clean; browser/device verify.
notes: VERIFICATION (draft 141168312419, Chrome DOM inspection): confirmed PDP bar = pdp mode, in-bar variant <select> (2 opts w/ data-price+availability), qty number input, variant->price sync WORKS (P860->P1290), desktop-top offset top:70 sits flush below the 70px sticky header (announcement bar is position:relative/scrolls away — 70px is correct), reveal CSS works (is-visible -> translateY(0) visible). Collection-mode bar confirmed top:70. BUG CAUGHT + FIXED: form was display:block -> select/qty/cta stacked -> bar 199px tall; fixed to flex + killed theme form/input !important margins -> ~64-70px. Final pixel re-measure pending (preview dropped mid-session). Quick-buy + sticky-cart also confirmed (see a7av.12/.13).

title: Native rec-card AJAX ATC (fixes B3 cart-drawer + B4 cart-page placement)
description:
Fixes B3 (2i8b.50, cart-drawer rec ATC buttons missing) AND B4 (2i8b.51, cart-page rec slider placement) — both blocked on the rec-card ATC mechanism.

CURRENT: snippets/vertex-rec-card.liquid wraps each card in {% form 'product' %} → POST /cart/add (full-page reload). Problems:
- B3: in the cart DRAWER the native ATC button isn't showing (STKY previously supplied drawer ATC, but STKY cart-drawer is DROPPED per ARCHITECTURE.md — theme has its own drawer). Fix = render the native rec-card ATC as a black button matching the cart page (bug doc's suggested option).
- B4: cart PAGE recs can't be placed before the order summary because the cart is one <form> (cart.liquid 30-205) and per-card {% form %} would nest <form>s (invalid HTML).

BUILD: convert the rec-card ATC from {% form %} to AJAX — a native button + JS POST to /cart/add.js (no per-card <form>), then refresh the existing cart drawer (reuse the theme's cart-add/drawer-refresh flow; do NOT introduce parallel cart state). This:
1. Makes the rec-card ATC formless → can be placed anywhere (fixes B4: render recs inside cart.liquid before the footer/order-summary; remove the standalone vertex_cart_recs section from cart.json).
2. Shows a native black ATC button in the cart drawer (fixes B3).
3. Unifies ATC across all rec placements (PDP, home, cart, drawer).

Affects: snippets/vertex-rec-card.liquid (form→button+AJAX), a JS handler (in hairmnl-common.js or a new asset), sections/cart.liquid (render recs before footer), templates/cart.json (drop vertex_cart_recs section). Shared rec-card → affects all placements; verify PDP/home still add correctly.

Acceptance: rec-card ATC adds via AJAX + opens/refreshes the cart drawer on all placements; cart-page recs render before the order summary (no form nesting); cart-drawer recs show a black ATC button; PDP/home recs still work.
notes: BUILT + deployed to draft 141168312419 (commit 8bd54f4). Formless [data-vrec-add] buttons on cart + drawer rec cards; §10 AJAX handler in hairmnl-common.js (POST /cart/add.js → re-dispatch theme:cart:popdown + theme:cart:change to reuse the theme drawer flow; header read from /cart.js); recs render in cart.liquid before the order summary; vertex_cart_recs section removed from cart.json. Verified via curl/pull: kt0 lint clean, JS handler deployed, cart.json section removed (persisted). NEEDS BROWSER VERIFY (non-empty cart): recs render before order summary w/ black ATC; drawer recs show ATC (B3); click → AJAX add + drawer open/refresh + header count update; PDP/home recs unaffected.

title: STKY replacement: mobile sticky ATC bar (parity gap)
description:
STKY parity gap (per ARCHITECTURE.md § "Stky Sticky Add-to-Cart replacement": "Mobile breakpoint: Required"). The native sticky bar (snippets/product-sticky-bar.liquid + .vrec-product-sticky-bar) is DESKTOP-ONLY — JS bails below 1024px (line 77) and css-overrides.liquid gates display:none <1024px. STKY provides the mobile sticky ATC bar today; the native replacement must cover mobile before a7av.7 cutover.

BUILD: extend product-sticky-bar.liquid + css-overrides to show the bar on mobile (<1024px), bottom-fixed. Per architecture: must coexist with env(safe-area-inset-bottom) and any fixed footer/mobile-nav; must NOT overlap the Reamaze chat bubble (kt0 regression history — bd lki). Reuse the existing PDP-mode IntersectionObserver (show when primary ATC scrolls out). 

CAUTION: fixed-position mobile overlay — kt0-sensitive. Verify no transformed/contain ancestor traps it, and z-index/safe-area against Reamaze + footer. REQUIRES device/browser verification (curl insufficient for fixed-overlay visual). 

Acceptance: mobile PDP shows the native sticky ATC bar when the primary ATC is scrolled past; ATC works; no overlap with Reamaze bubble / footer / safe-area; kt0 lint clean.
notes: BUILT + deployed to draft 141168312419. JS desktop-gate removed (product-sticky-bar.liquid) + mobile @media block added (css-overrides.liquid): bottom-fixed, safe-area-inset-bottom, compact layout, z-index below Reamaze. Verified via curl: kt0 clean, mobile rule + safe-area present, bar renders on PDP, gate removed, 0 Liquid errors. NEEDS DEVICE VERIFY (curl can't): shows on scroll; Reamaze bubble coexistence (kt0/lki history); iOS safe-area; mobile footer/nav. FOLLOW-UP (per ARCHITECTURE.md): add .vrec-product-sticky-bar to kt0 OVERLAY_PATTERNS to catch future containment regressions on this overlay (with kt0-OK ack on its transform).

title: Audit M6 include→render conversion for dropped args (theme-wide)
description:
Discovered during 2i8b.48 (brand collections blank). The M6 include→render conversion changed callers from {% include %} (shared parent scope) to {% render %} (isolated scope) but in at least one case dropped the required arguments.

Confirmed instance: sections/brand-collection.liquid calls `{% render 'product-grid-item-branded' %}` with NO args (lines 611, 694, 1442, 1525), but the snippet expects product + grid_offset + grid_width_small/medium/large from caller scope (see snippet header doc). Result: nil product → broken card images, empty data, and a banned `{% include 'judgeme_widgets' %}` inside the render context (line 196).

This ticket: AUDIT all `{% render %}` calls that replaced `{% include %}` during M6, theme-wide, and confirm each passes every variable the target snippet reads from caller scope. Also flag any `{% include %}` that remains INSIDE a render-context snippet (banned).

Approach: this is a bulk pattern across ~44 files / 223 include occurrences + the render call sites — good OC-offload candidate (M6 continuation). For each render-replaced-include: diff the snippet vars vs args passed; add missing args.

Acceptance: no snippet renders with nil/missing caller vars; no Liquid errors in rendered collection/PDP/cart output on the P8 draft theme; spot-check brand + default + sub-collection cards show images, titles, prices, review badges.
notes: AUDIT COMPLETE 2026-06-04. Method: empirical Liquid-error scan of the P8 draft theme across 5 page types (home, /collections/sulfate-free-shampoos, /collections/lorealprofessionnel, a PDP, /cart) = 0 Liquid errors on all, post-2i8b.48. Reviewed all 3 data-lazy-url='/?section_id=' instances: brand-collection.liquid (collection-context, was the active bug → fixed under 2i8b.48); section-collection.liquid (homepage featured-collection row, '/' is correct context, already passes product → NOT a bug); related.liquid (reads page product → hardened to request.path, commit pending). CONCLUSION: the M6 dropped-args regression was isolated to brand-collection.liquid; no other active breakage theme-wide. related.liquid hardened defensively. Awaiting operator browser spot-check (same pass as 2i8b.48) before close.

title: P8 all pages: branded title font not loading
description: Phase 5 visual QA. The branded title font (heading font used for collection titles, section headings) is not loading on the P8 theme across all pages — headings fall back to a default font. Check @font-face / font asset references and the font-family CSS variables on theme 141168312419 vs P6 live.
notes: FIXED (commit 20ca7b2): --FONT-STACK-HEADING was emitting only the Shopify font (Questrial), ignoring use_custom_heading_font + custom_heading_font:SelfModern. Now prepends the custom font. Verified on draft: --FONT-STACK-HEADING = "SelfModern", Questrial, sans-serif → branded serif applies to all headings.

title: P8 PDP: metafield sections missing (Benefits, How To Use, Ingredients)
description: Phase 5 visual QA (doc images #9-10). P8 PDP (e.g. /products/davines-essentials-momo-shampoo-...) shows only the Description section. The Benefits, How To Use, and Ingredients metafield-driven sections present on P6 live are missing. Likely the metafield section/snippet was not ported/wired into the P8 PDP template. Confirm metafield namespace+keys exist on products, then wire the section. P6-safe if the section is migration-new or gated.
notes: SUPERSEDED by 2i8b.69 (J10 S24): the web team pointed at the product metafields; the tabs are now wired in CODE (product-tabs.liquid metafield fallback) — no theme-editor config needed. Verified rendering on the draft.

title: P8: Branded collections render blank (kerastase, lorealprofessionnel)
description: Phase 5 visual QA (source: "Pipeline 8 - 4 Jun 2026.docx", doc images #19-23). On the P8 draft theme 141168312419, /collections/kerastase and /collections/lorealprofessionnel render COMPLETELY BLANK below the header — no title, no hero banner, no product grid. P6 live renders these fully. This is broader than 2i8b.27 (which was only the missing Davines hero banner). Likely root cause: the consolidated sections/brand-collection.liquid (M4) or the per-brand collection.*.json template wiring fails to render on this theme. Relates-to 2i8b.27. Fix on main (migration files are P6-safe — P6 live uses the 31 individual collection-*.liquid sections, not brand-collection.liquid), push --only to theme 141168312419, verify, never touch P6 live.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (3 layers), diagnosed 2026-06-04. P8 draft theme 141168312419; P6 live untouched (never pushed to 131664707683).

LAYER 1 — lazy-url path bug [FIXED, committed e02ef32, pushed to draft]:
brand-collection.liquid emitted data-lazy-url="/?section_id=..." (homepage). Rendering the collection section in the homepage context has no `collection` object → Section Rendering API returns a Liquid error → IntersectionObserver placeholder hydrates to nothing → blank page. Fix: build lazy-url from request.path + append preview_theme_id when theme.role != 'main'. Verified: corrected URL returns 1104 products (was 0).

LAYER 2 — branded card render-args regression [REMAINING — the actual card fix]:
brand-collection.liquid invokes the card via `{% render 'product-grid-item-branded' %}` with NO arguments (lines 611, 694, 1442, 1525). But snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid was DESIGNED for `{% include %}` (its header doc says 'Variables inherited from caller scope (via include): product, grid_offset, grid_width_small/medium/large'). {% render %} has ISOLATED scope, so none of these reach the snippet → product is nil → product.featured_image nil → image_url(nil) = 'invalid url input' (broken images), empty titles, etc. ALSO: line 196 `{% include 'judgeme_widgets' %}` is disallowed inside a render context → 'include usage is not allowed in this context' → no review badge. The working DEFAULT card (snippets/product-grid-item.liquid line 301) correctly uses `{% render 'judgeme_widgets', ..., product: product %}`.
FIX NEEDED: (a) the 4 render calls in brand-collection.liquid must pass product + grid_offset + grid_width_small/medium/large; (b) line 196 in product-grid-item-branded.liquid: include→render with product: product; (c) check the product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid stub does the same. This is an incomplete M6 include→render conversion (args dropped). Verify all 4 render sites after.

LAYER 3 — kerastase has NO collection.kerastase.json on the draft theme (only lorealprofessionnel does) [SEPARATE, OPEN]: kerastase is blank for a different/additional reason — either it's assigned a 'kerastase' template suffix in admin that doesn't exist, or it falls to the default collection template. Needs: check the collection's assigned template in admin. (loreal has the brand-collection template; kerastase does not.)

BROADER IMPLICATION (filed separately): the M6 include→render conversion may have dropped required args on OTHER render'd snippets too → theme-wide audit warranted.


RESOLUTION (2026-06-04, commits e02ef32 + 416500a, pushed to draft 141168312419):

Layer 1 (lazy-url) + Layer 2 (render-args) fixes together resolve BOTH collections. Layer 3 was NOT separate — kerastase also uses brand-collection (its template references brand-collection too), so it was blank for the identical reason and is fixed by the same change.

Curl smoke-test on the draft theme (section-render hydration source, the content the IO JS injects):
- /collections/lorealprofessionnel: Liquid errors 1008→0, broken-image errors 924→0, include errors 84→0, product images 6→925, review badges →107, titles →84. lazy-url correct (collection path + preview_theme_id).
- /collections/kerastase: Liquid errors 0, product images 649, review badges 103, titles 83. lazy-url correct.

Both pages now emit the correct lazy-url and the hydration source is clean (banner + title + products with images/prices/review-stars). The IntersectionObserver hydration (hairmnl-common.js §8, unchanged) injects this clean source.

STATUS: code fix complete + curl-verified. AWAITING operator browser confirmation on the draft theme before close (per Shopify-touching verification rule). Preview URLs:
- https://creations-gdc.myshopify.com/collections/lorealprofessionnel?preview_theme_id=141168312419
- https://creations-gdc.myshopify.com/collections/kerastase?preview_theme_id=141168312419

NOTE: this fixed only the brand-collection.liquid render path + the branded card's judgeme include. The theme-wide M6 dropped-args audit (other render'd snippets) remains as 2i8b.63.

title: [security] Escape + scheme-block article image-preload href (_first_body_img_url) in theme.liquid
description: Pre-existing stored-XSS vector in layout/theme.liquid (NOT introduced by the Reamaze defer epic — surfaced by its epic-audit: audit-reports/reamaze-defer-20260604.md, Phase 2). theme.liquid L271-285: the article first-body image URL is extracted by naive string splitting on src="..." / src='...' then output UNESCAPED into a preload link: L285: A crafted article whose first contains a double-quote (e.g. x" onload="...) can break out of the href attribute and inject attributes/event handlers onto the (preload links can fire onload/onerror) -> JS execution in every visitor's browser on that article page. Requires article-editing privileges (staff or a 3rd-party app with article write scope), so blast radius is gated on privileged input, but impact is all article-page visitors. Fix (proposed by the audit, report appendix): block javascript:/data: schemes and HTML-escape: {%- unless _unsafe_proto -%} {%- endunless -%} Verify on draft theme 140785582179 first, then push layout/theme.liquid to live per CLAUDE.md push rules. CC keeps this (security + Liquid) — do NOT offload.
close_reason: DONE (d2a1743): preload href now scheme-blocked (https:// | protocol-relative // | clean same-origin /path with backslash-normalization rejection) + | escape'd. ADVERSARIALLY VERIFIED: 20-case bypass corpus all blocked (javascript:/data:/vbscript:/mixed-case/control-char/http:); escape is load-bearing for the single-quote extraction path; legitimate CDN srcs still preload; theme check clean; fail-mode = skip preload, never a broken page. Known accepted nit: pre-entity-encoded ampersands double-escape into a mismatched (wasted) preload — rare, perf-only.

title: Final external verification: CS Reamaze rate (Railway) + T+28d clean CrUX LCP
description:
Final external verification gating epic meu close — the 2 acceptance criteria that
require resources Claude cannot reach unattended. The other 3 criteria are met (see
meu.4 close-out report).

(3) CS Reamaze inbound rate within +/-15% of pre-launch baseline (5.79 conv/day).
    14-day sustain window closed 2026-05-31T10:32Z. Verify:
      cd ~/Projects/seo-platform && railway login        # interactive, human step
      # feed the local probe to the prod container over stdin (argv = window hours):
      railway ssh -- bash -lc 'PYTHONPATH=/app:/app/api /app/api/.venv/bin/python - 336' \
        < scripts/reamaze_recent_rate_hairmnl.py
    Pass = matched/expected ratio within 0.85-1.15x of baseline.

(5) Mobile CrUX LCP p75 no regression. Clean 28-day field read lands ~2026-06-14
    (28d after 100% rollout on 2026-05-17). Pre-launch mobile CrUX LCP p75 baseline
    ~3,812ms (AVG). Read via scripts/run-psi.sh (needs PSI_API_KEY) strategy=mobile,
    originLoadingExperience.LARGEST_CONTENTFUL_PAINT_MS.percentile. Pass = <= baseline
    (flat or better). Mechanism: the Reamaze loader was async / non-render-blocking,
    so defer only removes page work -> LCP regression is mechanically near-impossible;
    criterion 5 is a guardrail, not an expected gain.

Close-out also folds in: update docs/HairMNL-Theme-Modernization-Deck.pptx perf-results
slide with the verified 212KB->0 byte win + the T+28d CrUX number once read (deferred to
this pass so the slide carries real field data rather than a placeholder).
notes:
CRITERION 3 — VERIFIED PASS (2026-06-04).
Probe: railway ssh into practical-renewal/production/api, ran scripts/reamaze_recent_rate_hairmnl.py
(delivered via heredoc; 2431 bytes confirmed in-container). Window: last 336h = 2026-05-20T22:59 to
2026-06-03T22:59 UTC (fully post-launch).
Result: 87 conversations with customer activity vs 81.06 expected from the 5.79/day baseline
= ratio 1.07x (+7%). Pages walked: 8. Read-only (counts + integration-cred read; no writes).
+/-15% band is 0.85-1.15x -> 1.07x is IN-BAND. Rate is slightly UP, not down -> no evidence
customers can't find/reach chat post-defer. CRITERION 3 SATISFIED.
Caveat: window is the most-recent 14 days (recent-rate script anchors to now), not the exact
2026-05-17..05-31 sustain window, but it is entirely post-launch and directly comparable.

meu.6 REMAINING: criterion 5 only — T+28d clean mobile CrUX LCP read (~2026-06-14) + deck/roadmap
doc landing. (railway login was NOT needed — already authed as jjoson@clickcommerce.digital.)

title: Wave 6 (closeout): epic-audit on Reamaze defer diff
description:
Wave 6 (closeout): epic-audit on the Reamaze defer-on-interaction diff.

Mandatory per the global closeout rule — epic meu produced an executable diff
(layout/theme.liquid +304, snippets/reamaze-placeholder.liquid +58; 362 insertions,
0 deletions; baseline b74b045 = parent of 3e15417, HEAD d6efd9e).

Command (run from repo root):
  git tag -f audit/reamaze-defer/baseline b74b045
  ~/Projects/epic-audit/epic-audit.sh reamaze-defer
Report: audit-reports/reamaze-defer-<YYYYMMDD>.md ; baseline tag audit/reamaze-defer/<date>.

Phase 3 framing: focus on the inline guard's script-interception surface
(createElement override, MutationObserver, script replay onto document.head),
GA4 dataLayer event correctness, and interaction effects with other inline scripts
in theme.liquid. NOT re-litigating the defer design itself.

Acceptance: audit ran + report committed; any code-changing findings filed as
follow-up bd issues (or escape-hatch: <3 code-changing follow-ups -> close won't-do
with triage tally).
notes:
GIT VERIFICATION (2026-06-04) for closeout. Audit ran on worktree HEAD d6efd9e (Pipeline 6,
live). Guard logic + placeholder confirmed BYTE-IDENTICAL to origin/main commit 487e0c9
(Pipeline 8 re-port, bd fsaa) — so the audit findings apply to BOTH live-P6 and main-P8.
Baseline b74b045 is on origin/main. d6efd9e is the P6 worktree/live commit (pushed to
origin/claude/kind-shaw-4100e5), intentionally not merged to main due to the P8 divergence.
Closing with --force: the work IS landed on main (487e0c9); only the commit hash differs,
because of the P8 re-port. The close-guard's documented override condition ("work genuinely
landed elsewhere") is satisfied.
close_reason: epic-audit ran clean (0 crit/high), report committed, 2 follow-ups filed (a6kr, rxh7). --force: cited worktree hash d6efd9e is P6-live; guard logic byte-identical to main 487e0c9 which IS on origin/main (bd fsaa re-port). Override condition (work landed elsewhere) verified.

title: [CLS] Desktop header collapse ~404px — cart .drawer__content renders in-flow at first paint (missing from critical CSS)
notes:
DIAGNOSIS 2026-06-01 (CC/Opus, from GSC CWV screenshots). GSC desktop CLS: 187 URLs need improvement, ~89% one group: blog articles CLS 0.20 (166 URLs, morenas example) + homepage 0.20 (20) + search page 0.11. Mobile is essentially solved (1,615 good, 1 NI = search page). ROOT CAUSE (Playwright desktop trace + poll): the cart drawer's .drawer__content renders position:STATIC (in-flow, ~372px) at first paint, inflating #shopify-section-header to ~474px; when the async-preloaded theme-core.css applies (~2.8s) it sets .drawer__content{position:fixed} (theme-core.css:14168) -> off-canvas -> header collapses to 70px -> MainContent jumps up ~420px -> CLS 0.85 worst-case (field p75 0.20). The mobile nav drawer (.header__drawer) is display:none in critical CSS so it's safe; the CART drawer was the gap (.drawer__content absent from snippets/critical-css.liquid). Explains why mobile CLS is fixed but desktop isn't. FIX: add .drawer__content{position:fixed;visibility:hidden;top:0;left:0} to the inline critical CSS (snippets/critical-css.liquid) so it's out of flow from first paint. No transform needed (visibility:hidden hides it; theme-core.css adds transform + --visible slide-in on open) -> avoids kt0. Verify desktop CLS trace 0.85->~0 on draft, then live.
FIX LIVE + SMOKE-TESTED 2026-06-01. Added .drawer__content{position:fixed;visibility:hidden;top:0;left:0} to snippets/critical-css.liquid. Pre-flight: live diff confirmed only my rule added (353-byte delta, no drift). Pushed live 131664707683 (--only --allow-live). SMOKE (Playwright, live, cache-busted): desktop homepage CLS 0.8762->0.0013, morenas blog ->0.0421, header 70px (not collapsed); cart drawer OPENS correctly desktop (drawer--visible, slid in left:683 w:683, renders content) AND mobile (drawer--visible, on-screen); mobile CLS still 0; .drawer__content position:fixed at rest; ZERO JS errors. kt0 lint: my file clean (10 violations are pre-existing theme-core.css uwfq backlog). NOTE: GSC/CrUX field (the 187 desktop URLs) will reflect this as the 28-day CrUX window rolls + Google re-validates — lab+live verified now.

title: OPERATOR: scope + lazy-load Judge.me — 220-446KB/21-96 reqs on EVERY page (incl blogs/home, no products)
notes: MEASURED 2026-06-01 (Playwright mobile, transferSize): Judge.me footprint per page type — home 220KB/21req, PDP 446KB/96req, collection 220KB/21req, blog 226KB/22req. Judge.me = product reviews; on BLOG pages (now 25% of traffic + the week's #1 LCP problem) and HOME it has no individual product to review = waste contributing to main-thread JS saturation (the blog-LCP + INP root cause, see 01vl + qx6x). ACTIONS (admin / Judge.me app settings, ranked): (1) DISABLE Judge.me on blog + non-commerce pages — certain win, -226KB/-22req on blogs directly. (2) PDP: enable Judge.me lazy-load / 'load reviews on scroll' — defers the 446KB/96-request eager load off the LCP/INP critical path (96 requests is the heaviest single contributor on PDPs). (3) Collection/home: confirm whether the star-rating badge / reviews carousel is actually used; if only badges, switch to Judge.me's lightweight badge mode (not the full review SDK). Verify each via re-trace after the change. Helps INP (qx6x) + blog LCP (01vl) sitewide. Operator action; may need Judge.me settings or support. CORRECTION 2026-06-01 (operator): medals MUST stay on ALL pages (incl blogs) — they are a deliberate trust element. The footer-medals gate I tested on the draft was REVERTED (rejected). Important distinction: the ~68KB footer medals are STATIC images (shop.metafields.judgeme.medals -> from judgeme-public-images imgix), rendered by sections/footer.liquid; they need NO Judge.me SDK to display. So 'show medals everywhere' and 'reduce Judge.me on blogs' are compatible: keep the static medals; the real blog target is ONLY the ~160KB Judge.me Theme App Extension SDK (cdn.shopify.com/extensions/.../judgeme*, loaded via the app embed / content_for_header). That SDK is NOT theme-gateable per-template — reducing it on blogs requires a Judge.me app-embed / app-settings control (admin), IF Judge.me supports per-page-type loading; if not, it loads globally as the cost of the app. Medals will keep rendering regardless. NET: no theme change here; this is admin-only via Judge.me settings, with medals preserved sitewide.

title: [za7u] Lazy-load embedded iframes in PDP product descriptions (eager ~1MB YouTube on video PDPs)
description: PDP descriptions with an embedded youtube iframe eager-load ~1MB of YouTube player on page load — raw iframe in server HTML (read_more/truncate path), re-triggered by theme.js truncateText/resizeEventTruncate + jQuery append = a load-time long task (hurts INP) + LCP/weight on every video PDP, even un-watched. NOT the media-gallery lite-youtube facade (the traced product has zero gallery video). Fix: lazy-ify description iframes at the source (product_description_content) in sections/product.liquid + section-product.liquid, mirroring the EXISTING pattern in page.liquid (_page_content_lazyiframe, line 45) and product-tabs.liquid (lines 27/229). stream:a. Serves za7u (defer YouTube on /products/*).
notes:
ATTEMPT 1 INSUFFICIENT 2026-05-31. Diagnosis refined: the eager ~1MB YouTube on davines-purifying PDP is a raw <iframe youtube> in the product DESCRIPTION (read_more path), NOT the gallery facade (this product has 0 gallery video). Tried loading=lazy on description iframes (product.liquid+section-product.liquid, mirroring page.liquid/product-tabs) -> pushed draft -> RE-TRACED: YouTube STILL loads. Why: (a) the iframe sits high in the description (within the browser lazy-load threshold) so loading=lazy doesn't defer it; (b) theme.js (build-source-blocked) re-inserts/re-loads it via ah/appendChild + jQuery append during truncateText/resizeEventTruncate. Reverted the lazy change (insufficient; clean tree). ROBUST FIX = convert description youtube <iframe> -> <lite-youtube> facade (click-to-load, position-independent) in the content post-processor + ensure lite-youtube.js loads on those PDPs + verify theme.js doesn't re-trigger. More involved; OR fix at theme.js build source. NOT a quick win.
ATTEMPT 2 VERIFIED ON DRAFT (140785582179) 2026-05-31. Robust fix = convert pasted description YouTube <iframe> -> <lite-youtube> click-to-load facade (reuses assets/lite-youtube.js, the theme's existing gallery-video facade).

- facade DOM: videoid=Cfqt8EwYTKw, hasWrapper=true, hasPoster=true.

- yt requests AFTER click: 12 + iframe appears + autoplay=1 (loads on demand, functional).
