title: Phase 2 CLS: investigate #MainContent late-injection sources (read-only)
description:
Parent: hairmnl-theme-987 (desktop CLS p75 0.20, 44% good)

GA4 RUM data shows ~160 CLS events where debug_target resolves to #MainContent or immediate children. These are the largest unaddressed CLS bucket on desktop.

### Goal
INVESTIGATION ONLY. No code fixes in this issue. Map which widgets/scripts inject content into #MainContent after initial render, causing layout shift.

### Investigation targets
1. LimeSpot recommendation strips (homepage, PDP, cart) — do they inject via JS after DOMContentLoaded?
2. Vertex AI rec rails — if any are wired to homepage or PDP, when do they insert?
3. Klaviyo embedded forms — any inside #MainContent that expand after JS hydration?
4. LoyaltyLion widgets inside page body (not just launcher)
5. Smart SEO / Judge.me review widgets that expand below product description
6. Reamaze chat widget if positioned inside #MainContent (unlikely but check)
7. Any Shopify app blocks (theme.liquid section-group) that have lazy JS init

### Output required (write to bd notes via bd update --append-notes)
- List each widget/source found, with:
  - CSS selector of the injected element
  - Approximate timing (before/after DOMContentLoaded, before/after load event)
  - Estimated CLS contribution (high/medium/low) based on element size + position
  - Recommended fix approach (reserve space via min-height, content-visibility, font-display:swap, etc.)
- Rank top 3 candidates for Phase 2 follow-up fix batches

### Files to read (no edits)
- layout/theme.liquid (section groups, app blocks)
- sections/featured-collection.liquid (LimeSpot integration points)
- snippets/ — any snippet that uses {{ app.block }} or JS app init
- assets/custom-theme.js — look for DOMContentLoaded / load handlers that inject content
- assets/theme.js (if present) — same scan

### Hard constraints
- READ ONLY. Do NOT write any code changes.
- Do NOT run shopify theme push.
- Do NOT close 987 (parent issue stays open until all Phase 2 fixes land).
notes:
Investigation-only dispatch. Cursor overnight chain 2026-05-12.
## hairmnl-theme-odv — #MainContent late-injection investigation (read-only)

### DOM boundary
- `#MainContent` = `<main class="main-content" id="MainContent">` in `layout/theme.liquid` (~779). Children: `{{ content_for_layout }}` (all route sections), then `<noscript>`, then `<limespot></limespot>` (unless `customers/*`), then BSS snippet renders — all still **inside** `<main>`.
- `{% section 'footer' %}`, `{% section 'popups' %}`, search popdown, `[data-modal-container]`, deferred `custom-theme.js`, Klaviyo listener, `{% render 'mbc-bundles' %}` are **after** `</main>` — GA4 `debug_target` on `#MainContent` usually means shifts attributed to an ancestor of the shifted node; some overlays attach under `body` but shift in-flow siblings still attributed upstream.

### 1) LimeSpot (Personalizer.io)
- **Selectors:** `<limespot></limespot>` (custom element); runtime DOM per theme docs / preview CSS: `[id^="LP_"]`, `[class*="LP_Inline"]`, `[class*="LP_GridContainer"]`, `[class*="LP_RecommendationsBlock"]`, `[class*="LimeSpot"]`, `[data-personalizer-fragment]`.
- **Timing:** `snippets/limespot.liquid` sets `window.LimeSpot` + `PageInfo` inline in `<head>`; SDK `edge.personalizer.io/.../storefront.min.js` loaded with **defer** (via app pipeline / theme). Population of `<limespot>` occurs **after** HTML parse when deferred script runs — effectively **after DOMContentLoaded ordering relative to parser**, often before `load` depending on network.
- **CLS estimate:** **Medium** (outer slot partially mitigated: `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` documents 0×0 inline unknown element → ~400px growth shifting footer; `limespot { display:block; contain:layout }` reduces **sibling** shift). **Residual:** internal Flickity/Swiper init can still reflow **inside** the slot (contained).
- **Fix approach:** Keep min-height/skeleton for worst-case rail height where needed; ensure images in injected cards have width/height; avoid adding new containing-block props on ancestors of fixed overlays (kt0 rule).

### 2) Vertex AI rec rails
- **Selectors:** `.vertex-rec-slot`, `[data-vertex-placement]`, `.vertex-buy-it-again` (when enabled).
- **Timing:** **Server-rendered Liquid** via `snippets/vertex-recommendations.liquid` / `vertex-buy-it-again.liquid` in sections (`sections/product.liquid`, `sections/cart.liquid`, `sections/section-banner-slider.liquid`, etc.). On **main** theme these renders are **commented out** (“Temporarily disabled on main until vertex snippet ships”).
- **CLS estimate:** **Low** when enabled (SSR HTML present at first paint); **N/A** on current main if stays disabled. Image lazy-load without dimensions could add **low–medium** shifts.
- **Fix approach:** Explicit image dimensions on cards; empty-state min-height when metafields nil.

### 3) Klaviyo
- **Selectors:** `body > [class*="klaviyo-form"]`, `body > [class*="kl-private-reset-css"]`, `body > [data-testid^="klaviyo-form"]` per `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` (e4a-γ) — **body-level** modal from App Embed; theme asserts **zero** `klaviyo-form` embeds in `sections/`/`snippets/`.
- **Timing:** After Klaviyo loader + form open — typically **post-DOMContentLoaded**.
- **CLS estimate:** **Low** for `#MainContent`-scoped shifts if forms stay body-only; **medium** if a future embed is placed in main (would push PDP content — PSI note in e4a-γ).
- **Fix approach:** Keep forms body-level; reserve overlay dimmer space; `font-display` for any Klaviyo webfonts if CLS ties to text.

### 4) LoyaltyLion
- **Selectors:** `#loyaltylion` (app embed root); `.lion-reward-item`, `.lion-reward-item__icon` on **pages/rewards**; header `.points-box` / `data-lion-points` (header is **outside** `#MainContent`).
- **Timing:** `snippets/loyaltylion.liquid` in `<head>`; rewards page `sections/page.liquid` uses **DOMContentLoaded** + `setInterval` polling until `#loyaltylion` exists, then mutates reward icons — **after** LL injects its widget into page body (inside `#MainContent` for that template).
- **CLS estimate:** **Medium** on `/pages/rewards` for icon swaps; **low** elsewhere unless in-body widgets added. Body notifications addressed separately (e4a-δ).
- **Fix approach:** Reserve card grid min-heights; replace polling with MutationObserver + single pass; static dimensions for reward icons.

### 5) Judge.me / Smart SEO
- **Judge.me selectors:** `#judgeme_product_reviews`, `.jdgm-widget.jdgm-review-widget`, `.jdgm-widget.jdgm-prev-badge`, `.jdgm-medals-wrapper`, `.jdgm-carousel-wrapper`, etc. (`snippets/judgeme_widgets.liquid`). Core `jdgm-settings-script` + `jdgm-script` via **`{{ content_for_header }}`** (theme comment ~726–732).
- **Timing:** Metafield HTML in Liquid first paint; **Judge.me JS** hydrates/expands (documented `.jdgm-hidden` → visible pattern in css-overrides). **After** deferred Judge scripts execute — typically **after DOMContentLoaded**.
- **Smart SEO:** Mostly **commented** meta wrappers in `layout/theme.liquid` / sections; `snippets/pluginseo-structured-data.liquid` is JSON-LD — **not** a late layout injector into `#MainContent`.
- **CLS estimate:** **High** for PDP `#judgeme_product_reviews` + preview badge row (large block below description); **medium** for grid `judgeme_preview_badge` on collection cards.
- **Fix approach:** Continue static stars + min-height patterns; `content-visibility` for below-fold review block; ensure widget container min-height matches hydrated content.

### 6) Reamaze
- **Selectors:** `#reamaze-widget-label`, `[id^="reamaze-widget"]` (css-overrides 7fz).
- **Timing:** Third-party embed after CDN script — **post-DOMContentLoaded**.
- **CLS estimate:** **Low–medium** (theme forces `position:fixed` + `contain:layout` on label/iframe hosts to stop flow participation). Unlikely “inside #MainContent” DOM placement; may still appear in GA4 as ancestor attribution.
- **Fix approach:** Keep fixed positioning; monitor if embed changes DOM structure.

### 7) Shopify app blocks + `content_for_header`
- **`{% when '@app' %}{% render block %}`** in `sections/product.liquid` (~129–130): any Online Store 2.0-style **app block** configured in Theme Editor can inject arbitrary HTML/JS **inside** the product column within `#MainContent` — **high variance**.
- **`{{ content_for_header }}`:** Injects app scripts (Judge.me, GTM, etc.). Late DOM is app-specific.
- **CLS estimate:** **High (unbounded)** for unknown app blocks; **medium** for known globals.
- **Fix approach:** Audit Theme Editor app blocks per template; reserve space per app docs; defer noncritical apps.

### 8) Other theme-identified injectors touching main flow
- **Zapiet Store Pickup:** `.product__pickup [data-store-availability-container]` — JS fills empty div (`theme.dev.js` pickupContainer). **Timing:** after deferred `storepickup.js`. **CLS:** mitigated with `min-height: 64px` + `contain:layout` on `.product__pickup` (e4a-α).
- **BOGOS / free gifts:** `#freegifts-main-page-container` in `sections/cart.liquid` (~35) inside main cart flow — **JS populates**. **CLS:** css-overrides BOGOS section (3uw/c8k) targets `#freegifts-main-popup-container`, `#sca-fg-notifications`. **Medium** on cart if inline glider shifts content.
- **Searchanise:** `//www.searchanise.com/widgets/shopify/init.js` **defer** in `layout/theme.liquid` (~172). Can enhance collection/search DOM — **medium** on listing pages inside main.
- **Bold Brain / MBC:** `{% render 'mbc-bundles' %}` after main — cart/bundle UI may still affect layout when interacting; mostly outside `#MainContent`.
- **custom-theme.js:** Multiple `DOMContentLoaded` handlers (e.g. `.cross-post-blogs` Swiper via IntersectionObserver ~8438+, `.metafield-multi_line_text_field` textContent strip ~8948+, cart consent modal ~8962+). **cross-post-blogs** lives in article template inside main — init on intersection can **resize** carousel — **low–medium** when scrolled into view.
- **theme.js / theme.dev.js:** Only `.main-content` usage found is **transparent header** feature detection selectors (~5314), not content injection.

### File scope note
- **`sections/featured-collection.liquid`:** **not present** in this repo (Pipeline uses other section names, e.g. collection templates). Investigation used `sections/collection.liquid`, `sections/product.liquid`, `layout/theme.liquid`, `snippets/*`, `assets/custom-theme.js` per bd.

---

### Top 3 Phase 2 follow-up fix batches (ranked)
1. **Judge.me** — PDP `#judgeme_product_reviews` + `.jdgm-prev-badge` / collection preview badges: largest predictable vertical hydration inside `#MainContent`; existing CLS fixes prove ongoing risk.
2. **LimeSpot** — `<limespot>` + runtime `LP_*` / `LimeSpot` grids on home, collection, PDP, cart: deferred SDK + rich carousel DOM; outer contain done, internal reflow + image metrics still fair game.
3. **Cart BOGOS + Searchanise** (tie-break batch) — `#freegifts-main-page-container` + Searchanise-enhanced collection DOM: route-specific late fill; second-order after Judge+LimeSpot unless GA4 shows cart/collection dominates.

---

title: Phase 2 CLS: scope-safe cart-template layout shift fix
description:
Parent: hairmnl-theme-987 (desktop CLS p75 0.20, 44% good)

GA4 RUM data shows ~40 CLS events whose debug_target is inside the cart template (#cart-template / .cart__te area). These are desktop-only events not addressed by the mobile sprint.

Suspected sources:
- Cart quantity input resize when JS loads (layout recalculation)
- Cart freegift widget injecting additional row(s) after initial render
- Cart upsell strips (LimeSpot/Vertex) inserting below line items
- Cart totals section shifting when discount code is applied (CSS-only reflow)

### Scope constraints (mandatory — regression lesson from xn4 first attempt 2026-05-12)
- Use BODY-SCOPED selectors: prefix ALL CSS rules with `body.template-cart` or target `.cart__te` directly. Pipeline 6 reuses layout classes (.grid, .grid--uniform, etc.) across templates — unscoped CSS WILL break /blogs, /collections, etc.
- No JS changes in this issue. CSS containment only.
- Stage changes to draft theme 140785582179 only. Live push requires separate coordinator review.
- git diff output must show ONLY files in assets/custom-theme.css (and possibly snippets/ if a specific cart snippet is the target).

### Acceptance criteria
1. cursor-agent reports which specific selectors were added and why
2. git diff shows body.template-cart-scoped CSS only, no unscoped rules
3. Draft theme push succeeds (--theme=140785582179 --only=assets/custom-theme.css)
4. Manual visual check on draft cart page: no layout shift visible on load
5. No regressions on /blogs/hair-education grid (run a quick visual check)

title: Blog CLS 0.52 — pro-blogger related-articles thumbnails unsized (136 URLs poor bucket)
description: GSC mobile CWV "CLS > 0.25 (poor)" bucket on 5/10 has 141 URLs, 136 of which are /blogs/hair-education/purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines (CLS 0.52). The other 5 are /pages/hairmnl-studio (covered by c2k Phase A page.content fix). Root cause: snippets/pro-blogger.snippet.related-item.liquid (legacy "Pro Blogger" app, used for related-articles carousels at the bottom of blog posts) renders with NO width/height attributes (lines 5 + 20). The article thumbnails use Shopify CDN's `?height=400` query-param format (e.g. cdn/shop/articles/Untitled_design_29.png?height=400&v=...). The existing snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid only parses URLs with the _WIDTHxHEIGHT.ext pattern (e.g. _480x480.jpg) — it does NOT recognize ?height=400 query params, so these images bypass the dim-injection logic. 8 unsized images observed in the live HTML for this blog post: - 6 article thumbnails with ?height=400 (related-articles carousel) - 2 .responsive-wide-image thumbs (also article-related) Each loads asynchronously, each shifts in-flow content below it. Cascading shifts = CLS 0.52. The fix is CSS-only. Affects every blog post that renders pro-blogger related-articles (likely all of them — the file is part of the theme's standard blog layout chain).

title: PDP CLS+INP — Judge.me preview badge reservation + gallery zoom yield (884 URLs)
description:
GSC mobile CWV report (5/10) shows 884 of 1,231 CLS-failing URLs (72%) are query-param variants of /products/kerastase-blond-absolu-purple-shampoo-250ml. Same product also accounts for 884/884 (100%) of the INP > 200ms cohort. RUM CWV dashboard top_inp_targets confirms: PDP image gallery (flickity-slider > product__media > lazy-image.mobile-zoom-wrapper > img.srcset) is the #2 INP target site-wide.

Two distinct fixes, both PDP-wide (benefit every product page with reviews + every product page period):

## CLS — Judge.me preview badge space reservation (top of PDP)

The product page renders <div class='jdgm-widget jdgm-prev-badge' data-id='...' data-number-of-reviews='21' data-average-rating='4.95'> just below the product title. The div is initially EMPTY in server HTML — Judge.me's loader.js hydrates it with stars + "21 reviews" text post-load.

Without space reservation, the badge grows from 0 → ~24px tall, pushing the price, variant selector, ATC button, and entire below-fold content down. This is a TOP-OF-PAGE shift, which CLS scores heavily because it shifts a large area of the viewport.

Existing CSS only covers bottom-of-PDP medals wrapper (snippets/css-overrides.liquid:168). The top-of-page snippet has no reservation.

## INP — PDP image gallery zoom handler

The flickity main gallery's mobile-zoom-wrapper is responsible for 27 INP events in the last 7d (target: div.flickity-slider>div.product__media.is-selected>div>div.lazy-image.mobile-zoom-wrapper>img.srcset). When user taps to zoom, the synchronous tap-handler does too much work (init zoom-pswp lightbox, load high-res image, animate). Goal: bring single-tap p75 INP under 200ms.
design:
## Phase A — CLS fix (low-risk, high-leverage)

Add to snippets/css-overrides.liquid (after the existing jdgm-medals-wrapper rule at line 168):

```css
/* CLS fix YYYY-MM-DD <bd-id> — Judge.me preview badge top-of-PDP shift.
   <div class="jdgm-widget jdgm-prev-badge"> renders empty at PDP top
   below product title; loader.js injects stars + "N reviews" → grows
   from 0 → ~24px → top-of-page shift → 884 URLs failing CLS on
   kerastase-blond-absolu PDP grouping. Reserve typical badge height
   so the hydration is paint-only, not layout. contain:layout
   isolates internal star-count changes from cascading. */
.jdgm-widget.jdgm-prev-badge {
  display: inline-block;
  min-height: 24px;
  min-width: 100px;
  contain: layout;
}
```

Files: snippets/css-overrides.liquid (CSS-only, ~10 lines).

Verification: 24h post-deploy, GSC validation should rerun and the 884 affected URLs should drop out of CLS-failing cohort.

## Phase B — INP fix (more invasive)

Investigation needed:
1. Identify which JS handler is the synchronous slow path on mobile-zoom-wrapper tap. Likely candidates:
   - flickity click → cellChange recompute (heavy if many slides)
   - zoom-pswp PhotoSwipe init on first tap (lazy, 1-time cost)
   - mobile-zoom-wrapper handler in custom-theme.js (no sourcemaps — deferred per 3sr)
2. Strategies:
   - Defer PhotoSwipe library import until idle (currently loaded eagerly?)
   - Yield with requestIdleCallback inside the tap handler so the layout/paint can occur within INP budget
   - Use scheduler.postTask if available (not all browsers)
3. Validation: synthetic Lighthouse PWA audit + real INP rerun in 24h.

Files: assets/zoom-pswp.js (or wherever zoom is initialized), assets/theme.dev.js + assets/theme.js (mirror discipline per .opencode_hints).

Phase A unblocks GSC quickly. Phase B is a separate dispatch.

title: RUM dashboard LCP regression is GTM instrumentation, not perf — diff V141 vs V140 + fix
description:
RUM CWV dashboard shows LCP-good dropped from 84% (May 7) to 66% (May 10) — but this is an INSTRUMENTATION regression, not a real perf regression.

Evidence:
- N halved between May 7 → May 8: LCP 25,497→12,046 (-53%); CLS -46%; FCP -60%; TTFB -33%; INP -1% (FLAT).
- Only INP (interaction-fired) preserved volume; the 4 load-time metrics dropped 33-60%.
- top_templates.LCP shows 95.7% of events have template="(not set)" — custom dimension parameter is no longer being captured.
- Dashboard query at scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:384 silently drops events with metric_rating="(not set)" by only counting good/needs-improvement/poor — masks the real issue.
- Timing aligns exactly with c03ab92 (l3g GTM fix, Container Version 141 published May 6-7).

Hypothesis: GTM Container Version 141 republish inadvertently affected the web-vitals GA4 tag(s) — likely either trigger config or eventSettingsTable param mappings. Need to diff V141 vs V140 to confirm.
design:
## Investigation + fix plan

### Phase 1 — Diagnosis (read-only via GTM API)
1. List all GTM container versions; locate V140 (pre-fix) and V141 (post-fix l3g publish).
2. Diff V141 vs V140: enumerate every tag/trigger/variable that changed. Should be exactly tag 776 + DLVs 773/774/775 if the l3g fix was clean.
3. Locate the web-vitals tags (firing on Custom Event = LCP|CLS|INP|FCP|TTFB). Inspect their firing triggers, eventSettingsTable parameter mappings (especially `metric_rating` and `template`), and tag IDs.
4. Confirm whether the metric_rating + template params reach GA4 via DebugView or via the V141 export inspection.

### Phase 2 — Fix in fresh workspace
- Create a NEW workspace (NOT 197 or 190 or any leftover) from current live (V141).
- For each broken web-vitals tag:
  - Restore the broken parameter mappings to their V140 form.
- Create + publish a new Container Version. Coordinator self-publishes since the user authorized autonomous fix.

### Phase 3 — Defensive dashboard fix
- scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:384 — count events with metric_rating="(not set)" into a separate "unknown" bucket (or surface count alongside totals) so silent param failures are visible going forward.

### Phase 4 — Verify
- Wait 24h; rerun dashboard. LCP-good should rebound to ~84%; template column should populate.
- After 7d, legacy "(not set)" rows age out of the rolling window.

## Out of scope
- Re-publishing the l3g fix (it was intentional and correct — JS error tag still needs trigger 772).
- Further changes to the 3 orphan-trigger tags (4r8, t4m, miy) — separate bd issues.
acceptance_criteria:
- GTM Container Version diff (V141 vs V140) captured in bd notes; smoking gun confirmed.
- Web-vitals tag(s) restored to working state via new container version published from a fresh workspace.
- Dashboard query at line 384 updated to count or surface (not set) ratings (defensive).
- 24h post-fix dashboard refresh shows LCP-good >= 80% and template column populated with real Liquid template names (index, product, blog, etc.).
- bd issue closed with phantom-completion guard (git show HEAD --stat in notes).

title: /cart INP regression — gjv fix did not move needle (41% poor 4d post-fix vs 30% pre-fix)
description: gjv (commit 3a22d94, 2026-05-05) scoped cart--loading class to active row to fix style-recalc. Field data 4d post-fix shows /cart INP poor% WORSE: May 4 30% → May 9 41%. Hypothesis: third-party widget orchestration (LimeSpot, Klaviyo, BOGOS, Reamaze, sticky-bar) is the dominant cost, not the style-recalc gjv addressed. Need DevTools investigation on /cart with line items + qty stepper interaction.
design:
## Goal
Diagnose why /cart INP is worse 4 days post-gjv (41% poor) than the May 4 pre-fix baseline (30% poor). gjv (commit 3a22d94, 2026-05-05) scoped `cart--loading` class to active row instead of page wrapper — should have dropped style-recalc from O(N) to O(1) on each stepper click. Field data shows the fix did NOT move the needle.

## Hypothesis
Third-party widget orchestration on /cart is the dominant INP cost, not our cart-stepper handler:
- LimeSpot SDK (gated 2026-04-28 but cart still has widget mounts)
- Klaviyo signup popup (deferred 20 sec + 60% scroll, but still hydrates)
- BOGOS free-gift recompute on cart change (see bd "bogos-error" for separate bug)
- Sticky add-to-cart bar
- Reamaze widget hydration

The gjv fix addressed the WRONG thing — it fixed style-recalc from a Liquid-level class addition, but the real INP cost is JS ORCHESTRATION across these widgets when cart state changes.

## Investigation steps (Brave + Claude-in-Chrome MCP, mobile emulation)
1. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/cart with at least 2 line items in mobile emulation (Moto G Power, Slow 4G).
2. DevTools → Performance → Record. Click qty stepper. Stop recording.
3. In the timeline, identify the LONG TASK that immediately follows the click event.
4. Expand the long task: which JS frame dominates? Which third-party origin?
5. Cross-check with GA4 RUM `debug_target` for /cart — look for the most-attributed selectors beyond `#shopify-section-template--XXX__main` (which is the generic cart body wrapper).
6. Repeat for: drawer open, line-item remove, freegift add.

## Likely fixes (ranked by leverage)
- A. Defer BOGOS recompute to requestIdleCallback (don't block first paint after cart change)
- B. Debounce qty stepper clicks (250ms — most users are spam-clicking)
- C. Yield to main thread between cart-update tasks (`scheduler.postTask()` or setTimeout 0)
- D. Disable widget re-init on cart state change (rely on the widget's own state hydration)

## Files likely to modify (post-investigation)
- assets/theme.dev.js + theme.js — CartItems class, drawer logic
- snippets/* — third-party widget includes
- Possibly snippets/limespot.liquid, snippets/klaviyo-signup-script.liquid, BOGOS-related snippets

## Out of scope
- Reverting the gjv fix (the row-scoping is still correct; just insufficient)
- Major refactor of cart UX
- Vertex cutover (separate, gated on Decision 2)

## Wait for clean signal
The full 7-day RUM window post-fix closes 2026-05-12. Investigate via DevTools NOW (synthetic) and confirm the directional signal with the May 12+ data.
acceptance_criteria:
After investigation + targeted fix:
- /cart INP poor% drops from 41% → ≤15% (matches site-wide 81% INP good)
- CrUX mobile INP p75 holds at ≤251ms or improves
- No regression on cart functionality (qty steppers, drawer, line-item remove all work)
- Specific dominant blocker (per debug_target) documented in commit message + bd notes
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-05-10 — root cause identified.

## /cart INP attribution (last 14d, GA4 Data API)
37/41 (90%) of poor-INP events on /cart target the SAME selector chain:
  #shopify-section-template--XXX__main > div.cart__template > div.wrapper.pt2.pb4

The two different template--XXX values are just different Shopify section instances. The browser couldn't isolate a more specific child target — meaning the slow handler is bound far up the DOM tree (event delegation OR full-wrapper re-render).

## Static analysis — CartItems.updateCart() flow (assets/theme.dev.js:4116)

When user clicks a qty stepper, the click→paint critical path includes ALL of:

1. `cart.js` GET (read current state)               ← network 1
2. `cart/change.js` POST (mutate)                   ← network 2
3. `theme:cart:change` event dispatch — TWO other listeners run (line 1716 = header cart count badge, line 2572 = drawer state sync)
4. `?section_id=api-cart-items` GET (re-render HTML)  ← network 3
5. `this.items.innerHTML = …` swap (full DOM re-creation)
6. `initQuantity()` re-binds ALL input + remove handlers
7. `initUpsell()` runs ProductAddButton constructor
8. `?section_id=api-cart-subtotal` GET (re-render subtotal) ← network 4
9. `this.subtotal.innerHTML = …` swap

**4 sequential network round-trips + 2 innerHTML swaps + 3-5 listener cascades, all on the click→paint critical path.** That's the INP cost.

The gjv fix (commit 3a22d94) optimized step 6's per-row class addition (O(N) → O(1)) — a real but TINY contribution to the total chain. It correctly fixed what it targeted but didn't address the dominant cost.

## Recommended Phase 2 fix (bounded, ~50 LoC)

Replace 4 sequential fetches with 1 using Shopify's bulk-sections cart API:

```
POST /cart/change.js
Body: { id, quantity, sections: "api-cart-items,api-cart-subtotal", sections_url: "/cart" }
Response: { ...cart, sections: { "api-cart-items": "<html>", "api-cart-subtotal": "<html>" } }
```

Eliminates: 1 GET (cart.js prefetch), 1 GET (api-cart-items), 1 GET (api-cart-subtotal). Keeps: 1 POST. Returns the new cart state AND both rendered section HTML in one round-trip.

**Files:**
- assets/theme.dev.js — `updateCart` method (~lines 4116-4169) + `loadForm`/`updateTotal`
- assets/theme.js — minified mirror (find by string `cart/change.js`)

**Expected impact (provisional):**
- /cart INP poor% drops from 41% → <20% (matches site-wide ~19% poor)
- Removes ~3 round-trips of network latency from the critical path
- Stacks additively with the BOGOS empty-cart gate shipped 2026-05-10 (commit c745875)

## Wait to dispatch

The BOGOS gate shipped today (commit c745875). Its effect on /cart INP won't be measurable until ~2026-05-17 (full 7-day post-fix RUM window). Recommend:
1. Wait until 2026-05-13 (3 days post-BOGOS-gate) — enough to see directional signal on whether BOGOS was material
2. If /cart INP poor% is still ≥30%, dispatch the bulk-sections-API fix
3. If it dropped significantly (e.g. <20%), close o0z and let the BOGOS gate do its work

## Other potential follow-ups (defer)
- Debounce qty stepper for rapid clicks (~10 LoC, in initInputs)
- Defer `theme:cart:change` listeners to requestIdleCallback (~5 LoC)
- Avoid full innerHTML swap (only update changed quantity field) — too invasive for now

Author: Y9378348C <y9378348c@MacBook-Air-de-Y9378348C.local>
Date:   Sun May 10 00:34:17 2026 +0200

    fix(cart): bulk-sections POST replaces 4-fetch cascade on qty change (closes o0z)
    
    CartItems.updateCart() previously made 4 sequential network round-trips
    on every qty stepper click → cart.js GET (read state), cart/change.js

    critical path. INP attribution data (last 14d) confirmed 90% of /cart

    task is the dominant cost.
    
    Replaced with a single POST to cart/change.js with `sections` param.

    - Critical path: 1 POST (was 4 fetches)

    - 0 JS errors from this change (pre-existing WPM telemetry errors unrelated)

    - assets/theme.js (minified mirror — also eliminated the redundant

title: Investigate + fix mobile LCP on top friction blog posts (47–63% poor)
description: Top 2 friction pages by LCP poor% are blog posts on /blogs/hair-education/. Article hero preload already in place (9hw). LCP element must be something else — likely the first in-body image, not the hero. Need DevTools investigation in Brave to confirm, then ship a targeted preload/fetchpriority extension.
design:
## Goal
Identify the actual LCP element on the two top friction blog posts and ship a targeted fix. Article hero preload (issue 9hw, 2026-05-04) is already in place — so the LCP element on these blog posts is something OTHER than the hero.

## Affected pages (from GA4 RUM 7d, latest snapshot 2026-05-09)
- /blogs/hair-education/before-and-after-the-transformative-power-of-purple-shampoo (49 poor / 77 total = 63% poor LCP)
- /blogs/hair-education/rinse-out-conditioner-vs-leave-in-conditioner-what-s-the-difference (41 poor / 88 total = 47% poor)

## Investigation steps (Brave + Claude-in-Chrome MCP, mobile emulation)
1. Open each URL in Brave with mobile (Moto G Power, Slow 4G) emulation.
2. Open DevTools → Performance → Record a fresh page load.
3. Identify the LCP candidate from the timeline (highlight the LCP marker → element).
4. Hypothesis to test: largest body image is the LCP, NOT the hero. Article images are 480x480 from the post-processor — likely too small for hero-replacement role on mobile.
5. Cross-check with GA4 RUM event params (event=largest_contentful_paint, debug_target field).

## Likely fixes (ranked by leverage, choose after investigation)
- A. Inject fetchpriority="high" + preload on the FIRST in-body image (not just the article.image hero) — extends 9hw pattern.
- B. Lazy-load below-fold body images more aggressively (current: native loading=lazy; could add IntersectionObserver-driven decode hints).
- C. If LCP is text-on-cover (overlay over hero), reserve aspect-ratio earlier.

## Files likely to modify
- snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid (extend post-processor to inject fetchpriority on first image)
- sections/article.liquid (template-level preload if first body image's URL can be derived server-side)

## NOT dispatchable
This requires DevTools-based investigation which OpenCode (headless) cannot perform. Keep on CC + browser MCP.
acceptance_criteria:
After fix:
- /blogs/hair-education/before-and-after-...purple-shampoo poor% drops below 30% (was 63%)
- /blogs/hair-education/rinse-out-...vs-leave-in poor% drops below 30% (was 47%)
- CrUX field LCP p75 mobile improves (currently 3.375s; aim ≤2.5s within 28-day window post-fix)
- No CLS regression (top friction CLS pages unchanged)
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-05-10 — root cause confirmed via GA4 RUM attribution. ## LCP attribution (last 14d, both pages) Purple shampoo blog (67 poor LCP events): 28 events div.blog__article>div.blog__article__content.rte.center>div>img ← FIRST IN-BODY IMAGE 18 events div.article__card.section--image.text--white>div.image__fill.fade-in-child>picture>img.fade-in-child ← hero (already preloaded via 9hw) 16 events h1.blog__article-title ← title text 2 events drawer-cart, header logo, etc. Rinse-out vs leave-in blog (69 poor LCP events): 31 events div.blog__article__content.rte > div > img ← FIRST IN-BODY IMAGE 10 events blog body

text 10 events blog title h1 10 events hero picture/img ## Conclusion Hypothesis confirmed. The 9hw fix correctly preloads the hero image (the `article__card` element in attribution), but the FIRST IN-BODY IMAGE is becoming LCP on 42-45% of events. Why: the hero has a CSS fade-in animation that delays its paint registration, while the first body image renders without animation and wins the "largest contentful element" race despite loading later. ## Fix scope (offload-ready) In snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid, the existing post-processor loop runs offset:1 over ` Date: Sun May 10 01:06:34 2026 +0200 fix(blog): fetchpriority=high + loading=eager on first body image (closes 7tq) GA4 RUM attribution (last 14d) on the two top-friction blog posts showed the first in-body image is the LCP candidate on 42-45% of poor LCP events — outpacing the hero (already preloaded via 9hw). Why: hero has CSS fade-in animation that delays its paint registration; the unanimated body image wins the largest-contentful- element race despite loading later. Fix: inside snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid post-processor loop, branch on forloop.first. First iteration injects fetchpriority=high + loading=eager. Subsequent iterations inject loading=lazy (existing behavior). decoding=async stays universal. Stacks additively with the hero preload from 9hw. Brave smoke test verified on /blogs/hair-education/before-and-after-...: 1st body img: fetchpriority=high, loading=eager, decoding=async, width=480 2nd body img: fetchpriority=null, loading=lazy, decoding=async 3rd body img: fetchpriority=null, loading=lazy 11 images total — only the 1st gets priority hints. Out of scope (deferred to follow-up if needed): - Disabling hero CSS fade-in on blogs (alternative path) - Body image responsive srcset (existing dimension injection covers CLS) - First-image priority on non-Shopify-CDN images (current logic gates injection on _w/_h dimension parsing — covers Shopify CDN _NNNxNNN pattern which is what the affected pages use) Expected impact (post-fix RUM, full window 2026-05-17): - Purple shampoo blog poor% drops from 63% → <30% - Rinse-out vs leave-in blog poor% drops from 47% → <25% - CrUX mobile field LCP p75 should improve over 28-day rolling window (currently 3.375s POOR, target ≤2.5s GOOD) Note on dispatch: tried via OC sub-orchestrator (/hh-orchestrate) twice; both attempts hung at the lead-architect step (free-tier minimax-m2.5 contention with concurrent dispatches in another project). Per CLAUDE.md 'two failed dispatches → escalate not retry', pulled back to CC. Fix is small enough (~10 LoC effective) that CC turnaround was faster anyway. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 .beads/issues.jsonl | 20 ++++++++++---------- snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid | 24 ++++++++++++++++++++---- 2 files changed, 30 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-) Pending RUM verification 2026-05-17 (full 7d post-fix window for blog poor% drop; 28d for CrUX field LCP).

title: GTM tag 415 (Klaviyo Form Submission Listener) references orphan trigger 2147479553 — email automation revenue tracking broken
description: Tag 415 in GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD live v140 references trigger ID 2147479553 which does not exist anywhere in the container. Tag tracks form submission events for Klaviyo email flows. Without a valid trigger, the tag cannot fire — email automation revenue attribution is broken (won't credit emails for purchases triggered after form submissions). Same root cause as bd hairmnl-theme-l3g: trigger was deleted in workspace cleanup but dependent tags weren't updated. Investigate which trigger Klaviyo expects (likely a Custom Event for klaviyo form events, OR All Pages). Coordinate with marketing/email-ops before reconnecting.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS UPDATE 2026-05-10T10:50:17Z — no GTM fix needed; misdiagnosis in original bd.

Trigger ID 2147479553 is GTM's BUILT-IN 'All Pages' trigger. Built-in triggers don't appear in workspaces().triggers().list() (why prior session flagged it as orphan) but ARE compiled into the live container.

Evidence:
1. Live gtm.js?id=GTM-M4NKSBD shows tag 415 compiled with predicate 1 = '_eq macro=0 arg1=gtm.js' — fires on every page load.
2. Klaviyo's GTM setup guide (Analytics Mania klaviyo-forms recipe; developers.klaviyo.com): 'Set this tag to fire on all pages.' Listener tags must attach early on every page.
3. Tag HTML pushes dl_subscribe to dataLayer on submit; downstream trigger 409 (Event - Klaviyo Email Form Submit) fires Klaviyo tags. Chain intact.

If GA4/Klaviyo email-attribution numbers look wrong in reports, the root cause is NOT this tag's trigger. File a fresh bd with the GA4 data showing the gap.

References: gtm-audit.py:100-101, scripts/gtm-inspect-orphan-trio.py

title: GTM tag 12 (Conversion Linker) references orphan trigger 2147479553 — GA4 conversion attribution broken
description: Tag 12 (Conversion Linker) in GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD live v140 references trigger ID 2147479553 which does not exist anywhere in the container. Without a valid trigger, the Conversion Linker tag cannot fire. Impact: all GA4 conversion attribution broken — last-click attribution fails, GCLID/braid cookies not set, cross-domain GA4 tracking degraded. The trigger likely existed in the 2019 workspace but was deleted without updating dependent tags. Fix requires deciding which valid trigger to reconnect (likely 'All Pages' or 'Initialization - All Pages'). Investigate via GTM admin UI; coordinate with marketing on which trigger semantics make sense. References bd hairmnl-theme-l3g handoff doc.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS UPDATE 2026-05-10T10:50:17Z — no GTM fix needed; misdiagnosis in original bd.

Trigger ID 2147479553 = GTM's built-in All Pages trigger. Live container shows tag 12 (Conversion Linker, type=gclidw) compiled with predicate 1 (gtm.js page load).

Vendor doc check (Google support.google.com/tagmanager/answer/7549390): 'In most cases, you should use the All Pages trigger to ensure that the linker tag is active on any potential landing page.' Tag is wired exactly as Google's docs prescribe.

Conversion Linker writes gclid/gbraid/wbraid to first-party cookies on landing. If Google Ads conversion attribution is actually showing gaps in reports, the cause is downstream (Google Ads ↔ GA4 linking, campaign URL tagging, consent gating, GA4 signals config), not this tag's trigger.

References: gtm-audit.py:100-101, scripts/gtm-inspect-orphan-trio.py

title: Fix GTM JS Error tag - wrong trigger + event name mismatch
description:
The GA4 - JS Error Event tag (GTM-M4NKSBD, live version 140) is misconfigured, causing GA4 to show (not set) for all error_type/error_message/error_source dimensions despite 40K+ js_error events.

**Root cause (confirmed by live GTM inspection, 2026-05-06):**
- Tag fires on: "Initialization - All Pages" (fires at page load, not on errors)
- Event name sent to GA4: "js_error" (old name)
- Theme pushes: "hairmnl_js_error" with fields error_type/error_message/error_source

**Result:** tag fires on every page load with empty dimension values, NOT on actual JS errors. The real error events (hairmnl_js_error) never reach the tag because the trigger is wrong.

**Also found:** 3 other critical tags have orphaned trigger ID 2147479553 (Conversion Linker, Klaviyo Form Listener, TrafficGuard) — these also need investigation.

**Fix required (GTM, not theme):**
1. Change GA4 - JS Error Event trigger from "Initialization - All Pages" → "Custom Event - hairmnl_js_error" (trigger 772)
2. Verify eventName is correct in tag parameters
3. Fix the 3 other broken tags (2147479553) 
4. Publish to live
5. Update dashboard query to also capture "hairmnl_js_error" events

**Dashboard also needs updating** (scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py): the JS error query filters eventName=js_error, but theme now sends hairminl_js_error. Need _in filter for both event names.

**Files involved:**
- scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py (dashboard query update)
- layout/theme.liquid (already correct - do not change)
- GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD (workspace 197) - fix via GTM UI or API

**Context:** theme was migrated 2026-05-02 (commit 5fb4be7) to hairmnl_js_error naming to avoid collision with Elevar's js_error. GTM trigger should have been updated at that time.
notes: Re-opened 2026-05-19: closed prematurely on 2026-05-06 as a handoff doc, but the GTM container fix was never published. Today's GA4 7-day window still shows 28,010 events with type='error' and message='(no message)' source=':0:0' — the exact symptom the handoff described. Tag 776 in live v140 still firing on trigger 2147479573 (WINDOW LOADED) instead of trigger 772 (Custom Event - hairmnl_js_error), per gtm-audit-js-error-2026-05-06.py. Re-executing the prepared fix script gtm-fix-js-error-tag-2026-05-06.py.
close_reason: Fix already live in GTM v143 since 2026-05-06. Tag 776 firingTriggerId:772 correct. v144 was 0-diff no-op. (no message) errors were cross-origin browser noise, suppressed by 8iom patch 2026-05-20. Re-open was misdiagnosis.

title: Lazy-init below-fold Swipers via IntersectionObserver to cut homepage INP
description: Homepage initializes 8 Swiper instances at DOMContentLoaded (7 .swiper.collection + 1 banner-slider). With 315 swiper-slides on the page, layout measurement + touch listener binding + initial positioning is serial main-thread work blocking the user's first interaction (97% of poor INPs are debug_target=(not set), i.e. TBT-bound). Strategy: keep above-fold (banner + first .swiper.collection) eager, defer remaining .swiper.collection instances until they enter a 200px-rootMargin IntersectionObserver. Implement as a wrap-and-override script in layout/theme.liquid so we don't touch the bundled custom-theme.js. Constraints: visual parity required, below-fold carousels must initialize before user scrolls to them, push to draft 140785582179 first.
notes:

.swiper.collection. On mobile that slider is below-fold (y~1500-2000px
on 320-414px viewport), so the safety net was wasted DCL work.

`top < innerHeight` viewport gate.

- Live theme 131664707683: PENDING user confirmation

VISUAL PARITY: above-fold sliders (rect.top < innerHeight) still eager;
below-fold use IO with 200px rootMargin so user never sees a blank
slider — behavior identical to ua7 except for the index===0 fallback
path. Banner-slider unaffected (separate eager init block at line 14330).

title: Verify Judge.me loader.js queries .jdgm-prev-badge vs .jdgm-preview-badge
description: Branch renames wrapper class from jdgm-preview-badge to jdgm-prev-badge. Open hairmnl.com/products/<any-product-with-reviews> in DevTools, find loader.js from cdn.judge.me, search source for both prev-badge and preview-badge. Whichever appears as querySelector argument is what loader hydrates. If loader queries the full word, click-to-scroll regression is permanent unless we add href wrapper. Blocks merge decision.
close_reason: Verified by fetching loader.js v490 from cdn.shopify.com/extensions/.../judgeme-490/assets/loader.js. Loader maps widget files to gating selectors; widget/main.js (the badge hydration code) is gated on '.jdgm-preview-badge, .judgeme-preview-badge' (full word, both spellings). Branch's renamed '.jdgm-prev-badge' (short) wrappers will NOT match — widget/main.js never downloads on pages with only preview badges. Bonus: even on PDPs with review widgets where loader fires for other selectors, the badge hydration JS is skipped. No race, no flicker, no double-render. Click-to-scroll regression is permanent (loader handler bound to .jdgm-preview-badge can't find renamed wrappers) — exactly what 68f41fb proactively fixed via <a href> wrapper.

title: J33: non-Vertex badges bigger than Vertex on MOBILE (web-team July 17) — cqw sizing inverts across breakpoints
description: Jessica July 17: 'Non-Vertex badges are still slightly bigger and more obvious on mobile' (green arrow at Best Sellers FREE GIFTS vs Vertex 10% OFF). ROOT CAUSE: J29's container-query (cqw) badge sizing ties size to CARD width, but the sliders' card widths INVERT between breakpoints — desktop Vertex rail cards (~179px) > Best Sellers grid cards (~159px) so Vertex badge bigger (approved); mobile 2-col grid cards (~175px) > Vertex peek-rail cards (~150px) so NON-Vertex badge ~1.4px bigger. Card-proportional sizing cannot be uniform when layouts flip. FIX (J33): @media(max-width:767px) pins a fixed uniform size (10px / 3px 8px) across all 6 slider badge selectors; desktop keeps the approved cqw sizing. Same specificity as J29 rule, later in source → wins on phones.

title: AUDIT-M: kt0 lint blind to container-type/container — add to FORBIDDEN_PROPS (+ epic-created overlays to OVERLAY_PATTERNS)
description: Phase4 closeout audit. scripts/check-overlay-css.py:64 FORBIDDEN_PROPS omits container-type/container although the kt0 class includes it and J29 introduced the repo first use (verified safe). A future copy onto a drawer/header ancestor passes CI silently — two prior production incidents in this class. FIX: add container-type/container (excluding normal/none) to the regex + docstring + CLAUDE.md list. ALSO (low, merged): OVERLAY_PATTERNS never gained the epic-created overlays — [data-qb-popover], .vrec-product-sticky-bar, .sticky-cart-bubble, .vrec-fly-clone; add them (kt0-OK ack path handles false positives). J29 selectors match no overlay pattern so lint stays green without new acks.
close_reason: FIXED (9eccb2e): lint gained container-type/container props, the epic's own overlays in OVERLAY_PATTERNS, a lookahead-backtracking fix (latent in the ORIGINAL exclusions), and — the big one — @media/@supports DESCENT: the parser attributed whole media bodies to the @media header, so EVERY media-nested rule was invisible (docstring claimed otherwise). Hardening immediately caught 5 unseen sticky-bar transform rules — all legitimately acked (transform is the overlay's own slide; QB popover portals to body). End-to-end verified: nested injection flags exit 1, clean pass exit 0, reported line numbers correct.

Callsite (sections/vertex-recommendations.liquid:52) arg-compatible. DEFERRED (post-cutover refactor): converting vertex-recommendations' inline copy to render the shared snippet — that rail is device-verified for cutover; comment in both files says KEEP IN SYNC. Still dormant until the precompute job is scheduled — guards now land first, as required.

title: AUDIT-M: related.liquid never populates on storefront (kr-product) — section:lazy-rendered has zero listeners
description: Phase4 closeout audit. hairmnl-common.js:387 dispatches section:lazy-rendered; no listeners repo-wide, theme.js section registry inits once at DOMContentLoaded (re-init designMode-gated). product.kr-product.json (only template with the section enabled) shows a permanent ~400px empty You-might-also-like band + dead recently-viewed rail. FIX: eager-render sections/related.liquid via the 2i8b.35 if-true pattern. NOTE dispatching shopify:section:load would NOT fix it.
close_reason: FIXED (9eccb2e): related.liquid gate -> if true (2i8b.35 eager pattern) — the lazy path could never populate (section:lazy-rendered had no section-init listeners). Eager content exists at DOMContentLoaded so theme.js's generic section registration initializes the tabs. theme check clean. Verify visually on a product using template product.kr-product.

close_reason: FIXED (9eccb2e): line-18 '{-%' -> '{%-' (theme check clean); mobile filter toggle bound at lazy-hydration via section:lazy-rendered (dispatched from the hydrated section, bubbles — verified) with idempotency marker + initial mobile hide; scoped to lazy sections so the 29 eager collection-* sections where shop.js binds are untouched. The file's two #toggleFilters are in mutually-exclusive render branches (design/SRA vs placeholder). Device-verify on a phone: any brand page <=768px, tap FILTERS.

title: AUDIT-M: facet-AJAX trio in hairmnl-collection.js — Back button leaves filtered grid; sticky filter offset lost after swap; nested shopify-section wrappers
description: Phase4 closeout audit. (1) :150-152 popstate gated on e.state.facetState, initial entry never replaceStated — Back from filtered view keeps filtered grid under unfiltered URL. FIX: history.replaceState before first pushState or reload on any popstate post-swap. (2) :24/36 writes sticky top offset onto .collection__nav that the innerHTML swap (:112) destroys — after any filter on <=600px the bar falls to top:0 under the z-5000 header. FIX: publish offset as CSS custom property on documentElement, top: var(--collection-nav-top,0). (3) :112 injects raw SRA response incl its own shopify-section wrapper — one nested duplicate-id wrapper per interaction. FIX: DOMParser unwrap, inject only doc.getElementById(sectionEl.id).innerHTML.
close_reason: FIXED (9eccb2e): sticky offset now a root CSS custom property (--collection-nav-top, read by css-overrides .collection__nav) so it survives facet-AJAX swaps; SRA response unwrapped via DOMParser (sectionEl.id verified = shopify-section-<id> wrapper in the response); initial history entry stamped via replaceState so Back-to-unfiltered reloads. Verified inline: JS syntax, var-name match JS<->CSS, sectionEl derivation traced. Facet interaction untestable in the CC browser (env fetch block) — smoke on a phone or at cutover.

title: J19 PDP: recolor add-to-cart (#322E34) + selected variant pill (#CAD1D3) — P8 only
description:
Web-team deck (2026-06-17, "TO ADDRESS"): Product Page button background colors.
- ADD TO CART button → #322E34 (currently terracotta = --color-primary). White text stays (readable on dark).
- VARIANT button → #CAD1D3 (arrow points to the SELECTED pill "90ML", currently dark var(--text-dark)). Interpreted as the SELECTED variant pill; needs dark text since white is unreadable on light grey.

Mechanism: .btn uses background-color:var(--btn-bg)/var(--btn-bg-hover); .btn--primary sets --btn-bg:var(---color-primary). Selected variant = .radio__fieldset .radio__button input:checked ~ label (bg var(--text-dark)). Override both with targeted CSS in css-overrides (P8 only; never live per standing constraint). MIGRATION-DEBT tagged + customization-audit row. Port difficulty EASY.

Apply to: P8 draft 141168312419 + STKY-free test theme 141655801955. NOT live.
notes: DONE + VERIFIED on P8 draft 141168312419 (origin/main f9220cb). Computed colors exact: ADD TO CART bg #322E34 / text #FFFFFF (contrast ~13:1); selected variant pill bg #CAD1D3 / text #0B090D (contrast ~14:1). Visual confirm: selected '50G' grey pill clearly distinct from white unselected '85G'. Also pushed to STKY-free test theme 141655801955 (same css-overrides). NOTE: BUY IT NOW left terracotta (deck specified only ADD TO CART) — flagged to operator in case they want it matched. Variant interpretation: SELECTED pill recoloured (arrow pointed at selected '90ML'); confirm if they meant ALL variant pills.
close_reason: Verified on P8 draft (origin/main f9220cb): ADD TO CART #322E34, selected variant pill #CAD1D3, both with readable contrast. Pushed to both P8 themes; never live.

AI-referred visitors arrive mid-intent: cited pages need an immediate answer-confirm block + visible ATC (a7av sticky bar is PDP-live) + review visibility + cross-sell. 63% mobile.
notes:
ZERO-CVR DIAGNOSIS 2026-06-11 (90d data): CLAUDE = country mismatch — 11/12 sessions non-PH desktop (JP/US/EU) on a PHP/PH-only store; NOTHING TO FIX. PERPLEXITY+COPILOT = landing-mix — 85-100% of sessions land on /blogs/ listicles they cite (sulfate-free, leave-in guides), engage well (33-42s) but never reach a PDP: funnel 11/1/1/0 (P) and 0/0/0/0 (C) vs ChatGPT 684/92/126/15. FIX SPEC (needs web-team one-shot approval, same pattern as the interception deck): 'Shop the products in this guide' product-card module on the ~6 AI-cited articles + PDP internal links. ChatGPT side confirmed transactional: 66% PH, 34 checkout-resume landings, begin_checkout 126 > ATC 92.
BUILT ON DRAFT 2026-06-11 (round 2): sections/section-shop-the-guide.liquid (link cards, zero JS, CLS-safe imgs, + content-matching ItemList schema) wired into article.faq-sulfate-free.json (6 cards) + article.faq-keratin-vs-rebond.json (4 aftercare cards) — DRAFT 140785582179 only, render-verified (0 liquid errors), live confirmed untouched (0 cards). Preview: article URL + ?preview_theme_id=140785582179. On approval: push section + 2 template JSONs live (note templates are admin-managed — re-pull before live push).
SHOP-THE-GUIDE PRODUCTS REVISED per operator 2026-06-16 (draft-verified, live untouched). SULFATE-FREE (6): 1.NOUNOU 2.Chroma Absolu 3.OI 4.K18 Damage Shield pH Protective Shampoo[NEW, replaces Davines Calming] 5.Purifying 6.LOreal Absolut Repair Molecular Sulfate-Free Shampoo[NEW, replaces Davines LOVE Smoothing]. KERATIN-VS-REBOND (4->6): 1.Davines LOVE Smoothing 2.Kerafill Daily Shampoo 3.Argila Amazonia Smooth-Prolonging 4.Olaplex No.4 Bond Maintenance 5.Davines NOUNOU 6.K18 Damage Shield. All handles resolved against live catalog + available-verified. Docx Appendix A updated to match. Still awaiting one-shot approval to push live.
SHIPPED LIVE 2026-06-16 (operator-approved): the 'Shop the guide' module is now LIVE on both AI-cited guides — sulfate-free listicle (6 cards) + keratin-vs-rebond (6 cards), operator-revised product lists. Pre-flight: read live template assets via Admin API (CLI pull flaky) — confirmed the push ONLY adds the shop-guide section, zero admin-section clobber, no drift. Live-verified uncached: 6 cards each in exact spec order, ItemList schema renders, 0 liquid errors, no a7av leak; regression smoke clean (home/cart/pdp/other-blog 200 + 0 errors); kt0 PASS. This closes WS4 — the zero-CVR diagnosis (Claude=geo, Perplexity/Copilot=landing-mix) is now actioned with the PDP path the diagnosis prescribed. Migration-debt logged in customization-audit.md. P8 port = follow-up (MIGRATION-DEBT tagged in the section).

title: Cart lab LCP 30-37s = BOGOS sca-promotion-glider late paint (both themes) — field-check, then static-first-slide or annotate as artifact
description:
EVIDENCE (2026-06-10 PSI matrix): ALL 12 cart cells (P6+P8 x mobile+desktop x 3 runs) have LCP element = div#sca-promotion-glider > .glider-track > p.content-promotion-message (the Secomapp/BOGOS free-gift promo ticker, "FREE Tote Bag when you purchase P4,500..."). Mobile 29-37s, desktop 6-7s. On a lab-EMPTY cart the rotating glider text is the largest paint and keeps emitting late LCP candidates -> grossly inflated lab LCP. Real-user carts have line items/images as LCP, so this is substantially a LAB ARTIFACT — but the glider also paints genuinely late (app embed: cdn.shopify.com/extensions/.../freegift).

PLAN: (1) FIELD-CHECK FIRST — pull /cart LCP p75 from GA4 RUM (scripts/web-vitals-report.py, property 248106289) + CrUX; if field LCP is healthy, annotate the PSI matrix (cart lab LCP = known artifact) and stop. (2) If a fix is warranted: server-render the first promo message statically (text paints with HTML) or pause glider rotation until first interaction — do NOT defer the app embed wholesale (merchandising banner). Note BOGOS Family-B defer guard (bogos.io) is separate from this theme-app-extension surface.
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-10 per the ticket's decision tree. FIELD CHECK (GA4 RUM, 28d): /cart LCP 79.2% good overall (above target); mobile 69.2%. The glider IS a small real field factor (19/211 mobile LCPs, 31.6% good / 26.3% poor when it wins) BUT its text is app-injected via deferred extension scripts — no legitimate theme-side early-paint (hardcoding promo copy rejected: merchandising drift). ACTIONS: (1) lab artifact annotated in scripts/psi-baseline-matrix.py cart cell + the 06-10 report — future matrix runs won't re-flag; (2) real fix routed to ur0 (BOGOS replacement — field data strengthens the case + $420/yr). Cart line-item rows (top field target, 67.3% good) = shared render-start program, thumbs already eager.

title: STKY parity extras: skip-to-checkout + fly-to-cart animation
description:
Web-team review follow-ups (STKY Add to Cart - May 18). Two STKY app extras the user asked to carry over for full parity:
(A) SKIP TO CHECKOUT on the sticky ATC bar — STKY had an optional toggle to send the shopper straight to checkout instead of adding+staying. Implement as a theme setting (config/settings_schema.json, default OFF) so the operator opts in (faithful to STKY). When ON: the sticky-bar button becomes "Buy it now" and on click adds the selected variant+qty via /cart/add.js then redirects to /checkout (snippets/product-sticky-bar.liquid + inline JS / hairmnl-common.js).
(B) FLY-TO-CART animation — STKY animated the product image flying to the sticky cart on add. Add a reusable flyToCart() in assets/hairmnl-common.js hooked into the [data-vrec-add] success path (covers quick-buy + rec cards): clone the source card image, animate (transform translate+scale+fade) to the [data-sticky-cart] bubble, remove after ~600ms, bump the bubble. Respect prefers-reduced-motion (skip). kt0: transient fixed clone with no fixed/absolute descendants — safe; add kt0-OK note.
Acceptance: skip-to-checkout setting toggles the bar button + redirects to /checkout when on; fly animation plays on quick-buy add (not under reduced-motion); kt0 clean; pushed to draft 141168312419; browser-verify.
notes:
Built + pushed to draft 141168312419: (A) Skip-to-Checkout theme setting (Cart > Sticky add to cart bar, default off) + Buy-it-now button + [data-vrec-checkout] handler (add.js -> /checkout); (B) fly-to-cart animation in flyToCart() hooked into [data-vrec-add], reduced-motion-aware, bumps the bubble. Operator playbook updated (parallel-run note). kt0 clean; JS/JSON syntax-checked. title: Branded collection grid (Shop the Range) gaps at some viewport widths — float-grid catch
description: Live P6 branded collection grids (e.g. /collections/kerastase-genesis 'Shop the Range') show large vertical gaps at certain viewport widths (~900px per operator screenshot 2026-06-05; renders fine wider). Root cause: collection-<brand>.liquid (collection-krgenesis.liquid etc.) use Pipeline float-based grids (.grid-uniform / 3x3_grid_tile / collection-branded with .product-grid-item, float:left) WITHOUT height-equalization (no image_autoheight_enable on these grids). Unequal card heights (MOST LOVED / 30pct SAVINGS badges, review counts, From-price vs fixed price) make shorter cards catch under taller neighbours -> gaps. NOT caused by FAQ work (gwct, article-scoped) or recent P8-migration card/font changes (2i8b.56/57/61 verified NOT on live; only old 2i8b.36/a7av.8 markers on live css-overrides; migration-contract held). Likely pre-existing. FIX OPTIONS: (a) add height-equalization to branded grids (JS equal-height or CSS), or (b) switch the branded grid items to flex/inline-grid so unequal cards do not float-catch. Verify at ~900px viewport. stream:a. Live push: --only + --allow-live + pre-flight diff.

title: P8 cart page: move Vertex slider before order summary
description: Phase 5 visual QA (doc images #16-17). On the cart PAGE, the Vertex "Recommended for You" slider currently renders AFTER the checkout button / referral / special-instructions block. It should appear BEFORE the order summary. Fix: reorder the Vertex recommendations section in the cart template/section.
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-06-04: cart page section order is [main(cart), vertex_cart_recs, recent_products]. The order summary (subtotal/checkout) lives INSIDE the main cart section's <form> (sections/cart.liquid: form opens line 30, closes line 205; footer/order-summary at line 70). The vertex placement is literally named 'cart_above_checkout' (intent = above checkout) but it's a separate top-level SECTION after main, so it renders below the whole cart. CONSTRAINT: cannot insert the recs before the footer because the cart is ONE <form> and vertex-rec-card.liquid uses {% form 'product' %} per card → nesting <form> inside <form> is invalid HTML. So B4's fix is COUPLED to the rec-card ATC mechanism (make it AJAX/formless, then it can render inside the cart form before the footer) — same dependency as B3 (cart-drawer ATC buttons) and the STKY replacement. GROUPING B4 with the native-ATC workstream (B3 + STKY) rather than a hacky standalone fix.
Resolved by a7av.10 (native rec-card AJAX ATC). Once the rec card is formless (AJAX), the recs can render inside the cart <form> before the footer/order-summary without nesting <form>s; a7av.10 also moves the render into cart.liquid before the footer + drops the standalone vertex_cart_recs section from cart.json. Blocked on a7av.10.
Fixed by a7av.10 (commit 8bd54f4): recs now render in cart.liquid after line items / before the order summary (formless cards, no <form> nesting); standalone vertex_cart_recs section removed from cart.json. Browser-verify with items in cart.

title: [hardening] Reamaze defer-guard: scope createElement override, consent-gate GA4, drop click listener on disarm
description:
Non-blocking hardening follow-ups for the Reamaze defer guard (layout/theme.liquid ~L830),
surfaced by epic-audit: audit-reports/reamaze-defer-20260604.md. The guard has run at 100%
for 18+ days with zero reported interference; these are future-proofing, not active bugs.

A (Medium) — Global document.createElement override is never restored. It only defers
  cdn.reamaze.com src-sets (everything else passes through) but redefines the src descriptor
  on every script element created via createElement, and stays patched after disarm. Risk:
  future interop/timing surprises with other apps/analytics. Fix: restore the original
  createElement after disarm (or after a bounded load-phase window); the MutationObserver is
  the primary interception mechanism and already covers the static content_for_header embed.

B (Low) — dl() GA4 events (reamaze_load_path, reamaze_load_latency_ms, reamaze_widget_open,
  reamaze_widget_message_sent) fire without checking consent state. If a CMP is in use, gate
  the pushes (e.g. window.consentState && window.consentState.analytics) or rely on GTM
  consent-mode. ACTION FIRST: confirm HairMNL GTM/consent setup — may already be handled downstream.

C (Low, cosmetic) — the document click listener can still call e.preventDefault() after disarm.
  Placeholder is hidden on disarm so it is unlikely; tidy via removeEventListener on disarm.

Scope cap: all one file (layout/theme.liquid), <120 LoC — OC-offloadable as a single increment.
kt0 CSS rule + push-verification rules in CLAUDE.md apply.

title: [CLS] /cart mobile CLS ~0.15 — theme.js relocates upsell-holder items->footer late
notes: DIAGNOSIS 2026-06-01 (CC/Opus, from weekly game-plan item 3). /cart RUM mobile CLS 30.8% poor (n=315). Reproduced via Playwright (4xCPU+slow4G, item added via /cart/add.js): CLS 0.1495 (NI). Attribution: dominant shift v=0.089 (~60%) at t=3790ms = .template__cart__footer grows 162->356px as <div class=upsell__holder [data-upsell-holder]> is inserted at footer top; remaining ~0.06 is the cascade reflow (main section +38px, items qty/price). ROOT: theme.js initUpsell() (theme.dev.js:4276) server-renders the upsell inside cart items (cart-line-items.liquid:173 render 'upsell-product'), then MOVES it to the footer top late (post-async .then, ~3.8s) via this.bottom.insertBefore(upsellProduct, bottom.firstChild). The move (remove-from-items + insert-into-footer) is the CLS. CLEAN FIX RULED OUT: cannot server-render in footer — initUpsell does oldUpsellProduct.remove() on any footer copy, and the Add button (isCartItem cart-refresh behavior) only wires when found in this.items. Fixes available are (a) hand-edit minified theme.js initUpsell (fragile; theme.dev.js is debug-only, prod=theme.js) or (b) bespoke re-implement the upsell card (P6 migration debt + risk breaking cart-context add-to-cart). NOT worth the revenue risk for a borderline-NI lower-traffic page. SAME theme.js-bundle blocker as blog LCP (01vl) + mobile INP (qx6x). Durable lever = theme.js reduction / P8 migration (2i8b, ujg6.14). Recommend: defer until that lands; do NOT hand-edit the bundle for this.

title: [FAQ] Write comprehensive FAQ Q&A doc (~20 guides, brand-voice) — 1-shot approval
description: Produce ONE doc with 3-5 brand-voice Q&As per candidate page (the ~20 in the epic), grounded in each article's actual content (read each before writing — no boilerplate; FAQPage must be genuine Q&A or it risks thin-schema penalty). Use the brand-voice skill/guidelines for tone. Format: structured doc (markdown or .docx) with one section per page = page URL + its Q&A pairs, ready for paste into section-faq blocks. Single approval gate. Optional: fan-out across the 20 articles via a workflow for speed/consistency (needs explicit opt-in).

title: Epic: FAQPage schema program — top ~20 GEO blog guides
description:
FAQPage schema program — add FAQPage JSON-LD (+ visible Q&A accordion) to HairMNL's top blog guides for GEO/AI-citation lift. FAQPage is the most-cited schema type in AI-generated answers; HairMNL currently has it on only 1 of its top 45 blog articles (an-ultimate-guide-to-anti-dandruff, 1617 views).

MECHANISM (verified, iaxt.5 trace): the theme section sections/section-faq.liquid emits BOTH a visible accordion AND FAQPage JSON-LD (mainEntity->Question->acceptedAnswer->Answer) from section BLOCKS (block.settings.title=question, block.settings.content=answer). Stock OS2.0 section -> freeze-friendly. Per-article: each guide needs its own section-faq instance with that article's Q&A (blocks are template-scoped; the anti-dandruff article carries its own).

SCOPE DECISION (data 2026-05-30, GA4 180d + FAQPage probe of top 45 blog articles):
- 44 of top 45 lack FAQPage.
- Traffic cliff: top ~10 = 900-3270 views; #10-20 = 500-900; #20-45 = 250-500 (long tail).
- RECOMMENDATION: ~20 pages, NOT 50. Rationale: (a) traffic cliff — top 20 capture the bulk of blog traffic + AI-citation likelihood; (b) QUALITY/spam risk — FAQPage must be genuine Q&A users ask; forcing FAQs onto 50 incl. thin style/trend pieces invites thin-schema penalties; (c) execution cost — each is a per-article theme-editor addition; 20 is a manageable batch with the highest ROI; (d) prioritize commercial/comparison/how-to intent over style/trend.

CANDIDATE SET (~20, FAQ-worthy + FAQ-less + high traffic; year-duplicate listicles canonicalized to the primary):
sulfate-free-shampoos(3270), rebonded-hair-summertime(2093), top-anti-hair-fall-shampoos(2069), keratin-vs-rebond(1992), best-hair-color-morenas(1961), serioxyl-vs-minoxidil(1358), volumizing-for-thin-fine-hair(1207), balayage-vs-ombre-vs-sombre(932), purple-vs-blue-shampoo(872), low-porosity-hair(680), kalawang-hair-blue-toners(656), rebonding-101(579), understanding-SLS(475), salon-color-vs-box-dye(437), rinse-out-vs-leave-in-conditioner(429), brazilian-blowout-aftercare(391), top-5-heat-protectant-sprays(385), hair-breakage-fix-damaged(352), best-serums-frizzy-hair(340), best-shampoos-color-treated(321), dry-scalp-vs-dandruff(320).

DELIVERABLE: a single comprehensive FAQ doc (3-5 brand-voice Q&As per page, grounded in each article's actual content) for 1-shot approval, then per-article theme-editor application + Rich-Results verification.

Origin: GEO audit iaxt.5 (superseded/expanded by this epic). stream:a.
notes: APPROVED SCOPE 2026-06-04: 24 pages (12 blog + 6 collections + 6 PDPs), method = git-committed alternate templates dev-first, 3 waves (blog -> collections -> PDPs) with Rich Results verify per wave. Wave 1 (12 blog) templates generated + validated.

title: [GEO] Wave 2 (closeout): re-run GEO audit + epic-audit on diff
description:
[Closeout] Wave 2 (closeout): re-run the Technical GEO audit + epic-audit on the epic's theme diff. BLOCKED until iaxt.1-iaxt.5 close (dep edges added; bd 1.0.3 requires explicit --status=blocked).

Phase-3 framing: focus on fix-regressions / incomplete fixes / interaction effects (e.g., did a schema JSON-LD block break existing Smart SEO output? did robots.txt.liquid drift get reconciled? did llms.txt edits stay valid markdown?).
