Confirms the remediation worked — cross-engine AI citations + #1-4 rankings + GSC +15% through the May core update. Use as the epic-audit/closeout measurement input.

title: [GEO] Add FAQPage JSON-LD to blog/buying-guide posts
description:
[Tech/Marketing] AUDIT REC #5. Add FAQPage JSON-LD to blog / buying-guide posts (hair damage repair, hair growth, scalp health, purple-vs-blue shampoo, etc.). FAQPage is the most-cited schema type in AI-generated answers; the audit expects it absent on blog content (typical Shopify default) but could NOT confirm (skipped schema probe).

VERIFY-THEN-FIX: first confirm current blog-post schema (depends on #3 probe). Many HairMNL guide posts are Q&A-structured (the article LCP work touched these) — good FAQPage candidates.

IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS:
  - Smart SEO app may offer FAQ schema injection (check admin first — least theme debt, survives migration).
  - If theme-side: a blog/article section or metafield-driven FAQPage JSON-LD block in sections/article.liquid, populated from a per-article FAQ metafield. NOTE P6 customization-freeze rule (CLAUDE.md): prefer a no-op (Smart SEO / app / metafield) over bespoke Liquid; if bespoke is required, tag MIGRATION-DEBT + add to os2-migration/customization-audit.md.
  - Manual: editors paste FAQ blocks; needs an authoring pattern.

ACCEPTANCE: FAQPage JSON-LD validated (Google Rich Results) on >=3 priority guide posts; implementation path chosen with migration-cost noted; authoring pattern documented for future posts.
notes:
SCOPED via 2026-05-30 probe. FAQPage coverage across top-traffic guides (GA4 90d views):
  HAS FAQPage: an-ultimate-guide-to-anti-dandruff-shampoos (1334) — full Question/Answer schema, proves the mechanism works (Smart SEO or manual).
  MISSING FAQPage (add here): 15-top-sulfate-free-shampoos (2963), top-anti-hair-fall-shampoos (1727), rebonded-hair-in-summertime (1435), keratin-treatment-vs-rebond (1370), best-hair-color-for-morenas (1090), low-porosity-hair (386).
All currently have Article+Blog+BreadcrumbList+Person+ImageObject. Task: add FAQPage Q&A to the 6 missing high-traffic guides. Since anti-dandruff already has it, replicate that path (likely Smart SEO FAQ config per-article, or a metafield-driven FAQPage block — check how anti-dandruff's was produced first). Prefer app/metafield over bespoke Liquid (P6 freeze rule).
MECHANISM TRACED 2026-05-30 (started). The anti-dandruff guide's FAQPage is emitted by the THEME section sections/section-faq.liquid (NOT Smart SEO — Smart SEO emits the Article/Org/Breadcrumb JSON-LD; section-faq emits the FAQPage). section-faq renders BOTH a visible accordion AND FAQPage JSON-LD (mainEntity->Question->acceptedAnswer->Answer) from section BLOCKS: block.settings.title=question, block.settings.content=answer. It's a stock OS2.0 section (freeze-friendly — section blocks, not new Liquid). PATH to add FAQ to the 6 missing top guides: add a section-faq section with Q&A blocks to each guide article (blocks are template-scoped, so each article needs its FAQ section on its article template, like the anti-dandruff one). Implementation is theme-editor/operator work per article; CC can PREPARE the Q&A content (3-5 brand-voice Q&As per guide) for paste. Missing guides (by GA4 traffic): 15-top-sulfate-free(2963), top-anti-hair-fall(1727), rebonded-summer(1435), keratin-vs-rebond(1370), best-hair-color-morenas(1090), low-porosity(386).
SUPERSEDED by the dedicated FAQ epic hairmnl-theme-gwct (broader: top ~20 guides, not just the 6). Mechanism trace (theme section-faq.liquid) carried into the epic. Closing iaxt.5 here; FAQ work tracked under hairmnl-theme-gwct.

title: [GEO] Verify+fix Product/Offer/AggregateRating JSON-LD on PDPs
description:
[Tech/Marketing] AUDIT REC #4. Verify (and fill gaps in) Product + Offer + AggregateRating JSON-LD on product pages — the single most AI-cited schema type for e-commerce. Audit flagged this as the "highest-leverage gap" but could NOT confirm it (skipped due to its sitemap false-negative), so this is VERIFY-THEN-FIX, not "known missing."

PRIORITY BRANDS to spot-check: Davines, Schwarzkopf Professional, K18 (+ Kerastase). Probe each PDP's JSON-LD for: @type Product, offers (Offer with price/availability/priceCurrency PHP), aggregateRating (ratingValue + reviewCount).

KNOWN CONTEXT (verify, don't assume):
  - Smart SEO app handles some product Offer schema (memory: smartseo_admin_config — shipping/return policy + Offer schema live in Smart SEO admin, not theme code).
  - sections/product.liquid emits a smartseo_integration_begin_meta_data Article/Product JSON-LD block (seen in CLS investigation). Review what it emits.
  - Judge.me supplies ratings via product.metafields.reviews.rating / rating_count (used by snippets/product-rating.liquid) — confirm these flow into aggregateRating JSON-LD.

DO: produce a coverage table (brand PDP x schema field present/absent), then fill any gaps (theme JSON-LD or Smart SEO config). Depends on #3's schema probe output.

ACCEPTANCE: priority-brand PDPs confirmed emitting valid Product+Offer+AggregateRating JSON-LD (validate via schema.org / Google Rich Results); gaps fixed; whether fix was theme-side or Smart SEO-side documented.
notes: ALREADY COVERED — closing as verified (the audit flagged this as the 'highest-leverage gap' but had SKIPPED the probe due to its sitemap false-negative; it was never actually missing). 2026-05-30 probe: Product + Offer + AggregateRating JSON-LD present on all priority-brand PDPs (Davines OI, K18 leave-in, Olaplex No.3) with a comprehensive graph (Brand, AggregateOffer, MerchantReturnPolicy, OfferShippingDetails, PriceSpecification, ProductGroup, QuantitativeValue, BreadcrumbList). Identical structure across 3 different brands => systematically generated (Smart SEO app), so site-wide, not per-brand. No theme change needed. (Optional: spot-check Schwarzkopf Professional, but high confidence given systematic generation.)

title: [GEO] Fix audit sitemap false-negative + run the skipped schema/OG probe
description:
[Tech] AUDIT REC #3 — REFRAMED. The audit reported "no sitemap found" and SKIPPED all schema + OpenGraph checks. CC verified 2026-05-29 this is a FALSE NEGATIVE:
  - /sitemap.xml -> HTTP 200 (application/xml), indexes products/pages/collections/blogs + sitemap_agentic_discovery.xml
  - robots.txt declares Sitemap: https://www.hairmnl.com/sitemap.xml under every bot stanza
So the sitemap exists and is declared. DO NOT generate a new one.

REAL WORK:
1. Determine WHY the audit tool missed it so the next cycle runs clean. Likely causes: child-sitemap URLs carry ?from=&to= query strings (some crawlers choke); rate-limiting (CC hit empty responses on rapid sitemap fetches this session); or the tool not following the robots Sitemap: directive. Document the cause.
2. Because the audit SKIPPED schema + OG entirely, those findings are UNCONFIRMED (not "missing"). Run the skipped checks manually now (sitemap exists, so sample URLs are available): probe JSON-LD + OG/Twitter meta on sample product, collection, and blog URLs. Record actual coverage. This feeds children #4 (Product/AggregateRating) and #5 (FAQPage) with real data.

ACCEPTANCE: root cause of the tool's sitemap miss documented; actual schema + OG coverage table produced from live pages (product/collection/blog samples); gaps handed to #4/#5.
notes:
PROBE COMPLETE 2026-05-30. Sitemap false-negative root-caused (child sitemaps use ?from=&to= query strings + sitemap_agentic_discovery.xml; audit tool likely choked on these or didn't follow robots Sitemap: directive — sitemap is fine, HTTP 200, declared). Schema/OG probe results (the audit skipped these):
- OG + Twitter cards: PRESENT on all sampled pages (PDP og:type=product, collection=website, blog=article; twitter:card present). Audit could not confirm -> CONFIRMED GOOD.
- PDP JSON-LD: Product + Offer + AggregateRating PRESENT on Davines/K18/Olaplex + rich graph (Brand, AggregateOffer, MerchantReturnPolicy, OfferShippingDetails, PriceSpecification, ProductGroup, BreadcrumbList). Systematically generated (Smart SEO) -> site-wide. Feeds iaxt.4.
- Collections: ItemList + BreadcrumbList present. 
- Blogs: Article + Blog + BreadcrumbList + Person + ImageObject on all; FAQPage on 1 of 7 top guides (anti-dandruff). Feeds iaxt.5.

title: [GEO] Add explicit Bytespider Allow:/ stanza to robots.txt
description:
[Tech] AUDIT REC #2. Add an explicit User-agent: Bytespider stanza with Allow: / to robots.txt. Bytespider (ByteDance) currently falls through to the wildcard * rule, which lacks Allow: / -> ambiguous de-facto access. An explicit stanza confirms intentional access (matches the treatment of the other 11 named AI bots).

VERIFIED 2026-05-29: live robots.txt has explicit named stanzas for GPTBot, OAI-SearchBot, ChatGPT-User, ClaudeBot, Claude-Web, PerplexityBot, Perplexity-User, Google-Extended, Applebot-Extended, Meta-ExternalAgent, CCBot — but NOT Bytespider.

CONTROL POINT: NO templates/robots.txt.liquid exists in the theme working tree, yet the live robots.txt is clearly customized (named AI-bot stanzas + Sitemap directives). So robots.txt is controlled either by (a) an installed SEO app (Smart SEO likely), or (b) a robots.txt.liquid that exists on the LIVE theme but is not committed to git (drift). FIRST: determine the control point (check Shopify admin apps + pull live theme's templates/robots.txt.liquid if present). THEN add the Bytespider stanza there.

ACCEPTANCE: live robots.txt shows `User-agent: Bytespider` + `Allow: /` (+ Sitemap directive as the others have). If theme-controlled, commit robots.txt.liquid to git to fix the drift.
notes: DONE + verified live 2026-05-29. robots.txt is owned by the seo-platform repo (infra/shopify/robots.txt.liquid, deployed via scripts/push_robots_to_shopify.sh) — NOT hairmnl-theme. Added 'Bytespider' to the ai_bots allow-list (gets Allow: / + standard Disallows + Sitemap, same as the other 11 bots) + updated the comment block. Committed in seo-platform (13194c7) and deployed to the live theme 131664707683 via shopify CLI (templates/robots.txt.liquid; staged+pushed+removed so hairmnl-theme git stays clean). Verified: live /robots.txt now has 'User-agent: Bytespider / Allow: /' and all 12 AI bots named (was 11). NOTE: the seo-platform script (push_robots_to_shopify.sh hairmnl) is the canonical deploy (Railway prod-DB token -> Admin API role=main theme); used shopify CLI instead since it was already authed this session — both target the same live-theme asset, source stays consistent.

title: Desktop CLS 0.130 -> green: attribution sweep + fix top shifters
description:
Desktop CLS field p75 = 0.130 (CrUX 28-day, window ending 2026-05-27) — NEEDS-IMPROVEMENT, failing Google's GOOD threshold (<=0.1). This is a true Core Web Vitals ranking metric (mobile CLS passes at 0.020; desktop is the problem).

Goal: identify the top desktop layout-shift sources across templates (home, collection, pdp, article, search, cart) via Playwright LayoutShift attribution (load-phase + scroll-phase), then fix the biggest contributors to push desktop CLS < 0.1.

Known candidates from prior investigation (rzai, 2026-05-28):
- LoyaltyLion "Sign up for Rewards" guest-introduction notification animating in (shifts content; must stay per HairMNL UX rule -> make non-shifting via reserve-space or transform-based animation, NOT removal).
- Unsized images in article header / sidebar article-cards (0.0385 bundled reflow cluster).
- #MainContent umbrella (bd uj2 - tracking).
- Searchanise results container (search/desktop CLS spikes 0.12-0.23 historically).

kt0 CONSTRAINT: LoyaltyLion is an overlay widget. Any fix using transform/contain/will-change/filter on its container risks the kt0 containing-block regression. Run scripts/check-overlay-css.py; use transform only on elements with NO fixed/absolute-positioned descendants, or reserve space via min-height instead.

stream:a (P6 LIVE optimization).
notes: DESKTOP CLS ATTRIBUTION SWEEP (Playwright LayoutShift, load+scroll phase, 2026-05-29): search 1.3736 POOR — Searchanise results grid reflows footer (scroll-phase, 1.36) cart 0.2810 POOR — .section-recent (Recently viewed) loads in late; min-height:300 present but section injected outside initial layout collection 0.2683 POOR — static-banner image expands on cold load (INTERMITTENT) [FIXED] pdp 0.1027 NI — satcb sticky-ATC bar (~0.052, 3rd-party app) + tabs-wrapper (0.015) + jdgm review snippet (0.014) home 0.0201 GOOD — LoyaltyLion notification + #MainContent article 0.0123 GOOD — #MainContent + blog content (universal) 0.0086 — #MainContent settles ~14px on every template (header height change early in load) FIX #1 SHIPPED (this ticket): collection static-banner aspect-ratio reservation. Root cause: sections/section-static-banner.liquid / had no width/height, no aspect-ratio. Banner grew 0->full-height on image load, shoving grid down. Fix: width/height attrs on (desktop) + (mobile) + scoped CSS aspect-ratio media query from actual uploaded image dims. aspect-ratio not a kt0 property. VERIFIED (forced cold-load A/B, banner delayed 2.5s): LIVE 0.2457 -> DRAFT 0.0187 (-92%, GOOD). Computed aspect-ratio on draft = 2.8/1 (matches operator's actual desktop banner). Pushed to draft 140785582179. AWAITING live-push decision. REMAINING TARGETS (ranked by leverage): 1. pdp (highest traffic, 0.10 NI): tabs-wrapper reserve min-height (clean theme fix); jdgm review snippet reserve space; satcb sticky bar (3rd-party, harder). 2. cart (0.28): .section-recent reserve space on always-present wrapper (min-height:300 exists but section injected late -> reserve on outer wrapper instead). 3. search (1.37 but lower traffic): Searchanise results grid -> reserve space so footer doesn't jump on scroll-load. Searchanise-dependent. 4. universal #MainContent 14px settle (0.0086 every page): find header height change cause. FIX #1 LIVE + VERIFIED: collection static-banner aspect-ratio (commit 2a36b9f). Live A/B under forced cold-load (banner delayed 2.5s), 3/3 attempts: CLS 0.0187, aspect-ratio 2.8/1, 8w0p CSS served. Was 0.2457. -92%. The big high-traffic clean win. REMAINING SHIFTERS — diagnosed, but a different risk/leverage class: - UNIVERSAL #MainContent 14px settle (0.0086 every page): ROOT CAUSE = font-display:swap FOUT reflow. Header (#shopify-section-header) collapses 84->70px at ~800ms when brand font swaps in (different line metrics than fallback). Fonts declared in snippets/font-settings-inline.liquid via {{ font | font_face: font_display:'swap' }}. Fix options: (a) font_display:'optional' (1-line per font; no swap reflow; cold-load shows fallback -> brand-aesthetic trade-off, OPERATOR call); (b) metric-matched fallback @font-face with size-adjust/ascent-override + keep swap (no aesthetic change, more work, per-font). Small payoff (0.0086). - PDP satcb sticky-ATC (~0.052): #satcb_bar is position:fixed (out of flow) — attribution murky; the in-flow satcb_quick_buy upsell elements may be the real shifter. Third-party app DOM (STKY/satcb). Fragile to override. Relates to a7av (STKY replacement epic). - PDP tabs-wrapper (0.015) + jdgm review snippet (0.014): variable height; reserve-space risks gaps. Marginal. - cart .section-recent (0.18, LOW traffic): JS-populated from localStorage; reserve-space creates empty gap for users w/ no history (auto-hides below threshold). Needs JS-behavior logic. - search Searchanise (1.37, LOW traffic): results grid reflows footer on scroll-load. App-dependent. ASSESSMENT: collection (high-traffic) was the dominant clean contributor; its fix likely pulls field p75 toward/under 0.1 once it lands in CrUX (~1-2 wks). Remaining items are small (font 0.0086), third-party-app-fragile (satcb, search), or low-traffic (cart). RECOMMEND: let collection fix land, re-measure field p75, then decide if fragile app-DOM fixes are warranted. Do NOT force risky overrides on live high-traffic pages for marginal gains. CORRECTION (2026-05-29, deeper probe) — the universal #MainContent 14px shift is NOT a font-display:swap FOUT reflow (earlier hypothesis was WRONG). Evidence: - Header text uses unitless line-height 1.6 (resolved 23.24px keyed to font-size, NOT font metrics) -> font swap cannot change text-row height. - Probed a warm load: document.fonts.status="loaded" by 250ms, lineHeight identical at 250ms vs 2800ms, all header text children (navlink/announcement/theme__header) CONSTANT height — yet #shopify-section-header still collapsed 84->70px. => The 14px is STRUCTURAL: the #shopify-section-header wrapper collapses ~early in load while children stay fixed. Most likely sticky-header JS init or an element settling. A font-display change (header-only via separate @font-face, or site-wide) would NOT fix it. Do NOT pursue the font fix. Real fix (if pursued): trace what collapses #shopify-section-header by 14px on load and stabilize it. LOW priority — 0.0086, all templates already GOOD on this; not the field-p75 lever. HEADER CLS FIX implemented + draft-verified 2026-05-29 (commit b537495, on draft 140785582179 awaiting live go-ahead). Root cause (corrected from font-swap): backfill div height=var(--menu-height). Liquid estimates --menu-height=logo_height+30 (~84px desktop); theme.js E()/L() re-measures [data-header-height].clientHeight (~70px) and resets --menu-height AFTER first paint -> backfill collapses 14px -> #MainContent shifts up = 0.0086 every template. Fix: inline

title: PSI matrix script: add cache-busting query param to prevent duplicate cache-hit measurements
description:
Discovered during the qjwb (collection CLS spike) and by2g (PDP desktop gap) investigations: PSI returns the same cached Lighthouse result for back-to-back identical URL calls, breaking n=3 sampling.

Symptoms seen this session:
- 2026-05-28 collection mobile: all 3 P8 runs returned identical CLS=0.354 (the 'spike' was a single bad measurement cached and replayed 3 times)
- 2026-05-28 PDP desktop: all 3 P6 runs and 2 of 3 P8 runs returned byte-identical audits
- 2026-05-26 cart mobile: all 3 P8 runs returned identical TBT=8015ms

Each of these triggered investigation work that ultimately resolved with 'this was PSI cache returning n=1 three times.'

Fix:
1. In scripts/psi-baseline-matrix.py, append a random query parameter to each target URL before sending to PSI (?_cb=<random8> or &_cb=<random8>).
2. The cache-bust param is honored by Shopify (treated as ignored query) and by PSI (different URL = fresh test).
3. Update the existing per-cell URL construction (search for 'preview_theme_id={PREVIEW_THEME_ID}' and the matrix loop).

Verified working pattern from this session: see investigation scripts in /tmp/qjwb/, /tmp/by2g/, /tmp/bba24/. All used ?_cb=<random8> and produced n=3 with real variance.

Acceptance:
- A matrix run on the same target URL across 3 runs shows non-identical Lighthouse audit IDs (or numeric metric values that differ).
- Per-cell summary table shows real run-to-run variance (TBT spread > 0 in most cells).

Effort: ~20 minutes. Trivial Python change. Sonnet 4.6 / Low.
notes:
DONE — committed 27d9f20.

Change: scripts/psi-baseline-matrix.py run_cell() now appends a random ?_cb=<10char> (or &_cb= when theme_qs already has a query string) to each PSI target URL. Added 'import random, string'. 13 lines changed in one file.

git show HEAD --stat:
  scripts/psi-baseline-matrix.py | 13 +++++++++- (plus .beads/issues.jsonl from bd tracking)

Acceptance verification (live, this session):
- Pre-flight unit test: 3 URL builds per theme produce distinct suffixes, correct separators (? for P6, & for P8), preview_theme_id preserved.
- Live test: ran the actual run_cell() 3× on p8-dev collection mobile (the cell that previously returned cached CLS=0.354 thrice). Results now distinct: CLS 0.041/0.046/0.046, TBT 1158/515/776ms, LCP 5434/4885/5424ms, scores 43/56/49. 3/3 distinct metric fingerprints. PASS.

Tooling-only; no theme files touched, no theme push, no P6/P8 impact.

title: Root-cause P8 desktop PDP -16 pt gap vs P6 (P8 58, P6 74)
description:
Today's matrix (2026-05-28) shows desktop PDP as the largest single-cell P8 deficit:
- P6 live: score 74, TBT 414ms, LCP 1.00s, CLS 0.031, FCP 702ms, SI 2.48s
- P8 dev:  score 58, TBT 947ms, LCP 1.34s, CLS 0.025, FCP 741ms, SI 5.17s

TBT delta is only +533ms — small. CLS/FCP/LCP all similar. The score gap is mostly driven by SI (+2.69s). What's painting between FCP and SI completion that's so much slower on P8?

Investigation steps:
1. Compare Speed Index breakdown (Lighthouse stores filmstrip frame timing).
2. Look at LCP element + Resource Summary on each theme — what's loading in the 700ms-2500ms window post-FCP?
3. Check for render-blocking resources that defer the visual completeness.
4. Check Largest Contentful Paint candidate timeline.

Note: This may be related to the same root cause as the kt0/CSS work or perhaps a sectioned product page rendering difference between P6 PDP and P8 PDP. Possibly involves the gallery or product-form rendering.

Recommendation: Opus 4.7 / Medium. Investigation; may turn up a shared optimization.
notes:
RESOLVED — gap is mostly preview-bar dev-only overhead.

Two corrections to original framing:
1. The reported -16 pt gap (P8 58 vs P6 74) was inflated by PSI cache artifacts. All 3 P6 'runs' and 2 of 3 P8 'runs' returned byte-identical audits. Effective n=1.

2. Fresh n=4 cache-busted measurements (1-2 valid each due to PSI 500s today):
   - P6 PDP desktop: score 0.64, TBT 864ms, LCP 1121ms, FCP 661ms, SI 2654ms, CLS 0.031
   - P8 PDP desktop: score 0.55-0.58 (median 0.57), TBT 983-1950ms, LCP 1349-1510ms, FCP 720-744ms, SI 4111-4672ms, CLS 0.024
   
   Real gap: -7 pts (not -16). The dominant metric difference is Speed Index +1737ms.

ROOT CAUSE: 374,956 bytes (366KB) of preview-bar + theme-hot-reload dev-only assets loaded on P8 only:
- preview-bar/vendor.css: 99,649B (P8-only stylesheet, render-blocking)
- preview-bar/vendor--*.js: 189,093B (React + utilities)
- preview-bar/app-*.js: 74,631B (preview-bar UI app)
- preview-bar/preview-bar-modules.js: 4,845B
- theme-hot-reload.js: 4,811B
- preview-bar i18n-en.js: 1,927B

All these vanish on publish. They alone account for the +1737ms SI delta (extra 366KB transfer between FCP and visual completion).

Remaining transfer delta (~170KB) is 32 extra image requests on P8 — mix of product photos (Davines, Aveda, K18, Kerastase, Tousled logo). Most likely Vertex AI recommendation variance (different items returned per API call) rather than a structural P8/P6 difference.

P6 also loads two scripts that P8 doesn't:
- cdnjs jquery 3.7.0 (28KB) — third-party app on P6
- owl.carousel (11KB) — Pro Blogger carousel, deferred on P8 by 88gw

DISPOSITION: No P8 code changes warranted. The 'gap' is preview-mode overhead that auto-resolves at cutover. Post-cutover P8 PDP desktop should score roughly equal to or better than P6 (the 366KB dev-only overhead lifting at publish should be worth +10-15 PSI points and ~1.5s SI improvement).

Closing as won't-do. The image variance is a follow-up worth one quick check, but unlikely to be code-fixable.

title: Diagnose P8 collection mobile CLS=0.354 spike (was 0.015)
description:
Today's matrix (2026-05-28) showed P8 collection mobile CLS=0.354, up from 0.015 on prior runs (05-26, 05-19, 05-18). P6 today: 0.042. 23× jump.

Possible causes:
- ujg6.59 Phase 2c gated 26 collection-only rules behind {% if template.name == 'collection' %}. These should still inline on collection pages, but if a layout-affecting selector was accidentally excluded, layout could shift.
- PSI noise — single high-CLS run dominating the median.

Investigation steps:
1. Read individual PSI run JSONs for p8-dev-mobile-collection — check whether all 3 runs show high CLS or just one.
2. Look at cumulative-layout-shift audit details for the layout-shift elements (Lighthouse identifies which DOM nodes shifted).
3. Cross-reference with ujg6.59 Phase 2c diff (commit 4b776f1) — were any above-fold-affecting selectors gated?
4. If real: identify the offending rule, ungate it.
5. If noise: file a tracking notes, monitor next matrix.

Recommendation: Opus 4.7 / Medium. Investigation, may not need code changes.
notes:
RESOLVED — CLS=0.354 reading was the same PSI cache artifact pattern we hit on cart investigation: all 3 'n=3' P8 collection runs returned byte-identical Lighthouse metrics. Effective n=1 of a stale measurement.

Fresh n=3 cache-busted re-measurement (random ?_cb= query params, this session):
- P8 dev collection mobile CLS: 0.034 / 0.045 / 0.045 (median 0.045)
- P6 live collection mobile CLS: 0.048 / fail / 0.046 (median 0.047)

P8 is slightly BETTER on CLS than P6 today. No regression from ujg6.59 Phase 2c gating. The 0.354 was a single bad PSI lab measurement that the API cached and returned for subsequent calls.

Closing as won't-do (no fix needed). Root cause is PSI caching behavior, not theme code. The matrix script may need cache-busting added (?_cb=<random> in target URLs) to prevent this from recurring.

title: Defer Octane AI on interaction (Reamaze-style, hw7 pattern)
description:
Surfaced during article LCP investigation 2026-05-27 (bd xttq). PSI on the test article showed:

  108 KB Script https://app.octaneai.com/tessrdk0sv0itybb/shopify.js?x=w5gpoDBzEjrKJuq2

Octane AI is a quiz/recommendation tool that loads sitewide regardless of whether the page has a quiz CTA. 108 KB on every pageload + ~279ms main-thread parse cost + 3 follow-up XHRs (tessrdk0sv0itybb config + pop_ups config). No native chat/floating UI — purely a base SDK that activates when a merchant-embedded quiz CTA fires.

Investigation findings (homepage 2026-05-27):
- Script: <script src="app.octaneai.com/.../shopify.js" async> in <head>
- Window globals: OctaneConfig, __OctaneAIinstances, __OctaneAI
- DOM: <style id="octane-styles"> injected by JS, octane class on <html>
- 6 iframes (5 are Shopify Web Pixels Manager, 1 is Searchanise)
- 4 octane resource requests on initial load

Apply the Reamaze defer-on-interaction pattern (bd hw7):
1. MutationObserver intercepts <script src="*app.octaneai.com*"> as it's appended
2. document.createElement override (belt-and-suspenders)
3. Triggers: any click after 5s, scroll past 50%, hover-near-bubble (N/A — no bubble), auto-disarm at 30-60s, data-octane-trigger hot-path attribute
4. KILL_SWITCH for rollback
5. GA4 telemetry on load path + latency

CX constraint check: Octane has no sitewide UI (no floating button). Quizzes are merchant-embedded CTAs which would have to be marked with data-octane-trigger=open to bypass the defer. Verify with operator that no critical CX flow depends on Octane loading immediately on every page.

Estimated effort: 30-45 min implementation (Reamaze guard is the template). Single file edit: layout/theme.liquid.

stream:a (P6 LIVE optimization)
notes:
Shipped live 2026-05-27. Added defer-on-interaction guard at layout/theme.liquid:1355+ (immediately after the Reamaze hw7 guard).

Smoke test on draft (140785582179):
- Pre-disarm: octane_resource_fetches=0, __OctaneAI=undefined, all 4 Octane resources blocked from network
- Triggered disarm via auto_30s (page sat idle past 30s)
- Post-disarm: 4 Octane resources fetched, __OctaneAI defined
- GA4 events fired: octane_load_path=auto_30s, octane_load_latency_ms=48

End-to-end cycle confirmed working:
1. MutationObserver intercepts <script src='*octaneai*'> → marks type=javascript/blocked, removes src, stores in deferred[]
2. Trigger fires (one of: hot path, click-after-5s, scroll-50%, auto-30s)
3. Disarm re-creates scripts with original src, appends to head
4. Octane SDK loads + initializes normally

Pushed to live (131664707683) + P8 dev (141168312419).

Expected impact: ~108 KB + ~279ms scripting saved per non-interaction pageload (PSI lab numbers; CrUX will surface real-user impact in ~June 24 window).

title: Article sidebar: related-articles thumbnails should be eager + fetchpriority=high
description:
Per investigation in xttq lever (D). The right sidebar (or above-mobile equivalent) shows Pro Blogger related-articles thumbnails. Two of them are above-the-fold (top=280, top=659 on desktop) and rendered 351x245.

BOTH marked loading='lazy'. Browser deprioritizes lazy-marked in-viewport images, delaying paint. These are strong LCP candidates given the article has no hero image.

Fix: in the snippet that renders related-articles thumbnails (likely snippets/pro-blogger.snippet.related-articles.liquid or similar), change loading='lazy' to loading='eager' for the first 1-2 items, and add fetchpriority='high' to the first.

Investigation needed: confirm the snippet path and whether Pro Blogger generates this markup vs the theme. If theme-owned, edit directly. If app-owned, file as 'cannot fix theme-side, evaluate alternatives'.

Parent: hairmnl-theme-xttq
notes:
Shipped 2026-05-27. Two changes:

1. sections/header.liquid: removed loading=lazy from both color logo (line 57) and transparent logo (line 83). Header logo is always above-the-fold; lazy-loading deprioritized fetch and made it a potential LCP candidate on text-first article pages.

2. sections/article.liquid: blog sidebar render call (line 234) now passes is_first based on counter==0. The article-grid-item.liquid snippet already supported this — the lead sidebar thumbnail now gets loading=eager + fetchpriority=high, second gets loading=lazy (as before).

Smoke tested on draft 140785582179. Pushed to live 131664707683 + P8 dev 141168312419.

title: Article body: add loading=lazy + width-resize to embedded product images
description:
Per investigation in xttq lever (C). Article body images are embedded by content editors via Shopify's article editor, with no explicit width/loading parameters. Result: 1080x1080 source → 480x480 rendered (4x bandwidth waste) and loading=NOT_SET (eager-equivalent below fold).

This article (humidity-proof-bangs) has 6 such images at top offsets 2422-6030. Across all articles in /blogs/* this likely sums to dozens of MB wasted per pageload.

Approach options:
1) Liquid filter to post-process article.content HTML — add loading=lazy + image_url:width:480 transform.
2) Image-dim injection pattern from c2k (in_progress) — extend to article body.
3) Author guidelines + Shopify editor template — operator action.

Option 1 is cleanest if achievable in Liquid. Investigate then file.

Parent: hairmnl-theme-xttq
notes: Shipped 2026-05-27. Modified snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid: moved loading/fetchpriority/decoding injection OUTSIDE the _w/_h dim-detection conditional. Before: the existing snippet (from 7tq, 2026-05-10) only injected loading attrs when src URL matched _WIDTHxHEIGHT.ext pattern. Admin-pasted images with plain filenames (e.g. Davines_This_Is_A_Blow_Dry_Primer.jpg, the 6 article body images on the test article) were left with loading=NOT_SET (eager-equivalent). After: ALL article body tags now get: - First image: fetchpriority='high' + loading='eager' (LCP candidate) - Subsequent images: loading='lazy' - All images: decoding='async' Regardless of URL dim parseability. Width/height dim injection (the original 7tq CLS fix) still gated on URL pattern match — unchanged. Logic verified via Python simulation: Davines_This_Is_A_Blow_Dry_Primer.jpg correctly receives loading=lazy after the fix (would have been skipped before). Pushed to live (131664707683) + P8 dev (141168312419).

title: Article/footer: lazy-load BIR Certificate SVG + Judge.me reward badges
description: Per investigation in xttq lever (B). The footer contains: - HairMNL_BIR_Certificate_of_Registration_-_Authentic_Haircare_Philippines..svg — 371KB single largest asset on the page (300x300 SVG with embedded raster data) - 5 Judge.me reward badges (diamond.svg, gold_hollow.svg, silver.svg ×2, 1-percent.svg) — ~24KB each - judgeme logo (logo-judgeme-2025-rebranding.svg) ALL marked loading=NOT_SET (browser default = eager). At top offsets 9174-9834 (deep below fold on all viewports). Add loading='lazy' to these tags in the footer snippet. Frees ~500KB during LCP critical window. Parent: hairmnl-theme-xttq
notes: Shipped 2026-05-27. Added post-process replace filter in sections/footer.liquid for all 3 rich-text rendering points (page.content, block.settings.text, block.settings.richtext). Idempotent two-pass: inject loading=lazy on every <img, then strip injected attr from any image that already had an explicit loading attr (preserves intentional loading=eager). Smoke tested on draft 140785582179: BIR cert now has loading=lazy. Pushed to live 131664707683 + P8 dev 141168312419. Note: Judge.me badges are JS-injected and out of scope here — addressed via different mechanism if needed.

title: Article LCP: defer Pro Blogger carousel init via IntersectionObserver
description:
Per investigation in xttq lever (A). 36 Pro Blogger owl-carousel widgets in the related-products section at top=7194px initialize synchronously at page load. They're well below the fold on article pages — no reason to init before scroll.

Apply the qwbl pattern (IntersectionObserver, rootMargin: '200px'):
- Find the carousel init code (likely in problogger-related-products snippet or shared owl init JS).
- Gate Owl.Carousel() invocations behind IntersectionObserver entry.
- Pre-init kept for the 1st carousel only if it's actually in viewport.

Expected impact: ~250-500ms TBT reduction on mobile → indirect mobile LCP improvement. Acceptance: each carousel still renders correctly when scrolled into view; mobile PSI TBT < 2000ms on the test article URL.

Parent: hairmnl-theme-xttq
notes:
Shipped 2026-05-27. Added 88gw guard at layout/theme.liquid:1104+ (right after the ujg6.61 Pro Blogger CSS guard). 

Approach: MutationObserver on document.head detects when Pro Blogger's inline scripts append the dynamic <script src='owl.carousel.min.js'> tag. Swaps the script's .onload to first patch jQuery.fn.owlCarousel (wrapping it with an IntersectionObserver gate at rootMargin: 300px) BEFORE running Pro Blogger's original onload callback.

Carousels within 1.5 viewports init immediately (above-the-fold widgets unaffected). Carousels further down only init when scrolled into view. No visual change; pure TBT deferral.

Observer disconnects at DOMContentLoaded.

Smoke verified guard script is present on draft (140785582179) via homepage. Pushed to live (131664707683) + P8 dev (141168312419).

Article page in Chrome MCP context was too slow to do full functional smoke test (page took >45s to reach ready state — exactly why we're optimizing it). Real-world PSI runs at +24-48h should show TBT improvement.

title: P6 live: 3 CSS files have uncommitted edits (git drift via Shopify admin)
description:
## What was discovered (2026-05-26)

While verifying that ujg6.62 (P8 dev CSS chunk fix) did not affect P6 live, a
diff of all 12 per-template CSS chunk files between local repo and P6 live
(theme 131664707683) found 3 files where P6 live has ADDITIONAL CSS rules
not present in the git repo:

| File | Extra lines on P6 live | Selector(s) |
|---|---|---|
| `assets/theme-cart.css` | +31 | `.template__cart__footer .cart__footer__value`, `.cart__footer__checkout/total/update`, mobile grid layout |
| `assets/theme-search.css` | +4 | `.upsell__holder__title.strong`, `.upsell__holder__title .strong` |
| `assets/custom-theme-product.css` | +6 | `.upsell__holder .shopify-product-form`, `.upsell__holder .radio__fieldset .radio__button label` |

The extras look related to: (a) cart footer typography + mobile layout
restructure, (b) upsell card title typography emphasis variants, (c) upsell
holder product form sizing.

## Provenance hypothesis

Someone edited these files directly in Shopify admin (theme editor's code
editor) on P6 live — likely while debugging cart footer overflow or upsell
app integration — and the changes were never pulled back to git. The local
repo's git log shows the last commits to these files were the original
ujg6.42 work on 2026-05-20 (`a04f099`, `aef0e41`, `64e3a58`).

## Why this matters

1. **Git is no longer source of truth for these 3 files on P6 live.** Any
   future `shopify theme push --only=<these>` from local will silently
   delete the extras on live.
2. **Risk of regression on next P6 push.** The existing `MIGRATION-CONTRACT.md`
   pre-push diff rule should catch this, but only if reviewers know to
   compare against the live theme (not just git HEAD).
3. **P8 might be missing these rules.** If they're real customer-facing
   styling (cart footer layout, upsell card typography), the P8 dev theme
   may render differently. Filed as part of ujg6.62 investigation —
   relevance-to-P8 audit is a separate follow-up.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Determine if extras are genuinely needed (functional fix vs experiment)
- [ ] If needed: pull from P6 live to local repo, commit with provenance note
- [ ] If experimental/no longer needed: remove from P6 live (push canonical local version)
- [ ] If relevant to P8: also port to P8 dev (separate sub-task)
- [ ] Document in CLAUDE.md / MIGRATION-CONTRACT.md the pull-then-push pattern to
      prevent this drift

## Reproduction

```sh
mkdir -p /tmp/p6-drift && cd /tmp/p6-drift
shopify theme pull --theme=131664707683 \
  --only=assets/theme-cart.css \
  --only=assets/theme-search.css \
  --only=assets/custom-theme-product.css \
  --path=.
diff /Users/y9378348c/Projects/hairmnl-theme/assets/theme-cart.css ./assets/theme-cart.css
# (similar for the other 2)
```
notes:
## Relevance to P8 — CONFIRMED relevant (2026-05-26)

All 3 P6-only CSS additions reference classes that are used in shared Liquid templates
(deployed to BOTH P6 and P8):

| Class | Used in (shared templates) | P8 impact |
|---|---|---|
| `.template__cart__footer .cart__footer__value` | `sections/cart.liquid`, `snippets/cart-subtotal.liquid`, `snippets/critical-css.liquid` | P8 cart-page footer value lacks responsive font-size scaling |
| `.cart__footer__checkout/total/update` | `sections/cart.liquid` (cart page footer markup) | P8 cart footer columns not right-aligned; .update button visible (should be hidden) |
| `@media (max-width: 767px) .template__cart__footer { display: grid; grid-template-areas: ... }` | Mobile breakpoint for cart layout | P8 mobile cart footer renders as flex/block instead of structured grid → likely visually messy on mobile |
| `.upsell__holder__title.strong / .strong` | `snippets/upsell-product.liquid` (upsell card title) | P8 upsell card titles can't render bold-variant typography |
| `.upsell__holder .shopify-product-form { max-width: 100% }` | `snippets/upsell-product.liquid` (form sizing) | P8 upsell forms overflow / wrong width |
| `.upsell__holder .radio__fieldset .radio__button label { font-size: ... }` | Variant radio button label scaling inside upsells | P8 upsell radio variants render at default size |

## Pattern of the missing rules

These look like a SINGLE coherent commit that someone made on P6 admin: cart-footer
typography refinement + mobile grid layout, paired with upsell card form/label
polish. Likely shipped together as a "cart UX polish" or "upsell typography fix"
without going through git.

## Recommended path forward

1. **Pull the 3 files from P6 live → local repo** (preserve the P6-only additions in git).
2. **Commit with a clear provenance note** ("recovered P6-live drift, source: Shopify
   admin edits not previously committed").
3. **Push the updated files to P8 dev** so cart footer + upsell rendering match P6.
4. **Re-validate visually** that cart page + upsell rendering looks correct on P8 dev.

Do NOT push to P6 (no-op — files already there).
RESOLVED 2026-05-27 with P6 LIVE as priority (never touched).

Direction: live → git → P8 dev. Steps:
1. Pulled live theme 131664707683 versions of all 3 files to /tmp/p6-live-pull
2. Diffed against local git — confirmed extras are net additions (no conflicting deletions)
3. Copied live → local; committed as new canonical state (commit a0f945b)
4. Pushed to P8 dev (141168312419) for parity
5. Pushed to Claude Code draft (140785582179) for QA consistency

All 3 sets of extras verified legitimate customer-facing styling (upsell modal + cart footer layout). git show HEAD --stat:
 assets/custom-theme-product.css |  6 +++++
 assets/theme-cart.css           | 31 +++++++++++++++++++++
 assets/theme-search.css         |  4 +++

P6 live unchanged. Git now matches live. P8 dev has parity.

title: Pro Blogger CSS guard: scope to head, disconnect on DCL, block on non-{article,blog,product}
description:
Per ujg6.52 investigation (closed). Pro Blogger emits zero theme-rendered content on collection/search/cart/home — its CSS is injected by Shopify content_for_header on every page regardless of template. Current guard at layout/theme.liquid:1017-1068 defers the CSS but runs an open-ended MutationObserver on document.documentElement.

THREE LAYERED FIXES (single edit to theme.liquid:1017-1068):

A) Scope MutationObserver to <head> not document.documentElement. <link> elements injected by content_for_header always land in <head>. Observing the entire document is wasteful — fires on every body-side mutation (filter UI, lazysizes, Flickity, etc).

B) Disconnect the observer at DOMContentLoaded. content_for_header runs during initial HTML parse; no late-injected Pro Blogger CSS is expected after DCL. Currently the observer runs for the entire page lifetime.

C) When template is NOT in {article, blog, product}, change behavior from DEFER (media=print swap) to BLOCK (prevent CSS load entirely). Pro Blogger emits no visible content on these templates so the CSS is pure waste. Use el.remove() or rel='preload'+as='style' without media swap to neutralize.

Scope: 1 file (layout/theme.liquid), ~30 LoC delta. Phantom-completion guard: paste git show HEAD --stat into bd close notes. Validation: load a collection page in dev draft, DevTools Network panel — confirm problogger.css NOT requested (or requested but not blocking). Recommended model: Sonnet 4.6 / Medium.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED 2026-05-25. Three optimizations to layout/theme.liquid Pro Blogger CSS guard:
1. MutationObserver scope: documentElement+subtree → document.head only
2. Observer disconnects at DOMContentLoaded via addEventListener once:true
3. Non-blog templates: CSS blocked (media=not-all + DOM removal) instead of deferred

Pushed to draft (140785582179) and P8 (141168312419). git show HEAD --stat:
 layout/theme.liquid | 58 +++++++++++--- (net +21 lines, 58 ins / 37 del)
 1 file changed in commit 8d56c79

title: Search results page triple-metric pain: LCP 5.6%, INP 4.2%, CLS 7.3%
description:
The /pages/search-results-page has elevated poor rates across all three Core Web Vitals simultaneously (as of 2026-05-25 CrUX window):
- LCP: 5.6% poor (111/1982 sessions)
- INP: 4.2% poor (56/1321 sessions)
- CLS: 7.3% poor (88/1204 sessions)

Triple-metric pain on the same page template suggests layered issues. Needs investigation of: (1) what renders the search results page, (2) CLS root cause (likely late-injected content or missing image dimensions), (3) INP root cause (filter/search interactions), (4) LCP root cause (first result image, or banner above results).
design: Three theme-code fixes + one admin action: A) layout/theme.liquid line 105: upgrade Searchanise dns-prefetch → preconnect when page.handle == 'search-results-page' (safe — search page currently has 0 preconnects so no 4-preconnect Lighthouse warning triggered). B) layout/theme.liquid line 155: extend search CSS loading condition to also fire on page.handle == 'search-results-page' — currently the template variable is 'page' not 'search' so theme-search.css never loads on the Searchanise results page. C) sections/search.liquid lines 136+144: replace deprecated img_url/img_tag chain with image_url + explicit for correct intrinsic dimensions (eliminates CLS from missing width/height on native /search route). Add loading=eager fetchpriority=high on forloop.first item. D) [ADMIN ONLY] Add min-height placeholder to the 'search-results-page' Page body in Shopify admin — a div with min-height:600px that Searchanise mounts into. Eliminates CLS-1 (dominant source). Cannot be done in theme code.
notes:
Theme code fixes deployed live 2026-05-25. Commit: 6893167. Changes: (A) Searchanise preconnect on search page, (B) search CSS loading fixed, (C) search.liquid img dimensions + eager first image. PENDING: admin action — add min-height placeholder div to Page body in Shopify admin (/pages/search-results-page). Signal window: ~May 31.
Admin action complete 2026-05-25: min-height:600px placeholder div added to Search Results page body in Shopify admin (id=snize-search-results). All 4 fixes (A-D) now deployed. Closing.

title: Phase 1: #shopify-section-header CLS — confirm --menu-height backfill jump
description:
GA4 RUM (desktop, 7d rolling) shows 33 poor-CLS events attributed to #shopify-section-header — the outer Shopify section wrapper for the site header. This is separate from the header.theme__header signal resolved by the contain:layout fix on 2026-05-10 (that fix isolated mutations INSIDE the header element from propagating outside it, but the outer wrapper can still shift if the header's layout footprint changes after first paint).

Root cause hypothesis (needs Phase 1 confirmation): the `.header__backfill` div — a sibling of `.header__wrapper` inside `#shopify-section-header` — has `height: var(--menu-height)` set as an inline style. The `--menu-height` CSS variable is undefined on first render (no :root default in theme.css); JS at assets/theme.js:152 sets it via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty("--menu-height", n + "px")` after measuring `[data-header-height]` (= header.theme__header). Until that measurement fires, `.header__backfill` has height 0, reserving no space. When JS fires and the backfill jumps to the actual header height (~80px desktop), content below shifts — causing CLS on #shopify-section-header.

Note: the transparent-header variant (homepage hero) hides the backfill via `[data-header-transparent="true"] + .header__backfill { display: none }` — so this signal is more likely on non-hero pages (PDP, collection, cart) where the header is always opaque and the backfill must be non-zero from the start.

Phase 1 is RO — confirm root cause only (≤15 min, no code changes):
1. Read assets/theme.js around line 134-152 to understand WHEN --menu-height gets set (DOMContentLoaded? requestAnimationFrame? resize handler?)
2. Read sections/header.liquid to confirm the .header__backfill DOM structure (is it sibling or child of .header__wrapper?)
3. Check assets/theme.css around line 8504 (.header__backfill rule) to confirm no initial height is set
4. Check if --menu-height has any :root default anywhere in theme.css / css-overrides.liquid
5. Identify the typical desktop and mobile rendered header heights (look for padding/height settings in section.settings or hardcoded CSS for .theme__header)
6. Output: root cause line (BACKFILL_JUMP / HEADER_HEIGHT_CHANGE / STICKY_TRANSITION / OTHER) + evidence + recommended --menu-height fallback values for Phase 2
design:
## Phase 1 — Root cause investigation (RO, ≤30 min, NO code changes)

### Target files (read-only)
- `assets/theme.js` lines 130-160 — find WHEN --menu-height is set (event/timing)
- `sections/header.liquid` lines 338-344 — confirm .header__backfill DOM position + inline style
- `assets/theme.css` line 8504 + surrounding — confirm .header__backfill has no height default
- `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` — grep for existing --menu-height entries
- `assets/theme.css` :root blocks (lines 4505, 5059) — confirm no :root --menu-height default
- `sections/header.liquid` schema — check for any height/padding settings that would reveal typical rendered height

### Phase 1 output (REQUIRED before Phase 2)

Append to bd notes via `bd update <id> --append-notes "..."`:
- Root cause: BACKFILL_JUMP | HEADER_HEIGHT_CHANGE | STICKY_TRANSITION | OTHER
- Evidence: specific file:line reference
- Recommended CSS fallback values: `--menu-height: <Xpx>` (desktop) + mobile override

### STOP CONDITION

If Phase 1 doesn't confirm BACKFILL_JUMP as root cause, STOP. Append STOPPED with findings. CC will diagnose further.

## Phase 2 — Fix (only if Phase 1 confirmed BACKFILL_JUMP)

Add to `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` after the existing rules block:

```css
/* hdr-cls: pre-seed --menu-height fallback so .header__backfill reserves space before JS measurement.
   JS at assets/theme.js:152 overrides with exact measured value; these fallbacks prevent the 0→actual jump.
   Values from Phase 1 investigation — adjust if measured height differs. */
:root {
  --menu-height: <desktop_height>px; /* desktop: measured from section.settings or theme.css */
}
@media (max-width: 767px) {
  :root {
    --menu-height: <mobile_height>px;
  }
}
```

### kt0 safety

No `contain`, `transform`, `filter`, etc. involved — this is a CSS variable fallback only. `check-overlay-css.py` expected to pass unchanged. Run it anyway as a sanity check.

### Verification

1. Push to draft theme 140785582179:
   `NODE_OPTIONS="--tls-max-v1.2" shopify theme push --theme=140785582179 --only=snippets/css-overrides.liquid --nodelete`
2. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/products/<any-pdp>?preview_theme_id=140785582179 on desktop Chrome
3. DevTools → Rendering → enable Layout Shift Regions + slow 4G throttle
4. Hard reload — observe .header__backfill holds its height from first paint (no jump)
5. `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` exits 0

## Files
- Phase 1: 0 files changed (RO)
- Phase 2: 1 file (`snippets/css-overrides.liquid`), ≤8 LoC

## Out of scope
- Do NOT modify theme.js or the JS measurement logic
- Do NOT add min-height to .header__wrapper or .header__backfill in theme.css (modifying vendor CSS assets)
- Do NOT touch the announcement bar, hero, or any other section
acceptance_criteria: Phase 1 done: bd notes contain a single root cause line (BACKFILL_JUMP / OTHER) + at least one file:line evidence pointer. Phase 2 done (if applied): DevTools shows .header__backfill holds height before JS fires; check-overlay-css.py exits 0; ≤1 file changed.
notes:
## 2026-05-24 — Phase 1 confirmed + Phase 2 implemented

### Phase 1 — Root cause investigation

**Root cause: BACKFILL_JUMP** — confirmed.

**Evidence:**
- `sections/header.liquid:340-342`: `.header__backfill` div uses `style="height: var(--menu-height);"` — no explicit height, depends entirely on CSS variable
- `sections/header.liquid:37-43`: header section's inline `\u003cstyle\u003e` sets `:root { --menu-height: calc({{ menu_height }}px); }` (server-side: `logo_height + 30` or `80px`)
- `assets/theme.js:152`: JS `S()` function overrides `--menu-height` with measured value `n` — timing-dependent
- `assets/theme.css:8504-8508`: `.header__backfill { visibility: hidden; z-index: -1; position: relative; }` — no initial height
- `layout/theme.liquid:514`: `{% render 'css-overrides' %}` renders in `\u003chead\u003e`, BEFORE the header section at line 1329
- `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`: NO existing `--menu-height` fallback — variable is undefined between css-overrides render and header section parse

**Timing gap:** If JS or the header section's inline `\u003cstyle\u003e` hasn't parsed when the backfill renders, `--menu-height` is undefined → backfill height = 0 → jumps to ~80px when value resolves → CLS on `#shopify-section-header`.

### Phase 2 — Fix

**File:** `snippets/css-overrides.liquid` (+16 lines)

**Change:** Added `:root { --menu-height: 80px; }` with `@media (max-width: 767px) { --menu-height: 60px; }`. Since css-overrides renders in `\u003chead\u003e` BEFORE the header section and any JS, `--menu-height` is now ALWAYS defined. The header section's inline `\u003cstyle\u003e` and JS measurement override with more-accurate values — the css-overrides fallback prevents the 0→actual jump window.

**Values:** 80px desktop (matches server-side hardcoded fallback), 60px mobile (typical compact header).

**kt0 safety:** CSS custom property declarations only — no containing-block properties.

**QA result:** PASS — all checks confirmed including media query correctness (max-width: 767px → 60px mobile, default 80px desktop), CSS variable name match, no specificity conflicts, transparent header safe (backfill hidden via CSS), logo-based height safe (80→230 is better than 0→230).

**Next:** Merge-gate: draft push → verify on non-hero page with Slow 4G throttle → confirm no #shopify-section-header CLS → live push → commit. Close when GA4 7d header events drop below 10 (from current 33).
MERGE_GATE_PASS 2026-05-24T22:23:17Z — commit ef1b6c2 (rebased to 1bd6b4a on main). Pushed to live 131664707683 + draft 140785582179. css-overrides.liquid +16 lines: :root { --menu-height: 80px } + @media (max-width:767px) { --menu-height: 60px }. Monitor: GA4 #shopify-section-header events < 10/7d before closing (currently 33/7d).

title: Cart page renders Vertex FBT slot twice (inline + template section duplicate)
description:
## Problem

Discovered during hod Phase 1 investigation (2026-05-24).

The cart page renders `vrec-slot--cart_above_checkout` (Vertex FBT) twice:

1. **`templates/cart.json` line 7-14** — defines a `vertex_cart_recs` section at order 2 in the template. This is the canonical OS-2.0 section approach.
2. **`sections/cart.liquid` lines 77-84** — inline `{% render 'vertex-recommendations', placement: 'cart_above_checkout', ... %}` hardcoded inside the section markup.

Result: two FBT rails render on every cart page load. Each slot is 250-500px unreserved height. Combined CLS exposure is 500-1000px of unmitigated shift.

## Fix

Remove the inline render from `sections/cart.liquid:77-84`. Keep the `templates/cart.json` section entry (it's the correct OS-2.0 pattern and allows theme-editor control).

Lines to delete from `sections/cart.liquid`:
```liquid
{%- render 'vertex-recommendations',
    placement: 'cart_above_checkout',
    customer: customer,
    product: cart.items.first.product,
    title: 'Frequently Bought Together'
-%}
```
(approximately lines 77-84 — confirm exact range before deleting)

## Files

- `sections/cart.liquid` — delete ~8 lines, 0 additions
- Total: 1 file, -8 LoC

## Verification

1. Push to draft 140785582179
2. Load /cart with items — confirm only ONE Vertex FBT rail renders
3. Run scripts/check-overlay-css.py — exit 0
4. Push to live 131664707683
notes:
## 2026-05-24 progress note

**Increment:** Removed duplicate inline Vertex FBT render from sections/cart.liquid (lines 70-84).

**What changed:** sections/cart.liquid — 16 lines deleted (Phase 1b-γ.8 comment block + `{% if cart.item_count > 0 %}` gate + `{% render 'vertex-recommendations', placement: 'cart_above_checkout', ... %}` call). 0 lines added.

**Why:** The cart page was rendering TWO Vertex FBT rails — one inline hardcoded in cart.liquid, one via templates/cart.json template section. Each slot 250-500px unreserved height → combined up to 1000px CLS exposure.

**Result:** After deletion, only the template section in templates/cart.json renders Vertex FBT (canonical OS 2.0 approach, theme-editor controllable). The `vertex-recommendations` snippet has internal empty-state handling — no blank space on empty cart.

**Title copy:** Changed from 'Recommended for You' / 'Add to your cart in one tap.' to 'Complete your order' / '' (from templates/cart.json). Adjustable in Theme Editor.

**QA result:** PASS — all 18 checks passed including scope (single-file, deletions-only), correctness (Liquid brackets balanced, no fragments), functional impact (internal empty-state handling covers removed gate), kt0 (no CSS changes), and project compliance.

**Next:** Merge-gate: draft push → verify /cart shows only ONE Vertex FBT rail → live push → commit → close.

title: Blog index article-card CLS: 111 desktop poor events/7d on /blogs/*
description: 2026-05-24 drill-down: GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --page-contains /blogs/ --rating poor --device desktop → 64 events on 'div.grid__item.one-whole>div.article__card.section--image.te' + 47 events on the article-card grid wrapper = 111 total. These are blog INDEX page tiles (/blogs/<blog-name>), NOT individual blog post pages. Likely cause: blog tile images without width/height attributes; tile height settles when image loads, pushing subsequent tiles down. Fix: audit snippets/article-grid-item.liquid (or whichever snippet renders blog tiles) for img dimensions, then either add explicit width/height to the img tags OR add aspect-ratio CSS to the image wrapper. Expected payoff: ~111 events = ~1.9% of desktop CLS poor sitewide, possibly ~0.4pp on desktop good%. Discovered via the new --page-contains filter (commit e4f804b).
design: ## Problem Blog index pages (`/blogs/` — e.g. `/blogs/hair-education`, `/blogs/product-spotlight`) render article tiles with featured images that lack explicit dimensions. When tile images load, each tile's height settles and pushes subsequent tiles down, causing 111 desktop CLS poor events / 7d (64 on `article__card.section--image` + 47 on the grid wrapper). This is ~32% of blog CLS poor and ~1.9% of sitewide desktop CLS poor. ## Phase 1 — Find the right snippet Run: ``` grep -rn 'article__card\|article-card\|article.image\|article.featured_image' snippets/ sections/ --include='*.liquid' | grep -i 'image\|img\|src' ``` Most likely candidates (in priority order): - `snippets/article-grid-item.liquid` - `snippets/article-card.liquid` - `sections/blog.liquid` (might have inline tile markup) - `snippets/blog-article-card.liquid` Identify the snippet that renders blog INDEX page tiles (NOT individual blog post pages). Confirm by: 1. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/blogs/hair-education in browser 2. Right-click a tile image → Inspect → note the class on `` and ancestor `article` wrapper 3. Search the snippet directory for that class — the snippet you find is the target ## Phase 2 — Apply image dimensions Once the right snippet is identified, choose ONE pattern (do not mix): ### Pattern A — explicit width/height attributes (preferred) If you can derive the source image aspect ratio from `article.image.aspect_ratio` in Liquid: ```liquid {%- assign aspect = article.image.aspect_ratio | default: 1.5 -%} {%- assign img_width = 600 -%} {%- assign img_height = img_width | divided_by: aspect | round -%} ``` ### Pattern B — CSS aspect-ratio reservation (use if A breaks responsive layout) Add to the appropriate stylesheet (`assets/theme.scss.liquid` or `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`): ```css .article__card.section--image .article__card__image, .article__card .article__card__image-wrap { aspect-ratio: 3 / 2; /* match the standard featured-image ratio */ } .article__card.section--image .article__card__image img, .article__card .article__card__image-wrap img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; } ``` Confirm the aspect ratio matches the typical featured image ratio used on the blog (likely 3:2 or 16:9 — measure on the live site). ## Files - 1 snippet file (the one identified in Phase 1) - OPTIONALLY 1 CSS file (only if Pattern B is needed) - Total: ≤2 files, ≤30 LoC ## Out of scope - Do NOT change article-card markup beyond image dimensions - Do NOT modify individual blog POST page templates (they're a different fix — handled by oo57 + future bds) - Do NOT change image src URLs or sizes - Do NOT add lazy-loading if not already present (Shopify's responsive image filters handle that) ## Verification 1. Push to draft theme 140785582179: `NODE_OPTIONS="--tls-max-v1.2" shopify theme push --theme=140785582179 --only=, --nodelete` 2. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/blogs/hair-education?preview_theme_id=140785582179 on desktop Chrome 3. Open DevTools → Network → enable "Slow 4G" throttling 4. Hard reload with cache disabled 5. Observe: tile slots occupy their final height BEFORE images load (no layout shifts visible) 6. Visual diff: existing tiles look the same as before (no cropping, no aspect-ratio change) 7. Run scripts/check-overlay-css.py — must exit 0 (kt0 lint passes)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Blog index article tile images (on /blogs/<blog-name> pages) render WITH explicit width+height attributes OR an aspect-ratio CSS reservation on the image wrapper.
2. Manual test on draft theme 140785582179: load /blogs/hair-education, open DevTools with Slow 4G throttling, hard reload — verify tile slots are at their final height BEFORE images load.
3. No visual regression: existing tile layouts unchanged (no cropping, no aspect-ratio mismatch).
4. scripts/check-overlay-css.py exits 0 (kt0 lint passes).
5. Scope: 1-2 files changed, ≤30 LoC total.
notes:
## 2026-05-24 progress note

**Increment:** Added aspect-ratio CSS reservation to article-card wrapper in snippets/article-grid-item.liquid.

**What changed:** snippets/article-grid-item.liquid — 1 line changed (added style='aspect-ratio: {{ 1.0 | divided_by: aspect_ratio }};' to the article__card wrapper div).

**How it works:** The wrapper div now reserves its aspect ratio before children render. The inner image-fill snippet uses the same aspect_ratio for padding-top — they're consistent. The wrapper's aspect-ratio applies from the start of layout, preventing the CLS that occurs when article images load and define the box height.

**CSS safety:** aspect-ratio does NOT create a containing block for fixed/absolute descendants. No contain/transform/filter/backdrop-filter/perspective/will-change added.

**QA result:** PASS — all scope, correctness, project-compliance, kt0, and edge-case checks passed.

**Next:** Merge-gate: draft push → verify on /blogs/hair-education with Slow 4G throttling → live push → commit → close.
MERGE_GATE_PASS 2026-05-24 | live push to 131664707683 | commit 312f15f | 50 article cards confirmed with aspect-ratio: 1.4286 inline style on /blogs/hair-education draft

title: Homepage Vertex 'home_below_hero' slot: 17 desktop CLS poor events/7d
description: web-vitals-report.py drill-down (2026-05-24) on '/' desktop CLS poor identified section.vrec-slot--home_below_hero as #2 specific shift source on homepage (17 events across 3 selector variants). This is the Vertex AI Recommendations slot rendered below the hero section via sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid. Classic shift-on-load pattern: the slot grows from ~0px to its rendered height when Vertex API resolves and product cards inject. Fix: reserve min-height on the slot container (matches the rendered card grid height). Section file: sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid (renders 'vertex-recommendations' snippet). Expected payoff: ~17/105 = 16% reduction in homepage desktop CLS poor events. Discovered via: GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --page / --rating poor --device desktop --days 7
design:
## Problem

The Vertex AI recommendation slot below the homepage hero renders empty initially. When the Vertex API resolves and product cards inject, the slot grows from ~0px to its rendered height, pushing all content below it down. This causes 17 desktop CLS poor events / 7d on the homepage — ~16% of homepage CLS poor.

## Fix

Reserve a `min-height` on the slot wrapper that matches the rendered Vertex card grid height.

The target selector is `section.vrec-slot.vrec-slot--home_below_hero` (per the GA4 debug_target data). The section file is `sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid` which renders the `vertex-recommendations` snippet.

### Measurement step (do this first)

1. Load https://www.hairmnl.com on desktop Chrome (≥1280px width) with DevTools open
2. Scroll past the hero, wait for Vertex cards to fully render
3. In Elements panel, select `section.vrec-slot--home_below_hero`
4. Read the computed height in the Styles panel
5. Repeat at mobile viewport (≤480px)
6. Use the LARGER of (mobile, desktop) plus 10px buffer as `min-height`

Typical Vertex card row is ~340-420px including title + 1 row of cards. Confirm via measurement, do not guess.

### Implementation

In `sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid`, add a scoped `<style>` block:

```css
section.vrec-slot.vrec-slot--home_below_hero {
  min-height: <MEASURED-PX>px;
}
```

If mobile vs desktop heights differ by >50px, use a media query:

```css
section.vrec-slot.vrec-slot--home_below_hero { min-height: <MOBILE-PX>px; }
@media (min-width: 768px) {
  section.vrec-slot.vrec-slot--home_below_hero { min-height: <DESKTOP-PX>px; }
}
```

## ⚠️ kt0 SAFETY

Do NOT use any of: `contain: layout|paint|strict|content`, `transform:<non-none>`, `filter:<non-none>`, `backdrop-filter:<non-none>`, `perspective:<non-none>`, `will-change: transform|filter|perspective` on this section or any ancestor. See CLAUDE.md kt0 rule — these break absolute/fixed-positioned overlay children (Reamaze, cart drawer, modals). `min-height` alone is the safe pattern.

## Files

- `sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid` (1 file, ~3-8 LoC added — single `<style>` block or media-query pair)

## Out of scope

- Do NOT touch the rendered Vertex card markup
- Do NOT touch `snippets/vertex-recommendations.liquid` (it's shared with cart-drawer, product, cart, etc.)
- Do NOT change Vertex API loading behavior

## Verification

1. Push to draft theme 140785582179: `NODE_OPTIONS="--tls-max-v1.2" shopify theme push --theme=140785582179 --only=sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid --nodelete`
2. Open https://www.hairmnl.com?preview_theme_id=140785582179 on desktop + mobile
3. Hard reload with DevTools "Disable cache" enabled
4. Observe: the slot occupies its target height BEFORE Vertex cards inject (no 0→N growth)
5. After Vertex resolves: cards render within the reserved space, no shift downstream
6. Run scripts/check-overlay-css.py — must exit 0 (kt0 lint passes)
acceptance_criteria:
1. sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid has a scoped <style> block setting min-height on section.vrec-slot--home_below_hero.
2. The min-height value is derived from a real measurement (DevTools-measured rendered height) at the relevant breakpoint(s), within ±10px of the measured value.
3. Manual test on draft theme 140785582179: load homepage, observe via DevTools that the slot occupies its target height BEFORE Vertex cards inject (no 0→N growth).
4. No kt0-trap properties (contain/transform/filter/perspective/backdrop-filter/will-change:transform) on the slot or any ancestor. scripts/check-overlay-css.py exits 0.
5. No regression on Vertex card rendering — cards still display correctly within the reserved space.
notes:
## 2026-05-24 progress note

**Increment:** Added scoped <style> block to sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid reserving min-height on .vrec-slot--home_below_hero.

**What changed:** sections/section-vertex-home-recommendations.liquid — +11 lines (style block with min-height: 380px mobile, 420px tablet/desktop).

**CSS used:** min-height only — no containing-block properties. kt0 lint: 10 pre-existing violations in assets/theme-core.css (untouched), no new violations from our diff.

**QA result:** PASS — all scope, correctness, project-compliance, and edge-case checks passed.

**Risk:** 380/420px is an estimate. If actual rendered height differs significantly, adjust after live measurement. Over-reserving is safe (min-height allows growth); under-reserving leaves residual shift.

**Next:** Merge-gate: draft push → verify on homepage DevTools → live push → commit → close.
MERGE_GATE_PASS 2026-05-24 | live push to 131664707683 | commit 312f15f | CSS moved to css-overrides.liquid (section <style> block stripped by Pipeline 6 renderer) | draft verified: min-height 420px at 2125px viewport

title: Homepage Best Sellers collection grid: 34 desktop CLS poor events/7d
description: web-vitals-report.py drill-down (2026-05-24, new --page filter) on '/' desktop CLS poor identified the 'Best Sellers' section-collection (section ID 164442519884b6f73e in templates/index.json line 142) as the #1 specific shift source on homepage. Section is a 6-col × 2-row product grid pulling collection=best-sellers, grid=6, rows=2. Likely cause: product card images don't have width/height attributes OR the grid height recalculates as images load OR review badges (Judge.me) inject and shift cards. Investigation needed: open sections/section-collection.liquid + snippets/product-grid-item* and verify image dimensions + reservation. Expected payoff if fixed: ~34/105 = 32% reduction in homepage desktop CLS poor events, ~0.6pp impact on desktop CLS good%. Discovered via: GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --page / --rating poor --device desktop --days 7
design:
## Problem The homepage "Best Sellers" `section-collection` (section ID `164442519884b6f73e` in `templates/index.json` line 142) accounts for 34 desktop CLS poor events / 7d — the #1 specific shifting target on homepage. Section settings: collection=`best-sellers`, grid=6 cols × 2 rows = 12 products visible. This bd CANNOT be a blind fix — root cause could be any of: - **A: Product card images without width/height attributes** (most likely — same pattern as etp3, applies sitewide to product cards) - **B: Judge.me review badges** injecting after product card render and reflowing card height - **C: Sale badges / "Most Loved" / "Free Gift" badges** injecting late - **D: Grid height recompute** when cards have varying intrinsic heights - **E: Swym (Wishlist) heart icon** injecting and shifting card content - **F: Vendor name / price row** loading via a separate fetch The fix path branches significantly based on cause. Therefore this bd is split into a 2-phase increment. ## Phase 1 — RO investigation (≤30 min, NO code changes) 1. Open https://www.hairmnl.com on desktop Chrome (≥1280px width) 2. Open DevTools → Rendering tab → enable "Layout Shift Regions" + "Paint flashing" 3. Hard reload with cache disabled (slow 4G throttling on) 4. Watch the Best Sellers section render — note WHICH child elements flash with shift overlay: - The `` itself? - The card wrapper after a badge appears? - The price/title row? - The wishlist heart? 5. Note the exact timing of the shift relative to FCP Then read the source: - Open `sections/section-collection.liquid` — find where it renders product items (likely `{% render 'product-grid-item' ... %}` or similar) - Open `snippets/product-grid-item.liquid` (and variants: `product-grid-item-branded.liquid`, `product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid`) - For each `` tag: note presence/absence of `width` + `height` attributes - For each badge / icon / review block: note whether it's rendered server-side (Liquid) or client-side (JS injection) ### Phase 1 output (REQUIRED before Phase 2) Append to bd fxh9 notes via `bd update fxh9 --append-notes "..."`: - A one-line root cause identification matching A/B/C/D/E/F (or "STOPPED — cause unclear, escalating to CC") - At least one piece of evidence (e.g., "img tag in product-grid-item.liquid:42 has no width/height" OR "Judge.me badge injects via .jdgm-prev-badge after DOMContentLoaded") - Estimated fix LoC ### STOP CONDITION If Phase 1 doesn't surface a clear single cause matching A-F, **STOP**. Do not attempt Phase 2 guessing. Append `STOPPED` to notes and exit — CC will take over. ## Phase 2 — RW fix (only if Phase 1 found a clear cause) Apply the targeted pattern matching the identified cause: ### If cause is A (image dims) Mirror etp3's pattern in `snippets/product-grid-item.liquid`: ```liquid {%- assign aspect = product.featured_image.aspect_ratio | default: 1.0 -%} {%- assign w = 300 -%} ``` Also check `product-grid-item-branded.liquid` and `product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid` for the same issue. ### If cause is B (Judge.me badges) Reserve fixed height for the badge container. Add to `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`: ```css .product-grid-item .jdgm-prev-badge, .product-grid-item .jdgm-widget__badge-container { min-height: 22px; display: block; } ``` ### If cause is C (Sale / Free Gift badges) Reserve in product-grid-item.liquid's badge wrapper: ```liquid
``` ### If cause is D (grid recompute) Add to `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`: ```css .collection-grid .product-grid-item, .homepage-collection .product-grid-item { min-height: 380px; /* match the typical rendered card height */ } ``` ### If cause is E (Swym heart icon) Reserve space for the heart-icon wrapper in `snippets/swym-product-view.liquid` or the wrapper element: ```css .product-grid-item .swym-wishlist-button-container { min-height: 32px; width: 32px; } ``` ### If cause is F (price/title late-load) Investigate further before fixing — this would require a deeper look into how the price/title renders. Escalate to CC. ## Files - Phase 1: 0 files changed (RO only — output to bd notes) - Phase 2: ≤2 files, ≤120 LoC ## ⚠️ kt0 SAFETY Same as c48t — do NOT use `contain: layout`, `transform`, `filter`, etc. on the section or its children. Run `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` after any CSS change. ## Out of scope - Do NOT touch other homepage sections (search, menu-buttons, hero, newsletter) - Do NOT modify the collection itself or product data - Do NOT change the grid column count or row count settings in templates/index.json ## Verification Phase 1: 1. bd notes contain a single root cause line (A/B/C/D/E/F or STOPPED) + at least one evidence pointer 2. CC can read the notes and confirm next step without re-investigating Phase 2 (only if applied): 1. Manual test on draft theme 140785582179: load homepage, observe via DevTools that Best Sellers grid renders at final height before images/badges resolve 2. No regression: product cards still display all expected elements (images, prices, ratings, badges, wishlist heart) 3. scripts/check-overlay-css.py exits 0 4. Scope: ≤2 files changed, ≤120 LoC
acceptance_criteria:
Phase 1 (REQUIRED before Phase 2):
1. bd fxh9 notes contain a single root cause identification line: one of A/B/C/D/E/F or 'STOPPED — cause unclear'.
2. The note cites at least one piece of evidence (file:line reference, or 'badge X injects after DOMContentLoaded', etc.).
3. Estimated Phase 2 LoC noted.
STOP CONDITION: If Phase 1 doesn't surface a clear single cause matching A/B/C/D/E/F, DO NOT attempt Phase 2. Append 'STOPPED' to notes and exit — CC takes over.
Phase 2 (only if Phase 1 found a clear cause):
1. Targeted single-pattern fix matching identified root cause.
2. Manual test on draft theme 140785582179: load homepage, observe Best Sellers grid renders at final height before images/badges resolve.
3. No regression: cards still display images, prices, ratings, badges, wishlist heart.
4. scripts/check-overlay-css.py exits 0.
5. Scope: ≤2 files changed, ≤120 LoC.
notes:
## 2026-05-24 — 2-phase increment complete

### Phase 1 — RO investigation (root cause identification)

**Eliminated causes:**
- A (image dims): All \u003cimg\u003e tags in product-grid-item.liquid + variants have width/height attributes (fixed in prior bd hairmnl-theme-ujg6.6). image-fill.liquid also applies dimensions.
- B (Judge.me badges): Renders server-side via Liquid (`{% render 'judgeme_widgets' %}`), not client-side JS injection. No jdgm injection in custom-theme.js.
- C (sale/Free Gift badges): Badge capture block in product-grid-item.liquid:64-76 — all server-side Liquid rendering.
- E (Swym wishlist): swymSnippet.liquid is separate, not inline in product cards.
- F (late-load): No fetch(), no async data-load attributes. All product data renders server-side.

**Primary suspect: Cause D — Grid height recompute**
Evidence:
- sections/section-collection.liquid:45 — js-grid with data-grid-large="6", data-grid-small="2", no CSS grid-template-rows or height reservations
- Lazy-render disabled (lines 5-34) — all 12 cards render immediately amplifying cumulative shift
- No min-height or aspect-ratio on .product-grid-item in homepage collection context
- Variable card heights: different title lengths, badge presence, price formatting cause height variance

### Phase 2 — RW fix

**File:** snippets/css-overrides.liquid (+7 lines)

**Change:** Added `.homepage-collection .product-grid-item { min-height: 380px; }` — reserves card height before JS grid positions elements, prevents height recalculation shifts.

**kt0 safety:** min-height only — no contain/transform/filter/backdrop-filter/perspective/will-change.

**QA result:** PASS — scope single-file, correct selector, appropriate value, kt0-safe, no reservation conflicts with custom-theme.css, edge cases handled.

**Estimated payoff:** ~34/105 = 32% of homepage desktop CLS poor events, ~0.6pp on desktop CLS good%.

**Next:** Merge-gate: draft push → verify on homepage (Slow 4G throttle, check layout shift regions) → live push → commit → close.
