title: P3.2: App deprecation monitoring (vendor changelogs)
description: For the top 6 apps from P1.1 (highest dependency: Klaviyo, Reamaze, LimeSpot, Elevar, Pro Blogger, Octane AI — adjust based on matrix), set up monitoring of their changelogs / release notes. Either: subscribe to vendor email lists with a dedicated alias, OR write a small scraper, OR add quarterly manual-check reminders. Goal: get advance notice when a vendor announces TAE-only future.
design:
# Design notes — dispatch prep (2026-05-17)

## Output path (explicit, do not deviate)

The single output file is **`os2-migration/vendor-monitoring.md`** — top-level `os2-migration/`, **NOT** `docs/os2-migration/`. The directory already exists with README.md, feasibility.html, app-compatibility-matrix.md, customization-audit.md, perf-baseline-comparison.md. Place the new file there. No `docs/` prefix.

## Input: which 6 apps to monitor

Read `os2-migration/app-compatibility-matrix.md` (closed bd hairmnl-theme-80z0.1) and pick the 6 apps with the highest combined "current dependency × deprecation risk." Suggested starting set if not adjusting:

1. Klaviyo (email/SMS — heavy on-page weight, snippet integration)
2. Re:amaze (live chat — 213 KB, hardcoded snippet)
3. LimeSpot / Personalizer.io (recommendations — 249 KB always-on)
4. Elevar (conversion tracking — 2.6s PSI latency reported)
5. Pro Blogger / SA (multiple legacy snippets, no TAE)
6. Searchanise (hardcoded script tag, NEED-VERIFY for TAE availability)

If you swap a different app into the top-6 based on the matrix, justify the swap in the doc.

## Approach to decide between

Per the ticket: pick ONE of the three (or a hybrid) and document the choice:

1. **Email-alias subscription** — set up a shared inbox (e.g., `migration-alerts@hairmnl.com`) and subscribe to each vendor's "product updates" / "release notes" / "developer changelog" newsletter. Pros: zero maintenance. Cons: vendors may not have a list.
2. **Scraper** — small Python or Node script that polls each vendor's changelog page weekly and diffs. Pros: catches changes even without email. Cons: maintenance burden when vendors restructure their docs.
3. **Quarterly manual check** — calendar reminder; one person spends 30 min per quarter scanning vendor changelogs. Pros: simple, reliable. Cons: lag time up to 90 days.

Hybrid is acceptable — e.g., email-alias for vendors who have lists + quarterly manual for those who don't.

## Doc structure (suggested)

1. **Approach chosen** — one paragraph justifying the pick.
2. **Per-app monitoring table** — for each of the 6 apps: app name, vendor changelog URL (or "n/a"), email-list signup URL (or "n/a"), monitoring mechanism, who's on the hook.
3. **First quarterly review** — calendar date (90 days out from doc-landing date, so 2026-08-15-ish).
4. **Escalation criteria** — what counts as a "TAE-only announcement" that fires migration trigger #1 (per `os2-migration/feasibility.html` triggers section).
5. **Maintenance** — how this doc gets updated when vendors change their changelog setup.

## Scope cap

≤120 LoC for the doc. If a scraper is the chosen approach, ≤80 lines of code in `scripts/vendor-changelog-watch.py` (or similar) — that's the second file. Keep total under 2 files / 200 LoC across the dispatch.

## What NOT to do

- Do not write the actual scraper / monitoring infra if email-alias or manual is the chosen approach. The decision doc is the deliverable; infra is a follow-up if needed.
- Do not implement the monitoring (subscribing emails, creating calendar invites, etc.) — those require credentials and human action. Document the plan.
- Do not touch any file outside `os2-migration/` or (if scraper chosen) `scripts/`.
- Do not create intermediate working-notes files (`raw-data.md`, `working-notes.md`, etc.) — the QA pass on P1.2 caught this and it produces clean-up debt.

## Phantom-completion guard

Before marking close in your bd notes, paste the output of `git show HEAD --stat` to prove the commit landed with the expected file(s).
acceptance_criteria: Monitoring approach decided + documented. Each of the 6 apps has a 'how we'll find out about deprecation' mechanism. First quarterly review scheduled.

title: Preload mismatch on article pages — wasted preload
description: OC's Phase 1 audit for epic 01vl (commit b6ptavvez) discovered that on at least 1 article page (/blogs/hair-education/salon-hair-color-vs-box-dye-which-one-is-best-for-you), the generated in layout/theme.liquid references articles/78.png but the actual hero is articles/47.png. Preload is wasted — the LCP element loads without preload benefit. This is the hero preload added by commit 9hw (LCP fix for hair-education articles). Logic must be picking up an article image other than article.image (probably article.image_url for a different field, OR cached state). Scope: layout/theme.liquid preload generation logic + verification on top 10 article pages. Likely 1 file, ~10 LoC fix once root cause is identified. Independent of the Pro Blogger removal scope (epic 01vl). File separately so it can ship as a fast standalone fix even before Pro Blogger removal lands.
notes: SHIPPED 2026-05-16 commit e639ee9 / pushed d1ac2d9. Root cause: not actually a 'preload mismatch' as OC initially framed. The real issue is that section.settings.blog_show_featured_image = false on top hair-education articles, so the hero block at sections/article.liquid:27-39 doesn't render. article.image preload (9hw, 2026-05-04) fires but the previewed image isn't shown — wasted preload. The actual LCP is the first body , marked fetchpriority+eager by article-content-with-img-dims (7tq, 2026-05-10) but never preloaded. Fix: layout/theme.liquid adds a second preload extracting the first from article.content via Liquid split chains. Browser handles double-preload safely. Verified: purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines now shows BOTH preloads (article.image AND first body image). Other top articles are on Shopify CDN-cached HTML; will pick up the fix after ~5-15 min expiry. CSS REQUIREMENTS:
- .sticky-atc-bar: position: fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 100; background: var(--color-body-bg); border-top: 1px solid var(--color-border); padding: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0) using fallback.

- Mobile breakpoint (≤768px): stack the layout vertically OR truncate the product title to a single line.
- Brand colors: reuse existing CSS tokens (--color-primary, --color-body-text, etc.).
- KT0-safe: NO contain/transform/filter/backdrop-filter/perspective/will-change on .sticky-atc-bar or its ancestors. The whole point of the lint update is to enforce this.

OWNER: OC via /offload.

SCOPE CAP: 2 file modifications, ≤80 LoC delta total.

DEPENDS ON: T1 (snippet defines the .sticky-atc-bar class).
design:
Reference the existing dated-block pattern in snippets/css-overrides.liquid — start the new block with a comment like:

/* 2026-05-16 — Sticky ATC bar (replaces Stky app) */

For mobile-safe-area: use env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for iOS notch devices.

OVERLAY_PATTERNS update in scripts/check-overlay-css.py — find the existing list (look for entries like ".notify-tab", ".modal--notify", ".cart-drawer", etc.) and append both `.sticky-atc-bar` and `.sticky-atc-bar.is-visible`.

Test the lint locally after the edit: `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` should exit 0 with no new violations.

Anti-pattern: do NOT use `transform: translateY(...)` on .sticky-atc-bar for the entrance transition. That creates a containing-block trap. Use `transition: opacity` or `transition: bottom` instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- snippets/css-overrides.liquid has the new dated block.
- scripts/check-overlay-css.py has .sticky-atc-bar and .sticky-atc-bar.is-visible in OVERLAY_PATTERNS.
- Running `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` locally exits 0.
- On the draft theme, sticky bar appears with brand styling.
- iOS safe-area-inset-bottom respected (test on mobile preview).
- No transform/contain/filter on .sticky-atc-bar or .sticky-atc-bar's ancestors.
notes: Superseded by a7av.8 (sticky-ATC bar implementation landed 2026-05-19). The work specified in this ticket was either: (a) absorbed into the single a7av.8 implementation (snippets/product-sticky-bar.liquid + IIFE in assets/hairmnl-common.js + CSS in css-overrides.liquid), or (b) made moot by the implementation path chosen (no separate .pptx deck needed once the working code existed). Closing as won't-do. See a7av.8 close notes for delivered work.

title: Stky replacement: assets/sticky-atc.dev.js + .js
description:
Create the JS module that drives the sticky add-to-cart bar.

NEW FILES:
- assets/sticky-atc.dev.js (readable source, ~150 LoC)
- assets/sticky-atc.js (minified mirror — dev↔min parity per house rule)

CONTRACT:
- Initialize on DOMContentLoaded.
- Use IntersectionObserver to watch the primary product form's ATC button (`[data-product-form] [type="submit"]` or equivalent).
- When the primary ATC scrolls out of view, remove `hidden` from the sticky bar and add `.is-visible` class for transition. Reverse when scrolled back into view.
- Wire the sticky bar's variant selector to the primary form's variant select via 'change' event listeners — single source of truth is the primary form.
- Sticky bar's Add to Cart submit should re-use the primary form's submission flow (call its submit handler or AJAX submit to /cart/add with the same payload).
- On successful add, trigger the existing cart drawer open event (whatever event the theme's cart drawer listens to).
- Fire a GA4 `add_to_cart` event from the sticky bar submit path (match the data shape of the existing primary-form event).
- Idempotent: safe to call init() twice (no double event listeners).

API KEY: none (no backend integration).

OWNER: OC via /offload.

SCOPE CAP: 2 files (dev + min), ≤200 LoC total in dev.js. If exceeded, decompose into T2a (IntersectionObserver + visibility) and T2b (variant sync + submit handler).

DEPENDS ON: T1.
design:
Pattern from any existing theme JS that uses IntersectionObserver. Check assets/theme.dev.js for the lazy-load and announcement-bar patterns.

State management:
- Module-level state: { observer, stickyEl, primaryFormEl, isVisible }
- No global window pollution beyond a single window.HairMNLStickyATC namespace if needed for debugging.

Edge cases to handle:
- Variant becomes sold-out → disable sticky bar ATC button (mirror primary form's disabled state)
- Page navigation (Shopify section rendering API) → re-initialize on section reload events
- Primary form not found (non-PDP) → bail silently
- IntersectionObserver unsupported (very old browsers) → polyfill or feature-detect and bail

Minification: use terser or simple manual minification. The dev.js and js files must produce IDENTICAL behavior — verify by manual diff of variable renames only.

Anti-pattern: do not duplicate the variant change logic. Use event delegation to the primary form's variant select.

Read-the-docs: not needed (browser APIs are stable).
acceptance_criteria:
- assets/sticky-atc.dev.js + assets/sticky-atc.js both exist.
- dev.js is ≤200 LoC.
- min.js is functionally identical to dev.js (manual diff: only whitespace + variable renames).
- On a sold-out PDP, sticky bar appears when scrolled past primary ATC.
- On an in-stock PDP, sticky bar appears with correct variant + price; ATC submit adds to cart and opens cart drawer.
- Variant change in primary form propagates to sticky bar (and vice versa).
- GA4 add_to_cart event fires once per successful add (verified in browser DevTools).
- No console errors on load or during scroll.
- IntersectionObserver feature-detect bails cleanly on unsupported browsers.
notes: Superseded by a7av.8 (sticky-ATC bar implementation landed 2026-05-19). The work specified in this ticket was either: (a) absorbed into the single a7av.8 implementation (snippets/product-sticky-bar.liquid + IIFE in assets/hairmnl-common.js + CSS in css-overrides.liquid), or (b) made moot by the implementation path chosen (no separate .pptx deck needed once the working code existed). Closing as won't-do. See a7av.8 close notes for delivered work.

title: Stky replacement: snippets/sticky-add-to-cart.liquid
description:
Create the new Liquid snippet for the theme-native sticky add-to-cart bar. NEW FILE: snippets/sticky-add-to-cart.liquid CONTENT: - A bottom-fixed bar markup containing: - Small product image (40×40 thumbnail) on the left - Product title + current variant price (truncated on mobile) - Variant selector dropdown (mirrors primary form's variant select) - Quantity input (qty=1 default) - Add to Cart button (primary CTA style, brand coral) - Wrapper element:
...
- Use Liquid `{{ product.featured_image | image_url: ... }}` and `{{ current_variant }}` from page context. - Hidden by default via `hidden` attribute; JS module toggles visibility. - Render-once: included via `{% render 'sticky-add-to-cart' %}` from layout/theme.liquid before . PATTERN: Mirror the structure used by snippets/product-form.liquid for variant + qty + ATC inputs. Reuse existing form action (/cart/add) and CSRF tokens. OWNER: OC via /offload. SCOPE CAP: 1 new file, ≤120 LoC. If exceeded, decompose before dispatching. DEPENDS ON: T0 (deck approved by stakeholders).
design:
Use the existing brand button styling and form CSRF tokens. Reference snippets/product-form.liquid lines around the {% form 'product' %} block.

The snippet must work both on PDPs (where product context is available) AND fail gracefully on non-PDP pages (where it should not render — guard with `{% if template contains 'product' %}` or render the empty shell and let JS handle product detection).

Variant selector should mirror the primary form's variant dropdown by ID/name so the JS module can sync state via event listeners.

For accessibility: bar must have role="region" aria-label="Sticky add to cart" and the qty input must have an associated <label>.

Read-the-docs: not needed (Shopify Liquid syntax stable).
acceptance_criteria:
- snippets/sticky-add-to-cart.liquid exists.
- File is ≤120 LoC.
- Renders empty/hidden when included in layout/theme.liquid before </body>.
- Markup parses cleanly in Shopify Theme Inspector.
- Variant selector reflects current_variant state.
- ATC button submits to /cart/add (verified via theme preview).
- Mobile layout doesn't overflow viewport horizontally at 375px.

The work specified in this ticket was either: (a) absorbed into the single a7av.8 implementation (snippets/product-sticky-bar.liquid + IIFE in assets/hairmnl-common.js + CSS in css-overrides.liquid), or (b) made moot by the implementation path chosen (no separate .pptx deck needed once the working code existed). Closing as won't-do. See a7av.8 close notes for delivered work.

title: BIS: SMS bridge serverless function (conditional on T0 decision)
description:
CONDITIONAL ticket. Only created/worked if T0's deck stakeholder review approves Option B (Semaphore, PH-local SMS) or Option C (Twilio).

If Option A (Skip SMS) is chosen: close this ticket as won't-fix.

WORK (if approved):
Build a minimal serverless function that receives a Klaviyo webhook on the BIS-flow fired event, formats the SMS body, and dispatches via the chosen provider.

LOCATION:
- Option B (Semaphore): new file in hairmnl-automation/scripts/bis-sms-bridge/index.js (serverless, deploy via Vercel or Cloudflare Workers — choose during ticket) or a new repo if hairmnl-automation isn't a great fit.
- Option C (Twilio): same shape, different provider.

INPUTS:
- Klaviyo webhook payload (verify signature using Klaviyo's webhook secret)
- Customer phone (E.164, "+63XXXXXXXXXX")
- Rendered SMS body (Klaviyo formats it; we just pass through)

OUTPUTS:
- Provider API call (Semaphore: POST https://api.semaphore.co/api/v4/messages; Twilio: POST https://api.twilio.com/.../Messages.json)
- Log row to JSONL or stdout (timestamp, recipient, provider message ID, cost in PHP)

OWNER: OC via /offload.

DEPENDS ON: T0 (decision), T1 (Klaviyo flow must have a webhook step).
design:
Stack TBD per deck decision. Recommended: Cloudflare Worker (low cold-start, generous free tier, easy secrets via wrangler.toml).

Authentication: Klaviyo signs webhooks with an HMAC-SHA256 hash of the body using a shared secret. Verify before processing.

Provider client: Semaphore has a simple REST API (just an api_key + number + message); Twilio uses basic auth (Account SID + Auth Token). Use the official Twilio Node SDK or fetch directly for Semaphore.

Cost tracking: write each send to a JSONL file in a Cloudflare R2 bucket or post a message to a Slack channel (configurable). hairmnl-data can later pull this for the analytics view (T8b extension).

Use context7 to fetch the chosen provider's latest docs before writing the call. Read-the-docs rule applies.
acceptance_criteria:
- Function deployed and reachable from Klaviyo webhook step.
- Klaviyo webhook signature verified on every request; invalid signatures rejected.
- Test SMS sent successfully to a real PH phone via the chosen provider.
- Cost-per-message logged.
- Klaviyo flow step pointed at the function URL.
- Documented secrets (env var names, where stored).

title: BIS: investigate Appikon CSV export + migration script
description:
Investigate whether Appikon offers a CSV export of in-flight back-in-stock subscriptions, and if so, write a one-shot import script to bring them into Klaviyo.

STEPS:
1. Email Appikon support: request CSV export of all pending BIS subscriptions (email, phone, variant ID, subscribe date). Subject line and body draft handled by CC.
2. If export is provided within 3 business days:
   - Write scripts/migrate_appikon_to_klaviyo.py — reads CSV, POSTs each row to Klaviyo BIS API using the server-side (private) Klaviyo key.
   - Idempotent: skip if email+variant already subscribed.
   - Dry-run mode first; actual import after sample-of-10 verification.
3. If no export or unresponsive:
   - Document the cutover risk: in-flight subscribers may silently miss their restock alert.
   - Mitigation A: leave Appikon flow in monitor-only mode for 30 days post-cutover (no theme code path; just don't uninstall yet).
   - Mitigation B: accept silent miss, add a banner to PDPs apologizing for the transition gap (T7 follow-up).

OWNER: Human (HairMNL ops, contacts Appikon) + CC (drafts email, writes script if needed).

DEPENDS ON: T1 (Klaviyo BIS API access needed to write import script).
BLOCKS: T7 (cutover decision rests on this).
design:
Email draft: friendly, business-tone, request: "We are migrating from Appikon to a Klaviyo-native back-in-stock implementation as part of our 2026 platform consolidation. Could you provide a CSV export of all currently pending back-in-stock subscriptions (customer email or phone, variant SKU or product handle, subscription timestamp) before we uninstall the app? This would help us avoid silently dropping in-flight subscribers." CC: jessica.joson@hairmnl.com.

If Appikon offers self-serve export in the app admin, prefer that path over support email.

Import script (if needed):
- Uses Klaviyo's server-side Profiles + BIS Subscription APIs (private key, set in env)
- Logs every row outcome (subscribed | already-existed | error) to a JSONL file
- Resumable: track high-watermark row index in a checkpoint file
acceptance_criteria:
- Appikon support contacted (email logged to bd notes).
- Either: CSV obtained AND import script run successfully (sample-of-10 verified, then full run), OR documented decision to accept cutover gap with explicit mitigation chosen.
- T7 (cutover) is unblocked.

title: BIS: add new overlay selectors to OVERLAY_PATTERNS (kt0 lint)

Add the new overlay selectors introduced by T2 + T3 to scripts/check-overlay-css.py's OVERLAY_PATTERNS list, so the kt0 lint catches any future containing-block regressions on the new overlay.

DEPENDS ON: T2 + T3 (snippets must exist before lint patterns reference their selectors).
BLOCKS: T5 (smoke test runs the lint; must pass).

Add the three new selectors using the existing entry format (regex pattern + a human-readable name). Run python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py to confirm the lint still exits 0 with the new patterns added.

- python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py exits 0 (clean) against the current tree.
- If any of the new selectors would currently fail the lint (e.g., due to transforms applied), the fix is committed in the same PR.

title: Theme audit + hardening pass (CWV-gated)
description:
Once the storefront's Core Web Vitals reach "all green" in CrUX field
data — Google's threshold for a "Good URL" — we should run a
comprehensive audit + hardening pass on the theme: code health,
security, dependencies, regression-prevention coverage, architecture
docs freshness, performance budgets. The idea is to lock in the
performance work before technical debt accumulates and before any
expansion (new features, new app integrations, Vertex AI cutover) can
introduce regressions.

This is intentionally DEFERRED until the trigger fires. Filed now so
the work isn't forgotten and so the trigger condition is documented
explicitly.

TRIGGER CONDITION (must hold for 14 consecutive days before this
unblocks — gate on day-15 to confirm sustained, not a noisy peak):

All 3 Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, INP) at ≥75% good in CrUX p75 for
BOTH mobile AND desktop = 6 metrics, all green.

CWV "good" thresholds (Google):
  LCP good ≤ 2,500 ms
  CLS good ≤ 0.10
  INP good ≤ 200 ms

Property-level good URL: ≥75% of pageviews in the good bucket per
metric per device.

Current state (CrUX window ending 2026-05-11, captured 2026-05-14):

  | Device  | Metric | Good % today | Threshold | Gap to trigger |
  |---------|--------|--------------|-----------|----------------|
  | Mobile  | LCP    |    61.9%     |   75%     |   +13.1 pp     |
  | Mobile  | CLS    |    82.3%     |   75%     |    PASSING ✓   |
  | Mobile  | INP    |    67.9%     |   75%     |    +7.1 pp     |
  | Desktop | LCP    |    72.8%     |   75%     |    +2.2 pp     |
  | Desktop | CLS    |    56.3%     |   75%     |   +18.7 pp     |
  | Desktop | INP    |    84.2%     |   75%     |    PASSING ✓   |

2 of 6 metrics already passing. 4 to climb 2-19 pp before trigger.
Realistic timeline at current trajectory: 60-90 days (mid-July to
mid-August 2026).

When trigger fires:
  - Flip this bd from status=deferred to status=open.
  - Decompose into per-area child bds (security, code health,
    deps, etc. — see Design below for the audit scope).
  - Each child becomes its own phased dispatch in CC or via OC,
    depending on scope.

Scope is intentionally NOT broken into children yet — audit work is
discovery-driven, and we don't know exactly what we'll find until we
look. The Design section below is a checklist of areas to cover;
break into children at trigger time.

Out of scope:
  - Ongoing CWV improvement (this epic is what comes AFTER the CWV
    work is done). The Phase 2 CLS sprints (bd 987 + children) and
    the Reamaze defer epic (meu) continue separately to drive the
    trigger condition.
  - Vertex AI cutover (bd oyw) — separate project.
  - Theme rebuild / migration to OS 2.0 — not on the table.
design:
Audit + hardening scope (each area becomes a child bd when trigger
fires; ordered by typical risk priority):

1. CODE HEALTH — re-run + tighten the theme-check linter sweep.
   The April pass took 1,554 → 33 offenses. Audit what crept back in
   over the next ~90 days. Look for:
   - New ImgWidthAndHeight regressions (CLS surface returning)
   - Liquid filter inefficiencies (img_url remaining cases)
   - Duplicate snippet renders / dead code that survived the BSS
     cleanup
   - Stale comments referencing removed apps / vendors (audit
     css-overrides.liquid, .opencode_hints, CLAUDE.md)
   - Dead asset files in assets/ (orphan after vendor removal)
   - Locale files drift (de, es, fr, pt-BR, pt — keys added/removed
     in en)

2. SECURITY AUDIT — every theme file in critical-path locations,
   focused on XSS + content escape:
   - All `{{ ... }}` outputs that touch customer-supplied content
     (product titles, descriptions, blog body, customer notes, line-
     item properties): verify | escape filter chain.
   - All `<script>` tags that interpolate Liquid variables: confirm
     | json filter is used for object/string interpolation.
   - Cookie/localStorage reads: confirm no eval / no Function ctor.
   - Inline event handlers (onclick=, etc.) on Liquid-rendered HTML:
     audit for risky patterns.
   - GTM container review: any custom HTML tags that haven't been
     reviewed since last audit (~April).
   - Reamaze + LoyaltyLion + Klaviyo + DataFeedWatch app embed
     scopes: confirm we still want these scoped where they are.

3. DEPENDENCY HARDENING — Move toward smaller/fewer dependencies:
   - jQuery removal (bd j0g, currently parked) — re-evaluate once
     vendor.js tree-shake (bd yzk) is feasible. Trigger this only if
     we have a build pipeline by then.
   - assets/vendor.js audit — list every library + version + usage
     count. Identify dead code (Rellax, axios, ellipsis baggage per
     bd yzk).
   - Self-host any Google Fonts / external scripts not yet self-
     hosted (already done for Playfair Display, audit others).
   - Verify all dns-prefetch / preconnect hints in theme.liquid are
     still active vendors (BSS already dropped, audit others).

4. REGRESSION PREVENTION — Expand the smoke test + lint coverage:
   - scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py — extend to PDP cover, blog,
     account/register, footer accordion (bd 1hq still has SKIPs for
     PhotoSwipe + search overlay; complete or fold into this audit).
   - scripts/check-overlay-css.py — expand OVERLAY_PATTERNS as the
     storefront gains new overlays.
   - Add a "CSS reservation parity" lint that flags any selector
     that exists in both snippets/css-overrides.liquid AND
     assets/custom-theme.css with different declarations (the hod
     NO-OP trap from 2026-05-13).
   - Add a "min-height verification" lint that confirms every CLS
     reservation rule has a corresponding computed-style smoke test
     in the Playwright suite.
   - Verify .github/workflows/kt0-css-lint.yml is still passing on
     every PR + the pattern list is current.

5. ARCHITECTURE DOCS FRESHNESS — Confirm the docs still match
   reality after ~90+ days of churn:
   - ARCHITECTURE.md sections 1-12: walk each section, confirm
     accuracy, update line numbers / file references.
   - CLAUDE.md push protocol + theme IDs.
   - .opencode_hints: confirm each hard-lock is still valid +
     update with any new patterns learned.
   - docs/coordinator-handoff.md: refresh format if patterns drifted.
   - bd memories: prune stale entries from `bd memories list`.
   - README.md / .env.example: confirm still accurate.

6. RUNBOOK DRILL — Verify recovery procedures actually work:
   - "Theme rebuild from git" drill: time how long to redeploy the
     entire theme to a fresh Shopify draft theme from `main` HEAD.
     Target: < 10 minutes. If it's slower, document why.
   - "Rollback a bad live push" drill: time how long from "we just
     shipped a bad commit" to "live theme reverted via shopify cli +
     git revert + push". Target: < 5 minutes.
   - "Dashboard recovery" drill: scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py
     fresh run on a new machine — does it actually work end to end?

7. PERFORMANCE BUDGETS — Lock in current state with automated alerts:
   - dashboard/build-perf-dashboard.py already has evaluate_alerts()
     for RUM regression. Verify it's actually wired to Slack.
   - Add lab-side budget: PSI mobile score median should not drop
     below 25 for 3 consecutive days (alert).
   - Add field-side budget: any CWV metric drops > 5 pp good-rate
     week-over-week (alert).
   - Add bytes budget: total JS payload on mobile homepage should
     not grow > 10% from current baseline. Compute baseline now,
     compare quarterly.

8. A11Y AUDIT (out of band but worth bundling at this point):
   - Re-run Lighthouse accessibility on key templates (PDP, cart,
     register, checkout, account, blog, search).
   - Cross-check the 3 a11y fixes from bd 9ct (April) are still
     in place + not regressed.

9. SEO HOUSEKEEPING:
   - Confirm Smart SEO admin config still produces valid Offer
     schema (bd memory smartseo_admin_config).
   - Audit canonical tags + hreflang for the locale-redirect
     scenarios.
   - Verify product metafields used in schema are populated
     consistently.

Notes on sequencing when trigger fires:
- Items 1-3 (code health, security, deps) are the substantive work.
- Items 4-5 (regression prevention, docs) are tooling around it.
- Items 6-7 (runbook, budgets) are operational sustain.
- Items 8-9 (a11y, SEO) are adjacent but worth bundling.

Estimated total effort when triggered: 2-4 weeks of focused work.
Suitable for OC dispatch with CC coordinator gating each area.
acceptance_criteria:
This bd is the umbrella tracker. Acceptance criteria:

1. Trigger verification: 14 consecutive days of CrUX showing all 6
   metrics (LCP/CLS/INP × mobile/desktop) at ≥75% good. Verified by:
     python3 scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py --no-psi --no-ga4
     (look at /dashboard/index.html CrUX cards — all 6 should be
     green/good badge for 14 days running)

2. When trigger fires: bd update --status=open + break into child bds
   per the 9 audit areas in Design. Each child must have its own
   description + design + acceptance before dispatch.

3. Epic close-out (post-audit + hardening pass complete):
   a. Code health: theme-check offenses < 50 (currently 33).
   b. Security: every Liquid output touching user-supplied content
      has documented escape filter chain.
   c. Dependencies: vendor.js documented; any removed dependencies
      have their dead code purged.
   d. Regression prevention: Playwright smoke covers cart drawer,
      mobile nav, search overlay, PDP cover, blog accordions; all 4
      passing in CI.
   e. Docs: ARCHITECTURE.md walks reality; CLAUDE.md + .opencode_hints
      reviewed + current; bd memories pruned.
   f. Runbooks: rebuild-from-git drill done + timed.
   g. Performance budgets: at least one automated alert wired to
      Slack (RUM regression OR PSI floor OR bytes budget).
   h. A11y + SEO: Lighthouse a11y ≥ 90 on top 5 templates; canonical
      + hreflang spot-checks pass.

4. If trigger fires but the founder/marketing team wants to PAUSE
   on hardening (e.g., a competing feature push), this bd stays
   open but with a clear "deferred to <date>" note. Do NOT close
   without doing the work.
notes: Status set to 'deferred' on creation. The trigger condition (14 consecutive days of CrUX all-green across LCP/CLS/INP × mobile/desktop) is NOT yet met. Current gap: mobile LCP +13.1 pp, mobile INP +7.1 pp, desktop LCP +2.2 pp, desktop CLS +18.7 pp. Re-check weekly via build-perf-dashboard.py until trigger fires.

title: 987 Phase 2: Desktop cart-template CLS (84 events, debug_target=cart__te)
description:
OC's 987 Phase 1 audit (2026-05-13, commit 6abcc1c) identified the cart template ('cart__te' debug_target prefix in GA4) as a major desktop CLS contributor: 84 poor-rated CLS events on body.template-cart pages in the 7-day desktop GA4 RUM window.

Likely structural cause (per OC audit): late-rendering content inside the cart line-items area pushes existing items down after first paint. Candidates:
- BOGOS free-gift injection into #freegifts-main-page-container (already has min-height:80px from bd dzi — verify still effective post-BSS cleanup)
- Cart drawer FBT (Vertex slot 2b) loading after line items render
- Discount line + savings line appearing after AJAX response on quantity update
- The Reamaze chat label re-positioning when cart drawer opens (kt0-related, lki was fixed but worth checking)
- Cart row image lazy-load shifting heights (cart-page.css fhh fixed the grid; image load timing may still cause shifts)

Tooling already in place:
- web-vitals-reporter.liquid pushes debug_target on every CLS event (the cart__te prefix selector)
- GA4 dimension debug_target registered + populated
- scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop --days 7 --top 30

Context from today's BSS cleanup (commits dyp 1c0da46 + gj0 7dee6f5):
- BSS B2B Solution + Lock + LTAP all removed today. Cart-related BSS files deleted: bss-b2b-tax-cart-item.liquid, bss-b2b-tax-cart-line-item.liquid, bss-b2b-tax-cart-styles.liquid, bss-b2b-tax-cart-subtotal.liquid (Solution VAT-on-cart). These were injecting late tax displays into cart rows.
- Cart-page.css media-query bug (bd fhh, commit 2768465) was fixed today — desktop 5-col grid is now wrapped in @media (min-width: 768px). Cart heading_wrapper layout no longer leaks onto mobile.
- The 84 events were measured BEFORE these fixes, so the actual current count may already be lower.
design:
Phase 1 — DATA (RO, no code changes):
1. Run `GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop --days 7 --top 30 | grep -E "cart|line_item|freegift|drawer|reamaze"` to refine which cart-page subselector contributes most (cart__te is a prefix; the full selector tells you which specific cell shifts).
2. Identify the top 3 cart-page debug_targets and their Liquid file paths. Common shapes:
   - `cart__te` (top-level) → sections/cart.liquid grid recompute

title: Restructure build_chart_data to {mobile, desktop} (Epic z3x child 1/3)
description:
Restructure `build_chart_data()` at scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:1838 to return `{"mobile": {...}, "desktop": {...}}` instead of flat mobile-only keys. Each strategy dict carries its own labels + all per-day series (score/lcp_s/tbt_ms/cls + min/max bands).

Source for medians: `s["psi"][strategy]`. Source for bands: `s["psi_minmax"][strategy]`. Both already exist in snapshots — the existing _merge_daily_snapshot (c0w) writes both strategies separately.
design:
## File: scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py

### Current shape (line 1838-1885)
```python
def build_chart_data(snapshots, max_points=30):
    pts = []
    for s in snapshots:
        psi = s.get("psi", {}).get("mobile")  # ← hardcoded mobile
        ...
    return {
        "labels": [...],
        "score": [...],
        ...
    }
```

### Target shape (mirror Omura render.py:477)
```python
def build_chart_data(snapshots, max_points=30):
    def _extract(strategy):
        pts = []
        for s in snapshots:
            psi = (s.get("psi") or {}).get(strategy)
            if not psi:
                continue
            ts = s["timestamp"]
            try:
                label = datetime.fromisoformat(ts.replace("Z", "+00:00")).strftime("%b %d")
            except Exception:
                label = ts[:10]
            mm = (s.get("psi_minmax") or {}).get(strategy) or {}
            pts.append({
                "label": label,
                "score": psi["score"],
                "score_min": mm.get("score_min", psi["score"]),
                "score_max": mm.get("score_max", psi["score"]),
                "lcp_s": round(psi["lcp_ms"] / 1000, 3),
                "lcp_s_min": round(mm.get("lcp_ms_min", psi["lcp_ms"]) / 1000, 3),
                "lcp_s_max": round(mm.get("lcp_ms_max", psi["lcp_ms"]) / 1000, 3),
                "tbt_ms": psi["tbt_ms"],
                "tbt_ms_min": mm.get("tbt_ms_min", psi["tbt_ms"]),
                "tbt_ms_max": mm.get("tbt_ms_max", psi["tbt_ms"]),
                "cls": psi["cls"],
                "cls_min": mm.get("cls_min", psi["cls"]),
                "cls_max": mm.get("cls_max", psi["cls"]),
            })
        pts = pts[-max_points:]
        keys = ("score", "score_min", "score_max",
                "lcp_s", "lcp_s_min", "lcp_s_max",
                "tbt_ms", "tbt_ms_min", "tbt_ms_max",
                "cls", "cls_min", "cls_max")
        out = {"labels": [p["label"] for p in pts]}
        for k in keys:
            out[k] = [p[k] for p in pts]
        return out
    return {"mobile": _extract("mobile"), "desktop": _extract("desktop")}
```

### Caller (line ~1895): no change needed
`chart_data = build_chart_data(snapshots)` then `json.dumps(chart_data)` already handles the new nested structure.

### Backward-compat note
Older snapshots written before psi_minmax was promoted will fall through the `mm.get(... psi["..."])` defaults — `band_min == band_max == median`, so the chart band collapses gracefully. Same as Omura's design.

### Out of scope
- Do NOT touch the JS / HTML in the same dispatch — Child 2 (lazy-build wiring) and Child 3 (template grid) handle those.
- Do NOT change snapshot writing logic (PSI fetch, `_merge_daily_snapshot`, `_strip_internal_snapshot_keys`) — they already write per-strategy minmax correctly.
- Do NOT commit — coordinator owns merge gate at end of Epic.

### Verification
```sh
python3 scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py --no-psi --no-crux --render-only
```
Then `grep "chart_data" -A2 dashboard/index.html | head -20` — the embedded JSON should now have `"mobile":{` and `"desktop":{` keys, each with their own `labels` array.
acceptance_criteria: 1. build_chart_data returns nested {mobile:{labels,score,...}, desktop:{labels,score,...}}. 2. Caller passes through unchanged. 3. --render-only succeeds and dashboard/index.html embedded CHART_DATA JSON has both strategy keys with labels+series. 4. Older snapshots without psi_minmax still produce non-empty arrays (defaults to median). 5. Single-file change to scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py.
notes: Phase 1 increment complete: build_chart_data restructured from flat mobile-only to nested {mobile: {...}, desktop: {...}}. Caller at line ~1894 unchanged. Render-only exits 0. CHART_DATA JSON in dashboard/index.html confirmed to have both strategy keys with full labels + series (score, lcp_s, tbt_ms, cls) + min/max bands. Backward compat preserved via .get() fallbacks. Ready for Child 2 (t3t) JS wiring.

title: Epic: Desktop trend charts + mobile/desktop toggle (Omura backport)
description:
HairMNL's perf dashboard has mobile/desktop tabs on the KPI snapshot cards but the day-by-day trend charts (Performance Score, LCP, TBT, CLS) only render mobile data. Omura's `~/OmuraProjects/omura-data` shipped the unified pattern in commits 052e754 (history accumulator), 9117420 (desktop trend toggle), and 940dec0 (band field consumption + fixture tests) — both the KPI cards AND the trend charts toggle together via the same tab switcher.

Goal: mirror Omura's pattern. After this Epic, clicking the Mobile/Desktop tab in the snapshot section swaps both the KPI numbers AND the four trend charts to the matching strategy. Desktop gets its own day-by-day trend with zone bands.

Reference: `~/OmuraProjects/omura-data/src/omura/perf/render.py` — canonical implementation. Diff against `scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py` to find exact deltas. Ship as ONE PR — three sub-tasks are tightly coupled (data shape ↔ JS shape ↔ HTML shape).

title: Three-band Web Vitals zones replace threshold lines in trend charts
description:
The dashboard's "Trend over time — PSI Lab mobile" chart section uses a single colored threshold line per chart (green dashed at 90 for Performance Score, red dashed for LCP/TBT/CLS targets). The color metaphor is inconsistent: green on score = "target you want to exceed," red on LCP/TBT/CLS = "target you want to stay below." Same color, opposite meaning — readers must know which axis direction is "good" for each metric.

Fix: replace the single threshold line with horizontal-band background zones using the official Web Vitals 3-band rating system (Good / Needs Improvement / Poor) for LCP/TBT/CLS, and a single green "good zone" band above 90 for the Performance Score. Each chart's line passes through the colored zones — readers instantly see how good or bad the value is without needing to know axis direction.
design:
## Files to modify
Only `scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py` (single file).

## Current state (read first)
- Lines 1106-1110: `CHART_TARGETS` object maps each chart to `{threshold, higherIsBetter}`.
- Lines 1113-1169: `baseConfig()` JS factory builds the Chart.js config for each chart. The threshold renders as a single `chartjs-plugin-annotation` line annotation (lines 1144-1161).
- Lines 1118: `lineColor` switches green/red based on `higherIsBetter` — this is what we're replacing.
- `chartjs-plugin-annotation@3.0.1` is already loaded (line 739) — supports `type: 'box'` annotations for rectangular zones.

## Web Vitals official thresholds
Use these EXACT band boundaries (do not invent new ones — match web.dev / Lighthouse spec):

| Chart | Good zone | Needs Improvement zone | Poor zone |
|---|---|---|---|
| score | ≥ 90 | 50 – 90 | < 50 |
| lcp   | ≤ 2.5s | 2.5 – 4.0s | > 4.0s |
| tbt   | ≤ 200ms | 200 – 600ms | > 600ms |
| cls   | ≤ 0.10 | 0.10 – 0.25 | > 0.25 |

## Implementation

### Step 1: Extend `CHART_TARGETS` (Python-side string in dashboard script)
Replace the existing object (~line 1106) with:
```js
const CHART_TARGETS = {
  score: { higherIsBetter: true,  good: 90,   needs: 50,   bestCase: 100 },
  lcp:   { higherIsBetter: false, good: 2.5,  needs: 4.0,  bestCase: 0 },
  tbt:   { higherIsBetter: false, good: 200,  needs: 600,  bestCase: 0 },
  cls:   { higherIsBetter: false, good: 0.10, needs: 0.25, bestCase: 0 },
};
```
- `good` = max value of Good zone (or min, depending on direction)
- `needs` = max value of Needs Improvement zone (boundary into Poor)
- `bestCase` = the "best possible" extreme (0 for lower-is-better, 100 for score)

### Step 2: Replace the single targetLine annotation with three box annotations
In `baseConfig()`, REMOVE the `targetLine` annotation block. REPLACE the `annotation: { annotations: {...} }` block with:

```js
annotation: {
  annotations: (() => {
    // Build 3 horizontal background zones using Web Vitals colors.
    // Colors use ~18% alpha so they sit behind the chart line without overpowering.
    const GOOD  = 'rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.12)';   // tailwind green-500 @ 12%
    const NEEDS = 'rgba(245, 158, 11, 0.12)';  // tailwind amber-500 @ 12%
    const POOR  = 'rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.12)';   // tailwind red-500 @ 12%
    if (t.higherIsBetter) {
      // score: best=100, good>=90, needs 50-90, poor<50
      return {
        zoneGood:  { type: 'box', yMin: t.good,    yMax: t.bestCase, backgroundColor: GOOD,  borderWidth: 0 },
        zoneNeeds: { type: 'box', yMin: t.needs,   yMax: t.good,     backgroundColor: NEEDS, borderWidth: 0 },
        zonePoor:  { type: 'box', yMin: 0,         yMax: t.needs,    backgroundColor: POOR,  borderWidth: 0 },
      };
    } else {
      // lcp/tbt/cls: best=0, good<=t.good, needs t.good-t.needs, poor>t.needs
      return {
        zoneGood:  { type: 'box', yMin: t.bestCase, yMax: t.good,  backgroundColor: GOOD,  borderWidth: 0 },
        zoneNeeds: { type: 'box', yMin: t.good,     yMax: t.needs, backgroundColor: NEEDS, borderWidth: 0 },
        zonePoor:  { type: 'box', yMin: t.needs,    yMax: 999999,  backgroundColor: POOR,  borderWidth: 0 },
      };
    }
  })()
}
```

### Step 3: Change the chart's main line color to neutral
Currently the line color is set per-chart by the caller (`'#1E2761'` score, `'#922525'` LCP, etc.). With zones doing the color work, the line itself should be a single neutral color across all charts so the eye reads zones for status and line for trend. Use `#1F2937` (slate-800 — dark, neutral, high contrast on all three zones). Update the four `baseConfig(...)` calls (lines 1174-1180) to pass `'#1F2937'` as the color argument, replacing the four current per-chart colors.

### Step 4: Drop the old green/red `lineColor` variable
`lineColor` at line 1118 is no longer used. Remove it.

### Step 5: Ensure y-axis includes the relevant zone (so all bands are visible)
For each chart, set `scales.y.suggestedMin` and `scales.y.suggestedMax`:
- score: `suggestedMin: 0, suggestedMax: 100`
- lcp:   `suggestedMin: 0, suggestedMax: 6` (covers Poor zone with headroom)
- tbt:   `suggestedMin: 0, suggestedMax: 800`
- cls:   `suggestedMin: 0, suggestedMax: 0.35`
The simplest way: pass an optional `yRange` arg into baseConfig per chart, and apply via `scales.y.suggestedMin/suggestedMax`. Or stuff the range into `CHART_TARGETS` (`yMax` key). Whichever is cleanest in the existing code shape.

### Step 6: Render verification
Run `python3 scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py --no-psi --no-crux --render-only` and confirm:
- Script exits 0
- `dashboard/index.html` updates
- `git diff` shows only the chart-rendering changes (no snapshot data changes)

Then open `dashboard/index.html` in a browser, navigate to the "Trend over time — PSI Lab mobile" section, and visually verify each of the 4 charts shows the 3 bands (or 2 for score) in green/amber/red, with the chart line clearly visible passing through them.

## Hard rules
- Do NOT change the snapshot data structure or PSI/CrUX query logic
- Do NOT change the c0w noise-band datasets (min/max band fill) — those are working and orthogonal to this change
- Do NOT change tooltip/legend behavior
- Do NOT change the range filter buttons (7/14/30 days)
- Single-file change to `scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py` only
- Do NOT commit — coordinator owns merge gate
acceptance_criteria: 1. scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py is the only changed file. 2. Each of the 4 trend charts shows colored zone bands instead of a single threshold line: 3 bands (good/needs/poor) for lcp/tbt/cls, 2 bands (good/needs+poor) for score. 3. Zone colors: green=good, amber=needs improvement, red=poor — consistent across all charts. 4. The chart line itself is a single neutral color across all 4 charts. 5. python3 scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py --no-psi --no-crux --render-only exits 0. 6. dashboard/index.html opens in browser and visually verifies zones render correctly.
notes:
Implemented 5-step zone-band replacement in scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:
- CHART_TARGETS extended with good/needs/bestCase properties
- lineColor variable removed
- targetLine annotation replaced with 3 box annotations (zoneGood/zoneNeeds/zonePoor) using IIFE with 12% opacity
- All 4 baseConfig call-site colors changed to neutral #1F2937
- scales.y now includes suggestedMin/suggestedMax per chartKey
- Render verification passed: python3 --render-only exits 0, dashboard/index.html updated (12 snapshots, 75,637 bytes)
- QA reviewer approved with no blocking issues
- Browser visual verification (AC #6) remains — needs human to open dashboard/index.html
## MERGE_GATE_LIVE_PASS (2026-05-12)
Visual verification on local Chrome render (file: dashboard/index.html via localhost:8765):
- Score chart: green band 90-100, amber 50-90, red 0-50 ✓
- LCP: green ≤2.5s, amber 2.5-4s, red >4s ✓
- TBT: green ≤200ms, amber 200-600ms, red >600ms ✓
- CLS: green ≤0.1, amber 0.1-0.25, red >0.25 ✓
All 4 charts use neutral #1F2937 line. Chart line passes through bands clearly.
GHA pages.yml will auto-deploy this dashboard render.

title: Gate VQB JS to /apps/quiz via document.createElement guard
description:
Guard VQB (cdn.visualquizbuilder.com, 447KB) from loading on non-quiz pages via a small inline script injected before {{ content_for_header }} in layout/theme.liquid. Live investigation (2026-05-12) confirmed VQB is dynamically injected at runtime by the SATCB script manager (satcb.azureedge.net) at head script position 91 — well after content_for_header. document.createElement override placed before content_for_header (line 683) intercepts it. VQB is NOT in the HTML stream and NOT in content_for_header itself. No window.VQB namespace — safe to suppress on non-quiz routes.

VQB script URLs confirmed:
- https://cdn.visualquizbuilder.com/quiz/production/vqb.2.9.1892.js (async+defer)
- https://cdn.visualquizbuilder.com/quiz/production/lib.hammer.js (async)

Quiz route allowlist (to ALLOW VQB through):
- /apps/quiz (and subpaths)
- /pages/ paths containing "quiz" (fallback)
acceptance_criteria: 1. PSI mobile homepage network_by_origin: cdn.visualquizbuilder.com ABSENT. 2. https://www.hairmnl.com/apps/quiz loads and quiz functions correctly. 3. No JS console errors on homepage or quiz page related to guard. 4. git diff layout/theme.liquid shows only the guard block added before content_for_header and removal of dns-prefetch for cdn.visualquizbuilder.com. 5. Dashboard PSI snapshot after deploy shows ~447KB reduction in mobile transfer for www.hairmnl.com origin cluster.

title: Dashboard: bump GHA --psi-runs from 2 to 3
description:
The perf-dashboard GHA workflow passes --psi-runs 2 to build-perf-dashboard.py. The script's run_psi_median() runs PSI n times and returns the median. With n=2, median([a,b]) = (a+b)/2 (an average, not a true median). With n=3, the middle value is a proper median that rejects a single outlier run. The script default is already 3; the workflow override of 2 is below the useful minimum.

One-line fix in .github/workflows/perf-dashboard.yml.

title: Dashboard: show daily PSI median + noise band instead of latest-run value
description:
The PSI lab trend charts (Score, LCP, TBT, CLS) show the LATEST PSI run of each day. When one bad late-night Lighthouse run fires (e.g. network jitter, cold CDN cache, one slow third-party), it replaces the earlier good values and the chart looks like a regression even though real-user CrUX is flat.

Root cause confirmed 2026-05-12: May 11 had a 23:51Z run with LCP=32s/CLS=0.65/score=11, immediately followed at 00:02Z by score=32/LCP=8s/CLS=0.02 — same page, same code, 10 min apart. The 23:51Z run became the day's data point because _merge_daily_snapshot keeps the latest PSI timestamp.

Fix: accumulate all PSI runs per day, expose median + min/max so the chart shows a noise band rather than a noisy line.

title: Phase 2 CLS: LimeSpot card image dimension reservation
description:
Parent: hairmnl-theme-987 (desktop CLS p75 0.20, 44% good)

odv investigation: LimeSpot (Personalizer.io) injects LP_* / LimeSpot* grid cards after deferred SDK loads. Outer slot containment is already in place (limespot { display:block; contain:layout } in css-overrides.liquid). The residual CLS comes from internal image reflow — injected product images lack explicit width/height attributes, so the browser doesn't know their aspect ratio before they load.

Fix: target the product images inside LimeSpot-injected containers and reserve their aspect ratio via CSS.

### Files to modify
- assets/custom-theme.css (append only)

### What to add
```css
/* ── CLS fix 2026-05-12 hairmnl-theme-BDID (987 Phase 2 — LimeSpot card image dims) ── */
[class*='LP_GridContainer'] img,
[class*='LP_Inline'] img,
[class*='LP_RecommendationsBlock'] img,
[data-personalizer-fragment] img {
  aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;
  width: 100%;
  height: auto;
  object-fit: cover;
}
```

These selectors target only LimeSpot-injected card images (runtime DOM). Standard pipeline product images have their own aspect-ratio rules and are not matched by these selectors.

Note: aspect-ratio 1/1 matches HairMNL product image format (square shots). If LimeSpot renders non-square images, adjust to 3/4 or 4/3.

### Hard constraints
- These selectors are inherently LimeSpot-scoped (LP_* classes are unique to LimeSpot SDK). No body.template-* prefix needed, but do NOT remove it if adding one.
- NO contain/transform/filter/will-change on these selectors (kt0 rule — LimeSpot carousel slides may have fixed/absolute overlays).
- CSS only. No JS.
- Only stage assets/custom-theme.css.
- No shopify push, no git push, no bd close.

title: Phase 2 CLS: BOGOS free-gift cart container min-height
description:
Parent: hairmnl-theme-987 (desktop CLS p75 0.20, 44% good)

odv investigation identified #freegifts-main-page-container as a late-fill CLS source on the cart template. The Bold Bundles / BOGOS app populates this div via JS after DOMContentLoaded, causing the cart item list to shift down.

Selector: #freegifts-main-page-container (inside sections/cart.liquid ~35, inside #MainContent on cart template)

Fix: reserve the approximate height of the free gift strip before JS populates it.

### Files to modify
- assets/custom-theme.css (append only)

### What to add
```css
/* ── CLS fix 2026-05-12 hairmnl-theme-BDID (987 Phase 2 — BOGOS cart container) ── */
body.template-cart #freegifts-main-page-container {
  min-height: 80px;
}
```
80px ≈ one-row gift strip height. If the container is empty (no active BOGOS offer), min-height has zero visual impact.

Note: css-overrides.liquid documents separate BOGOS selectors for popup/notification containers (3uw/c8k). This fix targets only the inline page container, not the popup.

### Hard constraints
- body.template-cart scope mandatory. No unscoped selectors.
- CSS only. No JS.
- NO contain/transform/filter/will-change (kt0 rule).
- Only stage assets/custom-theme.css.
- No shopify push, no git push, no bd close.

title: 987 Phase 2 article-cards: redo with template-body scope (Option B)
description:
First attempt (commit 3b9cf26, reverted 2026-05-12 in commit 279b2c9) broke /blogs/hair-education and any page using .homepage-blog class because the new CSS (display:flex on .grid--uniform + float:none on .grid__item) collided with Pipeline 6's float-based width-class grid (.one-third / .medium-up--one-third etc.). Flex items lost their width and stacked vertically full-width instead of laying out 3-up.

Root cause learned: Pipeline 6 reuses .homepage-blog beyond the literal homepage (blog index templates share the section). Scoping by class wasn't enough — needed to also preserve the width-class math.

Three viable re-approaches:

Option A — flex-basis mirroring float widths: Add flex-basis calc(100%/3) (and breakpoint variants) to grid items so flex layout matches the float layout's width semantics.

Option B — template-body-scope: Use selector body.template-index .homepage-blog .grid.grid--uniform so the rule only fires on the literal homepage. Doesn't affect blog index templates.

Option C — keep JS, accept the small cost: .homepage-blog is below-fold on homepage (IntersectionObserver already gates the JS). The actual CLS contribution may be small in practice — 78 poor events / 7d is modest vs #MainContent (160) and cart template (40). Acceptable to skip article-cards and prioritize higher-impact contributors.

Reference: bd 987 notes contain full Phase 1 audit + regression root cause. Reference commits: 3b9cf26 (broken), 279b2c9 (revert).
design:
Recommended path: Option B (template-body-scope).

Reasoning:
- Cleanest scope: only fires where the homepage actually uses the section
- Doesn't have to math-match float widths (Pipeline 6 grid breakpoints are non-trivial)
- Blog index templates continue working as-is

Concrete change:
1. Restore the same JS removal (delete the IIFE at the original location in custom-theme.js).
2. Add CSS to custom-theme.css scoped by body.template-index:
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .grid.grid--uniform: display flex, flex-wrap wrap
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .grid__item: display flex, flex-direction column, float none
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .article__card.section--details: display flex, flex-direction column, flex 1 1 auto
   - body.template-index .homepage-blog .article__card.section--details .title: flex 1 1 auto

Verification:
- Homepage on draft: article cards 3-up grid, equal-height titles
- /blogs/hair-education on draft: 3-up grid unchanged
- Smoke test 4 PASS
- No kt0-class CSS properties
acceptance_criteria:
1. Article-cards JS height calc removed from custom-theme.js (same lines as 3b9cf26 deleted)
2. CSS added to custom-theme.css with body.template-index scope
3. Draft preview verification: homepage shows 3-up grid with equal-height titles; /blogs/hair-education unchanged
4. Smoke test 4 PASS / 0 FAIL on draft
5. No kt0-class CSS properties on any selector
6. Coordinator pushes to live theme + commits + bd append MERGE_GATE_PASS

title: Investigate + fix desktop CLS p75 stuck at 0.20 (44% good)
description:
Desktop CLS p75 is 0.20 in CrUX 28-day data (window ending 2026-05-09). Distribution: 44.0% good / 46.1% needs-improvement / 9.9% poor. The Apr 29–May 2 CLS sprint took mobile CLS p75 from 0.30 → 0.06, but targeted mobile-specific elements only (cart freegifts, Reamaze launcher position, Klaviyo popup positioning). Desktop never got the same treatment — its 0.20 is the worst-performing field metric on the site.

Likely desktop-specific contributors (NOT covered by the mobile sprint):
- Hover-state layout changes on mega-menu / category nav
- Wider image slots loading without reserved aspect-ratio
- Sidebar widgets (LoyaltyLion launcher, Reamaze, Klaviyo embedded forms) appearing late
- LimeSpot recommendation strips inserting after hero on PDP/homepage
- Vertex AI rec rails (if any wired)
- Header search popdown or dropdown opening without containment
- Sticky elements (sticky add-to-cart, sticky header) recomputing on scroll/resize

Tooling already in place:
- web-vitals-reporter.liquid pushes debug_target on every CLS event (page element selector of the shifted node)
- GA4 custom dimension debug_target is registered + populated
- scripts/web-vitals-report.py exists with --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop options

design:
Phase 1 — DATA (RO, no code changes):
1. Run `GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --days 7 --top 30 --device desktop` (add --device filter if not supported, extend the script).
2. Compare desktop top contributors vs mobile baseline (memory 'ga4-cls-poor-events-7d-baseline-as-of'). Surface the desktop-specific debug_targets that don't appear in mobile or have disproportionately higher desktop counts.
3. For each top-3 desktop contributor: identify the structural cause by inspecting the element in the live theme (Liquid file + CSS rules + JS that touches it). Categorize: late-load injection / unreserved image space / hover-state reflow / sidebar widget / sticky-element recalc / other.

Phase 2 — FIX (RW, one fix per dispatch — per Free-tier scope discipline, even though we're paid tier, this keeps the kt0-killer auditable):
For each top contributor, the standard fix patterns are:
- **Late-load widget injecting into flow** → reserve space with min-height or aspect-ratio at the parent (NOT contain: layout on a parent of fixed descendants — see kt0 lesson in .opencode_hints).
- **Image without dimensions** → add width/height attributes + aspect-ratio CSS.
- **Hover-state reflow** → use transform: translate (no layout) instead of margin/padding.
- **Sidebar widget appearing late** → reserve placeholder space sized to the widget's known dimensions.
- **Sticky-element recompute** → use position: sticky correctly (no JS-driven top/bottom changes).

Phase 3 — VERIFY:
1. Run `python3 scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py --config=scripts/smoke-test-drawers.config.json --theme=draft` — must still PASS (no cart drawer regression).
2. Run `python3 scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py --no-psi --no-crux --render-only` — exit 0.
3. Push to draft theme (140785582179) only — coordinator (CC) reviews diff + pushes to live (131664707683).
4. Acceptance verified at +28d when CrUX rolling window catches up.

Hard rules:
- Do NOT modify mobile-targeted fixes (the May 2 work is correct as-is).
- Do NOT add contain: layout to any parent of a fixed/absolute descendant (see kt0 lesson + .opencode_hints 'CSS containment + position gotchas').
- Do NOT push to live theme (131664707683) — coordinator owns live push.
- Do NOT close this bd — coordinator closes after +28d CrUX verification.
- Phantom-completion guard: paste `git show HEAD --stat` into bd notes before reporting done.

3. Recommendation written for which top-3 to fix first in Phase 2 dispatches.

Phase 2 (per-contributor fix) acceptance — separate dispatches:
1. One commit per contributor (≤2 prod files + ≤120 LoC each).
2. Smoke test PASSes post-fix.
3. Draft theme pushed; coordinator reviews diff for kt0-class regression (no contain: layout / transform / filter on parents of fixed descendants).

Phase 2 article-cards fix REVERTED 2026-05-12 — visual regression on /blogs/hair-education and any blog index using .homepage-blog class.

Root cause: the CSS scoping (.homepage-blog .grid.grid--uniform { display: flex }) was correct, but the existing Pipeline 6 grid relies on width-class floats (.one-third / .medium-up--one-third etc.) on .grid__item children. When the parent flips to display:flex and items lose 'float: none', the width classes (which are floats with percentages) don't translate to flex-basis — items default to content-size, can't fit a row, wrap each to its own line → vertical stack with full-width images. Screenshots from user showed /blogs/hair-education with single-column stacked articles instead of 3-up grid.

Pipeline 6 reuses .homepage-blog beyond the homepage (blog index pages share the section/template). Scoping by class wasn't sufficient — the fix must either:
1. Use .homepage-blog selector AND preserve the width-class behavior (add flex-basis: calc(100%/3) etc. mirroring the float widths), OR
2. Use a more specific selector that only matches the actual homepage usage (e.g. body.template-index .homepage-blog), OR
3. Stay with the JS approach but defer it more aggressively (IntersectionObserver already gates it; the layout cost is small if homepage-blog is below the fold).

The reverted commit's intent was correct (eliminate forced layout reads); the implementation under-specified the grid math. Re-dispatch should be scoped to a SINGLE template/page where the .homepage-blog class is used as intended, with manual visual verification on every page that includes the section before push.

title: Fix missing metric_rating param on 50-60% of CWV RUM events
description:
Dashboard's 'unknown' rating bucket shows ~50-60% of CWV events arriving without metric_rating populated. This is separate from the template param fix (vux, commit 64a0a6b) which is now working. The defensive 'unknown' bucket surfacing shipped with vux made the gap visible.

Symptoms:
- web-vitals-reporter.liquid pushes metric_rating to dataLayer for each CWV event (good/needs-improvement/poor based on web-vitals.js thresholds)
- Dashboard query reads metric_rating from GA4 event params
- GA4 shows 50-60% of CWV events with metric_rating = (not set)

Likely cause (analogous to template fix in vux): GTM CWV tag (or per-metric tags) may not be mapping a 'metric_rating' event parameter from dataLayer → GA4 event params. The template fix wired one DLV; metric_rating needs the same treatment but for a different field.

Investigation needed:
1. Inspect GTM live container — find the GA4 CWV event tag(s) (one per metric: LCP, CLS, INP, FCP, TTFB, or one combined). Check event parameters table for 'metric_rating' mapping.
2. Verify web-vitals-reporter.liquid dataLayer push includes metric_rating field name matching what GTM expects.
3. Check GA4 admin — metric_rating must be registered as a custom dimension (event-scoped).
4. Reference vux fix (commit 64a0a6b) + template handoff pattern in docs/gtm-js-error-handoff-2026-05-06.md for the workspace/version/publish pattern.

Out of scope: theme code changes (reporter is correct per vux). Auth/security. Schema migrations.
design:
Fix is GTM-only (parameter mapping in existing CWV tag(s)) + possibly GA4 admin custom dimension registration. Pattern identical to vux template fix. Workflow:
1. Audit live GTM container for CWV tags (scripts/gtm-audit.py)
2. Create fresh workspace from live (NOT workspace 197 — stale 2019 changes)
3. Add metric_rating event parameter mapping in CWV tag(s)
4. Create Container Version (do NOT publish; coordinator reviews + publishes)
5. After publish + 24h, verify dashboard 'unknown' bucket drops to <5%

Hard rules: do NOT touch workspace 197 or 190; do NOT modify theme code; do NOT publish without coordinator review.

title: Automated drawer smoke test (Layer 3 — Playwright-based kt0-killer gate)
description: Today's kt0 cart-drawer regression (commit ff1ef80) was a structural bug pattern that's destined to recur: a parent selector gets a CSS property that silently creates a containing block (contain:layout, transform, filter, etc.), and a position:fixed descendant gets trapped inside the parent's bounds instead of overlaying the viewport. Layer 1+2 (already shipped in commit d0334b0) added author-time guardrails and a manual pre-push smoke checklist to .opencode_hints. This bd ships Layer 3 — a deterministic automated test that catches kt0-class regressions BEFORE live push, converting the manual checklist into a CI-grade gate.
design:
## Design decisions LOCKED (do not re-litigate)

1. **Browser automation tool: Playwright (Python).**
   - Production-grade, headless, fast (~2-5s/test).
   - Other options ruled out: Chrome MCP (session-coupled, wrong for CI gate), cursor-agent (wrong tool category).
   - Setup: `pip install playwright && playwright install chromium`. Document in script's docstring + .opencode_hints.

2. **Auth: `?preview_theme_id=<id>` query param.**
   - No Shopify Storefront API token needed.
   - Default test target: draft theme `140785582179`.
   - Allow `--theme=live` for live-theme verification too (uses `131664707683`).

3. **Assertion set per overlay (the kt0-killer set).**
   For each overlay test:
   a. Trigger click opens the overlay within 2s timeout
   b. Panel's `position` computed style is `fixed` (or `absolute` with `<body>` as containing block)
   c. Panel `getBoundingClientRect()`: height >= 0.9 * viewport.height AND width >= 300px
   d. **Containment audit**: walk panel's parent chain to `<body>`. Test FAILS if ANY ancestor has:
      - `contain: layout | paint | strict | content`
      - `transform != 'none'`
      - `filter != 'none'`
      - `backdrop-filter != 'none'`
      - `perspective != 'none'`
      - `will-change` containing `transform` or `filter`
      This is the kt0-killer assertion — it catches the structural bug pattern.
   e. On fail: capture screenshot to `/tmp/smoke-fail-<test>-<ts>.png` and log the offending ancestor selector chain.

## Overlay tests to implement

| # | Test | Page | Trigger | Panel selector | State indicator |
|---|------|------|---------|----------------|-----------------|
| 1 | Cart drawer | `/products/davines-naturaltech-purifying-shampoo-for-scalp-with-oily-or-dry-dandruff` | `[data-drawer-toggle="drawer-cart"]` | `.cart__drawer .drawer__content` | `.drawer--visible` class on `.cart__drawer` |
| 2 | Mobile nav drawer | `/` (viewport: 375×667, mobile) | `[data-drawer-toggle="drawer-nav-mobile"]` or similar — INSPECT FIRST via Chrome MCP or grep sections/header.liquid for the trigger; document the exact selector found | `.nav__drawer .drawer__content` | `.drawer--visible` |
| 3 | PhotoSwipe lightbox | same PDP as #1 | `[data-zoom-button]` (first one) | `.pswp` (the photoswipe root) | `.pswp--open` class |
| 4 | Search overlay | `/` | `[data-search-toggle]` or `.header__search-toggle` — INSPECT FIRST and document | `.search__form-wrapper` or `.search__overlay` | aria-expanded / visible class — INSPECT FIRST |

For tests #2 and #4, the senior-developer must INSPECT the live theme HTML (via `curl` + grep, or via the rendered HTML in `/tmp/cart.html` from earlier session work) to identify the exact trigger + panel + state-class selectors. Do NOT hardcode selectors that may not exist. If a selector cannot be confirmed for a given test, log a skip + warning rather than failing — the goal is high signal, not false positives.

## Script architecture

**File:** `scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py` (~200 LoC including docstring)

**CLI:**
```
python3 scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py [--theme=draft|live] [--headless=true|false] [--store=creations-gdc] [--verbose]
```

**Defaults:**
- `--theme=draft` (so the coordinator runs it on draft preview BEFORE live push)
- `--headless=true`
- `--store=creations-gdc` (matches existing shopify CLI usage in repo)
- `--verbose=false` (only print failures + summary)

**Theme ID map (constant in script):**
```python
THEME_IDS = {
    "draft": 140785582179,
    "live":  131664707683,
}
STORE_URL = "https://www.hairmnl.com"
```

**Exit codes:**
- 0 — all overlay tests passed
- 1 — at least one test failed (CI gate: this blocks the push)
- 2 — script error (Playwright crash, network error, etc.) — distinguishable from real test fail

**Output format (verbose):**
```
[1/4] Cart drawer on PDP ... PASS (210ms)
[2/4] Mobile nav drawer on Home ... SKIP (trigger selector not found)
[3/4] PhotoSwipe lightbox on PDP ... PASS (340ms)
[4/4] Search overlay on Home ... PASS (180ms)

Summary: 3 PASS, 1 SKIP, 0 FAIL
```

**Output format on fail:**
```
[1/4] Cart drawer on PDP ... FAIL (250ms)
  Reason: Containment audit failed.
  Panel: .cart__drawer .drawer__content (position: fixed, but...)
  Offending ancestor: .header__wrapper has `contain: layout`.
  This creates a containing block for fixed descendants per CSS Containment spec,
  trapping the drawer inside .header__wrapper's bounds.
  See: bd memories contain (kt0 lesson, 2026-05-11).
  Screenshot: /tmp/smoke-fail-cart-drawer-20260511T143000Z.png
```

## Integration into push protocol — update .opencode_hints

After the existing "## Pre-push smoke checklist (CSS / layout changes)" section (shipped in d0334b0), insert a NEW section called "## Automated drawer smoke test (Layer 3)":

```
## Automated drawer smoke test (Layer 3)

The manual smoke checklist above is augmented by an automated Playwright
script that programmatically verifies fixed-position overlays open and
overlay correctly:

  python3 scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py --theme=draft

Run this BEFORE every `shopify theme push --allow-live` that touches:
  - snippets/css-overrides.liquid
  - assets/theme.css
  - snippets/css-variables.liquid
  - layout/theme.liquid
  - sections/header.liquid
  - any popup/modal wrapper snippet

Script exit codes:
  0 = all overlay tests passed → safe to push to live
  1 = at least one overlay broken → STOP, do not push to live
  2 = script setup error (Playwright missing, network down, etc.)
      → investigate before pushing

The script's key assertion (the kt0-killer) walks every fixed-position
overlay panel's parent chain and fails if any ancestor has a CSS property
that creates a containing block for fixed descendants (contain:layout,
transform, filter, perspective, will-change:transform, backdrop-filter).
This catches the exact bug pattern from the 2026-05-11 kt0 regression
(commit ff1ef80) at the structural level — before it can ship to live.

Setup (one-time per workstation):
  pip install playwright
  playwright install chromium

If Playwright is unavailable, fall back to the manual smoke checklist
above. The manual checklist remains the source-of-truth verification —
the script is an additional belt-and-suspenders layer.
```

## Files in scope

## Implementation notes for the senior-developer

- Use `playwright.sync_api.sync_playwright()` for simplicity (sync API is easier to read than async for this use case).
- Run with `headless=True` by default; `--headless=false` for visual debugging.
- Use `page.wait_for_selector(state_indicator, timeout=2000)` for the open-state check.
- For the containment audit, evaluate JavaScript in the page context:
  ```python
  result = page.evaluate("""
      (panelSelector) => {
          const panel = document.querySelector(panelSelector);
          if (!panel) return {ok: false, reason: 'panel not found'};
          let el = panel.parentElement;
          while (el && el !== document.body) {
              const cs = getComputedStyle(el);
              if (cs.contain && /\\b(layout|paint|strict|content)\\b/.test(cs.contain)) return {ok: false, reason: `contain: ${cs.contain}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.transform && cs.transform !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `transform: ${cs.transform}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.filter && cs.filter !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `filter: ${cs.filter}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.backdropFilter && cs.backdropFilter !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `backdrop-filter: ${cs.backdropFilter}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.perspective && cs.perspective !== 'none') return {ok: false, reason: `perspective: ${cs.perspective}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              if (cs.willChange && /transform|filter/.test(cs.willChange)) return {ok: false, reason: `will-change: ${cs.willChange}`, selector: getSelectorPath(el)};
              el = el.parentElement;
          }
          function getSelectorPath(node) {
              if (!node) return '';
              const parts = [];
              while (node && node !== document.body) {
                  let s = node.tagName.toLowerCase();
                  if (node.id) s += '#' + node.id;
                  if (node.className && typeof node.className === 'string') s += '.' + node.className.trim().replace(/\\s+/g, '.');
                  parts.unshift(s);
                  node = node.parentElement;
              }
              return parts.join(' > ');
          }
          return {ok: true};
      }
  """, panel_selector)
  ```
- Allow the script to be run in dev (Python 3.10+, no other deps).
- Include a `if __name__ == '__main__':` block + a clean main() function.
- Top-of-file docstring explaining the purpose, setup, usage, and the kt0 lesson.
