title: Codify CSS containment gotchas + pre-push smoke checklist (prevent kt0-class regressions)
description:
Today's kt0 CLS hotfix (ff1ef80) was caused by an author-time misunderstanding of how `contain: layout` interacts with fixed/absolute descendants. The CSS rule's own comment incorrectly claimed "contain:layout is no-visual-impact" — but the property actually establishes a containing block for fixed/absolute descendants per CSS Containment spec. That trapped the cart drawer's `position: fixed; z-index: 20050` inside `.header__wrapper`, causing a 24+ hour live regression where the drawer rendered inline instead of overlaying the viewport.

Bug surfaced via user report; would have been caught instantly by either:
  (a) An author-time guardrail listing the CSS properties that create containing blocks for fixed/absolute descendants, OR
  (b) A merge-gate smoke checklist requiring drawer-open verification on draft before live push.

This bd ships BOTH guardrails as documentation-only updates to .opencode_hints (the project-root file already read by all offload-dispatched agents), plus a `bd remember` entry so the lesson is searchable via `bd memories contain`.
design:
## Two additions to /Users/y9378348c/Projects/hairmnl-theme/.opencode_hints

Both additions go in NEW sections at the END of the file, preserving all existing content verbatim. Insert in this order:

### Addition 1 — new section "CSS containment + position gotchas"

Insert before the final "## Project context" section. Verbatim content to add:

```
## CSS containment + position gotchas

Adding any of the following CSS properties to a selector silently creates a
new containing block for `position: fixed` and `position: absolute`
descendants. If you apply one of these to a parent that contains a fixed
overlay (cart drawer, mobile nav, search modal, Klaviyo popup, LoyaltyLion
notification, PWA install banner, cookie consent), the descendant's
positioning resolves relative to that parent — NOT the viewport — and the
overlay renders inline within the parent's bounds instead of covering the
viewport.

This is the bug pattern that broke the cart drawer on 2026-05-11 (the kt0
fix's `contain: layout` on `.header__wrapper` trapped the cart-drawer's
fixed position inside the header wrapper's bounds; see commit ff1ef80 and
`bd memories contain` for the post-mortem).

| Property                          | Effect on fixed/absolute descendants                        |
|-----------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
| `contain: layout`                 | Containing block + new stacking context                     |
| `contain: paint`                  | Same as layout + clips overflow                             |
| `contain: strict` / `content`     | Includes both layout and paint                              |
| `transform: <anything>` (not `none`) | Containing block + new stacking context                  |
| `filter: <anything>`              | Same                                                        |
| `backdrop-filter: <anything>`     | Same                                                        |
| `perspective: <anything>`         | Same                                                        |
| `will-change: transform`          | Same (pre-emptive)                                          |
| `will-change: filter`             | Same                                                        |
| `overflow: hidden\|auto\|scroll`  | Doesn't create containing block, but clips fixed children   |
|                                   | visually if they exceed bounds (similar symptom)            |

**Required audit before adding any of these to a parent selector:**

```
# What position:fixed / position:absolute is inside the targeted selector?
grep -rn "position:\s*fixed\|position:\s*absolute" sections/ snippets/ \
  assets/theme.css assets/theme.dev.js | grep -i "<selector-scope>"
```

If the audit surfaces any fixed/absolute descendants, either:
- Move the descendant OUT of the selector's scope (DOM restructure), OR
- Narrow the selector to a sibling element that does NOT contain the descendant, OR
- Skip the containment fix (accept the CLS cost)

DO NOT trust comments in existing CSS that claim `contain: layout` is
"no visual impact" or "purely a hint". That phrasing was the source of
the kt0 regression — it's actively wrong.
```

### Addition 2 — new section "Pre-push smoke checklist (CSS / layout changes)"

Insert directly after the new "CSS containment + position gotchas" section.
Verbatim content to add:

```
## Pre-push smoke checklist (CSS / layout changes)

Before any `shopify theme push --allow-live` that touches:
  - `snippets/css-overrides.liquid`
  - `assets/theme.css`
  - `snippets/css-variables.liquid`
  - `layout/theme.liquid`
  - `sections/header.liquid`
  - any snippet that establishes a stacking context (Klaviyo, LoyaltyLion,
    Reamaze, PWA-install, Booster cookie bar wrappers)

… visit the DRAFT preview URL `?preview_theme_id=140785582179` and verify
these interactions render correctly. Each should overlay the viewport
fully — drawers slide in as full-height panels, modals render center-screen
with a backdrop dim:

1. Cart drawer — click the cart icon on a PDP. Drawer slides in from
   right, ~50vw wide, full viewport height, white panel with backdrop
   dimming the rest of the page.
2. Mobile nav drawer — on a mobile viewport, click the hamburger. Drawer
   slides in from left, same behavior.
3. Product gallery zoom (PhotoSwipe) — tap a zoom button on a PDP. The
   PhotoSwipe lightbox should overlay the viewport (black/dark background,
   image centered).
4. Search overlay — click the search icon. The search panel should
   overlay the viewport.
5. Klaviyo popup (if enabled) — trigger via test event or wait for
   timer-based fire. Popup centers on screen with backdrop dim.
6. LoyaltyLion launcher / notification — visit any page logged in as a
   customer with rewards points. The launcher should anchor bottom-right
   without affecting in-flow layout.

If ANY of these render inline / below the page / clipped / behind other
elements, STOP. The most likely cause is a parent selector with one of
the properties listed in the "CSS containment + position gotchas"
section above — audit before pushing to live.

Skip this checklist only for diffs that:
- Touch only image-attribute CSS (`.foo img { aspect-ratio: ... }`,
  `.bar { object-fit: cover }`)
- Touch only color / typography tokens
- Are pure comment / documentation changes

When in doubt, run the checklist anyway. 30 seconds of clicking is cheap
vs. a live regression.
```

## Bd memory entry

After the .opencode_hints file is updated, run this single shell command verbatim:

```
bd remember "CSS containment gotcha (kt0 cart-drawer regression 2026-05-11, commits 4e6ecc2 / ff1ef80): contain:layout on .header__wrapper trapped position:fixed cart drawer inside header bounds. Per CSS Containment spec, contain:layout (and contain:paint, transform, filter, perspective, will-change:transform) establishes a containing block for fixed/absolute descendants. ALWAYS audit descendants before adding these to a parent selector. The kt0 author comment claiming 'contain:layout is no-visual-impact' was actively wrong and propagated the misunderstanding. See .opencode_hints 'CSS containment + position gotchas' section + 'Pre-push smoke checklist' for the prevention playbook."
```

## Files in scope

- `/Users/y9378348c/Projects/hairmnl-theme/.opencode_hints` (additions only)

No other file edits. NO `git push`. NO `shopify theme push`. NO `bd close` — coordinator owns those per .opencode_hints rules.

title: Extend image-dim injection to /pages/ + investigate collection/blog LCP gaps (~463 URLs)
description:
GSC mobile CWV (5/10) flags 4 URL groups with LCP > 2.5s; the same 4 URL groups also appear in CLS-failing list:

| URL group | URLs | LCP | CLS |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| /collections/products-for-rebonded-hair | 155 | 2.6s | 0.16 |
| /blogs/salon-services/best-hair-color-for-morenas | 152 | 2.8s | 0.22 |
| /blogs/hair-education/purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines | 136 | 2.9s | (not flagged) |
| /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu | 20 | 3.0s | 0.21 |
| /collections/kerastase-heat-protectors | 20 | (not flagged) | 0.12 |

The blog posts (152 + 136 URLs) are already covered by article-content-with-img-dims.liquid + the 7tq LCP-priority preload. If they're STILL at 2.8-2.9s, the residual gap is either the hero image itself or a specific markup pattern that doesn't match the snippet's parser.

The /pages/ page (azta-urban-salon-service-menu) is at /pages/, NOT /blogs/ — its `page.content` doesn't go through article-content-with-img-dims (which is rendered only from sections/article.liquid). Same dimension-injection treatment likely needed for sections/page.liquid.

Collection pages (products-for-rebonded-hair, kerastase-heat-protectors) need separate investigation — their LCP element is typically the first product card image; possible causes: hero banner, deep grid, slow filter logic.
design:
## Phase A — extend dimension injection to /pages/ (Shopify pages)

The existing snippet at snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid is parameterised on `content` — re-use it for page.content too.

1. Inspect sections/page.liquid (or wherever page.content is rendered).
2. Replace `{{ page.content }}` with `{% render 'article-content-with-img-dims', content: page.content %}`.
3. Repeat for any other page-template variants.

Validation: visit /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu, view-source, confirm body images now have width/height attrs from URL pattern.

## Phase B — collection page LCP investigation (read-only)

For products-for-rebonded-hair and kerastase-heat-protectors:
1. PSI run + Lighthouse trace, identify the LCP element.
2. Check if hero image / first product card image is preloaded.
3. Audit srcset breakpoint coverage.
4. File specific fix bd issue per finding.

## Phase C — blog post residual LCP

For best-hair-color-for-morenas + purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines:
1. Confirm article-content-with-img-dims is firing (view-source check).
2. PSI trace — what's the LCP element? If it's the hero, check the article-hero-with-fetchpriority pattern from issue 9hw.
3. If hero is fine, look for something blocking render: heavy script, large CSS stylesheet, slow font.

Files (Phase A scope only):
- sections/page.liquid (add render call)
- possibly templates/page.*.json if there are multiple page templates

Phase B + C are separate read-only investigation tasks (can be batched into one bd later).

## Phase B

## Phase C
- bd issue filed for blog posts identifying remaining LCP gap (or "no further fix available without app removal" if root cause is third-party).

No forloop.first check, no loading_priority param, no fetchpriority attribute. **Contrast with product-grid-item.liquid:** Lines 166-193 implement a 3-tier loading strategy: - forloop.first -> loading=eager + fetchpriority=high - forloop.index 2-4 -> loading=eager (no fetchpriority) - forloop.index 5+ -> loading=lazy (default) product-grid-item.liquid also has WebP picture pattern with conditional width checks. product-grid-item-branded.liquid already has WebP (lines 151-158) — that's fine. **Feasibility of porting the pattern:** HIGH — straightforward. product-grid-item-branded.liquid uses {% include %} (not {% render %}), so variables set in the calling section's forloop scope (like forloop.first) ARE accessible inside the snippet. No need to pass a parameter. The change is: 1. Wrap the img tag loading attribute (line 169) in a conditional pattern: - forloop.first -> loading=eager + fetchpriority=high - forloop.index <= 4 -> loading=eager - else -> loading=lazy 2. The calling sections use {% include 'product-grid-item-branded' %} which shares parent scope, so forloop.first is available. 3. BUT: The branded sections have MULTIPLE for-loops (e.g., collection-branded.liquid has loops for best_sellers, new_arrivals, each with their own forloop). The forloop.first in a best_sellers swiper-slide loop means the first product in that carousel, not the first product on the page. 4. Key insight: In collection-branded.liquid, the BEST SELLERS carousel (>6 products) uses product-grid-item (NOT branded) inside swiper-slides (line 566). The <=6 products path uses product-grid-item-branded (line 598). For the <=6 path, the forloop is iterating over best_selling_products, so forloop.first would eager-load the first best-seller — a reasonable LCP candidate. 5. For the other branded sections (collection-lorealprofessionnel, etc.), they all call {% include 'product-grid-item-branded' %} inside their main product forloop. forloop.first will correctly identify the first product card. **Complication:** The branded sections also call product-grid-item-branded inside best_sellers and new_arrivals blocks where there is a SEPARATE forloop. The first item in those loops would ALSO get eager loading. This is acceptable — carousel first items are likely above-the-fold too. #### Template matching for target URLs: - **/collections/products-for-rebonded-hair**: No dedicated template file exists (no templates/collection.products-for-rebonded-hair.liquid or .json, and no cat.rebonded-hair.liquid). This collection uses the **default collection.json** template -> section 'collection' -> section collection.liquid -> **product-grid-item.liquid** (the REGULAR grid). **ALREADY HAS the eager/loading_priority fix (lines 95-101).** LCP issue on this page is NOT from the branded snippet; it uses the already-fixed default collection template. - **/collections/kerastase-heat-protectors**: No dedicated template (no matching .liquid or .json). Also uses **default collection.json** -> collection.liquid -> product-grid-item.liquid. **ALREADY HAS the fix.** - **IMPORTANT ADDENDUM:** CLS 0.12 on kerastase-heat-protectors could come from OTHER sources (hero image, layout shifts from fonts/ads). The product grid itself already has loading_priority. **BUT:** Branded collection pages (e.g., /collections/kerastase-symbiose, /collections/loreal-professionnel, /collections/blond-absolu) DO use the branded sections and ARE affected by the all-lazy bug. This fix would benefit ALL ~25+ branded collection landing pages. #### Recommended next bd issue: - **Title:** Port forloop-based eager loading from product-grid-item to product-grid-item-branded - **Files to change:** snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid (1 file, ~15 lines changed) - **Specific change:** Replace line 169 loading="lazy" with a conditional for forloop.first/forloop.index pattern. Add fetchpriority="high" for forloop.first. Keep WebP picture as-is. - **Estimated LoC:** ~10-15 lines in 1 file - **Risk:** LOW — forloop scope is accessible via {% include %}. All 25+ branded sections benefit. --- ### Phase C: Blog Residual LCP Code Audit #### Hero fade-in CSS location + properties: 1. **`.fade-in-child .background-size-cover`** in assets/theme.css lines 6858-6861: - Sets opacity:0 by default, opacity:1 on .lazyloaded - This targets elements with class .background-size-cover INSIDE .fade-in-child - The hero snippet (snippets/hero.liquid line 19) adds fade-in-child to the outer .image__hero__frame div - HOWEVER, hero.liquid's img does NOT have .background-size-cover class (removed in 2026-04-26 hotfix, line 49-55 comment). **So this CSS does NOT affect the hero image.** 2. **`.fade-in`** in assets/theme.css lines 12193-12196: - Sets opacity:0 with transition, opacity:1 on .lazyloaded - The hero img (hero.liquid line 82) has class="" — empty class. This CSS does NOT apply. 3. **`.fade-in-child` on img in image-fill.liquid** (line 114): The CSS rule targeting fade-in-child requires a .background-size-cover DESCENDANT, not the class on the img itself. **No opacity issue.** 4. **AOS hero override in custom-theme.css** (lines 2562-2565): ```css .aos-initialized [data-aos="hero"] { opacity: 1 !important; animation-name: none !important; } ``` This disables the AOS fade-in for hero text. **About hero text, not the hero image.** 5. **collection-hero.liquid** (line 21) uses fade-in-child on the wrapping div AND renders hero snippet with eager: true. The hero snippet renders img with loading="eager" and fetchpriority="high" — BUT the wrapping div has fade-in-child class. Since `.fade-in-child .background-size-cover` is opacity:0 and the img doesn't have .background-size-cover, **the image won't be affected by fade-in-child opacity rules.** 6. **collection-sub.liquid** (line 12) uses fade-in-child on each subcollection item div AND passes loading/fetchpriority to image-fill. image-fill renders ``. Again, no opacity impact. **VERDICT:** The `.fade-in-child` CSS is NOT the cause of article/collection hero LCP delays. The opacity:0 rule specifically targets `.fade-in-child .background-size-cover` (a descendant pattern), and the `background-size-cover` class was removed from hero images in the 2026-04-26 hotfix. The hero img elements render with loading="eager" + fetchpriority="high" and no opacity-delaying CSS classes. #### Whether removal for eager heroes would help: **No — the CSS is already inert for hero images.** The background-size-cover class was already removed from hero images in a previous hotfix. The fade-in-child class on the wrapper and on img elements via image-fill does NOT trigger opacity:0 because the CSS selectors require both .fade-in-child AND .background-size-cover on a descendant. **However, there IS a residual risk:** If any future code adds .background-size-cover to hero images (or if lazysizes JS adds .lazyloaded), images could flash to opacity:0 then fade in. A defensive fix would be to NOT apply .fade-in-child to wrapper divs when eager loading, but this is LOW priority since the current selectors are already inactive for these elements. #### content_for_header preconnect audit: From layout/theme.liquid lines 276-313: **DNS prefetches present (lines 307-310):** - link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//www.googletagmanager.com" - link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//edge.personalizer.io" - link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//static.klaviyo.com" - link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//tracking.aws.judge.me" **Preconnects:** Only `` (line 25) and font preloads (lines 30-37) are present. **No preconnects for Shopify CDN images, Google Analytics, or other third-party origins.** **Scripts before content_for_header (lines 182-183):** - vendor.js with defer - theme.js with defer - Inline scripts for theme config, viewport heights, A/B testing, loading shimmer logic **content_for_header placement:** End of head (line 313), before closing head tag. Correct position — all Shopify app scripts injected here. The defer on vendor.js/theme.js means they don't block parsing. **Potential improvements:** - Add preconnect (not just dns-prefetch) for //www.googletagmanager.com, //static.klaviyo.com, //edge.personalizer.io - Add preconnect for Shopify CDN image domain (cdn.shopify.com) for product images - The 4 dns-prefetches could be upgraded to preconnects for faster TCP+TLS - Missing preconnect for Google Analytics gtag endpoint #### Recommended next bd issue (if any): 1. **Add preconnects for critical domains** — upgrade the 4 dns-prefetches to preconnects, add preconnect for cdn.shopify.com. ~5 lines in layout/theme.liquid. LOW risk. 2. **The fade-in-child CSS is NOT causing hero LCP delays** — the background-size-cover class was already removed in the 2026-04-26 hotfix. No code change needed here. 3. **The real remaining blog LCP issues** are likely from: (a) content_for_header third-party script blocking (Shopify injects analytics + app scripts), (b) article body images without width/height (article-content-with-img-dims.liquid handles this), or (c) network latency to Shopify CDN. These need PSI field data to confirm. ### EXTERNAL API BLOCKER PSI/Lighthouse investigation flagged — requires human to run externally: ``` python3 scripts/run-psi.sh "https://www.hairmnl.com/collections/products-for-rebonded-hair" --device mobile python3 scripts/run-psi.sh "https://www.hairmnl.com/collections/kerastase-heat-protectors" --device mobile python3 scripts/run-psi.sh "https://www.hairmnl.com/blogs/salon-services/best-hair-color-for-morenas" --device mobile python3 scripts/run-psi.sh "https://www.hairmnl.com/blogs/hair-education/purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines" --device mobile ``` ## 2026-05-18 Closeout Investigation complete via OC HH-SWARM dispatch. Phase A (page-template image-dim): MOOT. OC audit found /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu LCP is NOT from missing image-dim attrs. Phase B (collection LCP): Real gap identified — snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid has all loading="lazy", affects ~25 brand collection pages. Filed as hairmnl-theme-987.1 (P2, parent detached — LCP not CLS). Phase C (article residual LCP): .fade-in-child CSS confirmed inert for hero images (background-size-cover already removed 2026-04-26). Real residual gap: dns-prefetch → preconnect upgrade + missing cdn.shopify.com preconnect. Filed as hairmnl-theme-t8vd (P3). External PSI runs on the 4 target URLs flagged in c2k notes — not blocking c2k close, can be done opportunistically after 987.1 + t8vd land.

title: BOGOS app: 2,721 'reduce of empty array' errors / 7d from apps-bundles-cluster.makebecool.com/js/app.js:277:3
description: JS error pipeline (post-l3g fix, 2026-05-06) surfaced this as the #4 most frequent error type by count. Real bug in BOGOS's code — they call .reduce() on what can be an empty array. Two-track: vendor support email + theme-side gate so BOGOS doesn't init on empty cart.
design:
## Goal
Address ~2,721 "reduce of empty array" errors / 7d coming from BOGOS app's bundle (apps-bundles-cluster.makebecool.com/js/app.js:277:3). Real production bug in their code triggered when cart has no items eligible for the offer (or cart is empty). Two tracks: vendor outreach + theme-side defer.

## What we know
- Error: `reduce of empty array with no initial value`
- Source: `https://apps-bundles-cluster.makebecool.com/js/app.js:277:3` (BOGOS CDN)
- Volume: 2,721 events / 7 days = ~390/day
- BOGOS-related issue 3uw (CLS) was already shipped 2026-05-04 — separate concern.

## Two-track action

### Track A — Vendor (admin)
1. Email BOGOS support (apps@makebecool.com or via Shopify app contact) with:
   - Error message + stack frame (app.js:277:3)
   - Reproduction: empty cart visiting any page that mounts BOGOS widget
   - Volume metric (~390/day, our shop)
   - Request: fix the .reduce() call to provide an initial value (`.reduce((acc, x) => ..., [])`).

### Track B — Theme-side defer (mechanical, OC-dispatchable)
Find the BOGOS include / SDK script tag in the theme. Add a guard so BOGOS doesn't initialize until cart has at least 1 item:
- Find: `grep -rn -iE "bogos|sca-gift|secomapp|conversion-bear" --include='*.liquid' .`
- Wrap the include/script in `{% if cart.item_count > 0 %}` (or equivalent JS-side guard).
- This avoids the empty-array reduce error AND saves bundle weight on empty-cart pages.

Risk: if BOGOS provides a banner / call-to-action on empty cart, gating breaks that. Audit before shipping.

## Files likely to modify
- snippets/bogos-* (or wherever the app embed lives)
- layout/theme.liquid (if include lives there)

## Out of scope
- Replacing BOGOS — there's a separate parked issue ur0 for custom BOGOS replacement (1 day, $420/yr saved). This is a defensive fix only.
acceptance_criteria:
After fix:
- BOGOS "reduce of empty array" error count drops from 2,721/7d → 0 (or near-zero)
- Tracked via dashboard JS errors section
- No regression on BOGOS widget functionality on cart with items
- Vendor support ticket filed (CC the response/ticket# in bd notes)
notes:
Dispatched to OC lead-architect via /offload — new SOP, free-tier opencode/minimax-m2.5-free. Track B (theme-side empty-cart guard) only — Track A vendor email is admin/user track. Brief explicitly forbids git commit/push (CC merge-gate).
MERGE_GATE_PASS at 2026-05-09T22:13:05Z — Live push verified via curl: 0 occurrences of apps-bundles-cluster.makebecool.com on /. Track B (theme-side guard) shipped. Track A (BOGOS vendor email) remains as user/admin task — not blocking close.

title: Legacy tab content unescape — render escaped HTML in product-tabs without merchant re-save
description: After 0ru shipped, tab content saved pre-fix renders escaped (legacy richtext-stored data). Adding a defensive heuristic-gated unescape pass to product-tabs.liquid avoids requiring the merchant to re-save every product manually.
design:
## Goal
Defensive Liquid filter to render legacy tab content that was HTML-escaped by the old `richtext` schema before issue 0ru shipped (commit c6cc2a0, 2026-05-06). Without this, every product whose tab content was authored before the fix shows literal `<ol>` / `<strong>` etc. as text — merchant must re-save each product manually.

## Context (read first, do not skip)
- Issue 0ru changed schema for raw_content_1..5 from "richtext" → "html" in sections/product.liquid + section-product.liquid.
- New edits work fine. Old data — Shopify stored it pre-escaped (`<ol><li>...`) when the schema was richtext.
- After the schema change, that escaped string passes through `{{ _content }}` and renders the literal characters (visible markup).
- Issue 0ru's resolution noted this as a follow-up: "Bug 3 caveat: existing escaped tab content stays escaped after schema change — merchant must re-save each affected product. If many products affected, follow-up with a bulk-unescape ticket."

## Target file
- snippets/product-tabs.liquid — single file. Two render paths inside: `tabs` style block (~line 51) and `accordions` style block (~line 102).

## What to do
Add a defensive unescape branch to the existing `_resolved_content` chain that the metafield-syntax detector lives in (already added by 0ru). The unescape should ONLY trigger when the content "looks escaped" — heuristic detection to avoid double-decoding legitimate entities.

Heuristic detection (conservative):
- Content contains `<` AND `>` AND at least one of `<ol`, `<ul`, `<li`, `<p`, `<div`, `<strong`, `<em`, `<br` (lowercased substring match).
- Content does NOT contain `<` followed by an alpha character (i.e. no real HTML tags already present — that would suggest mixed/double-encoded content, which we should NOT touch).

If heuristic matches, run a bounded unescape chain:
- `replace: '<', '<'`
- `replace: '>', '>'`
- `replace: '"', '"'`
- `replace: ''', "'"`
- `replace: '&', '&'` (LAST — must come after others so we don't double-decode)

Apply after the existing metafield-syntax detection logic — the metafield resolver returns rendered HTML which doesn't need unescape, so the unescape pass should run on `_resolved_content` only IF no metafield resolution happened.

Apply in BOTH `tabs` AND `accordions` paths (lines ~51-66 and ~102-119 — same structure, both need it).

## Invariants to preserve
- DO NOT modify the existing metafield-syntax resolver from 0ru.
- DO NOT touch sections/product.liquid or section-product.liquid (schema is correct now).
- DO NOT add unescape logic elsewhere in the codebase — bounded to product-tabs.liquid.
- The heuristic must NOT match content that already contains real HTML — that's a sign of double-encoding which we should NOT decode.

## How to verify
1. python3 -m py_compile is N/A (Liquid file).
2. Push to draft theme 140785582179 with `--only=snippets/product-tabs.liquid` (the coordinator will do this — you do NOT push).
3. Coordinator will smoke test on:
   - A product that has `<ol><li>` content authored pre-fix (will need to find one — likely the No.4 Fine Bond Maintenance Shampoo from the original screenshot).
   - A product that has metafield syntax (`{{ product.metafields...}}`) — must still resolve correctly.
   - A new product with native HTML — must render correctly with no double-decode.

## Failure modes to avoid
- Double-decoding: if a product had a literal `&` in its content for a real ampersand, our chain would convert that to `&` then still match the heuristic on the next pass... wait, the heuristic runs ONCE per render so this is fine.
- Over-eager match: if a product's content discusses HTML in plain text (e.g. "use the <strong> tag for bold"), the heuristic would unescape it. That's an acceptable false positive — the merchant can put real `<strong>` tags instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- Existing pre-0ru products with escaped tab content render as proper HTML (lists, bold, paragraphs, NOT literal `<ol>`).
- Products with metafield syntax (`{{ product.metafields.hmnl.* }}`) continue to resolve to actual metafield values.
- Products with native HTML (post-0ru, properly authored) render unchanged.
- No double-decoding regression — products with intentional HTML-encoded entities in body text render correctly.
- Diff scope: ONLY snippets/product-tabs.liquid modified. No JSON schema changes, no section file changes.
notes:
Dispatched to OC lead-architect via /offload — new SOP (sub-orchestrator pattern), free-tier opencode/minimax-m2.5-free
