title: VisualQuizBuilder app embed gating — 447KB on every mobile pageload
description: VQB (cdn.visualquizbuilder.com, ~447KB JS) loads on every mobile pageload despite being used only on quiz routes. ASSUMPTION (per user 2026-05-12): VQB admin offers no per-template gating — manual theme-side intervention required. Investigate JS interception pattern to block VQB scripts on non-quiz routes while preserving full functionality on /apps/quiz.
design:
**Goal:** Block VisualQuizBuilder (cdn.visualquizbuilder.com, ~447KB JS) from loading on non-quiz pages, given that VQB admin offers no per-template gating and we cannot edit the app embed itself.

**Approach (theme-side JS interception):** Inject a small guard script EARLY in `layout/theme.liquid` (before the app-embed slot renders) that:
- On non-quiz routes (`pathname` does NOT match `/apps/quiz` or `/pages/quiz*` or any other VQB-bearing route — to be discovered), prevents script tags pointing at `cdn.visualquizbuilder.com` (and any other VQB origin) from executing.
- Most robust patterns: (a) `MutationObserver` watching `head` + `body` for inserted `<script>` nodes and removing them before they fetch, OR (b) override `HTMLScriptElement.prototype.src` setter / `document.createElement` to filter. Pattern (a) is preferred because it doesn't break global JS contracts.
- Preserves full VQB functionality on quiz routes — must NOT touch the embed there.

**Investigation tasks for OC (read-only, no code changes):**

1. **Discover VQB injection mechanism.** Open a non-quiz page (e.g. homepage), capture network waterfall + DOM scripts. Identify:
   - Exact script src URL(s) and all origins involved (cdn.visualquizbuilder.com plus any auxiliary domains).
   - WHERE the script tag appears in the DOM (head, body, position relative to theme.liquid markers).
   - WHEN it's injected (sync in app-embed slot? async via a loader? lazy via IntersectionObserver?).
   - Whether VQB sets any `window.*` properties BEFORE the main bundle loads (would constrain interception ordering).
2. **Discover the quiz route(s).** Walk the storefront for any URL that legitimately uses VQB (likely `/apps/quiz`, possibly `/pages/quiz`, plus PDP-embedded quiz widgets if any). The allowlist must be precise — false-negative breaks the product.
3. **Confirm app-embed slot location.** Find where in `layout/theme.liquid` the `{% render_app_blocks %}` or equivalent slot renders, so the guard script can be placed strictly BEFORE it.
4. **Propose intervention.** Write a ~30-line guard snippet (do NOT commit it, paste into bd notes). Include the pathname allowlist regex, the MutationObserver filter, and the origin denylist. Justify the ordering choice.
5. **Risk audit.** Enumerate failure modes: VQB script bundles other vendors (would also be killed), VQB loaded via Web Component / iframe (MutationObserver might miss it), VQB injected post-load via async fetcher (guard must persist). For each, propose a mitigation or a check.

**Out of scope for this investigation:**
- Any code commit. This is research only.
- Modifying VQB admin settings (assumed unavailable per user direction).
- Replacing VQB with a different quiz vendor (separate evaluation).

**Deliverable:** bd notes appended with sections (1)–(5) above. Include a `git status`/`git diff --stat` confirming zero changes were staged.
acceptance_criteria: 1. bd notes contain: (a) VQB injection mechanism + origin list, (b) authoritative quiz-route allowlist, (c) app-embed slot location in theme.liquid, (d) proposed ~30-line guard snippet, (e) risk audit with mitigations. 2. Zero code changes staged (read-only investigation). 3. Investigation output sufficient for a follow-up implementation task to be filed with no further research needed.

title: Investigate + fix desktop CLS p75 still at 0.20 (POOR; mobile dropped to 0.07)
description: Apr 29–May 2 CLS sprint took mobile CrUX CLS from 0.30 to 0.07. Desktop CLS p75 stayed at 0.20 because the sprint targeted mobile-specific elements (cart freegifts, Reamaze, Klaviyo). Need separate desktop investigation.
design:
## Goal
Find why desktop CrUX CLS p75 = 0.20 (POOR threshold ≥0.10) didn't improve like mobile. The Apr 29–May 2 CLS sprint shipped 8 fixes that took mobile field CLS p75 from 0.30 → 0.07. Desktop didn't budge.

## Hypothesis
The CLS sprint's targets were mobile-specific:
- Cart freegifts 160 px reserve (mobile cart layout)
- Reamaze widget label position:fixed (mobile widget)
- Klaviyo + LoyaltyLion overlays position:fixed (mobile overlays)
- Shop Sheriff PWA modal hide (mobile install prompt)
- Header announcement min-height (likely mobile-specific)

Desktop probably has its OWN unique CLS sources we never measured because mobile dominated the field-data attention.

## Investigation steps
1. Open homepage in Brave with desktop viewport (1920x1080).
2. DevTools → Performance Insights → record load + idle phase.
3. Inspect Layout Shift events — note source elements + shift score.
4. Repeat for /products/* (PDP), /collections/* (collection grid), /cart, /blogs/* (article).
5. Cross-reference with GA4 desktop RUM (filter device_category=desktop).
6. Look for desktop-specific late-loading widgets: BSS B2B portal scripts (might still hydrate on desktop), LimeSpot widgets, Smart SEO injections.

## Files likely to modify
- snippets/css-overrides.liquid (aspect-ratio reserves)
- assets/theme.dev.js / theme.js (defer / lazy-init for desktop-only widgets)
- specific section files (one per identified shift source)

## NOT dispatchable
DevTools-based investigation. Keep on CC + browser MCP.
acceptance_criteria:
- CrUX desktop CLS p75 drops from 0.20 → ≤0.10 (28-day window post-fix)
- No mobile CLS regression (currently 0.07; must stay ≤0.10)
- Specific shift sources documented in commit message + bd notes
notes:
MERGE_GATE_PASS at 2026-05-09T23:20:43Z — Brave smoke verified contain:layout active on both header elements. Live push complete.
commit 4e6ecc2422d4b30c8c9ab4ef30271b56c0d3a9a7
Author: Y9378348C <y9378348c@MacBook-Air-de-Y9378348C.local>
Date:   Sun May 10 01:20:33 2026 +0200

    fix: kt0 desktop header CLS + p8j richer JS error_type categorization
    
    kt0 (desktop CLS):
    GA4 RUM (last 14d, deviceCategory=desktop) confirmed header.theme__header

title: GTM tag 94 (TrafficGuard) references orphan trigger 2147479553 — fraud detection disabled
description: Tag 94 (TrafficGuard) in GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD live v140 references trigger ID 2147479553 which does not exist anywhere in the container. TrafficGuard verifies traffic quality before allowing tracking — without firing, fraud detection is disabled and invalid traffic may inflate GA4 metrics. Same root cause as bd hairmnl-theme-l3g (orphan trigger). Fix requires reconnecting tag 94 to a valid trigger — likely 'All Pages'. Verify with TrafficGuard's documentation on which event/page lifecycle the script expects to fire on.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS UPDATE 2026-05-10T10:50:17Z — no GTM fix needed; misdiagnosis in original bd.

Trigger ID 2147479553 = GTM built-in All Pages. Live container shows tag 94 (TrafficGuard, type=html) compiled with predicate 1 (gtm.js page load), pushing dataTrafficGuard.push(['event','pageview']) and loading //tgtag.io/tg.js?pid=tg-g-007145-003 on every page.

Vendor doc check (TrafficGuard help.trafficguard.ai/en/articles/6761119): 'In the new tag's triggering card, choose All Pages.' Tag matches official setup.

If TrafficGuard's portal shows gaps in fraud-detection coverage, the cause is downstream (account config, ad-platform integration, property-group setup), not this tag's trigger.

References: gtm-audit.py:100-101, scripts/gtm-inspect-orphan-trio.py

title: GHA dashboard refresh failed silently — secrets misconfigured or write-mangled
description: First run of perf-dashboard.yml (run 25403751810, 2026-05-05 21:38 UTC) reported success but did no work. Logs show: GA4 key file 'is not a valid json file' (JSONDecodeError char 0 — file empty or starts with non-JSON), and all PSI calls returned HTTP 400. Hardened the workflow with a Verify-secrets step + JSON validation after writing + printf instead of echo. Next run should surface the exact failure mode. Likely cause: GA4_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY secret was set with the wrong name, empty value, or the workflow's echo mangled the multi-line JSON.

title: Tooltip mobile UX — tap-to-toggle, viewport-edge clamping, focus-visible
description: Current tooltips are hover-only (CSS :hover in _tooltip() ~line 811). Add a small inline JS tap-to-toggle so they work on touch devices. Clamp tooltip position so it never overflows the viewport edge. Add :focus-visible styles.

title: Mobile responsive pass — chart stacking, URL truncation, breakpoints
description: In render_html() CSS block (~lines 540-700), add responsive breakpoints so charts stack vertically at <768px, long URL strings truncate with ellipsis, and layout doesn't overflow at 375px (iPhone SE). No horizontal scroll at any viewport.

title: Staleness banner: amber alert when newest snapshot >24h old
description: In render_html(), compute age of newest snapshot. If >24h, inject an amber banner at top of page: snapshot is stale with a link to manually trigger the GitHub Action workflow_dispatch. Banner must NOT render when data is fresh.

title: [deferred] Per-template CWV slicing (PDP vs Cart vs Collection)
description: Add Shopify template dimension to GA4 web-vitals events for template-level CWV slicing. Requires: (1) GA4 admin: create custom dimension Shopify.template; (2) snippets/web-vitals-reporter.liquid: include {{ template }} in the event payload; (3) scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py: add per-template slice in friction pages and RUM cards. NOT in this dashboard sprint — deserves its own focused session with theme deploy verification.

title: WoW volume sanity warning when this_total < prev_total/2
description: WoW scorecard at line ~1057 compares percentages only. If RUM volume drops 50%+ (holiday traffic, instrumentation regression), percentage shifts can lie about real performance. Add a warning block under the scorecard when this_total < prev_total/2 explaining that % changes may be misleading at low volume.

title: Sample-size guards — render (N=...) on every RUM percentage
description: RUM cards can render '100% good' with N=3 events, giving false confidence. In render_rum_cwv_cards() at line ~997, append (N=...) annotation to every percentage. If total<50, prefix with warning glyph (e.g. ⚠️ N<50).

title: Snapshot deduplication on read — keep latest-per-UTC-day in load_snapshots()
description: Multiple same-day runs append duplicate snapshots to dashboard/data/snapshots.jsonl. Dedup at read time (not file mutation): in load_snapshots() at line 431, group by UTC calendar day and keep the latest snapshot per day. Preserves the raw append-only file as historical record.

title: /collections/all 40.7% poor CLS — identify and fix layout shift source
description: GA4 7-day RUM shows /collections/all at 40.7% poor CLS (24/59 sessions). Collections are not in the PDP widget stack already fixed (Klaviyo, LL, BOGOS). Likely culprits: LimeSpot upsell widget on collection pages, filter sidebar rehydration, or a collection-specific app embed.\n\nInvestigation: scrape live page for body-level injected divs + check which third-party scripts fire on /collections/all vs PDPs. Fix: same position:fixed + contain:layout pattern as e4a-γ/δ/0u4/3uw.
notes: Investigation 2026-05-04: Exhaustive static analysis of /collections/all HTML (661KB). Ruled out: BSS B2B collection VAT (disabled with if false), filter sidebar JS toggle on mobile (position:fixed outer, no layout impact), Judge.me preview badge (server-side rendered via judgeme_static_stars, not JS-injected), recently-viewed section (min-height:300px already applied), BOGOS clone removal (skipped when #secomapp_freegifts_version present). PRIMARY fix: 3uw BOGOS containers (commit shipped same session) apply on /collections/all — #freegifts-main-popup-container + #sca-fg-notifications present on page, now position:fixed. SECONDARY fix: <limespot> custom element pre-declared display:block + contain:layout to prevent LimeSpot SDK injection from cascading to footer siblings (commit 93e8295). Validate GA4 /collections/all CLS trend in 7 days (2026-05-11).

title: BOGOS .sca-gift-image PDP CLS — confirm injection point + reserve dimensions
description:
PSI 'Image elements do not have explicit width and height' audit flags .sca-gift-image as unsized on PDPs. BOGOS / Secomapp Free Gifts app injects this element. Without DevTools confirmation of WHERE on the PDP it renders (PDP gallery? cart drawer? variant selector?), we can't safely reserve dimensions — blanket sizing risks regressing wherever it appears.

Investigation steps:
1. DevTools → Elements panel → find .sca-gift-image on a PDP that has BOGOS offers active (e.g., Wella all-shades flagged as worst at 44 events / 52% poor in 7d).
2. Note its parent container + intrinsic rendered dimensions.
3. Check whether it's reused across multiple page locations (cart drawer too?).

Once located:
- Add CSS rule with explicit width/height in snippets/css-overrides.liquid following the .jdgm-verified-by__image pattern at line 44.
- Or reserve min-height on its parent container if dimensions vary.

Parent of e4a.

title: Identify cookie consent banner source app — reserve CSS space to prevent CLS
description:
PSI filmstrip frame 5 on serioxyl PDP shows a cookie consent banner appearing mid-pageload. No theme snippet renders it — verified by 'grep -rn cookie\|consent snippets/ layout/ sections/' returning only inline-script cookie-helper code (no banner UI). The banner must be injected by a Shopify app (Avada GDPR Cookie Consent, GTM consent CMP, or Shopify's native customer privacy block).

Investigation steps:
1. Open serioxyl PDP in Chrome DevTools incognito (no consent cookie set).
2. Performance tab → record pageload → identify banner DOM node + creator JS source.
3. Check Shopify Admin → Apps for installed cookie/consent apps.
4. Check theme settings + GTM consent triggers.

Once source identified:
- If app-injected with body-level container: add 'body > #<banner-id> { position: fixed !important; bottom: 0; contain: layout; }' to snippets/css-overrides.liquid following e4a-γ/δ pattern.
- If theme-side or section-level: reserve min-height on its parent container.

Parent of e4a (PDP residual CLS investigation).

title: Tree-shake vendor.js (Rellax/axios/ellipsis baggage) — needs build-pipeline access
description:
vendor.js is 100KB on disk (~30-35KB gzipped), bundles Rellax (~10KB, only used when conditional [data-rellax] present — never on homepage), axios (~8KB, can be replaced with native fetch — most call sites already use fetch), and ellipsis (~2KB, used by truncate widget on PDP/article only). Total ~20KB JS savings. theme.js line 7243 IIFE pulls these vendor bundles in even when unused; the cost is parse + initial heap allocation, not runtime calls.

Why blocked: this repo only has the BUILT vendor.js — the source is in a separate build pipeline (esbuild config + node_modules) we don't have access to. Tree-shake requires editing the source, rebuilding, and pushing the regenerated vendor.js.

When unblocked: estimated -50 to -100ms parse-time on cold cache, -1ms or so on warm cache. Worth doing once build access is available, but not the highest-leverage perf win without it.

title: Wella Koleston PDP — 52% poor CLS (66/126), highest %poor in 7d dataset
description: 2026-05-02 dashboard: /products/wella-professionals-koleston-perfect-ppd-free-permanent-hair-color-tube-all-shades shows 52% poor CLS in 7d — the HIGHEST %poor CLS in the entire dataset, even higher than blog pages. Only 126 events/7d so low traffic, but worth investigating since it's a per-page pattern. Likely cause: this product has 'all-shades' = many variants. The variant selector likely re-renders/re-paints on selection change, causing CLS. Also a hair color product so may have specific schema/UI elements (color swatches injected as images without dimensions). Investigation: test the page in a browser with DevTools Performance > Layout Shifts panel. Apply targeted CSS reservation if specific element identified. Could share fix with other 'all-shades' / 'all-colors' multi-variant products in the catalog.
notes: 2026-05-02 re-query: PDP product price column (div.wrapper.product__align-left...div.grid.product-single) shows 57 poor-CLS events in 2d vs 251/7d pre-fix (35.9/day pre → 28.5/day post). Modest ~21% rate drop, but within normal variance. Fix shipped 2026-05-02. Re-check 2026-05-09 for clean 7d post-fix window.
close_reason: Added .product__price__wrap { min-height:44px; contain:layout } to prevent BSS B2B price collapse. Root cause: bss-b2b-product-vat.liquid inline style hides .product__price (display:none) → 251 poor-CLS events/7d on PDP info column across all PDPs.

title: /cart INP 21% poor — investigate quantity stepper / drawer interactions
description: 2026-05-02 dashboard: /cart shows 12/58 (21%) poor-INP events in 7d, second-highest %poor INP page after PDPs. Cart UX is interaction-heavy (qty steppers, drawer toggles, line-item remove). Likely synchronous JS work on click handlers — quantity update fetches, then re-renders cart, then re-fires totals/freegifts/sticky-bar logic. Investigation: GA4 INP attribution targets filtered to pagePath=/cart, find which target dominates. Then audit the click handler chain. Fixes typically: yield to main thread between updates, debounce qty steppers, defer non-critical re-render (sticky bar, freegifts) to requestIdleCallback.
notes:
2026-05-04 STATIC ANALYSIS (no DevTools): Investigation complete.

ATTRIBUTION (last 14d, /cart, poor INP only):
  17 / 22 poor INPs → '#shopify-section-template--XXX__main>div.cart__template>div.wrapper.pt2.pb4'
  This is the cart body container — generic ancestor of all qty steppers, remove buttons,
  drawer toggles. The browser couldn't isolate a more specific target, which means the slow
  handler is either (a) attached at this container level via delegation, or (b) the click
  bubbles to a slow document/body listener registered by a third-party app.

CLICK → PAINT CHAIN (sync work in INP window):
  Source: assets/theme.dev.js (readable copy of bundled assets/theme.js).
  1. Ns.increase/decrease (line 4633): preventDefault + value++ + input.dispatchEvent('change') SYNC
  2. CartItems.initInputs change listener (line 4083): parseInt + closest + lockState + updateCart
  3. lockState (line 4111):
       - this.latestClick.querySelector('.item--loadbar').style.display = 'block'   (1 style write)
       - this.loader.classList.add('cart--loading')                                  (1 class add)
  4. updateCart returns immediately (fetch is async — NOT in INP window)
  5. Browser must paint → forced style recalc

INP COSTS (in priority order of suspicion):
  A. Broad CSS cascade from .cart--loading. Three rules (.cart--loading .quantity__wrapper,
     .quantity__input, .quantity__button) match ALL N items × 3 = ~3N elements. Adding the
     class on the high-level loader wrapper forces O(N) style recalc.
  B. External app listeners on input/change events: BSS B2B price recalc, BOGOS gift state,
     LoyaltyLion redeem — all run synchronously in the same tick.
  C. Document-level delegated click handlers from other apps (less likely).

RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP (live DevTools):
  1. Open https://www.hairmnl.com/cart in Chrome with full cart (~5-10 items).
  2. Open DevTools → Performance → Record.
  3. Click +/- on a quantity stepper.
  4. Stop recording. Look for the Long Task on the click.
  5. Expand the click → Recalculate Style segment. Note the duration + 'elements affected' count.
  6. Inspect the Event listeners panel for [data-quantity-input] inputs — see all attached
     listeners (theme + BSS B2B + BOGOS + LoyaltyLion). The non-theme listeners are the targets.

LOW-RISK FIX CANDIDATES (in order of safety):
  1. CSS-only: scope .cart--loading rules to the active row only. Not trivial without JS change.
  2. JS bundle patch (theme.js + theme.dev.js): in CartItems.lockState, add 'cart--loading'
     to this.latestClick instead of this.loader. CSS .cart--loading .quantity__* rules then
     only match within ONE row → O(1) recalc instead of O(N). Low semantic risk, but minified
     bundle modification → must test on draft theme 140785582179 first.
  3. Yield-to-paint shim in custom-theme.js: intercept stepper +/- clicks in capturing phase,
     wrap dispatchEvent('change') in requestAnimationFrame so click handler returns before
     downstream change listeners run. Lets the browser paint first. Risk: races with theme.js
     handler attachment timing.

NOT RECOMMENDED THIS SESSION:
  Any modification of theme.js without DevTools-validated profile. Cart is a high-stakes
  page (conversion-critical) and silent regressions on stepper/remove behavior are costly.
  Static analysis points strongly at fix #2 above, but ship only after DevTools confirms
  Recalculate Style is the dominant cost.

title: Cart CLS 49% poor remains after T2 freegifts fix — find second shift target
description: 2026-05-02 dashboard: /cart shows 88/178 (49%) poor-CLS events in 7d, even after 2026-05-01 T2 fix (#freegifts-main-page-container min-height:80px). Earlier 7fz attribution showed cart shifts attributed to '#shopify-section-template--16528967270499__main>div.cart__template>div.wrapper.pt2.pb4' at 73 events — current is 88. There's a SECOND shift target on cart pages not yet identified. Investigation needed: GA4 query filtered to pagePath=/cart with debug_target dimension, find what's shifting beyond freegifts container. Likely candidates: cart line-item update injection (qty change re-renders), Gifted Free Gifts main page container itself growing post-fix-shipped, BSS B2B cart price hide, sticky atc bar height. Fix: add additional CSS reservation in css-overrides.liquid for the identified target.
notes: 2026-05-02 re-query: cart wrapper (#shopify-section-template-16528967270499 main>div.cart__te) shows 70 poor-CLS events in 2d post-fix vs 87/7d pre-fix baseline (87/7=12.4/day pre → 70/2=35/day post). Rate appears HIGHER, likely due to traffic concentration in last 2d (same 2d is included in the 7d window, meaning most pre-fix events clustered near fix date). Too early — fixes shipped 2026-05-01 → 2026-05-02. Re-check 2026-05-09 (7d fully post-fix). pwa-modal CONFIRMED zero in 2d window (nu6 fix holding).
close_reason: Increased #freegifts-main-page-container min-height 80→160px (2 gift rows) + added .section-recent min-height:300px for recently-viewed CLS. Root cause: freegifts 2-row injection overflowed 80px reservation → 141 poor events on outer cart wrapper.

title: GTM: js_error tag missing event param mapping (32K events, all params (not set))
description: 2026-05-02 dashboard finding: js_error tracking pushes 32K events to GA4 in 7d, but error_type/error_message/error_source all show (not set). theme.liquid line 594-599 pushes to dataLayer with correct field names (renamed 2026-04-29 from js_error_* → no prefix per code comment line 521-528). GA4 custom dimensions exist. Issue: GTM tag firing on event=js_error doesn't map dataLayer params to GA4 event parameters. Same admin fix pattern as 2026-05-01 GTM v139 bot exclusion. Required: open GTM container GTM-M4NKSBD → find GA4 - JS Error tag → Event Parameters section → add or fix mapping for error_type, error_message, error_source as DLVs reading the new no-prefix field names → publish as new version. Until fixed, JS errors are blind — 32K errors/7d but no diagnostic data. ~10 min admin task.
notes: 2026-05-02 re-query (29k hairmnl_js_error rename, GTM v140 published today): 2d window shows 28,980 (not set) errors vs 30,519/7d pre-fix. 2d rate (~14,490/day) appears HIGHER than 7d rate (~4,360/day) — but this is misleading: GTM v140 only published 2026-05-02 (today), so the 2d window is almost entirely pre-fix. Additionally, privacy-policy bot traffic spikes may explain day-to-day variance. Re-check 2026-05-09 with a 7d window that is fully post-fix. Expect: (not set) events to drop to near-zero or shift to hairmnl_js_error events with proper params.
close_reason: Renamed dataLayer event js_error → hairmnl_js_error (theme.liquid + GTM trigger v140). Root cause was Elevar old install pushing {event:'js_error'} — triggered our DLVs which returned undefined for Elevar's fields. GA4 eventName unchanged. Expect js_error params to populate within 24h.

title: Verify #pwa-modal CLS dropped 24h post-display:none ship (2026-05-02)
description: Phase 2 (perf audit) shipped display:none on #pwa-modal in css-overrides.liquid 2026-05-01 11:53 CEST. Root cause was Shop Sheriff main.js animating CSS top property (not transform). Re-pull GA4 RUM 2026-05-02 to verify: #pwa-modal debug_target events drop from ~2,127/7d toward <50/7d. Also check mobile CrUX p75 CLS over next 14d (will move slowly due to rolling window). Run: GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 /usr/local/bin/python3.14 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --days 7 (note: requires Python 3.14 with google-analytics-data installed). Or pull just CrUX via build-perf-dashboard.py.
close_reason: Verified via GA4 API 2026-05-02: #pwa-modal CLS events dropped from 1,196/3d (PRE 04-28→04-30) to 0/2d (POST 05-01→05-02). display:none fix in css-overrides.liquid:86-89 confirmed effective in production.

title: Defer cross-post-blogs Swiper init to IntersectionObserver (custom-theme.js:13891)
description: Same pattern as ua7 (collection-slider lazy-init shipped earlier). custom-theme.js scripts/cross-post-blogs.js initializes a Swiper for '.cross-post-blogs .swiper-slide' at DOMContentLoaded. The cross-post-blogs section appears at the BOTTOM of article pages (the 'Read more from this blog' carousel). Always below-fold on initial render. Should be lazy-init via IntersectionObserver with 200px rootMargin, same as ua7. Estimated 50-150ms TBT shaved on article pages. Implementation: extract initCrossPostBlogs() into a function, wrap Swiper instantiation in IntersectionObserver. ~30 min work.
notes: Originally closed with cross-post-blogs reason (correct). The earlier close action with theme.dev.js audit reason was a misfire — see hairmnl-theme-6xd close note for the actual theme.dev.js audit findings.
close_reason: Audit complete. theme.dev.js:9277 DCL handler is mostly essential — load('*') is the Pipeline section bootstrap (cannot defer), AOS init must run before scroll, body class flags + table/iframe wrap are lightweight DOM ops. No meaningful lazy-init candidates. theme.dev.js itself already loads deferred. Skipping further work; impact would be marginal.

title: Task 2b: Liquid post-processor for blog body image dimensions
description: Eliminate blog-body-image CLS by parsing Shopify CDN _WIDTHxHEIGHT.ext URLs and injecting explicit width/height attrs. Builds on Task 2's CSS aspect-ratio fallback. Implementation: snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid (split-based parsing, no regex). Wired into sections/article.liquid where article.content was rendered raw. Pure server-side — no client JS, no perf cost.
close_reason: Snippet article-content-with-img-dims.liquid live + verified — 15 of 16 blog images now have explicit width/height injected. The 1 SVG without _NNNxNNN pattern falls through to the CSS fallback as designed.

title: LoyaltyLion double-init bug — fix theme snippet
description: GA4 js_error pipeline (newly working after GTM tag fix today) caught a real prod error: 'Uncaught Error: Cannot call lion.init more than once' fires on every page load. Root cause: theme's snippets/loyaltylion.liquid AND the LoyaltyLion Shopify App Embed block BOTH call loyaltylion.init(). Theme runs first with secure customer auth, then App Embed's second init call throws. Fix: after theme init, neutralize loyaltylion.init to a no-op so the App Embed call becomes silent. Preserves both the app-embed auto-update path AND the theme-side customer auth. Identified via curl /tmp/home.html line 895 (theme) vs line 1371 (app embed).
close_reason: loyaltylion.init no-op guard added after theme init. App Embed double-init no longer throws. Live + committed.

title: GTM container audit + cleanup for perf
description: Audit the HairMNL GTM container (GTM-M4NKSBD, account 4702257664) for tags/triggers/variables that add unnecessary client-side JS load. Review: (1) all enabled tags — can any be paused, deferred, or removed; (2) Custom HTML tags (heaviest type — every line of HTML they inject runs in the page); (3) Tags firing on All Pages vs more specific triggers; (4) Stale/duplicate tags; (5) 3rd-party pixels with no current marketing campaign attached. Goal: trim TBT contribution from GTM-injected JS. With OAuth access now established, this can be done programmatically via the Tag Manager API. Companion to the js_error pipeline fix.
close_reason: Tier A+B cleanup executed via gtm-cleanup-via-new-workspace.py. Created workspace 192 'Cleanup 2026-04-30', deleted 65 tags (16 Elevar-redundant UA + 47 other dead UA + 2 mid-migration leftovers), created Container Version 136, published to live. Verified: live container.js shrunk from ~614KB to 569KB (-45KB / -7.3%), zero references remaining to any of the deleted tag names. Tier C (28 pixel tags) remains for separate review against Elevar destinations.

title: WebP rollout to remaining snippets (hero, banner-slider, branded-subcollection)
description: Task 5 batch — extend the WebP <picture>+<source> pattern from image-fill.liquid + product-grid-item-branded.liquid to the 3 remaining snippets that still emit JPEG-only: hero.liquid, sections/section-banner-slider.liquid, snippets/product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid. Banner-slider previously parked 2026-04-27 due to <source> line being silently dropped — re-attempting with same pattern as proven snippets.
close_reason: 3 snippets shipped to live (hero, product-grid-item-branded-subcollection, section-banner-slider). Source code verified via theme pull. Runtime render verification of banner-slider WebP requires DevTools spot-check (curl blocked by Cloudflare). Other two snippets follow proven pattern from already-shipped image-fill.liquid.

title: Patch Task 4: replace node-html-parser usage with native textContent (runtime win)
description: See custom-theme.js scripts/domparser.js section. Native el.textContent = el.textContent replaces 1,500-line library call. Eliminates parser instantiation + tree walk on every page DCL. Bundle bytes preserved (library is dead but loaded; cleanup filed separately).
close_reason: Native textContent shipped to live. Smoke-test confirmed via theme pull. Bundle byte cleanup follow-up filed.

title: CLS fix on Judge.me medals wrapper hidden→visible transition
description: Wella Koleston PDP showed 49.1% poor CLS (n=116). Image gallery is dimensioned (1180x1180); only CLS source left is .jdgm-medals-wrapper which starts .jdgm-hidden (display: none) and gets revealed when Judge.me loader hydrates badges, growing 0 → ~80px tall and pushing content below. Same pattern likely on all PDPs with Judge.me. Fix: override hidden state with visibility: hidden + min-height to reserve space upfront.
close_reason: Shipped CSS reservation to css-overrides.liquid. Smoke-test confirmed via shopify theme pull (Cloudflare rate-limiting curl).

title: CLS fix on blog article body images (4 of top 5 worst CLS pages are blogs)
description: Per CrUX/RUM: 4 of top 5 worst CLS pages are blog posts (49-55% poor on mobile). Root cause: Shopify rich-text editor doesn't set width/height on inline article body images. Solution: aspect-ratio: auto 16/9 fallback in css-overrides.liquid for .blog__article__content img and .rte img selectors. Pre-reserves space, falls back to intrinsic when image loads. Caveat: portrait images still snap once loaded; perfect fix requires Liquid HTML parser.
close_reason: CSS aspect-ratio fallback shipped to live in css-overrides.liquid. Reduces but doesn't eliminate blog-image CLS. Follow-up: Liquid post-processor for explicit width/height from Shopify CDN URL pattern _NNNxNNN.ext (filed as Task 2b).

title: Fix JS error tracking pipeline (3,075 events with no message/source)
description: Dashboard shows 3,075 js_error events captured by GA4 but ALL have type=?, message=(no message), source=(unknown). The web-vitals-reporter.liquid emits js_error events but the data dimensions aren't populating. Read snippet, find the bug, fix the listener so we get real error visibility. Without this, we're blind to client-side errors which is essential debugging signal.
close_reason: Renamed js_error_* dataLayer keys to error_* matching GA4 registered dimensions. Pushed to live + committed. Data flow depends on whether GTM has auto-forward or explicit mapping; documented in code comment.

title: Self-hosted PWI/CWV performance dashboard (HTML + Python)
description: Build a single-file HTML dashboard rendered by a Python script that combines PSI lab (via run-psi.sh) + GA4 RUM (via web-vitals-report.py code) into one view. Free, accurate, programmatically refreshable. Sections: today's snapshot (PSI mobile+desktop + GA4 7-day), trend chart (last 30 days), real-shopper rating distribution per CWV, top pages by friction, top INP attribution targets, JS errors, vs-baseline comparison. Output: dashboard/index.html. Sister to existing Looker Studio dashboard (issue 24q) — this one is fully Claude-controllable, doesn't need admin UI clicks.
close_reason: Self-hosted dashboard shipped: scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py + dashboard/index.html + dashboard/README.md. GA4 path verified end-to-end with rich real-shopper data (LCP 81% good, CLS 68% good failing 75% threshold, top friction pages identified). PSI path implemented (same pattern as run-psi.sh). Open: open dashboard/index.html. Refresh: ./scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py

title: Phase 0: CRO data layer foundation (Clarity + PostHog + GrowthBook + BigQuery + consent gate)
description: Umbrella for the 8-task Phase 0 setup defined in /Users/y9378348c/Downloads/HairMNL-CRO-DataLayer-Proposal.md. Defaults confirmed by user 2026-04-29 EOD: self-host OK (Hetzner CX11 ~$5/mo), no existing CMP (add CookieYes free tier), A/B winner workflow human-review for first 5 then auto-promote, enable GA4→BigQuery export as Step 0, perf regression cap ≤200ms TBT hard threshold. Tasks: (1) provision Hetzner VPS, (2) deploy PostHog via Docker compose, (3) deploy GrowthBook on same VPS, (4) enable GA4→BigQuery daily export, (5) sign up Microsoft Clarity + configure PII masking, (6) install CookieYes consent banner, (7) wire Clarity+PostHog scripts behind consent gate with lazy-load after LCP, (8) verify perf threshold via pre/post PWI. Total est ~6 hrs dev. Cost: $5/mo. Break down into sub-tasks when starting Phase 0 work.
notes: 2026-05-20: parked until P8 cutover is stable. Revisit post-cutover.

title: Lazy-init below-fold Swipers on homepage via IntersectionObserver
description:
Production INP attribution (RUM 2026-04-29) shows homepage at 67% good / 13.1% poor — worst on site. 97% of poor INP events have (not set) debug_target = page-load JS work blocking first interaction.

Homepage has 8 Swiper instances initializing at DOMContentLoaded:
  - 7 .swiper.collection (custom-theme.js:14292)
  - 1 banner-slider (separate init)
  - 315 .swiper-slide elements total

Each Swiper does layout measurement + touch/pointer/keyboard listener binding at init. Doing this for 8 swipers serially in one task is the most likely source of the (not set) INPs.

PROPOSED FIX:
Add a tiny IntersectionObserver wrapper that defers new Swiper(...) for below-fold .swiper.collection elements until they scroll into the viewport. The first slider (above fold) keeps eager init for visual polish; the other 6-7 init lazily.

Implementation options:
A. Modify the custom-theme.js bundle itself (requires source repo + build pipeline — likely not in this repo)
B. Add a wrapper snippet that runs BEFORE custom-theme.js on DOMContentLoaded, removing the .swiper.collection class from below-fold elements, then restoring + triggering the init manually when they intersect. Brittle but doable from theme code only.
C. Add a snippet that disables Swiper auto-init by removing .collection from class, then re-adds + manually calls Swiper constructor on intersect. Needs Swiper to be exposed globally — currently it's IIFE-bundled inside custom-theme.js.

RECOMMENDED: Option B with extensive testing. Estimated win: 200-400ms TBT reduction on homepage initial load = direct INP improvement (since INP is bounded by main-thread availability).

VALIDATION: Re-run web-vitals-report.py --metric INP --by page after 14d to confirm homepage INP %good moves from 67% toward 75% target.
close_reason: IntersectionObserver wrapper live on both draft and live theme. 6 of 7 .swiper.collection instances now lazy-init 200px before viewport entry.

title: Remove Snapchat Pixel (admin-side: Custom Pixel, app embed, or GTM)
description:
User flagged Snapchat analytics in Network tab (sc-static.net/scevent.min.js + tr.snapchat.com, ~26KB total). Theme-code grep finds ZERO references — pixel is not in any liquid/json/js file. Live HTML grep also returns zero. Pixel is loaded admin-side via one of:

1. Shopify Admin → Settings → Customer events → Custom pixels → find Snapchat-related custom pixel → Disconnect
2. Shopify Admin → Apps → find Snapchat Channel or any connected Snapchat app → Uninstall
3. Google Tag Manager (if installed) → tagmanager.google.com → find Snapchat-related tag → pause or delete
4. GA4 / GTM-bound third-party pixel manager

Need user (admin access) to identify which of the above is loading it and disable. Theme-side has nothing to remove. Once removed, verify via Network tab: no requests to sc-static.net or tr.snapchat.com.
notes: Removed via Shopify Admin (Custom Pixel or app — confirmed admin-side, not theme code). No theme code existed to remove. Closed 2026-05-06.

title: Roll out forloop.first eager+fetchpriority pattern to other above-fold sections
description:
Today shipped the LCP fix on section-gallery.liquid (forloop.first → eager + fetchpriority=high). Same pattern should apply to other sections that render above-the-fold images via image-fill snippet:

- section-hero.liquid — homepage hero, single image, always above fold → eager+priority
- collection-hero.liquid — top of collection page, always above fold → eager+priority
- snippets/hero.liquid — wrapper used by multiple sections → check usage pattern
- section-banner-slider.liquid — homepage banner slider, FIRST slide above fold → eager+priority on first slide
- snippets/article-grid-item.liquid — blog article grid, FIRST card above fold → eager on first
- snippets/collection-grid-item.liquid — same as above for collection grids
- sections/section-video.liquid — first video poster image above fold → eager

Approx time: 1-2 hr to update all 7 sections + verify each via PSI's lcp-discovery-insight on representative page types.

Why P2: this directly attacks the 1.41K mobile URLs flagged in GSC for LCP > 2.5s. Each section fixed = a class of pages improved.
close_reason: section-hero + collection-hero updated to pass eager:true. section-video skipped (passes image.src string, would break hero.liquid image filters). article-grid-item + collection-grid-item already done 2026-04-28.

title: Blog template CLS — secondary culprits beyond #pwa-modal (low-porosity 0.54, best-hair-color 0.53)
description:
GSC CrUX field data shows TWO blog pages with CLS in 0.50+ range (>2x the poor threshold of 0.25):
- /blogs/hair-education/low-porosity-hair-what-is-it-and-what-should-you-do-about-it: CLS 0.54 (135 affected)
- /blogs/salon-services/best-hair-color-for-morenas-complement-your-natural-glow: CLS 0.53 (158 affected)

GA4 RUM debug_target attribution on these pages showed:
- 55 events: #pwa-modal (PRIMARY, addressed by separate CSS fix)
- 1 event: #shopify-section-footer (footer late-render, minor)

But 0.54 CLS is more than just #pwa-modal — there must be additional shifts not captured by the dominant attribution. Likely candidates on blog template:
1. AOS scroll animations (data-aos elements might shift)
2. Bloggle's own lazysizes loader (sections/main-bloggle-article.liquid:358 — different lazysizes than ours)
3. Long-form text reflow as web fonts load (font-display: swap on body fonts)
4. Inline blog images without explicit width/height
5. Social share buttons / table of contents widgets

Investigation needed:
1. Open one of the blog URLs in Chrome DevTools → Performance → record load → identify all CLS shift sources
2. Verify that #pwa-modal CSS fix (containment) gets blogs from 0.54 → ~0.20-0.30 (still poor but reduced)
3. Address remaining shifts per filmstrip

Worth a 2-3 hr focused session AFTER #pwa-modal fix lands and we re-measure.
close_reason: Substantially resolved by #pwa-modal containment fix (commit 29e0f3c). Blog page low-porosity-hair CLS dropped 0.54 → 0.030 — well within 'good' threshold (<0.1). Remaining tiny shifts (0.015 inline blog img + 0.008 bta-reservation widget + 0.006 lion-notification) sum to 0.030 total — not worth dedicated work. Re-open if CrUX field data shows blog CLS staying poor after 28-day refresh window.

title: Theme-check linter pass: 1,562 offenses across 163 files (171 errors, 1391 warnings)
description:
2026-04-28 ran 'shopify theme check' on the entire theme. Comprehensive findings:

By severity:
  - 171 errors
  - 1391 warnings

Top checks (issue types):
  848 VariableName — naming convention warnings (cosmetic)
  206 DeprecatedFilter — img_url -> image_url (bulk find-replace, low risk if done carefully)
  151 UnusedAssign — unused {% assign %} vars (cosmetic, may save bytes)
   81 DeprecatedTag — old Liquid tags
   50 MatchingTranslations — locale file mismatches
   44 RemoteAsset — assets loaded from external URLs (could self-host)
   38 LiquidHTMLSyntaxError — mostly false positives in svg.liquid + js.liquid
   34 MissingTemplate — broken {% render %} calls (real bugs, see below)
   32 ImgWidthAndHeight — img tags missing width/height (CLS impact, see e4a)
   29 UndefinedObject — references to undefined Liquid objects

FIXED in 2026-04-28 commit (3 small fixes from this audit):
  - snippets/pro-blogger.snippet.related-item.liquid:23,48 — 2x /cart/add hardcoded → routes.cart_add_url
  - snippets/collections-sidebar.liquid:74,92,117 — /collections/all hardcoded → routes.all_products_collection_url
  - snippets/collections-sidebar.liquid:79 — /collections/{handle} hardcoded → routes.collections_url

CRITICAL BUG found, NOT YET FIXED:
  layout/theme.liquid:676 has 6 broken {% render %} calls with double-extension paths:
    {% render 'bss-passcode-to-see-price-js.liquid' %}  (should be 'bss-passcode-to-see-price-js')
    {% render 'bsscommerce-login-to-see-price-custom-js.liquid' %}
    {% render 'bss-ltap-fl-redirect.liquid' %}
  These render calls FAIL SILENTLY (the {% unless contains 'Liquid error' %} swallows the error).
  Effect: BSS B2B 'login to see price' + 'passcode to see price' + 'first-login redirect' features are non-functional.
  Decision needed: are these BSS features supposed to work? If yes, fix the render paths. If no, delete the dead code.
  See beads bjk for the broader BSS B2B uninstall decision.

OTHER MissingTemplate refs (all dead-app leftovers — likely safe to delete after verifying app uninstall):
  - icon-error.liquid (referenced 6x by various BSS B2B passcode forms)
  - ly-global.liquid (referenced by pluginseo-langify — Langify language app)
  - wof_product_limit, wlm-search, wcp_discount, wcp_variant, wcp_vd_product, wlm-product (referenced by search.wof/collection.wof — WishList Plus app?)
  - section-static-banner.liquid (judgeme_allReviewsPage)
  - bss-ltap-custom-passcode-form.liquid
  - sections/collection-lp-serioxyl.liquid (template references missing section)

MissingAsset refs (8):
  - assets/sca.affiliate.refer-customer-data.js (SCA affiliate app)
  - assets/filename.png (mbc-bundles — placeholder bug?)
  - assets/nil.jpg (3 references — likely intentional placeholder pattern)
  - assets/ico-select.svg, cursor-zoom.svg, cursor-zoom-2x.svg (in css-variables.liquid)

Worth a dedicated 4-6 hour clean-up sprint. Most impactful fixes:
1. Fix or delete the 6 broken BSS renders in theme.liquid:676 (real broken feature)
2. Address 32 ImgWidthAndHeight (CLS-positive)
3. Bulk img_url → image_url migration (modernization, allows format:'webp', etc)
4. Audit 44 RemoteAsset for self-hosting candidates

title: Stale URL redirect from product rename: /products/davines-momo-shampoo → / (admin fix)
description:
Discovered 2026-04-28: navigating to /products/davines-momo-shampoo as a guest browser (no BSS B2B cookies set) returns the 404 page instead of the product. This affects:

1. PSI testing — PSI lab user has no cookies, so all PSI runs of this URL test the 404 page, NOT the actual PDP. The CLS/LCP numbers we've been quoting for 'momo' are for the 404 page (which has a Judge.me medals widget at the bottom contributing CLS).
2. Real shoppers — guests landing on this product from Google search/etc presumably also see 404. SEO impact + lost conversion.
3. This product is presumably restricted via BSS B2B Customer Portal app's 'Hide product' rules.

Traced to sections/product.liquid:1-4 — bsscommerce-redirect-product-page-logic snippet runs server-side, redirects unauthenticated guests away.

Need founder/web-team decision:
1. Is davines-momo INTENTIONALLY B2B-only? If yes, set noindex robots tag on the redirect target so Google doesn't surface it.
2. If unintentional, fix the BSS rules to allow guest access.
3. If it's a phased/staged rollout, document which products are restricted and ensure SEO accordingly.

Quick check: 'bd show hairmnl-theme-bjk' (existing BSS B2B uninstall decision ticket) — full BSS uninstall would remove this whole class of issue.
acceptance_criteria: Reamaze widget visible at correct position with non-zero dimensions on homepage + PDP + collection. Fixed 2026-05-12 by narrowing selector to #reamaze-widget-label only and removing contain:layout. Commit 59fb4d4.
notes:
2026-05-04 VERIFIED STILL BROKEN: curl of https://www.hairmnl.com/products/davines-momo-shampoo confirms 301→ homepage (soft-404). Canonical URL https://www.hairmnl.com/products/davines-essentials-momo-shampoo-moisturizing-shampoo-for-dry-or-dehydrated-hair is HTTP 200. FIX REQUIRED IN SHOPIFY ADMIN (cannot be done via theme code): Admin → Online Store → Navigation → URL Redirects → find /products/davines-momo-shampoo → change target from '/' to '/products/davines-essentials-momo-shampoo-moisturizing-shampoo-for-dry-or-dehydrated-hair'. Also audit all redirects pointing to '/' — any product-handle sources are stale and losing traffic.
VERIFIED FIXED 2026-05-24: live curl test shows /products/davines-momo-shampoo returns 301 → /products/davines-essentials-momo-shampoo-moisturizing-shampoo-for-dry-or-dehydrated-hair (x-redirect-reason: shop_redirect), then 200. Confirmed via operator screenshot of Shopify admin → URL Redirects. The 2026-05-04 'VERIFIED STILL BROKEN' note was correct AT THE TIME but the fix was applied in admin between then and now. Closing as resolved. Lesson: don't trust stale notes — re-verify before stating current state.

title: PDP residual CLS — late-loading widgets shift <main> + <footer> (~0.42 + 0.16)
description:
After the 2026-04-28 cap-removal fix on --default-ratio (commit 3cfa9b9), the Flickity-init column shift on PDPs is gone. davines-momo CLS dropped from 0.600 to 0.421 (median of 3). But residual ~0.42 shift on <main> and ~0.16 on <footer> remains.

These page-level shifts are caused by late-loading third-party widgets injecting content above-the-fold or pushing the main content area:
- Klaviyo signup modal (visible in PSI filmstrip frame 8 on serioxyl)
- LoyaltyLion notification ('Sign up for HairMNL Rewards to get a Php100 voucher')
- BSS B2B price hide injection (loading-prompt element on guest pageload)
- BOGOS / SCA free-gift element (.sca-gift-image present in PSI unsized-images audit)
- Reamaze chat widget (small shifts but cumulative)
- Cookie consent banner (visible in PSI filmstrip frame 5 on serioxyl)

Next investigation steps:
1. Use Lighthouse 'Avoid large layout shifts' diagnostic at the per-element level to identify the SPECIFIC widget causing the 0.42 main shift
2. Check if any of these widgets can be reserved-space (height pre-allocated via CSS) before they inject content
3. Klaviyo + LoyaltyLion specifically: their popup/notification timing — can we delay until after first paint stabilizes?
4. Cookie banner: where is it injected from? Should always reserve space.

Worth ~2-3hr of dedicated investigation. Likely needs Chrome DevTools Performance tab to see which JS triggers each widget injection.
notes:
2026-05-04: 0u4 (cookie banner) + 3uw (BOGOS) both shipped today (commits fca4725, 3cb98f2). Only remaining blocker is b5f (Reamaze chat shift). GA4 re-check scheduled 2026-05-09 to measure cumulative #MainContent CLS impact of all fixes. If #MainContent CLS drops significantly by 2026-05-09, close e4a. If not, investigate b5f + Reamaze.
Closed 2026-05-09 (per the May 4 condition: 'If #MainContent CLS drops significantly by 2026-05-09, close e4a.'). Current state: CrUX mobile CLS p75 dropped from 0.30 → 0.07 (POOR → GOOD). GA4 RUM 7d CLS good% = 89.32%. The cumulative impact of cookie banner reserve (0u4 fca4725), BOGOS (3uw 3cb98f2), Reamaze widget label (existing 7fz), Klaviyo+LoyaltyLion overlays (CLS sprint), and Shop Sheriff PWA (osj) was the win. Desktop CLS still 0.20 — separate follow-up filed as kt0.

title: Elevar /a/elevar tracking endpoint takes 2.6s on every PSI run
description:
2026-04-28 PSI diagnostic showed /a/elevar (Elevar's GTM-proxy / event-collection endpoint at the shop's own domain) consistently taking 2,634ms — the single biggest network-time hog on the homepage.

Elevar proxies tracking events through the shop's own domain (under /a/elevar) to bypass ad-blockers and 3rd-party-cookie restrictions. Slow response from this endpoint adds to TBT (because subsequent JS waits on the response) and overall page load time.

Diagnostic data (/tmp/psi-result.json from 2026-04-28 morning):
  - URL pattern: /a/elevar?source_url=...
  - Method: Fetch
  - Time: 2,634ms
  - Transfer size: 1KB

Next steps:
1. Open Elevar dashboard → check event-collection latency metrics. Compare HairMNL vs Elevar's average client.
2. Email Elevar support: 'Why is /a/elevar consistently 2.6s on PSI? Can we narrow this?' Include the diagnostic data.
3. Consider whether the Elevar proxy is necessary, or if direct GTM (without Elevar's proxy) would be faster. Trade-off: ad-blockers will block direct GTM more, losing tracking signal.
4. If Elevar can't speed up: defer the Elevar script load further, or fire events on requestIdleCallback only (not on page load).

Admin / vendor-side work, not theme code. ~30 min initial investigation.
notes:
2026-05-01 investigation: Elevar loads via Shopify app embed (content_for_header) as <script type='module' dynamic>. Three chained awaits: config.js → handler JS → /a/elevar tracking endpoint (the 2.6s call). Since type=module is inherently deferred, this does NOT block HTML parsing or rendering — the 2.6s is Elevar's server response time, not a theme-side blocking issue. No theme code change possible. Action items: (1) Check Elevar admin for deferred/idle firing mode; (2) Email Elevar support with the diagnostic data — 2,634ms is far above acceptable latency; (3) If Elevar can't fix: consider firing GTM's Elevar tags on requestIdleCallback via GTM trigger config. Reassigning to admin/vendor track.
2026-05-26 full investigation. Elevar app embed is <script type='module' dynamic> in <body> (position 202007, body starts at 92845). type=module is deferred by spec — never blocks parsing. The /a/elevar?source_url= call (2.6s server latency) is a fire-and-forget fetch from within the module; it does not hold the main thread. Today PSI shows endTime=0ms for all Elevar requests — LH trace closed before Elevar's server responded, confirming it fires well outside the LCP window. TBT contribution: none. No theme code change is possible. Action items are operator/vendor: (1) Check Elevar admin for idle/deferred fire mode. (2) Email Elevar support with the 2.6s diagnostic — far above acceptable server latency. (3) GTM idle trigger option — needs coordinator review before touching workspaces 197/190. Closing as vendor-side.

title: Cloudflare bot challenge fires on rapid add-to-cart sequences (admin-side, not theme)
description:
Reported 2026-04-28 night: shoppers adding several items rapidly trigger Cloudflare's bot/rate-limit verification screen. Once challenged, /cart/add.js POSTs may be blocked or delayed, and theme.js asset loading may also be affected, leaving the cart drawer in a broken state.

This is NOT a theme code issue — Cloudflare is rate-limiting based on request volume per IP per window. The theme can mitigate the post-challenge UX (see hairmnl-theme-dc2 follow-up: drawer-toggle intercept rewritten to be self-contained, no longer depends on theme.js binding handlers), but cannot prevent the challenge itself.

Admin-side fixes to investigate (Shopify admin → Online Store → Domains → Cloudflare config, or via Cloudflare dashboard if account-managed):
1. Whitelist /cart/add.js, /cart/change.js, /cart.js endpoints from bot detection — these are AJAX endpoints that legitimate users hit several times per session.
2. Reduce 'Bot Fight Mode' or 'Under Attack Mode' sensitivity for those paths.
3. Set up a Page Rule that bypasses the WAF for these paths.
4. Consider increasing the rate-limit threshold or adjusting the window.

Theme-side mitigations already shipped today:
- Self-contained drawer-toggle intercept (so cart click still works even if theme.js loading is throttled)
- Liquid-side conditional render of cart message (no JS dependency for correct initial render)

Theme-side mitigations NOT shipped (would require build):
- Client-side throttle on add-to-cart button (e.g., disable for 500 ms after click). Risk: shoppers click multiple times thinking nothing happened, worsens UX.
- Optimistic UI updates that don't depend on /cart.js after each add. Possible but invasive.

Worth a 30-min admin session to check Cloudflare config.

title: Cart drawer dual-message bug: success + default both render when subtotal >= free-shipping threshold
description:
When cart subtotal qualifies for free shipping, the drawer's promo banner shows BOTH .cart__message-success ('Congratulations! Your order qualifies for free shipping') AND .cart__message-default ('Spend ₱-1,150 more...') simultaneously, concatenated as one long line. Expected: only .cart__message-success visible per cart.css line 43-45 rule: .cart__message.is-success .cart__message-success ~ .cart__message-default { display: none } Observed (2026-04-28 night, hotfix-confirmed-shipped state): both visible. Likely cause: the AJAX drawer-rerender path at theme.js:5205 (renderPopdown) replaces drawer.innerHTML with freshly-rendered Liquid fetched via ?section_id=api-product-popdown. The fresh fragment may render with class_message='' (cart state mismatch with Liquid render context) OR the freeShippingMessageHandle JS listener (theme.js:1737) doesn't re-fire after innerHTML swap. Diagnostic next steps: 1. Use Chrome MCP to inspect live DOM right after drawer opens — confirm whether parent

has is-success class 2. If is-success missing: check Liquid render context for the popdown section (cart state availability) 3. If is-success present: check for higher-specificity override or inline style 4. Consider robust fix: change cart-drawer.liquid lines 105-111 to conditionally render ONLY ONE span based on class_message, eliminating the CSS-toggle dependency. Note: this loses dynamic JS update; may need re-trigger of CartShippingMessage class on AJAX rerender.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Resolve duplicate sale labels on product cards (product__badge--custom vs product-labels system)
description: Pre-existing tension found during Judge.me draft smoke test 2026-04-27. Some product cards (e.g., AVEDA Nutriplenish on /collections/aveda or homepage) render TWO sale labels when compare_at_price > price: (1) the original .product__badge.product__badge--custom red ribbon with text like '10% Off' (custom per-product badge from theme system, possibly metafield-driven), and (2) the new .product-label.product-label--sale auto-computed '-10%' from snippets/product-labels.liquid (added in commit 06fd1d8 to replace BSS Product Labels). Both fire when product is on sale. Decide: (a) remove the auto-computed sale label from product-labels.liquid (keeping it only for tag-based labels and Sold Out), letting the existing custom badge handle sale display; (b) remove the custom badge system entirely, migrate any per-product custom text to label:* tags, let product-labels handle everything; (c) keep both but visually de-duplicate via CSS so only one shows when both fire. Currently visible on draft theme 140785582179 only because draft was just synced; live theme renders only the custom badge because live's product-grid-item-branded.liquid is older and doesn't include the {% render 'product-labels' %} call.
notes:
UPDATE 2026-05-02 (spz auto-mode review): Bug is already prevented in code — all three product-grid-item variants (product-grid-item.liquid:260-261, product-grid-item-branded.liquid:173-174, product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid:159-160) have {% render 'product-labels' %} commented out as of 2026-04-27. Live + draft both render only product__badge--custom; no duplicate possible. Remaining work is a TEAM/ADMIN decision (not theme code):

(B-migrate path) ~30 min Shopify bulk edit:
  1. Bulk-edit products currently using _badge_<text> tag pattern → change to label:<text> tag pattern
  2. For sale products: rely on auto -X% from product-labels.liquid (no per-product tag needed)
  3. Uncomment {% render 'product-labels', product: product %} in all three grid-item variants
  4. Delete custom_badge logic from product-grid-item.liquid:43-52 and the elsif branch at line 68
  5. CSS cleanup: drop .product__badge--custom rules

(Won't-fix path) Keep current state. Founders/marketing keep free-form per-product badge text (e.g. '10% Off', 'free gift', 'sale', 'limited'). Lose auto-compute -X%. BSS Product Labels app already removed; perf win retained either way.

DEFER until founders pick a path. Not blocking shipped perf work.

title: Investigate top field-CLS shift targets via GA4 web-vitals attribution (real-shopper data)
description: Lab CLS today is 0.047 (GOOD) but field CrUX CLS p75 is 0.30 (POOR, 3x threshold). Lab/field divergence means CLS is happening post-load (interaction-driven, not initial paint). Specific fix in y89 (hover redraw) is shipped — accounts for ~0.05 of the 0.30. Remaining 0.25 needs attribution data we are already capturing. The web-vitals v4 reporter (snippets/web-vitals-reporter.liquid, shipped commit 367f920) sends largestShiftTarget per CLS event to GA4 with debug_target param. title: Vertex AI Search for Commerce — replace LimeSpot (4-6 week build, separate session)
description: Replace LimeSpot ($4,836/yr) with Google Vertex AI Search for Commerce (~$50-150/mo + $600 free credit covers first 6mo). Net savings $3,000-4,200/yr. Direct replacement, no A/B test (decided 2026-04-27). Build phases: (1) GCP setup + billing + service accounts. (2) Catalog sync (Cloud Function: Shopify products + brand metafields + per-brand profit margins → Vertex catalog, daily + webhook). (3) Event tracking (page views, add-to-cart, purchase → Vertex events, real-time, deferred analytics). (4) Server-rendered Liquid widgets on Homepage / PDP (FBT + YMAL) / Cart (Complete the Routine). Profit re-rank in Liquid: score × (1 + brand_margin_pct/100). (5) Cutover: disable LimeSpot widgets, ship Vertex, monitor RPV/AOV/CVR/mobile-TBT 14 days, instant rollback by re-enabling LimeSpot. (6) After 14 clean days, uninstall LimeSpot. Performance non-negotiables: server-side render (zero JS to display), <100ms p95 predict latency via caching, <50ms TBT impact, mobile TBT lower than LimeSpot baseline. Build in a fresh Claude Code session with the prompt already prepared.
notes: Status update 2026-05-09: per bd memory 'vertex-project-state' (and the May 3 modernization deck Slide 13 + 22), Phases 0-3 are LIVE on the draft theme. PR-1 universal foundation + PR-2 cart-page FBT + PR-3 cart-drawer FBT + PR-4 PDP YMAL + PR-5 Home RFY + PR-6 Slot 3 home RFY all shipped, with 3 rounds of team-review feedback applied (header pin, drawer nav, cart inline ADD, font inheritance). Remaining: Phase 4 catalog sync, Phase 5 event tracking, Phase 6 cutover with 14-day monitor. Phase 4-6 gated on user Decision 2 (founder approval) before live cutover.

title: Judge.me admin: enable Lite mode + reduce reviews-per-page (web team)
description: Diagnosed as dominant mobile TBT blocker (~5,000ms script eval on widget_*.js files). Hand off to web team for in-app optimization. Steps: Judge.me admin → Settings → Customization → enable Lite/Performance mode if available; disable unused widgets (Q&A, Photo Reviews, FAQ); reduce reviews-per-page to 5-10 with pagination; limit which page templates show widgets. If TBT contribution still >1500ms after these tweaks, escalate to Judge.me support requesting defer/lazy options.
notes:
ADMIN UPDATE COMPLETE 2026-05-11T12:20:48Z — web team executed Judge.me admin tweaks (HairMNL 90-Day Modernization Roadmap).

Status of original 4 steps from description:

1. ✅ Lite / Performance mode toggle — DONE.
   BONUS: Review Widget upgraded to the new mobile-first widget at the same time.

2. ✅ Disable unused widgets (Q&A, Photo Reviews, FAQ block, etc.) — DONE.

3. ✅ Reduce reviews-per-page to 5-10 with pagination — DONE.

4. ✅ Limit page templates that load widgets — DONE.

Active widget footprint POST-tweaks:
- Product page: Review widget, Write a review, Review snippets
- Website footer: Judge.me Medals (sitewide)
- Review page: All Reviews Counter
- Home Page: Reviews Carousel (Classic)

Pages NO LONGER loading Judge.me widgets (per the 'limit templates' fix):
- Cart page (was previously loading the snippet widget; the cart-page Judge.me CLS attribution should drop)
- Blog pages (was loading via theme footer; medals still load via separate footer block, but the heavier widget JS no longer initializes on blog templates)
- Account pages
- Other non-product, non-home templates

Expected perf impact (verifiable 24-48h post-deploy via GA4 RUM dashboard):

LCP — moderate improvement
- Blog template top_pages.LCP should drop. Existing residual blog LCP (best-hair-color-for-morenas 2.8s, purple-vs-blue-shampoo-philippines 2.9s — open in bd c2k Phase C) was partly attributable to Judge.me widget_*.js script eval blocking the LCP frame; removing those widgets from blog templates removes that blocker.
- Cart page LCP should also improve marginally.

INP / TBT — likely the biggest win
- Original diagnosis attributed ~5000ms of mobile TBT to Judge.me widget_*.js. Lite mode + mobile-first widget upgrade + template limiting should collectively cut this 50-80%.
- top_inp_targets — Judge.me selectors (jdgm-rev-widg, jdgm-rev-snippet, jdgm-paginate) should drop in rank or fall off the top 10.
- PDP INP — the kerastase-blond-absolu-purple-shampoo 290ms p75 (z9y target, already addressed by gallery yield) should see further reduction since Judge.me scripts won't compete with the gallery zoom handler for main-thread time.

CLS — small additional protection
- Reviews-per-page reduction (down from previously-larger count to 5-10) means fewer DOM nodes injected by Judge.me's hydration on Product page. The existing z9y Phase A jdgm-prev-badge reservation (24px) is unchanged and continues to handle the top-of-PDP CLS.
- Cart page CLS — the cart-page Judge.me widget removal removes an injection source that contributed to cart-page CLS attribution.

Combined with today's earlier shipped fixes, this brings the total CWV-improving changes for the day to:
- z9y Phase A (Judge.me preview badge reservation on PDP)
- z9y Phase B (PhotoSwipe gallery zoom INP yield)
- ojx (pro-blogger thumbnail aspect-ratio reservation)
- c2k Phase A (already shipped via prior _page_content_lazyiframe work; verified no-op needed)
- ff1ef80 hotfix (cart drawer overlay regression unblock)
- LimeSpot PDP items reduced to 8 (fw8 partial — Fix #2 of 4)
- Judge.me admin tweaks (this issue — 4 of 4 steps done)

When attributing CWV metric movement over the next 24-48h, the right mental model is 'these seven changes stack' — not any single one.

Closing per AC if perf verification shows expected delta. If TBT contribution still >1500ms 7d post-deploy (per original escalation threshold), reopen and escalate to Judge.me support requesting defer/lazy SDK loading options.

title: [search-page] LCP app+bundle-bound (Searchanise client render); CLS resolved by 18oi
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-06-01 (CC/Opus). /pages/search-results-page (Searchanise, #snize_results) was the last GSC item: mobile LCP 2.7s (1 NI URL), desktop LCP 3.2s + CLS 0.11.

CLS: RESOLVED by 18oi (today's cart-drawer/header-collapse critical-CSS fix). Re-traced w/ consent suppressed: mobile CLS 0.0428, desktop 0.0592 (both good, was 0.11 desktop). The search page shared the header-collapse shift; 18oi fixed it. GSC will clear as CrUX 28-day rolls. Prior yblz 2026-05-25 work (preconnect + #snize_results min-height reserve) handles the skeleton->results CLS.

LCP: APP + BUNDLE BOUND, not theme-codable. Page chrome paints fast (FCP ~680ms) but the LCP element is a Searchanise RESULT IMAGE (.snize-item-image) that renders client-side: Searchanise init.js (deferred, //www.searchanise.com/widgets/shopify/init.js?a=7K0f1C4k5q) loads after the ~1MB theme-JS bundle, fetches results from the Searchanise API, then builds the cards -> result image paints at 6s (desktop) / 15s (mobile) lab. Two compounding causes: (1) theme.js bundle saturation delaying init.js execution (same blocker as INP/blog-LCP qx6x/01vl -> migration 2i8b); (2) Searchanise's own client-side fetch+render latency (app). Theme already did preconnect (yblz). The .snize-item-image markup is injected by Searchanise -> theme cannot set fetchpriority/prerender it. Admin levers: check Searchanise for SSR/result-prerender or image-priority settings. NOT worth bespoke theme code for 1 GSC URL. Recommend: defer to migration (bundle) + optional Searchanise admin config.

title: Review/remove 88gw owl-carousel defer guard — no-op for PB carousels (display:none pre-init)
description:
Discovered during rzai fix (2026-05-28): 88gw (Pro Blogger owl-carousel defer via IntersectionObserver) is a no-op for its intended purpose and was never functionally verified when shipped.

WHY IT NO-OPS: owl.carousel.min.css sets `.owl-carousel{display:none}` until `.owl-loaded`. So PRE-init, every PB carousel is display:none -> getBoundingClientRect() returns all-zero -> rect.top=0 -> 88gw's `if (rect.top < innerHeight*1.5) return _orig.apply()` immediate-init branch ALWAYS taken. The IntersectionObserver deferral path is never reached. Net: 88gw patches owlCarousel then immediately inits anyway = zero TBT benefit.

(This is also why 88gw is HARMLESS post-rzai: it just inits carousels normally. Verified via Playwright: carousels render HORIZONTAL + loaded with 88gw present.)

ALSO: 88gw was pushed 2026-05-27 without a functional smoke test (Chrome MCP couldn't render the slow article page at the time; only script-presence was confirmed).

OPTIONS:
1. Remove 88gw entirely (cleanest — it does nothing useful). Low risk.
2. Re-implement deferral with a display:none-aware approach: observe the carousel's PARENT (not display:none) or gate on `el.offsetParent !== null` / computed display, so below-fold carousels actually defer. Only worth it if the ~250-500ms TBT saving is confirmed real on a properly-rendered article page first.

RECOMMENDATION: Option 1 (remove) unless a measured article-page TBT win justifies re-implementing. Verify any re-implementation with Playwright geometry + TBT before/after.

stream:both (shared guard). P3 — no live impact (current state is harmless no-op).

title: Footer section CLS: 55 desktop poor events/7d (debug_target=#shopify-section-footer)
description:
## Signal

GA4 RUM 7d (2026-05-24): #shopify-section-footer is the 4th-highest specific CLS debug_target on desktop with 55 poor events/7d.

Surfaced during the c48t/etp3/fxh9 CLS drill-down. Not previously tracked.

## Likely causes (to investigate)

- **A: LimeSpot slider injecting below footer** — oo57 fixed this for blog article pages (MutationObserver moves limespot-container before footer). Unclear if same injection is happening on other page types (homepage, collection, product pages).
- **B: Newsletter/email capture form expanding** — footer often contains Klaviyo or similar embed that injects after render.
- **C: Cookie consent / trust badge late-inject** — app-injected content appending inside or after the footer wrapper.
- **D: Footer itself lazy-rendering** — e.g. a section or block inside the footer that renders conditionally via JS.

## Phase 1 (RO investigation)

1. Run: GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by page --rating poor --device desktop --days 7 --top 20
   Cross-reference which pages have the most footer CLS — if it concentrates on blog/article pages, likely A (LimeSpot).
2. Inspect sections/footer.liquid and snippets/ for any JS-injected or async-rendered content inside #shopify-section-footer.
3. Check if limespot-container still body-appends on non-article pages (homepage, collection, PDP).

## Phase 2 (fix)

Depends on root cause:
- If A: extend oo57's MutationObserver pattern to layout/theme.liquid (global scope) instead of only sections/article.liquid.
- If B/C: reserve min-height on the footer wrapper (similar to c48t pattern in css-overrides.liquid).
- If D: inline the footer content or use SSR.

## Out of scope

- Do NOT touch the footer's visual design or layout.
- Do NOT change LimeSpot admin settings.

## Files (estimated)

- Phase 1: 0 files (RO)
- Phase 2: 1-2 files, ≤30 LoC
notes:
## 2026-05-24 — Phase 1 investigation complete

### Phase 1 — DATA (RO investigation)

**GA4 findings:**
- Footer CLS is SITEWIDE, not page-specific. All pages show 100% poor CLS.
- Top pages by count: Homepage (106), /cart (99), blog articles (60+49+29+15). Footer specifically: 55 events — 4th-highest debug_target.
- Not concentrated on one page type.

**Cause analysis (all 4 hypotheses investigated):**

| Cause | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **A: LimeSpot** | **LIKELY** | oo57 MutationObserver exists ONLY in sections/article.liquid:292-311. Zero LimeSpot references in layout/theme.liquid. On non-article pages (home, cart, PDP, collections), no observer catches body-appended limespot-container. Blog articles dominate page distribution. |
| **B: Newsletter** | UNLIKELY | newsletter-form.liquid at footer.liquid:108 is Shopify native — server-rendered, fixed DOM, no async expansion. |
| **C: Cookie/trust badge** | PARTIALLY handled | No cookie/trust badge in footer.liquid. Nova EU Cookie Bar is body-injected app embed (css-overrides:638-645, position:fixed). Judge.me medals IS at footer.liquid:1 — has min-height 411px mobile/134px desktop + visibility:hidden + contain:layout when .jdgm-hidden (css-overrides:250-299). |
| **D: Footer lazy-render** | UNLIKELY | Zero async patterns found: no setTimeout, requestIdleCallback, IntersectionObserver, or onload. All content server-rendered Liquid. |

**Footer structure confirmed:**
- sections/footer.liquid: Judge.me medals (metafield blob → JS-hydrated after load) + <footer> wrapper (logo img with explicit width/height ✓, grid columns 1-4 blocks, newsletter-form, payment SVGs)
- No Klaviyo embed, no cookie consent, no trust badge in footer
- No async/conditional rendering in footer itself

**LimeSpot injection analysis:**
- LimeSpot Personalizer loads via Shopify content_for_header (theme.liquid:1221) — global app embed
- oo57 MutationObserver covers article pages only (sections/article.liquid:292-311)
- Non-article pages: NO OBSERVER. LimeSpot body-append causes footer-area shifts.
- oo57 is NOT sufficient — needs extension to global scope (layout/theme.liquid)

### Phase 2 recommendations (for next dispatch):
1. **Port oo57 MutationObserver from sections/article.liquid to layout/theme.liquid** (before </body>) — catches all templates. ~20-25 LoC.
2. **Add min-height reservation for limespot-container** in css-overrides.liquid. Need DevTools measurement first. ~5-10 LoC.
3. **Verify Judge.me medals min-height** (411/134px from May 2026) matches actual rendered height. Adjust if needed.

**Estimated Phase 2: 30-40 LoC, 2 files**

**Alternative (non-code):** Set Placement Sibling in LimeSpot admin → fixed-position container → no body-append → oo57 becomes no-op. Requires LimeSpot admin access — escalate to CC if preferable.
## 2026-05-24 — Phase 2 RW increment complete

### Phase 2 — Implementation

**Files changed:** 2 files, +33 LoC total (within Phase 2 estimate of 30-40 LoC)

**Fix 1 — layout/theme.liquid (+20 lines):**
Ported the oo57 MutationObserver from sections/article.liquid:292-311 to global scope. Inserted before `\u003c/body\u003e` (after the cookie consent observer, line 1556). The observer watches `document.body` for `limespot-container` elements injected by the LimeSpot app embed and moves them before `#shopify-section-footer`. Auto-disconnects after 15 seconds. The `moved` flag prevents duplicate moves, and null guards ensure no exceptions. Now fires on ALL templates (home, cart, PDP, collections, blog, article), not just article pages.

**Fix 2 — snippets/css-overrides.liquid (+13 lines):**
Added `limespot-container { display: block; min-height: 300px; }` (350px desktop at ≥768px). The selector is an element selector (no dot) — matches the custom HTML `\u003climespot-container\u003e` tag that LimeSpot injects. `display: block` is required because custom HTML elements default to `inline`, and `min-height` is ignored on inline elements.

**Combined effect:** CSS reserves space BEFORE LimeSpot injects → MutationObserver moves the container before the footer → LimeSpot fills the reserved space in-place. Zero CLS exposure.

**Article page redundancy:** The oo57 observer still exists in sections/article.liquid — the `moved` flag prevents double-move if both fire. Removing it would be out-of-scope cleanup (prohibited by hard scope rules). Harmless.

**kt0 safety:** `display: block` + `min-height` only. No contain/transform/filter/backdrop-filter/perspective/will-change.

**QA result:** PASS — 21 checks including scope (2 files, 33 LoC), correctness (proven oo57 pattern, selector consistency JS↔CSS, proper guards and cleanup), kt0 (no containing-block properties), project compliance (no theme.css edits), edge cases (empty cart, LimeSpot absent, article page double-observer redundancy harmless).

**Next:** Merge-gate: draft push both files → verify on homepage and blog article (check footer area with Slow 4G throttling, confirm no LimeSpot-induced shift) → live push → commit. Close when GA4 footer debug_target events drop below 10/7d.
MERGE_GATE_PASS 2026-05-24 | live push to 131664707683 | commit 31bd0a9 | draft verified: observer_in_page=true, css_rule confirmed | MONITORING — close when footer debug_target <10 events/7d

title: Suppress Shop Sheriff SW unhandled rejection noise in JS error reporter
description: Shop Sheriff PWA app's main.js (2020-07-22) calls reg.update() with no .catch(). When SW updates fail (iOS 7-day eviction, incognito, in-app browsers, network blips) the rejection bubbles as unhandledrejection and is captured by our error reporter. This generates ~10,649 noise events/week — 'Cannot update a null/nonexistent service worker registration' (5,680/7d) and 'Failed to update a ServiceWorker for scope' (4,969/7d). These pollute the JS error dashboard and crowd out real errors. Fix: add early-return guard in layout/theme.liquid unhandledrejection handler to skip these two known-harmless SW error patterns before calling send().
