title: Triage real JS errors once GTM l3g publishes (post-cleanup)
description:
Dashboard JS error panel currently shows 15,530 "(no message)" rows + 5,618 + 5,043 ServiceWorker errors + 2,134 "Load failed" + 1,226 "Failed to fetch" in the last 7 days. Most of the volume is junk caused by **GTM tag 776 mis-trigger** (tracked separately in bd `hairmnl-theme-l3g`, which is OPEN P1).

Once `l3g` publishes:
1. The dashboard query in `scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:503` should already capture both `js_error` and `hairmnl_js_error` event names (verify it does, per the l3g coordination plan)
2. After 7d, the `(not set)` rows age out of GA4's rolling window
3. Real error categories with populated type/message/source should emerge
4. Triage the top 3 patterns into separate bd issues (or close as third-party-not-actionable)

This issue is the "after l3g lands" follow-up — purely verification + triage, no blocking work.

Stream: `stream:a` (LIVE optimization)
Blocked by: hairmnl-theme-l3g
design:
## Phase 1 — Wait for l3g publish (coordinator owns this)

Do NOT publish GTM container yourself. Wait for operator/coordinator to:
- Publish GTM container version with tag 776 trigger fix
- Verify GA4 DebugView shows real `js_error` events with populated dimensions

## Phase 2 — Verify dashboard query

Confirm `scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:503` filters on BOTH event names:
```python
dimension_filter=_and(_in("eventName", ["js_error", "hairmnl_js_error"])),
```

If it still filters on a single name, update it per the l3g plan. Run:
```
GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py --no-psi --no-crux --render-only
```
Should exit 0; no diff in snapshots.jsonl from the render-only mode.

## Phase 3 — Synthetic error verification

In incognito + DevTools console on hairmnl.com:
```
throw new Error("verify-cleanup-2026-05-21")
```
Check GA4 DebugView within 30s for `js_error` event with:
- `error_type=Error`
- `error_message=verify-cleanup-2026-05-21`
- `error_source=<some hairmnl.com URL>`

## Phase 4 — Wait 24h, then refresh dashboard

```
GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py
```
The JS errors section should now show categorized real errors.

## Phase 5 — Triage top 3 patterns

For each of the top 3 error patterns:
- ServiceWorker "Cannot update a null/nonexistent" — likely stale SW registration, action: investigate `assets/service-worker.js` or whatever registers it
- "Load failed" / "Failed to fetch" — likely third-party scripts on flaky connections; classify and decide if actionable
- Any new genuine bugs that emerge

File a P2/P3 bd issue per actionable pattern; close-as-wontfix for confirmed third-party noise.
acceptance_criteria:
- After l3g publish + 7 days: dashboard JS errors section shows ≥3 distinct error categories with actual type/message/source values populated
- "(no message)" rows < 10% of total errors
- Top 3 patterns triaged: each is either filed as a new bd issue or noted as third-party-not-actionable in this issue's notes
- `scripts/build-perf-dashboard.py:503` confirmed to handle both event names
notes: l3g closed 2026-05-21 — fix was live in GTM v143 all along. Cross-origin (no message) errors suppressed by 8iom patch (2026-05-20). Defer triage until 2026-05-28 to allow 7 days of clean post-8iom data to accumulate in GA4. At that point run dashboard refresh and review JS errors section for real error_type/message/source data.

title: IA #11: footer section title buttons need h2 semantics
description: Per p8-ia-typography-audit.md finding #11. footer.liquid:37,56,69,84,97 — accordion section titles ('Information', 'Customer Care', etc.) are <button class="uppercase footer__title"> with no heading semantics. These ARE content navigation. Two paths: (a) wrap each button in an <h2> so heading hierarchy is present, OR (b) restructure as h2 directly with nested button-trigger and aria-controls on h2. Currently the h2-equivalent has no heading semantics. Worth doing alongside IA #6 (footer accordion ARIA) since same files.
close_reason: all 5 footer accordion buttons wrapped in <h2 class=footer__title__heading>; CSS reset in custom-theme.css prevents h2 visual scale; P8-only

title: IA #6: footer accordion aria-expanded hardcoded to 'true' (a11y bug)
description: Per p8-ia-typography-audit.md finding #6. footer.liquid lines 37,56,69,84,97 — five accordion triggers all have aria-expanded="true" as a static Liquid string. Never updated by JS when accordion toggles. Assistive tech reports accordion as always-expanded regardless of actual state. Fix path A: audit assets/theme.js for accordion toggle handler — if it doesn't update aria-expanded, add that. Fix path B: if accordions never collapse on desktop (and the design intent is non-collapsible), remove the aria-expanded attribute entirely (incorrect ARIA worse than absent ARIA). Investigate first, fix second.
close_reason: aria-expanded now derives from accordion_default setting (true if accordion-is-open, else false); JS continues to manage runtime state; footer.liquid P8-only

title: IA #4: add h2 wrappers to PDP description/tabs/pickup/sharing sections
description: Per p8-ia-typography-audit.md finding #4. sections/product.liquid renders product description (line 135), tabs (line 159), and other named blocks as <div>s with no h2 headings. After the h1 product title, the next semantic heading is Vertex AI rec rail or Judge.me reviews — gaps in between. Wrap each named PDP block in a real h2 (use <h2 class="visually-hidden"> to preserve current visual design): Description, Tabs, Sharing, Pickup, Store availability. Affects PDP a11y outline + SEO. Medium-impact; can ship post-cutover.
notes: Pushed commit 3c28b29 to P8 dev 141168312419. Added visually-hidden h2 to description/sharing/pickup blocks; promoted product__subheading div→h2 for text block. All 4 insertions verified, no smart-quote contamination.

title: OPERATOR: investigate LDT Gift Wrap template-gating in theme editor (-37KB on non-cart)
description: Follow-up from ujg6.43 audit (os2-migration/ujg6.43-app-gate-audit.md). LDT Gift Wrap is the only one of the 5 ujg6.34 app-CSS candidates that's both theme-code-gatable AND inside the HairMNL UX-availability rule. Action: open Shopify admin → Online Store → Themes → P8 dev (141168312419) → Customize → App embeds → find 'LDT Gift Wrap'. Check for a 'load on specific templates' or 'template restrictions' toggle. If present, restrict to 'cart' only. If not present, app doesn't support template gating from the embed block — leave as-is and close this ticket as won't-do. Expected savings: 37KB CSS on every non-cart template (homepage, PDP, collection, search) on Brand-mobile baseline. ~10 minutes operator time. Verify post-change via Lighthouse Coverage on the dev theme — ldt-gift-wrap CSS should disappear from non-cart waterfalls.

title: Critical-css restructure (Path B) — per-template Coverage audit + properly grouped split
description:
Follow-up to ujg6.58 (closed won't-do because byte-position bucketing was the wrong methodology).

Path B approach:
1) CC runs Chrome DevTools Coverage tool against each of 5 templates (home, collection, pdp, cart, brand) at desktop + mobile = 10 captures.
2) For each capture, extract the list of CSS selectors that the page ACTUALLY used (not just loaded).
3) Cross-reference across 10 captures to build a class → template-usage map:
   - "Universal" = used on ≥4 of 5 templates (always inline)
   - "Per-template" = used on 1-2 templates (gate via Liquid)
   - "Unused" = never used in any capture (delete)
4) Re-restructure snippets/critical-css.liquid using empirically-grounded grouping.
5) Re-dispatch to OC if needed for bulk regrouping, OR keep on CC if scope is small.

EFFORT:
  ~2 hours CC Sonnet/Medium for Coverage captures + class-mapping script
  ~1 day OC paid for bulk regrouping (or CC Sonnet/Medium ~3hr if scope <250 LoC)

PRIORITY: P3 — post-cutover. Not blocking.

PREREQUISITE FOR NEXT ATTEMPT:
  - Empirical class→template map saved as os2-migration/critical-css-template-map.md
  - Acceptance criteria stated in terms of OBSERVED universal-class set, not byte targets

REFERENCE: bd ujg6.58 closeout notes (the original byte-bucket design was flawed; this ticket fixes the methodology).
notes:
Phase 1 investigation complete 2026-05-27 (Opus 4.7). 

Method: Instead of Chrome DevTools Coverage (browser was overwhelmed by slow third-party scripts), used curl + Python BeautifulSoup/soupsieve to fetch 7 templates' static HTML and run querySelectorAll equivalence for each of the 430 critical-css selectors.

Templates audited: home, collection, pdp, cart, brand, article, search

Coverage results:
  - Universal (matches ≥5/7): 181 selectors (42%) — keep always inline
  - Per-template (matches 1-4/7): 174 selectors (40%) — gate via Liquid template.name
  - Unused (matches 0/7): 48 selectors (11%) — removal candidates
  - Errored (pseudo-element selectors soupsieve can't query): 27 (6%) — keep inline conservatively

Top per-template clusters:
  - 51 selectors only on pdp
  - 33 selectors only on collection  
  - 17 selectors on brand+cart+home+pdp (4-of-7 — could be treated as universal)
  - 13 selectors only on article
  - 12 selectors on brand+collection+home
  - 9 selectors only on home

Estimated impact:
  - Unused removal: ~4.3KB / ~10% of current critical CSS
  - Per-template gating: universal block shrinks 430 → ~210 selectors (~50%)
  - Combined: critical-css.liquid universal block ~20-25KB (down from 39.6KB)

Caveat on unused list: includes selectors like .flickity-enabled, .swiper-button-prev.swiper-button-disabled, .is-selected that ARE used after JS state changes but don't match initial static HTML. Safe to remove from CRITICAL CSS because they don't affect initial paint — full theme.css (deferred) retains them.

Full map + per-template selector lists + implementation plan saved to: os2-migration/critical-css-template-map.md (17.7KB).

Next phase: actual restructure of critical-css.liquid using the map. Estimated 2-4 hours of focused work + smoke testing across 7 templates. Worth a separate session — could be CC Sonnet 4.6 / Medium if scoped tightly, or offloaded to OC for the bulk rule-bucketing.
Phase 2a + 2b shipped 2026-05-27 (Opus 4.7).

PHASE 2A — Remove 48 unused selectors (safest delta):
- Source: 39,564 → 35,119 bytes (-4,445 / -11%)
- 48 rules removed where ALL selectors had zero matches in static HTML across 7 audited templates
- Shipped live (131664707683) + draft (140785582179) + P8 (141168312419)
- Verified via Shopify CLI pull: deployed bytes match expected

PHASE 2B — Gate 46 PDP-only rules behind {% if template.name == 'product' %}:
- Source: 35,119 → 35,169 bytes (+50 Liquid tag overhead)
- 46 rules gated (3,768 bytes of CSS)
- 5 selectors from the 51 audit-identified PDP-only list kept universal as generic utilities: input[type=number], select, option, .hide, .medium-up--one-half
- Expected impact: non-PDP critical CSS drops 35,119 → 31,351 bytes (-3,768 / -10.7% on every non-PDP page)
- PDP critical CSS unchanged
- Shopify CLI pull confirms live theme has the Liquid gate (35,169 bytes)

VERIFICATION STATUS (Phase 2b):
- Source-level: ✅ verified (file on live theme has correct Liquid syntax)
- Rendered HTML: ⚠️ blocked on Shopify CDN cache. Background poll waiting for CDN to expire and serve fresh HTML.
- Plan: confirm gate works as expected via fresh CDN HTML, then proceed to Phase 2c (collection-only gating, 33 selectors).

Cumulative savings (when CDN refreshes):
- Non-PDP pages: 39,564 → 31,351 bytes (-8,213 / -21% from original)
- PDP pages: 39,564 → 35,119 bytes (-4,445 / -11% from original)
Phase 2b VERIFIED via Playwright 2026-05-27 (Opus 4.7).

CDN finally propagated. Playwright bypassed cache and got fresh HTML for all 7 templates.

VERIFICATION RESULTS (Phase 2b gate):
| Template | critical-css size | has PDP rules | gate result |
| home | 31,351 b | NO | FALSE ✓ |
| collection | 31,351 b | NO | FALSE ✓ |
| pdp | 35,119 b | YES | TRUE ✓ |
| cart | 31,351 b | NO | FALSE ✓ |
| brand | 31,351 b | NO | FALSE ✓ |
| article | 31,351 b | NO | FALSE ✓ |
| search | 31,351 b | NO | FALSE ✓ |

Visual smoke test (Playwright screenshots at /tmp/playwright-smoke/):
- All 7 templates render correctly above the fold
- PDP particularly validates the gate: ADD TO CART button, quantity selector, variant picker, price formatting all properly styled (these depend on PDP-gated rules)
- No visual regressions detected

JS errors observed are all pre-existing and unrelated:
- ServiceWorker registration quirk (Shopify infrastructure)
- Vertex CSS 404s (existing issue tracked elsewhere)

Phase 2b CONFIRMED working end-to-end. Live PDP critical CSS: 35,119 b. Live non-PDP critical CSS: 31,351 b (saves 8,213 bytes vs original 39,564 — that's a 21% reduction on every non-PDP page).

Cumulative ujg6.59 progress (Phase 2a + 2b combined):
- Non-PDP pages: 39,564 → 31,351 bytes (-8,213 / -21%)
- PDP pages: 39,564 → 35,119 bytes (-4,445 / -11%)

NEXT: Phase 2c (gate 33 collection-only selectors). Same pattern. Expected savings: another ~3KB on non-collection pages.
Phase 2c pushed 2026-05-27 — awaiting CDN refresh for Playwright verification.

Phase 2c gates 26 rules (1,885 bytes) behind {% if template.name == 'collection' %}. Brand pages (which are also template.name='collection') inherit the gate too — verified via audit cluster analysis (37 selectors exclusive to collection+brand templates, of which 26 are in pure rules eligible for gating).

Deployed draft file verified at 35,222 bytes with BOTH gates present at correct offsets:
- Collection gate at 29466-31404 (1,885 bytes inner)
- PDP gate at 31404-35222 (3,768 bytes inner)
- Universal section: 29,466 bytes

Projected rendered sizes (when CDN refreshes):
- Home/cart/article/search: 29,466 b (universal only)
- Collection/brand:         31,351 b (universal + collection gate)
- PDP:                      33,234 b (universal + PDP gate)

Polling for CDN refresh in background. Once home drops below 30KB, will re-run Playwright across all 7 templates.
2026-05-29: Phase 2b (PDP gate) + 2c (collection gate) REVERTED — they caused a LIVE cart-stepper regression (bd 9lyw): the static-HTML coverage probe was blind to the JS-populated cart drawer/line-items, so it mis-classified .quantity__* layout selectors as PDP-only and gated them, and the .quantity__wrapper{display:flex} rule is critical-CSS-only (no theme.css fallback) = hard break on cart. critical-css.liquid reverted to post-2a (35,119b, ungated). Phase 2a (48 unused removals) retained + re-audited safe. ANY future per-template critical-CSS split MUST use a DYNAMIC-state coverage capture (cart drawer open + populated, predictive search, FBT, quick-add) — static HTML is insufficient. Recommend leaving critical CSS ungated (post-2a) unless a dynamic-capture methodology is built.

title: ujg6.42 follow-up: verify-and-remove _unused CSS bucket (~225KB sitewide savings)
description:
After ujg6.42 Wave C lands and the SAFE split is verified stable on live, run a dedicated FOUC + interaction-state verification pass on the _unused bucket (2,918 rules / 225 KB across theme.css + custom-theme.css).

Per Wave A's coverage analysis (docs/ujg6.42-coverage-analysis.md), the _unused bucket captures CSS rules that real Chrome Coverage measured as untouched during page load + scroll + cart-icon-hover. They MIGHT still be needed for:
- Form validation error states (.has-error, .errors)
- Drawer / modal open states
- Toast/notification states
- Loading state classes (.btn-state-loading)
- Some :focus-visible rules

Process:
1. List rules in the _unused bucket (from docs/ujg6.42-buckets.json under '_unused' key in each file).
2. Categorize by selector pattern (state-class vs static).
3. For state-class patterns, manually trigger each on dev theme + verify FOUC absence (e.g. submit invalid form, open cart drawer, etc).
4. Delete confirmed-dead rules in batch.
5. Leave behaviorally-tested state-class rules in core.

Expected payoff: 225 KB off every pageview if even half the _unused bucket is genuinely dead. Higher-leverage than the SAFE split but riskier.

## Hard requirements
- Don't dispatch until Wave C is live AND has soaked for >7 days.
- Don't auto-delete based on Coverage data alone — manual FOUC verification is mandatory for state-class candidates.

## Model recommendation
Opus 4.7 / High — judgment-heavy categorization + manual verification orchestration.

title: Investigate: is Pro Blogger CSS/JS needed on collection pages?
description: Per ujg6.33 audit Quick Win #9. layout/theme.liquid lines 889-940 + section render at line 146 add Pro Blogger CSS defer guard + section JS. Pro Blogger is a blog-articles app. Determine if collection pages use Pro Blogger features (e.g., related-articles widget, blog teasers). If not, gate to blog/article templates only. Investigation output: short markdown report; then file targeted gating bd if confirmed unused on collections.
notes:
# ujg6.52 investigation — Pro Blogger on collection pages

## Findings

### Theme-code surface area (12 files)
- `snippets/pro-blogger-section-wrapper.liquid` → only entry point in theme code (included at `layout/theme.liquid:235`). Includes 2 sub-snippets, BOTH gated to `template.name == 'article'`. On non-article templates: outputs nothing.
- 11 other `pro-blogger.snippet.*` files: only referenced by each other or via `{% include %}` from article-template sections. Zero callsites in collection sections, search sections, cart sections.
- Two CSS files in assets: `problogger.css`, `problogger-custom.css`. NEITHER is `<link>`-included in any theme file.

**Conclusion: from the theme's perspective, Pro Blogger emits zero output on collection pages.**

### App-side injection (the actual cost)
Pro Blogger CSS is injected by the Shopify app via `content_for_header` on **every page** regardless of template. Theme.liquid:1017-1068 catches these `<link>` tags (PATTERNS `problogger.css`, `owl.carousel`) and defers them via `media=print` + `onload` swap.

The defer guard runs on every page and has two ongoing costs:
1. `MutationObserver` on `document.documentElement` with `childList:true, subtree:true` — fires on every DOM mutation page-wide (collection pages have many: filter UI, sort, lazysizes image swaps, Flickity init, etc.)
2. Never disconnected — observer runs for full page lifetime
3. `document.createElement` override wraps every dynamic `<link>` creation sitewide

## Recommendation

**File targeted gating bd (now confirmed): three layered fixes.**

| Fix | Risk | Effort | Win |
|---|---|---|---|
| Scope MutationObserver to `<head>` (not document) | Low | 1 line | Eliminates per-mutation cost outside head |
| Disconnect observer at `DOMContentLoaded` | Low | 3 lines | Eliminates ongoing cost after parse |
| **Block** (not defer) Pro Blogger CSS on non-{article,blog,product} templates | Low-Med | ~10 lines | Saves ~40-60KB transfer on cart/collection/home/search |

The third fix is what the bd really asks for — but the first two are bonus quick wins on the same code path and worth bundling.

title: JS errors dashboard noise: filter cross-origin '(no message)' errors in theme handler
description:
## Symptom

GA4 dashboard JS errors section shows 28,010 events per 7-day window with:
- type: 'error'
- message: '(no message)' (i.e. empty)
- source: ':0:0'

These swamp the real error data and make the dashboard's 'JS errors' section
useless for finding real bugs.

## Root cause (NOT a GTM bug — verified 2026-05-19)

The theme's error handler at layout/theme.liquid:682-690 catches all
window.onerror events including those from cross-origin scripts. Per
browser CORS spec, when a script throws an error from a cross-origin source
without proper crossorigin attribute + CORS headers, the browser redacts:
- e.error → null
- e.message → 'Script error.' or empty
- e.filename → empty
- e.lineno / e.colno → 0

The handler still forwards these to dataLayer with error_type='error',
error_message='', error_source=':0:0'. That populates GA4 with 28K
useless events per week, drowning out real first-party errors.

Note: GTM tag 776 (GA4 - JS Error Event) is correctly configured in live
V143 (published 2026-05-12). Trigger 772 (Custom Event - hairmnl_js_error),
DLV mappings, eventSettingsTable all correct. This is purely a theme-side
handler issue.

## Fix

At layout/theme.liquid ~line 682, in the addEventListener('error', ...)
handler, skip pushing to dataLayer when the error is the cross-origin
redacted pattern:

  addEventListener('error', function (e) {
    var msg = String(e.message || '');
    var src = (e.filename || '');
    // Cross-origin redacted error: browser blocks details for privacy.
    // No actionable info; skip rather than spam GA4.
    if (!e.error && (msg === '' || msg === 'Script error.') && !src) {
      return;
    }
    var t = (e.error && e.error.name) ? e.error.name : 'error';
    var srcStr = src + ':' + (e.lineno || 0) + ':' + (e.colno || 0);
    send(t, e.message, srcStr);
  }, { capture: true });

## Acceptance

1. After deploy, 7-day GA4 js_error event volume drops by ~28K
2. Remaining js_error events have populated error_type, error_message,
   error_source (real bugs visible)
3. No regression in first-party error capture (theme-side test:
   force a TypeError, verify it still surfaces in GA4 DebugView)
4. Dashboard 'JS errors' section becomes useful (shows real bugs, not '(no message)')

## Scope

1 file, ~10 LoC. Phase: stream:a (live perf/observability).
acceptance_criteria: See description § Acceptance
notes: CLOSED 2026-05-20. Commit f59a068 (rebased from 2e498bf). 7-line early-return guard added to addEventListener('error') handler in layout/theme.liquid. Condition: !e.error && (msg==='' || msg==='Script error.') && !e.filename. Pushed to P6 live (131664707683) and P8 dev (141168312419). git show --stat: layout/theme.liquid | 7 +++++++, 1 file changed, 7 insertions(+). Expect ~28K/week GA4 noise to drop to near-zero within 24h. Follow-up: check GA4 js_error event volume in 7 days.
close_reason: shipped to both P6 live and P8 dev; f59a068

title: P8.B2-fu: Wire scripts/smoke-tests/ into Lighthouse CI workflow + GitHub Actions
description:
Follow-up to the Playwright behavioral smoke suite (commit landed alongside scripts/pre-cutover-smoke.sh).

## Goal

Add the Playwright suite to a GitHub Actions workflow so it runs on every PR (gating merges) and also on a schedule (catch dev-theme drift since smoke tests are mostly orthogonal to git state).

## Steps

1. Coordinate with ujg6.23 (Lighthouse CI workflow) — the file .github/workflows/lighthouse-ci.yml that's spec'd there should be EXTENDED to include this Playwright suite rather than a separate workflow file (single CI run, parallel jobs).
2. Add a repo secret SHOPIFY_AUTH with the contents of .auth/storageState.json (operator-side: capture once via `npx playwright codegen` after Shopify admin login).
3. Workflow:
   - Trigger: pull_request + scheduled (e.g., daily at midnight PHT)
   - Job matrix: project={mobile, desktop} × smoke-spec={01..07}
   - Setup: npm install + npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
   - Run: npm test (with SHOPIFY_AUTH env from secret)
   - Upload: playwright-report/ artifact on failure for debugging
4. PR comment summary: pass/fail per smoke check (probably via a Marketplace action or simple JSON parse + comment write)

## Constraints

- Don't fail the workflow on Reamaze-CDN-unavailable issues (smoke 4 has a known flakiness vector on third-party backend availability). Mark those tests as `test.skip` if CDN unreachable.
- Auth cookie expires periodically — workflow should print a useful error pointing at re-running auth capture when storageState fails.

## Acceptance

- .github/workflows/lighthouse-ci.yml extended (or .github/workflows/smoke-tests.yml created)
- Workflow runs successfully against a no-op PR (passes all 34 tests on the current dev theme state)
- Workflow runs successfully on a deliberate-regression PR (fails the relevant smoke test)
- PR summary comment displays pass/fail per smoke check

## Out of scope

- Visual regression (screenshot-diff) — separate ticket if wanted
- Reamaze chat-message-delivery testing — flaky vs backend; assertion stays at SDK-load only
- Adding more smoke tests beyond the 7 in the playbook

stream:b. CC Opus 4.7 / Medium for the YAML + secret-management; OC could handle the YAML if dispatched.

title: tyio.2: Defer BOGOS free gifts to requestIdleCallback on /cart
description:
## Context
Parent ticket hairmnl-theme-tyio. BOGOS free gifts loads two scripts on
/cart (215ms + 201ms = 416ms CPU) to check free gift eligibility, then
updates the cart DOM when the eligibility API responds. This DOM mutation
creates secondary long tasks in the 20-35s window.

## Goal
Wrap the BOGOS script initialization in requestIdleCallback on /cart pages,
so the eligibility check fires during CPU idle time rather than competing
with user interactions.

## Design

Check if BOGOS is loaded via:
(a) app embed tag (content_for_header) — use createElement guard or
    `window.addEventListener('load', () => requestIdleCallback(initBogos))` pattern
(b) direct script tag in a section/snippet — wrap the <script> src in
    a Liquid condition that outputs a `defer` attribute + JS wrapper

Most BOGOS installations use the app embed. If so, the requestIdleCallback
approach via a small inline script is cleanest:

```js
// In a cart-page-only script block
if (window.location.pathname === '/cart') {
  window.__bogos_idle = true;
  const _rIC = window.requestIdleCallback || (cb => setTimeout(cb, 200));
  _rIC(function() {
    window.__bogos_idle = false;
    // BOGOS initialization fires naturally once unblocked
  });
}
```

The key mechanic: BOGOS checks `document.readyState` or `DOMContentLoaded`
to init. Wrapping with rIC delays its initialization until the browser has
idle cycles, moving it out of the user-interaction window.

## Acceptance criteria
- BOGOS scripts still load and the free gift offer appears on /cart.
- The post-load long task at t=20-35s (from BOGOS DOM mutation) is no
  longer >200ms in a Lighthouse performance trace.
- No regression in free gift conversion rate (Shopify admin / BOGOS
  dashboard — check 7d post-fix).

## Scope
≤1 file. ≤20 LoC. Lower priority than tyio.1 (LoyaltyLion is the bigger
contributor). Address after tyio.1 ships and shows INP improvement.
acceptance_criteria:
1. BOGOS scripts (cdn.bogos.io) do not block the main thread during initial
   /cart page load — they fire during idle time (>100ms after DOMContentLoaded).
2. Free gift offer still renders on /cart within 500ms of page becoming
   interactive (rIC deadline respected).
3. Cart PSI mobile TBT improves by additional ≥150ms vs post-njj1 baseline.
4. No regression in BOGOS-driven free gift attachment rate (check BOGOS
   dashboard 7d post-deploy).
5. git show --stat pasted to bd notes before closing.
notes: Unblocked 2026-05-19: parent njj1 verified live with v2 (appendChild/insertBefore) guard. Same proven pattern is available for BOGOS scripts on /cart.
close_reason: BOGOS rIC guard live 2026-05-19. commit=2d52686 origin/main. appendChild/insertBefore intercept chains after njj1 v2. Dynamic BOGOS init scripts deferred to idle. Live LL chain verify: PASS pre+post.

title: OPERATOR (post-cutover): Mobile cart TBT recovery — GTM cleanup + Judge.me cart defer
description:
Surfaced by tonight's deep-dive into the one cell where P8 dev meaningfully loses to P6 live in the PSI matrix: mobile cart score 21 vs 35.

Full analysis: os2-migration/p8-mobile-cart-investigation.md

## Cause breakdown (main-thread +3908ms, +75% vs P6)

scriptEvaluation +2273ms accounts for 58% of the regression. Top contributors:

| Cost | ms | Recoverable | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| shopify-perf-kit-3.3.1.min.js (NEW on P8) | 545 | 🔒 NO | structural |
| vendor--61rXTEhfnWH.js (NEW on P8 stock vendor) | 343 | 🔒 NO | structural |
| GTM container drift since P6 baseline | 305 | ✅ ~200-400ms | operator (admin) |
| Judge.me widget_base.js (loads on cart but not needed) | 213 | ⚠️ ~100-200ms | operator (Judge.me admin) |
| Cart inline template JS (P8 stock bulk-sections init) | 333 | ⚠️ ~100-200ms | theme (risky) |

## Recovery work (do AFTER cutover)

1. Operator: Shopify admin → Settings → Customer Events → audit active sources + GTM workspace tags. Remove redundant. Recovers ~200-400ms.
2. Operator: Judge.me admin → Load on demand / per-template enable. Disable on cart-page if toggle exists. Recovers ~100-200ms.
3. Theme: investigate deferring non-critical cart inline JS via requestIdleCallback wrapper. RISKY — breaks cart UX if mishandled. Estimated ~100-200ms.

## Acceptance

- Mobile cart score recovers to ≥28 (currently 21) — partial recovery acceptable; structural floor likely caps at ~30-32
- TBT contribution drops from 1537ms → ≤1200ms
- No regression on cart UX (add-to-checkout completion rate per RUM unchanged or improved week-over-week)

## Why not cutover-blocking

- ~888ms of the regression is STRUCTURAL stock-P8 baseline (shopify-perf-kit + vendor--*) — can't be fixed
- Cart is post-engagement; users have already committed to buy
- Cart URL-level CrUX returns 404 (insufficient traffic) — can't validate field impact
- The recoverable work is mostly admin-side; better to do after cutover when admin attention is on the new theme

stream:b. CC Opus 4.7 / Medium for the theme-side piece; operator for the admin piece.
notes:
CODE SIDE COMPLETE — no new code needed (2026-05-20 investigation).

CC investigation finding: the theme-side cart inline JS was already optimally handled:
- Cart pre-init dispatcher: eager, necessary (rIC wrapper was INTENTIONALLY removed 2026-04-28 — comment explains why: drawer skeleton appeared without it)
- LoyaltyLion defer (tyio.1 v2): already in P8 theme.liquid, defers LL to first user interaction
- BOGOS rIC defer (tyio.2): already in P8 theme.liquid, defers BOGOS to requestIdleCallback
- Reamaze defer guard (fsaa in_progress): already in P8 theme.liquid
- P8 stock JS (+333ms 'bulk-sections init' = shopify-perf-kit + vendor bundle): CANNOT touch — Shopify stock

Bonus fix landed during investigation: ujg6.51 LL template-gating code reverted from P8 theme.liquid (operator closed ujg6.51 won't-do today; the gating would have been a cutover regression where anonymous home/collection visitors lost the LL rewards widget). Pushed to P8 theme 141168312419.

REMAINING RECOVERABLE WORK (admin-side, operator-only):
- GTM cleanup (~200-400ms): Shopify admin → Settings → Customer Events → remove redundant tags
- Judge.me per-template toggle (~100-200ms): Judge.me admin → disable widget on cart template if toggle exists

Recommend relabeling this ticket as 'OPERATOR: admin GTM cleanup + Judge.me cart defer' and re-triaging as post-cutover operator work.


---

REFRESHED INVESTIGATION 2026-05-28 (post ujg6.59):

The 2026-05-26 matrix showed P8 cart mobile TBT=8015ms across n=3 runs — but all 3 JSON files contained byte-identical Lighthouse audits (score 0.35, TBT 8015ms, LCP 6458ms exact). PSI returned a single cached result 3 times. Effective n=1.

Fresh cache-busted measurements (n=3 via random query params, this session):
- P6 cart mobile TBT: 382/710/2984ms (median 710ms)
- P8 cart mobile TBT: 1289/1386ms (1 fail; median 1338ms)
- P8 LCP: 5612ms vs P6 19049ms = -13.4s ✅
- P8 cart score median 0.35 vs P6 0.27 — P8 already winning on score

Fresh bootup-time deltas (P8 vs P6, ms): gtm +412, judge.me +225, klaviyo +141, perf-kit +163, preview-bar [DEV-ONLY] +354, 'other' +1933 (Web Pixel Manager, BOGOS, TagTag, Trekkie). Total +3208ms bootup.

Scope check: layout/theme.liquid is byte-identical between P6 live (131664707683), P8 dev (141168312419), and git HEAD. There is no separate P8-only file tree and no theme-ID-based gating mechanism in any current file. Any change to theme.liquid affects both themes when pushed (or diverges git from P6, creating a 'do not push' landmine).

Under the constraint 'P8-only changes': no code change is reachable. The residual +628ms gap is dominated by shared third-party infra (GTM/Judge.me/Klaviyo/BOGOS/Web Pixel Manager) which is admin-configured at the shop level, not theme-level.

Free production speedup at cutover: preview-bar (354ms) and theme-hot-reload artifacts vanish when P8 is published, accounting for ~55% of the current measured gap. Real production P8 cart TBT vs P6 should be roughly +275ms post-cutover before any admin cleanup.

The ticket remains scoped to operator post-cutover admin work (GTM cleanup + Judge.me per-template toggle). No CC-side code changes warranted.

title: Collection page INP: Flickity slider touch/click handler >200ms
description: Collection pages have 109 poor INP events out of 3,127 total (3.49% poor) — the highest absolute count of any page type. The Flickity product image slider (div.flickity-slider>div.product__media.is-selected>div>div.lazy-image.mobile-zoom-wrapper>img.srcset) is the #3 ranked INP target aggregate-wide (3,380 hits). Flickity initializes eager touch/click handlers on DOMContentLoaded; on collection pages with many product cards, these handlers compete with scroll events. Suspected: Flickity init not deferred; handlers not passive. Investigate deferring Flickity init to user-visible scroll or first interaction.
design:
Root cause: the er mixin (theme.js:3978-3992) calls new tr() for ALL [data-slider] elements synchronously during section onLoad(). On collection pages with section-collection-tabs (N tab blocks = N Flickity instances) + section-double (1 Flickity instance), this batches N+1 eager Flickity inits, each doing a forced offsetHeight layout read plus Flickity cell measurement. This blocks the main thread during page load, causing input delay (event queue) for users who tap during the init window — the dominant INP contribution on collection pages (109 poor / 3127 total).

Fix: keep first slider synchronous (active/visible tab); defer remaining sliders via requestIdleCallback({ timeout: 2000 }) / setTimeout(200) fallback. The .brick__collection:not(.flickity-enabled) CSS (theme.css:11330) already hides non-first products before init, so deferred sliders are visually correct before they init.
notes: Fix deployed live 2026-05-25. Commit: 70c7393. Changed er.onLoad() (theme.js:3978) and productSliderSection.onLoad() (theme.dev.js) to use IntersectionObserver with rootMargin:200px. Synchronous init removed from critical page-load path; Flickity instances initialize asynchronously as each slider enters viewport. Falls back to synchronous for browsers without IO (<0.1% global). Signal window: ~May 31 for INP collection page poor-rate to improve.

title: Homepage INP: announcement ticker interaction >200ms on cold load
description: Homepage has 9.16% poor INP rate (61/666 events, May14-18). The announcement bar animated ticker (div.announcement__message>div.announcement__scale>div.announcement__text.ticker--animated>p) is the #6 ranked INP target in the aggregate snapshot data (1,381 hits). On cold pageload, the ticker is in the critical rendering path and the animation+interaction handler fires synchronously. Suspected: owl-carousel or similar init in ticker code blocking main thread at interaction time.
notes: LIVE 2026-05-25. Commit 0aabadb (origin/main 65fae9f). Root cause: will-change: transform, padding, width on .ticker--animated prevented GPU-compositor hit-testing → forced main-thread layout on every ticker tap → INP >200ms. Fix: css-overrides.liquid override to will-change: transform only. Zero UX change (visual/behavioral). Signal window: ~May 30-31. Secondary suspect (Flickity pointerdown handler in vendor theme.js) left for follow-up.

title: P8.B.2-fu: PDP related-products lazy-render (ujg6.18 increment 3)
description:
Increment 3 of ujg6.18. Apply the lazy-render Section API + IO + noscript pattern to the PDP related-products section.

Files: identify the PDP related-products section file (likely sections/related-products.liquid or similar). Apply the same branching pattern as section-collection.liquid (increment 1).

JS hook already in assets/hairmnl-common.js section 8 (no JS changes needed).

Model: CC Sonnet 4.6/Medium — mechanical pattern repeat.
design:
## Files - `sections/related.liquid` (167 LoC) — apply lazy-render conditional pattern **DO NOT modify** `assets/hairmnl-common.js` (JS hook section 8 already shipped in ujg6.18 increment 1). ## Pattern (copy verbatim from ujg6.18 increment 1) Same conditional wrap as 2i8b.22 / ujg6.18 increment 1. The section renders the PDP related-products + recently-viewed products tabs. ```liquid {%- assign has_related_products = section.settings.related_products_enable -%} {%- assign has_recently_products = section.settings.show_recently_viewed -%} {%- if has_related_products and product.collections.size < 1 -%} {% assign has_related_products = false %} {%- endif -%} {% if has_related_products or has_recently_products %} {%- if request.design_mode or request.page_type == null -%} {%- else -%}
{%- endif -%} {% endif %} ``` Note: the `{% if has_related_products or has_recently_products %}` outer gate stays at the top — only lazy-render when section actually has content to render. ## Caveats specific to related.liquid - Uses Shopify's `product.related` / recommendations data — make sure both branches access the same data - The "recently-viewed products" tab is JS-driven from localStorage (per snippets/products-recently-viewed.liquid). Its empty server-side shell is tiny — including it in noscript fallback is harmless ## Constraints - File scope: `sections/related.liquid` ONLY - Do NOT modify other files - Placeholder min-height 400px reasonable starting estimate (PDP-bottom section, smaller than homepage rows) ## Verification - `python3 scripts/check-overlay-css.py` → OK - both branches identical content - `git show HEAD --stat` to bd close notes ## Model OC paid senior-developer. Same pattern as 2i8b.22 but smaller file = faster.

title: 2i8b.B2-fu: Audit Klaviyo onsite features for sync-vs-defer load mode
description:
**Surfaced by ujg6.28 desktop home TBT investigation.** Per `os2-migration/desktop-home-tbt-root-cause.md`, Klaviyo loaded additional onsite bundles on P8 dev that didn't appear in the P6 measurement:

- `vendors~Render~ClientStore~~~onsite-back-in-stock.7d7b5b811b79d6bdfaae.js` (back-in-stock module)
- `default~in_app_forms~signup_forms.fa6bf1a33ef2add72ef8.js` (signup forms module)

Klaviyo overall: P6 mainThreadTime 146ms → P8 165ms (+19ms). Small delta but worth verifying these onsite features aren't running synchronously when they could be defer-on-interaction.

## Steps

1. Open Klaviyo dashboard for the connected account
2. Check active onsite features (back-in-stock, signup forms, etc.)
3. Verify each is configured to load defer-on-interaction or async
4. If any are loading synchronously: change configuration in Klaviyo admin

## Output

Note in the bd ticket whether each feature is async/defer-loaded or sync. If sync, document why it's not deferred.

## Acceptance

- Inventory of active Klaviyo onsite features
- Per-feature load mode (sync / defer / async)
- Recommendation per feature

## Out of scope

- Klaviyo flow/template config — that's marketing-owned, not perf
- Migrating Klaviyo to Web Pixels Manager (covered by `ujg6.12`)
notes:
## CC-side audit 2026-05-18

### Klaviyo loader on LIVE — load mode evidence

From `curl https://www.hairmnl.com/`, the Klaviyo TAE app block (`shopify://apps/klaviyo-email-marketing-sms/blocks/klaviyo-onsite-embed/2632fe16-...`) emits exactly:

```html
<script async src="https://static.klaviyo.com/onsite/js/JAws9h/klaviyo.js?company_id=JAws9h"></script>
<script>!function(){...}</script>   <!-- ~750 char Proxy queue stub -->
<script>window.klaviyoReviewsProductDesignMode = false</script>
```

**Verdict: klaviyo.js loads `async` ✓** — does NOT block HTML parse. The Proxy queue stub is tiny inline (<1 KB) and provides the queueing surface for `window.klaviyo(...)` calls made before klaviyo.js boots.

The script is also referenced inside Shopify's `script_tag` proxy URL list (visible in `<!-- shopify essential -->` inline script), which is the SAME URL — Shopify's own script-tag mechanism adds the same async-loaded script under the hood.

### Per-feature bundle inventory (cited in ticket)

The two bundles cited as appearing on P8 but not P6:

| Bundle | Source | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| `vendors~Render~ClientStore~~~onsite-back-in-stock.7d7b5b811b79d6bdfaae.js` | webpack chunk loaded by klaviyo.js at runtime | Klaviyo admin → Onsite → Back in Stock feature ENABLED |
| `default~in_app_forms~signup_forms.fa6bf1a33ef2add72ef8.js` | webpack chunk loaded by klaviyo.js at runtime | Klaviyo admin → Signup Forms feature ENABLED |

**These chunks have no direct load-mode control from the theme.** They're loaded by `klaviyo.js` after its own async boot. The only operator-side lever is **toggle the feature on/off in Klaviyo admin** — disabling a feature removes its chunk from runtime load.

### Why the P8 measurement showed these but P6 didn't

Most likely explanation: **the features were ENABLED in Klaviyo admin between the P6 and P8 measurements**. Either:
- New signup form was created (e.g., for a campaign)
- Back-in-stock was turned on as part of inventory management changes
- Existing form was edited (Klaviyo re-bundles + updates chunk hash on form edits)

This is admin-config drift, not a regression introduced by the P8 theme.

### Load-mode optimization options (operator decision)

For ANY async script that's not critical to first paint, three options ranked by aggressiveness:

1. **Defer-on-interaction** (like the Reamaze guard shipped in `fsaa`): intercept the script load, replace src with `javascript/blocked` until user interacts. Removes ~165ms main-thread cost on non-interacting pageloads.
2. **defer** (vs current `async`): script downloads in parallel but executes AFTER HTML parse completes. Marginal LCP improvement vs async; significant safety improvement (no parse-interrupting execution).
3. **Disable features**: turn off Onsite features that aren't actively used. Removes per-feature chunks.

The Reamaze defer pattern (shipped tonight in fsaa, ~174 LoC inline script in theme.liquid) COULD be replicated for Klaviyo. But:
- Klaviyo's value is signup forms displayed on first visit (deferred = won't fire until interaction = loses conversion)
- Klaviyo's `_learnq` event collection is migration-worthy (covered by ujg6.12)
- Defer-on-interaction would break first-visit signup form display — a marketing regression

**Recommendation: don't defer Klaviyo. Audit features in admin instead.**

### Operator action items (admin-only — cannot do from CC)

1. **Klaviyo admin → Signup Forms → list active forms:** Confirm each is intentional. Disable any test/draft forms.
2. **Klaviyo admin → Onsite → Back in Stock:** Confirm intentionally enabled. If not used, disable.
3. **Klaviyo admin → Reviews (if app installed):** Confirm enabled (or disable if not used).
4. Re-measure desktop home TBT after admin audit. Expected: Klaviyo bundle count drops by 1-2; mainThreadTime delta closes.

### Verdict per ticket acceptance criterion

> Inventory of active Klaviyo onsite features

**From client-side observation:** klaviyo.js + (back-in-stock chunk visible in P8 measurement) + (signup-forms chunk visible in P8 measurement) + Klaviyo Reviews enabled (per `window.klaviyoReviewsProductDesignMode` config var in TAE block).

> Per-feature load mode

| Feature | Load mode | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| klaviyo.js (main loader) | `async` ✓ | Cannot improve from theme |
| Back-in-stock chunk | runtime (after klaviyo.js boots) | Feature toggle in admin |
| Signup forms chunk | runtime (after klaviyo.js boots) | Feature toggle in admin |
| Reviews (`klaviyoReviewsProductDesignMode = false`) | runtime config var only | Feature toggle in admin |

> Recommendation per feature

- **klaviyo.js**: leave as-is. async is correct for this surface. Defer-on-interaction would break first-visit signup form (loses conversion).
- **Back-in-stock**: audit in admin — if not actively used for marketing, disable.
- **Signup forms**: audit in admin — confirm each form is current; archive stale ones.
- **Reviews**: audit if app is actually installed/configured.
- **`_learnq` data collection migration**: covered by `ujg6.12` (Web Pixels Manager).

## Closing recommendation

Closing as `done-cc-side-complete-admin-audit-deferred-to-operator`. The CC-side audit is complete; admin-side audit is the operator's next step.

title: Delete 16 confirmed-dead snippets from snippets/ (stream:b)
description:
Delete 16 confirmed-dead snippets from `snippets/` and remove their rows from `os2-migration/intentionally-orphan.txt`.

## Why now

The wiring lint (`scripts/check-snippet-wiring.py`) introduced in commit `dac1c7d` (2026-05-18) now tracks all orphan snippets. The allowlist exists to suppress lint noise for _intentionally_ dormant snippets. Deleting the DEAD ones shrinks the allowlist surface, removes stale files, and makes the allowlist clearly signal only active deferral decisions.

## Snippets to delete (all have 0 external references — verified 2026-05-18)

### [DEAD] — P8 migration replaced functionality
- `snippets/bgset.liquid` — P6 hero carousel background-image helper; only ref is self-render (line 4)
- `snippets/cross-post-blogs.liquid` — defunct CMS integration
- `snippets/product_infox.liquid` — old product info-x variant
- `snippets/product-tabs-v1.liquid` — v1 product tabs, replaced by P8 mechanism
- `snippets/template-swatch.liquid` — P6 color swatches, P8 uses different mechanism
- `snippets/snippet-tag-lists.liquid` — blog tag list, no template renders it

### [DEAD] — Vendor prefix, app likely uninstalled
- `snippets/wc_cart.liquid` — Wholesale Club/Genie cart hook; 0 refs
- `snippets/wc_product_json.liquid` — same app, 0 refs
- `snippets/wsg-header.liquid` — Wholesale Gorilla/Suite header; 0 refs
- `snippets/wsg-status.liquid` — same app, 0 refs

### [TAE] dead apps
- `snippets/smile-initializer.liquid` — Smile Loyalty (replaced by LoyaltyLion); dead app
- `snippets/limespot.liquid` — LimeSpot retired in Phase 1 cutover; dead

### [TAE] Wishlist Hero (confirmed replaced by Swym, which loads via TAE)
- `snippets/wishlisthero-collection-product.liquid`
- `snippets/wishlisthero-custom-button.liquid`
- `snippets/wishlisthero-header-icon.liquid`
- `snippets/wishlisthero-styles.liquid`

Swym TAE confirmed: `snippets/swymSnippet.liquid` exists (loads via content_for_header/TAE embed); WishlistHero has 0 external refs in layout/sections/templates/assets.

## Out of scope for this ticket
- [P4-PORT] snippets: custom-fonts, critical-css, feature-flags, font-settings-inline — deferred until P8 migration wave
- [INVESTIGATE] snippets: pro-blogger, vertex-preview-toolbar — need separate triage
- [DYNAMIC] icon snippets — loaded via variable render, not orphans
design:
## Approach

### Step 1 — Re-verify zero external refs (at dispatch time)
Run for each snippet name:
```
grep -r "render '<name>'\|include '<name>'" layout/ sections/ templates/ snippets/ assets/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^snippets/<name>"
```
If any snippet has external refs, skip it and note in bd (do NOT delete).

### Step 2 — Delete files
```bash

  snippets/product-tabs-v1.liquid snippets/template-swatch.liquid snippets/snippet-tag-lists.liquid \
```

title: Upgrade 4 dns-prefetch → preconnect + add cdn.shopify.com preconnect (layout/theme.liquid)
description:
Upgrade 4 existing `dns-prefetch` entries to `preconnect`, and add a `preconnect` for `cdn.shopify.com`. Identified by OC HH-SWARM 2026-05-18 c2k Phase C audit.

## Current state (layout/theme.liquid lines 307-310)

```
<link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//www.googletagmanager.com">
<link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//edge.personalizer.io">
<link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//static.klaviyo.com">
<link rel="dns-prefetch" href="//tracking.aws.judge.me">
```

Only preconnect present is for `fonts.shopify.com` (line 25). No preconnect for `cdn.shopify.com` despite every product image being served from it.

## Why preconnect > dns-prefetch

`dns-prefetch` resolves DNS only. `preconnect` does DNS + TCP + TLS handshake — saves ~100-300ms on first request to that origin. Critical for origins serving render-blocking or LCP-critical assets.

## Why these 5 origins

- `cdn.shopify.com` — every product image (LCP candidate on homepage + collection + PDP)
- `www.googletagmanager.com` — GTM container script (624 KB/pageview per dashboard data)
- `edge.personalizer.io` — LimeSpot recs (249 KB/pageview)
- `static.klaviyo.com` — Klaviyo SDK (133 KB/pageview)
- `tracking.aws.judge.me` — Judge.me review tracking (small but blocks PDP)
design:
## Files to change

- `layout/theme.liquid` (1 file, ~5 lines net change)

## The change

In layout/theme.liquid, replace lines 307-310 with:

```liquid
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://cdn.shopify.com" crossorigin>
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://www.googletagmanager.com" crossorigin>
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://edge.personalizer.io" crossorigin>
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://static.klaviyo.com" crossorigin>
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://tracking.aws.judge.me" crossorigin>
```

Notes:
- Use `crossorigin` attribute since these are all cross-origin and may be used for fetches (preconnect without crossorigin only opens a non-credentialed connection; with crossorigin it covers both modes).
- Use `https://` not `//` — preconnect requires an absolute URL with scheme.

## Verification

1. Pre-push grep: `grep -n "preconnect\|dns-prefetch" layout/theme.liquid` — confirm the 4 dns-prefetch lines are replaced and the new preconnects are in place, plus the existing `fonts.shopify.com` preconnect remains.
2. View-source on live homepage after push: confirm the 5 new preconnects appear in `<head>` and no orphan dns-prefetch for these 4 origins remain.
3. Chrome DevTools → Network tab → look at initial connection timing for cdn.shopify.com — should show no DNS/TCP/TLS time for product images after the preconnect lands.

## Risk

LOW. Preconnect with crossorigin is a strict improvement over dns-prefetch — worst case is a wasted connection if the origin isn't used, but all 5 of these origins ARE used on most pageloads.

## Push checklist

1. Draft theme 140785582179 first: `shopify theme push --theme=140785582179 --only=layout/theme.liquid --nodelete`.
2. View-source smoke test as above.
3. Live theme 131664707683 with `--allow-live`.
4. Commit + push with reference to this bd id.
acceptance_criteria:
1. layout/theme.liquid has 5 preconnect lines (cdn.shopify.com, www.googletagmanager.com, edge.personalizer.io, static.klaviyo.com, tracking.aws.judge.me) with crossorigin attribute.
2. The 4 dns-prefetch entries at lines 307-310 are removed (replaced by preconnect).
3. View-source on live homepage shows the new preconnects in <head>.
4. No regressions in PSI / Lighthouse scores.
5. Pushed to live theme 131664707683, commit referenced in bd notes.

title: Slider C: remove spinner element + render pre-init slides natively
description: Structural follow-up to A+B (see sibling bd). A+B caps the gray placeholder size; C removes it entirely. ~25-30 LoC across snippets/collection-slider.liquid + assets/custom-theme.css + assets/custom-theme.js. Spec includes full visual QA matrix. Blocked on A+B passing post-live verification (so A+B stays as safety net during C implementation).
design:
# Structural slider-loading fix (Option C): remove spinner element + render pre-init slides natively

## Why this exists
A+B (sibling bd, see hairmnl-theme-* fix-homepage-slider-gray-placeholder) is a containment fix — it makes the spinner-wrapper smaller and helps sliders init in parallel. But the spinner is a UX leftover: it was designed for an AJAX-loaded slider where products were fetched and injected, but in our current code products are server-rendered into the DOM at page render time. There is no actual "loading" — Swiper just needs to take over presentation.

The cleaner fix: remove the spinner-wrapper element entirely and use CSS to make the pre-init `.swiper-wrapper` look reasonable until Swiper inits.

## Spec

### Required changes

1. **`snippets/collection-slider.liquid`** — Remove the entire `.spinner-wrapper` div block (the spinner + 3 bounce divs). The element exists only to cover unstyled slides while Swiper loads; with proper pre-init CSS it becomes unnecessary.

2. **`assets/custom-theme.css`** — Replace the A+B max-height cap (the `.collection-slider .swiper.collection:not(.swiper-initialized)` rule added in fix A) with a more comprehensive pre-init style:
   ```css
   /* Pre-init: show first row of slides at their final width.
    * After Swiper init, the .swiper-initialized class lands and this rule no longer applies. */
   .collection-slider .swiper.collection:not(.swiper-initialized) {
     overflow: hidden;
     max-height: 500px;  /* keep for safety */
   }
   .collection-slider .swiper.collection:not(.swiper-initialized) .swiper-wrapper {
     /* Lay out slides as a non-scrolling row at their actual post-init width.
      * Desktop: 6 slides per view → each ~16.67% width.
      * Mobile: 1.67 slides per view → each ~60% width. */
     display: flex;
     flex-wrap: nowrap;
   }
   .collection-slider .swiper.collection:not(.swiper-initialized) .swiper-slide {
     flex: 0 0 calc(100% / 6 - 8px);  /* 6 cols desktop, 10px gap */
   }
   @media (max-width: 1008px) {
     .collection-slider .swiper.collection:not(.swiper-initialized) .swiper-slide {
       flex: 0 0 60%;  /* matches Swiper config slidesPerView: 1.67 */
     }
   }
   ```
   (numbers above are illustrative — tune per actual Swiper config breakpoints around line 8883 in custom-theme.js)

3. **`assets/custom-theme.js`** — In `initCollectionSlider`, the `spinnerEl.style.display = "none"` line in the `on: init` callback becomes a no-op (spinner element no longer exists). Replace with a defensive guard `if (spinnerEl && spinnerEl.classList.contains('spinner-wrapper')) { spinnerEl.style.display = 'none'; }` OR remove the line entirely if `previousElementSibling` is guaranteed to no longer be a spinner. Safer to leave the guard for backward compat across other pages that might use the same snippet.

4. **Search other places using `spinner-wrapper`** — `grep -rn 'spinner-wrapper' --include='*.liquid' --include='*.css' --include='*.js'`. If other sections or snippets share the spinner CSS, leave those untouched; only modify the homepage collection-slider behavior.

### Visual QA matrix (required before live push)

Test on draft theme 140785582179, take screenshots at each combination:

| Breakpoint | Sliders visible | Expected pre-init appearance |
|---|---|---|
| Desktop 1440x900 | First slider in viewport | First 6 slides visible as a row at correct width, no gray block |
| Desktop 1440x900 | Below-fold sliders | Same: first 6 slides visible as a row, no gray block |
| Mobile 414x896 | First slider in viewport | First 1-2 slides visible at correct width |
| Mobile 414x896 | Below-fold sliders | Same |
| Slow 3G throttled | All | No visible content jump when Swiper takes over (use Chrome DevTools throttling) |

### Files touched
- `snippets/collection-slider.liquid` (-~7 lines)
- `assets/custom-theme.css` (-13 lines from A, +~20 lines new)
- `assets/custom-theme.js` (~3 lines for defensive guard)

Total: ~25-30 LoC across 3 files.

### Out of scope
- Touching the banner-slider, judge.me slider, cross-post-blogs slider, or any non-collection-slider Swiper instance.
- Removing the spinner CSS from custom-theme.css (leave the spinner CSS rules; just don't render the element). Cleaning up the dead CSS is a P4 nicety, separate ticket if anyone cares.

## Risk assessment
- **Medium risk** for the visual change: pre-init slides will be briefly visible at non-final styling before Swiper takes over. Mitigation: visual QA matrix above + throttled test.
- **Low risk** for performance: removing the spinner-wrapper element saves a tiny amount of DOM. The pre-init CSS is just a few flex rules.
- **A+B serves as safety net**: if C is rolled back, A+B can stay as the interim fix.

## Recommended model
Sonnet 4.6 / Medium for implementation (mechanical changes per the spec). OR offload to OC paid tier via `/hh-orchestrate` if you want it off the CC critical path. Visual QA verification: Sonnet/Medium or Opus/Medium for judgment on whether the pre-init layout looks acceptable.
acceptance_criteria: snippets/collection-slider.liquid no longer renders .spinner-wrapper div. Pre-init swiper-wrapper styled to show first row of slides at correct width. Visual QA matrix completed across 5 viewport/network combinations with screenshots. Live DOM check confirms no spinner element exists post-fix. A+B containment rule replaced with C's more comprehensive pre-init styling. No regressions to banner-slider, judge.me, or cross-post-blogs Swiper instances.
notes:
SHIPPED to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-05-16.

Commit: 7a55ecd

What changed:
- snippets/collection-slider.liquid: removed 7-line spinner-wrapper div
- assets/custom-theme.css: replaced A+B max-height rule with comprehensive
  pre-init flex layout — slides flex to calc(100% / 6 - 8px) desktop / 60%
  mobile (≤780px) — matching Swiper's slidesPerView 6 / 1.67 breakpoints
- assets/custom-theme.js: defensive guard on previousElementSibling
  classList.contains('spinner-wrapper') before display:none

QA matrix verified on live:
- Desktop 1440×900: 6-col flex, 332-390px height, no gray, nav arrows OK
- Mobile 414/492×896: 1 full + 0.67 peek slide, 60% width, overflow:hidden
- Live DOM check: 0 spinner-wrappers, all prevSibling=null, heights 373-411px

Result: pre-init heights are now within ±30px of post-init (341px) — near
zero CLS from slider init. The 1185px gray placeholder issue is fully
structurally resolved (A+B were patches; C removes the root cause).

design:
Reuse the Micromodal pattern already in the theme (snippets/video-popup.liquid and the BIS modal from hairmnl-theme-g1n.3).

Add .quick-buy-overlay and .quick-buy-modal to OVERLAY_PATTERNS in scripts/check-overlay-css.py.

Anti-pattern: do NOT replicate Stky's exact selector names (.satcb_quick_buy) — use our own brand-aligned names (.quick-buy-overlay, .quick-buy-modal).

title: 987 Phase 2: Announcement bar CLS (35 events, ticker JS init timing review)
description:
OC's 987 Phase 1 audit (2026-05-13) identified the announcement bar as a desktop CLS contributor: 35 poor-rated CLS events in the 7-day desktop GA4 RUM window.

Existing protection (per OC audit): the announcement bar element already has min-height: 36px in CSS, so the bar itself doesn't collapse-then-expand. The 35 events likely come from the ticker JS swapping content / fading messages / initializing dimensions on a slight delay after first paint.

Suspected mechanisms:
- announcement bar shows rotating messages (sections/announcement-bar.liquid uses a slider/ticker)
- between paint and JS init, the bar's INNER text may shift / wrap differently as fonts swap or as the slider library (Flickity? Swiper?) re-measures
- on desktop where text fits, mid-content shifts could trigger small CLS events that accumulate to 35

Investigation pattern:
1. Identify the ticker JS source (likely vendor.js Flickity init or a section-bound script)
2. Capture a CrUX-style trace on /pages/homepage with desktop UA + cache disabled to see the shift
3. Determine if the fix is: (a) explicit min-height on inner ticker container, (b) reserve text-width via line-height + height, (c) lazy-init the ticker only on user interaction, (d) preload the font that's swapping
4. Most likely fix: pin inner ticker container height + use font-display: optional for the announcement bar's font

Tooling: scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop --days 7 --top 30 (look for announcement / announce / shopify-section-announcement debug_targets).

This is lower-priority than the cart-template work because the impact is smaller (35 events vs 84) and the existing min-height protection already partially mitigates.
design:
Phase 1 — DATA:
1. Run `python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop --days 7 --top 30 | grep -iE "announc|ticker"` to confirm the specific debug_target prefix.
2. Inspect sections/announcement-bar.liquid + sections/announce-bar.liquid + any slider/ticker JS init (Flickity / Swiper / custom).
3. Reproduce on /pages/homepage in Chrome devtools with cache disabled. Look for the shift moment:
   - between paint and JS init (font swap, slider re-measure)
   - between slide transitions (text re-flow)
4. Identify whether shifts come from inner ticker container or from outer announcement bar height.

Phase 2 — FIX (one increment, ≤1 prod file + ≤60 LoC):
Most likely fixes (pick one based on Phase 1):
- (a) Pin inner ticker container min-height matching the tallest message line-height × line-count.
- (b) Add font-display: optional / swap to the announcement bar's font so layout doesn't re-flow on font load.
- (c) Render first message server-side at exact final dimensions; lazy-init slider only after user interaction.
- (d) If shifts come from Flickity re-measure, set `flickity-resize: false` and rely on CSS-driven sizing.

Hard rules (inherited from 987):
- Do NOT add contain: layout to the announcement bar element (it's a fixed/sticky-position parent in some templates — risk).
- Do NOT push to live — coordinator owns.
- Do NOT close — close when 7-day announce/ticker debug_target events <10 OR parent 987 closes.

Phase 3 — VERIFY:
1. kt0 lint passes.
2. Smoke-test the homepage announcement bar visually on draft — first paint should show final dimensions.

title: PDP Option 3 — Featured review module (left-column white-space fix, Q3)
description:
Per task-2-pdp-progression.md PR-3. Replace the remaining left-column void on long PDPs with a server-rendered featured-review module: aggregate score, 5/4/3/2/1-star distribution histogram, single highest-helpful review card.

CURRENT STATE (post Phase 1.5):
- PR-1 (sticky gallery) shipped — commit 3cef732 on phase1-cutover-fbt-clean
- PR-2 (sticky purchase bar) shipped — same commit
- PR-3 deferred until Judge.me data is server-renderable

FEASIBILITY GAP:
Standard Shopify product review metafields only expose aggregate data:
- product.metafields.reviews.rating.value.rating  (avg, e.g. 4.9)
- product.metafields.reviews.rating_count         (total count)
- product.metafields.reviews.rating.value.scale_max (usually 5)

NOT available as Liquid metafields (required for PR-3 mockup):
- 5/4/3/2/1-star bucket counts (distribution histogram)
- Single highest-helpful review text + reviewer name + helpful-vote count
- Verified-purchase badge, tags

PATHS TO UNBLOCK (pick one before claiming this issue):
1. Judge.me Admin API backfill: scheduled job pulls reviews + helpful votes,
   writes summary to a product metafield (review_summary.histogram +
   review_summary.featured_review). Server-renderable from Liquid afterward.
   ~2-3 days eng. Highest quality, follows the rest of the Vertex/precompute
   pattern (server-rendered, not client-rendered).
2. Judge.me's pre-rendered metafield_tag widget: embed Judge.me's
   server-rendered HTML chunk in the module slot. Loses the custom histogram
   + editorial styling from the mockup but fills the void quickly.
3. Client-side fetch from Judge.me public widget endpoint, render with
   inline JS. Worst CLS profile; not recommended.

RECOMMENDATION:
Path 1 once Phase 1 + 2 are stable in production. Allocate to Q3 polish slot
per the original task-2 timeline.

REFERENCES:
- task-2-pdp-progression.md PR-3 (full DoD)
- Mockup: vertex-placement-guide-mockups.html → Addendum B → 'Option 3 · Featured-review module under gallery'
- Existing Judge.me wiring: snippets/judgeme_widgets.liquid, snippets/judgeme_static_stars.liquid, snippets/product-rating.liquid (aggregate-only)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Featured-review module renders in left column below thumbnails, ≥1024px only
- Distribution histogram pulls from server-rendered metafield (Path 1) or app-rendered HTML (Path 2)
- Featured review card renders if review_count > 0; empty-state hidden otherwise
- 'Read all N reviews →' link anchors to existing #reviews accordion + opens it
- Lighthouse CLS regression check at or below baseline
- Visual diff vs Addendum B 'Option 3' mockup frame
notes: Blocked-by: needs decision on Path 1 (Judge.me API backfill + server-render via metafield) vs Path 2 (Judge.me prerendered HTML chunk). Path 1 preferred for Vertex/precompute consistency. Surface to engineering lead before claiming.

title: xex-2: block BTA bootstrap loader (cdn block alone insufficient)
description:
xex-1 (closed 2026-05-12) blocked `cdn.bookthatapp.com` script-tag injection via document.createElement override. PSI run on 2026-05-12 09:46 UTC shows cdn.bookthatapp.com still delivering 171KB on the mobile homepage despite the guard.

Root cause: the BTA bootstrap loader at `https://www.hairmnl.com/apps/bookthatapp/sdk/v1/js/bta-widgets-bootstrap.min.js` (Shopify app proxy URL, NOT cdn.bookthatapp.com) is loading on every page. Chrome DOM inspection on 2026-05-12 shows ZERO script tags from cdn.bookthatapp.com — the 171KB is being fetched by the bootstrap via fetch() or XHR as a non-script resource (likely the main BTA bundle data, CSS, or API responses).

Our document.createElement guard only intercepts <script> tag creation. It cannot intercept fetch()/XHR.
design:
## Approach: block the bootstrap loader, not the CDN

Since blocking cdn.bookthatapp.com (the data origin) does not work via createElement override, block the BOOTSTRAP LOADER URL pattern instead. The bootstrap is loaded as a `<script>` tag — likely created dynamically by Shopify's content_for_header app-block render OR by SATCB. Either way, our guard CAN catch it if the URL is in BLOCKED_ORIGINS.

Bootstrap URL pattern: `https://www.hairmnl.com/apps/bookthatapp/sdk/v1/js/bta-widgets-bootstrap.min.js`
We cannot block on full URL (it's stored as src after baseurl resolution). Block on the path segment `apps/bookthatapp` — this is unique to BTA.

### Step 1 — Verify the bootstrap is createElement-injected
In Chrome on homepage, check: are bta-widgets-bootstrap.min.js script tags injected dynamically (visible in MutationObserver) or static in content_for_header HTML? If static → no fix possible without server-side change. If dynamic → proceed.

### Step 2 — Extend BLOCKED_ORIGINS
Update layout/theme.liquid guard:

```js
// BEFORE
var BLOCKED_ORIGINS = ['cdn.visualquizbuilder.com', 'cdn.bookthatapp.com'];

// AFTER
var BLOCKED_ORIGINS = [
  'cdn.visualquizbuilder.com',
  'cdn.bookthatapp.com',
  '/apps/bookthatapp/',  // BTA bootstrap path — blocking this prevents the bootstrap fetching cdn assets
];
```

The substring-match (`String(v).indexOf(o) !== -1`) catches both `cdn.bookthatapp.com` AND `/apps/bookthatapp/` patterns.

### Step 3 — Verify allowlist still works
Studio pages (`/pages/studio-*`, `/pages/hairmnlstudio-*`) bypass the guard entirely (allowed=true) — so BTA continues to load there. No allowlist change needed.

### Step 4 — Smoke test on draft
- Homepage in Chrome: cdn.bookthatapp.com transferSize = 0, /apps/bookthatapp/ script tags = 0
- /pages/hairmnlstudio-main: bootstrap loads, cdn.bookthatapp.com loads (or at least guard doesn't block it)
- Run PSI mobile homepage: network_by_origin shows cdn.bookthatapp.com absent

### Hard rules
- DO NOT remove cdn.bookthatapp.com from BLOCKED_ORIGINS — keep belt-and-suspenders
- DO NOT touch the createElement/MutationObserver logic — only extend the BLOCKED_ORIGINS array
- DO NOT touch allowlist regex — already correct
- DO NOT commit or push to Shopify — coordinator owns merge gate
acceptance_criteria: 1. layout/theme.liquid BLOCKED_ORIGINS includes '/apps/bookthatapp/' or equivalent path filter. 2. Homepage PSI mobile network_by_origin: cdn.bookthatapp.com = 0KB. 3. /pages/hairmnlstudio-* still loads BTA (verified via Chrome). 4. Single-file change to layout/theme.liquid only.
notes:
2026-05-13: Extended BLOCKED_ORIGINS in layout/theme.liquid L690 to include '/apps/bookthatapp/' — a single-line addition that blocks the BTA bootstrap loader script from being injected via createElement on non-studio pages. cdn.bookthatapp.com retained (belt-and-suspenders). Allowlist regex, MutationObserver, and createElement logic untouched. kt0 overlay check passed (430 files, 0 violations). Ready for coordinator merge-gate: draft push → PSI homepage check → live push. Acceptance criteria #1 and #4 satisfied. Criteria #2 (PSI 0KB) and #3 (studio still loads BTA) require coordinator live deployment verification.
SHIPPED 2026-05-13: layout/theme.liquid BLOCKED_ORIGINS += '/apps/bookthatapp/'. Pushed live via creations-gdc.myshopify.com (real store URL — hairmnl.myshopify.com was wrong alias). Commit 212db65.

title: Periodic sync of admin-managed Shopify files (templates/*.json, config/settings_*.json) from live to git
description:
Shopify admin auto-edits these files every time someone changes settings in the Theme Editor (banner images, homepage section blocks, settings_schema additions, etc.):

- templates/*.json (notably templates/index.json — homepage banners + featured collections)
- config/settings_data.json
- config/settings_schema.json

Per .opencode_hints §4, agents must NOT edit these files (auto-clobbered by Shopify). But that policy leaves the GIT copies progressively stale as admin makes Theme Editor changes. The divergence surfaced today 2026-05-12:

- templates/index.json in git (from 2026-05-03 snapshot) referenced March-April 2026 banner image filenames; live had been rolled forward to May-June 2026 banners. When draft theme drift required re-pushing templates/index.json, the stale local copy would have re-introduced broken image refs. We worked around by pulling from live and pushing directly via --path, leaving git unchanged. That's a band-aid — git is now MORE stale.

- config/settings_schema.json was 7 bytes behind live (admin made a small settings change since 2026-05-02). Same band-aid applied.

Long-term solution: a scripted periodic pull from live into a separate 'admin-sync' branch (or a dedicated git operation) that snapshots these files weekly. Doesn't violate the no-edit rule because the source is live itself. Reduces emergency surface area when draft drift is detected.

Out of scope: agents editing these files via Theme Editor admin (still forbidden); merging admin changes back to live (admin is already the source-of-truth for these files via Shopify).
design:
Implementation spec for Cursor dispatch:

### Files to create/modify
- .github/workflows/admin-file-sync.yml (NEW file)
- scripts/admin-file-sync.sh (NEW file, called by the GHA workflow)

### What to build
A GitHub Actions workflow that runs weekly (Sunday 02:00 UTC) and on workflow_dispatch.

The workflow:
1. Checks out the repo
2. Installs Shopify CLI (shopify/shopify-cli-action or direct curl install — check what version is available)
3. Authenticates via SHOPIFY_CLI_PARTNERS_TOKEN secret (already set in repo secrets)
4. Pulls these files from live theme 131664707683 into a temp path:
   - templates/index.json
   - templates/product.json
   - templates/collection.json
   - templates/cart.json
   - config/settings_data.json
   - config/settings_schema.json
5. Compares each file against repo HEAD (git diff)
6. If any file differs, opens a PR or commits to a branch 'admin-sync/<date>' with a commit message like:
   chore: sync admin-managed theme files from live (YYYY-MM-DD)
7. The PR body lists which files changed and notes they are admin-managed (not to be hand-edited)

### Hard constraints
- Workflow MUST NOT auto-merge to main — create a PR or draft branch only
- Workflow MUST NOT push to live theme
- Use --nodelete flag on shopify theme pull so it doesn't delete local-only files
- SHOPIFY_STORE must be hairmnl.myshopify.com
- All secrets (SHOPIFY_CLI_PARTNERS_TOKEN, SHOPIFY_FLAG_STORE) assumed already present in repo secrets

### Acceptance criteria
1. .github/workflows/admin-file-sync.yml passes yamllint
2. Workflow file shows correct cron schedule, correct theme ID, correct file list
3. Script is executable (chmod +x scripts/admin-file-sync.sh) if separate script file used
4. git diff shows ONLY the 2 new files; no other files modified
acceptance_criteria:
1. .github/workflows/admin-sync.yml exists and runs weekly (chosen cadence, e.g., Sunday 02:00 UTC).
2. Workflow pulls listed admin files from live theme via Shopify CLI + commits to main with message 'chore(admin-sync): refresh admin-managed files from live'.
3. First successful run lands within 1 week of merge.
4. Documentation: ARCHITECTURE.md §3 or §4 updated to note the cron job exists and which files it owns.
5. Verification: after one successful run, ls -la with mtime shows the listed files were refreshed and git log shows the chore commit.

title: hover-line.js layout thrash — mousemove handler write→read loop (highest INP impact)
description:
3sr audit (commit pending) flagged hover-line.js in assets/custom-theme.js as the highest-INP-impact layout-thrash hotspot. Pattern: write setProperty → read offsetLeft / getBoundingClientRect / clientHeight in a tight loop inside what appears to be a mousemove handler. This forces a sync layout on every mouse movement when the line indicator is active, contributing to INP poor events.

Source location: assets/custom-theme.js around line 8549-8593 (minified IIFE section).

Suggested fix: batch reads before writes, OR move to CSS transforms (translate3d for line position) which don't trigger layout. If hover-line is a underline-tracking effect on nav links, CSS-only with :hover + transition on transform should fully replace the JS.

References: 3sr audit findings in bd hairmnl-theme-3sr notes.
design: Per .opencode_hints source/min mirror discipline: custom-theme.dev.js does NOT exist for this file (3sr audit confirmed), so single-file edit OK. Cap: ≤120 LoC change. Smoke test must PASS post-fix. No kt0-class CSS properties on hover targets (would trap descendant fixed overlays).
acceptance_criteria:

2. Hover indicator still visually tracks correctly (manual eyeball on nav with hover line)

title: Complete smoke-test-drawers.py PhotoSwipe + search overlay coverage (currently SKIP)
description:
The Layer 3 smoke test (scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py, shipped in commit 6335c01) currently has 2 working tests (cart drawer, mobile nav) and 2 SKIPs marked with `incomplete_reason`:

1. PhotoSwipe lightbox on PDP — the zoom button (.media__zoom__icon) reveals only on parent .product__media hover. Programmatic click via headless Playwright doesn't reliably fire the PhotoSwipe init chain. Need a different trigger strategy (e.g., hover parent first, OR call the init JS directly via page.evaluate).

2. Search overlay on Home — #search-popdown opens as a 107px-tall top-anchored bar (intentional design — popdown, not full-viewport overlay). The default kt0-killer geometry assertion (height >= 90% viewport) is the wrong assertion shape for popdowns.

Manual smoke test (Layer 2 checklist items #3 and #4 in .opencode_hints) covers both today. This bd is for upgrading the automated coverage when bandwidth permits.
design:
## PhotoSwipe (test #3)

Try this trigger strategy (in order of preference):
  a. Hover the parent .product__media element, then wait for .media__zoom__icon to be visible, then click. Validate via headless Playwright before assuming hover-events fire correctly.
  b. If (a) doesn't work: directly invoke the PhotoSwipe init via page.evaluate() targeting the zoom-pswp JS path (look at assets/theme.dev.js productPhotoswipeZoom function for the entry point).
  c. As a last resort: keep the test skipped indefinitely and rely on the Layer 2 manual checklist.

State selector .pswp--open should be correct — verified from theme.dev.css. If state still doesn't appear, try .pswp.pswp--visible or inspect via Chrome DevTools.

## Search overlay (test #4)

Two paths:
  a. RECOMMENDED: Add an `overlay_shape` field to OverlayTest with values like "fullscreen" (default, current assertion) | "popdown" (just checks position:fixed + width >= 300, skips height check) | "modal" (checks centered within viewport). The kt0-killer containment audit still runs in all cases — only the geometry assertion varies.

  b. Simpler: hardcode an `expect_height_pct` field (default 0.9) and set it to 0.05 for popdowns. Less semantic but smaller patch.

The CONTAINMENT_AUDIT_JS stays identical — that's the kt0-killer and shouldn't change between overlay shapes.

## Acceptance after this fix lands

- `python3 scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py --theme=draft` returns 4 PASS, 0 SKIP, 0 FAIL on a clean theme.
- Artificially injecting `contain: layout` onto `.header__wrapper` (or `.product__media`) and re-running causes the corresponding test(s) to FAIL with the kt0-killer reason.
- `.opencode_hints` Layer 3 section can drop the "currently 2 tests work, 2 skipped" caveat.

## Out of scope

- Klaviyo popup, LoyaltyLion launcher, Reamaze chat — these are app-injected, require user state (logged-in customer, etc.), and are well-covered by manual Layer 2 checks. No automated coverage needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py PhotoSwipe + search tests no longer have `incomplete_reason` set.
- Both tests return PASS when run against a clean draft theme.
- Both tests return FAIL when `contain: layout` (or transform / filter / perspective) is artificially added to a relevant ancestor selector via page.add_style_tag during the test run.
- `.opencode_hints` Layer 3 section updated if needed to reflect the expanded coverage.
- Single git commit, scoped to scripts/smoke-test-drawers.py + .opencode_hints.
