title: Unregister stale Shop Sheriff PWA service workers — 3,585 SW errors / 7d (osj leftover)
description: Shop Sheriff PWA app was uninstalled 2026-05-02 (issue osj). Client-side SW registrations persist in returning shoppers' browsers and fail to update because the upstream serviceworker.js file is gone. JS error pipeline surfaces this as 1,975 (null/nonexistent SW) + 1,610 (failed update) errors / 7d. Bounded fix: small JS in layout/theme.liquid to unregister stale SWs on page load.
design:
## Goal
Unregister stale Shop Sheriff PWA service workers in returning shoppers' browsers. App was uninstalled (issue osj closed 2026-05-02) but the service worker is still registered client-side and failing to update because there's no upstream worker file. ~3,585 events / 7d combined.

## What we know
- Error 1: "Cannot update a null/nonexistent service worker registration" — 1,975 events / 7d
- Error 2: "Failed to update a ServiceWorker for scope ('https://www.hairmnl.com/') with script ('Unknown')" — 1,610 events / 7d
- Source: `(unknown)` (browser-internal SW machinery)
- Cause: Shop Sheriff PWA registered `/serviceworker.js` (or similar). Removing the app uninstalls the upstream file, but client-side SW registration persists.

## Fix (theme-side, mechanical)
Add a small JS snippet to layout/theme.liquid (top of <head> or in a defer'd block) that:
1. Checks if `navigator.serviceWorker` exists.
2. Lists registrations.
3. For each registration whose scriptURL contains 'serviceworker' or 'shopsheriff' or 'pwa', calls `.unregister()`.
4. Logs to console (not GA4 — too noisy) once per session.

Conservative version: only unregister if the registration's `scriptURL` returns 404 (file gone). That way we don't accidentally unregister legitimate SWs from other apps.

## Files to modify
- layout/theme.liquid — add a <script> block, ideally near other one-shot client cleanup logic

## Test plan
1. Push to draft, smoke test in Brave with DevTools → Application → Service Workers visible.
2. If a stale SW is registered, the snippet should remove it within ~1 second of page load.
3. Check console for "[sw-cleanup] unregistered: <url>" or similar log.
4. After live push, watch the dashboard JS errors section over 7 days — both error types should decay to near-zero as users' browsers all get the cleanup script.

## Bounded scope
- ≤30 LoC of JS
- 1 file (layout/theme.liquid)
- No risk if guarded by feature-detection (older browsers without SW support no-op)
acceptance_criteria:
After fix shipped + 7 days of decay:
- "Cannot update null/nonexistent SW" + "Failed to update ServiceWorker" combined < 100 events / 7d (was ~3,585)
- No regression on legitimate SWs (none currently in use)
- Console log shows the cleanup ran once per session for affected users
notes: Closed 2026-05-09 wontfix — user correction: Shop Sheriff PWA is STILL INSTALLED (only Shop Sheriff AMP was uninstalled, issue osj). The SW errors are Shop Sheriff PWA's own code bugs, not theme-side leftovers. Cannot touch — vendor's responsibility. If error volume becomes problematic, route to Shop Sheriff PWA vendor support.

title: BookThatApp gating — 171KB on every mobile pageload
description: cdn.bookthatapp.com loads 171KB on every mobile pageload via SATCB dynamic injection (same mechanism as VQB, confirmed 2026-05-12). Theme-side gating IS possible using the document.createElement guard already installed in layout/theme.liquid (bd 8z9). Task: extend the existing guard to also block cdn.bookthatapp.com on non-booking pages, after discovering which routes legitimately use BookThatApp.
design:
## Context
layout/theme.liquid already has a VQB guard (bd 8z9, merged 2026-05-12, commit cc8d92d) that:
- Overrides document.createElement to swallow script.src assignments to VQB_ORIGINS
- Uses a MutationObserver fallback
- Has a regex allowlist for quiz pages: /\/pages\/[a-z0-9-]*quiz/i

The guard is at roughly line 680 (before {{ content_for_header }}). BookThatApp's loader `bta-widgets-bootstrap.min.js` was observed at head script index 40 (async+defer), loaded from `https://www.hairmnl.com/apps/bookthatapp/sdk/v1/js/bta-widgets-bootstrap.min.js`. The main bundle comes from `cdn.bookthatapp.com`.

## What OC needs to do

### Step 1 — Discover booking route(s)
Search theme templates and sections for BookThatApp references:
- `grep -rn -i "bookthatapp\|book.that\|bta" templates/ sections/ snippets/ config/`
- Look for any page template JSON or section referencing BTA widgets
- Check if there's a `/pages/book-appointment`, `/pages/appointments`, `/pages/services` or similar

### Step 2 — Extend the guard in layout/theme.liquid
Find the VQB guard block (search for `VQB performance gate` comment or `VQB_ORIGINS`). Extend it:

**Current:**
```js
var VQB_ORIGINS = ['cdn.visualquizbuilder.com'];
var path = window.location.pathname;
var allowed = /\/pages\/[a-z0-9-]*quiz/i.test(path);
```

**Target:**
```js
var BLOCKED_ORIGINS = ['cdn.visualquizbuilder.com', 'cdn.bookthatapp.com'];
var path = window.location.pathname;
var allowed = /\/pages\/[a-z0-9-]*quiz/i.test(path)      // VQB quiz pages
           || /\/(pages|apps)\/[a-z0-9-]*(book|appoint)/i.test(path); // BTA booking pages
```
Also update the `{%- comment -%}` header to reference both bd 8z9 and xex.
Replace `VQB_ORIGINS` with `BLOCKED_ORIGINS` in both the `set` handler and the MutationObserver filter.

**IMPORTANT:** The booking page allowlist regex `/\/(pages|apps)\/[a-z0-9-]*(book|appoint)/i` is a starting guess — adjust based on what Step 1 finds. If BTA lives on a different path pattern, use the real paths.

### Step 3 — Verify JS is syntactically valid
Run: `node --check layout/theme.liquid 2>&1 | grep -A2 "SyntaxError"` (will error on Liquid tags but JS errors show up clearly). Or check braces/parens balance manually.

### Step 4 — Stage only layout/theme.liquid
`git add layout/theme.liquid`
Do NOT commit — coordinator (Claude Code) owns the draft-push smoke-test and live push.

## Hard rules
- Do NOT push to Shopify (no `shopify theme push`)
- Do NOT commit
- Do NOT modify any file other than layout/theme.liquid
- Do NOT change the MutationObserver or createElement override logic — only extend BLOCKED_ORIGINS and the allowlist regex
acceptance_criteria: 1. layout/theme.liquid diff shows: BLOCKED_ORIGINS includes both cdn.visualquizbuilder.com and cdn.bookthatapp.com; allowlist regex covers booking routes. 2. Only layout/theme.liquid changed. 3. JS brace/paren balance confirmed. 4. Not committed — staged only for coordinator push.
notes:
2026-05-12 09:13 UTC — Implementation complete:

- Extended existing guard block in layout/theme.liquid (lines 680–731)
- Renamed VQB_ORIGINS → BLOCKED_ORIGINS, array now: ['cdn.visualquizbuilder.com', 'cdn.bookthatapp.com']
- Extended allowlist regex to cover BTA routes: /pages/studio-*, /pages/hairmnlstudio-*, /apps/bta-*
- All 3 VQB_ORIGINS references renamed (zero remaining)
- Liquid/JS validation: Liquid 199/199, braces 552/552, parens 288/288 — all balanced
- QA review: PASS — all 4 code-review acceptance criteria met, no regressions
- Only layout/theme.liquid touched (9 additions, 5 deletions)
- Not committed — staged only, awaiting coordinator draft push + PSI validation
## MERGE_GATE_LIVE_PASS (2026-05-12)

Smoke test on draft 140785582179:
- Homepage: vqbScripts=[], btaCdnScripts=[], isOverridden=true ✓
- /pages/hair-quiz: vqbScripts=[vqb.2.9.1892.js], isOverridden=false ✓
- /pages/hairmnlstudio-*: isOverridden=false (guard exits early) ✓

Known limitation: on studio pages, both BTA+VQB are allowed (single 'allowed' gate). Acceptable — studio pages are low-traffic. Improvement can be filed separately.

BTA allowlist verified against templates/page.hairmnlstudio-*.json + templates/page.studio-*.json + settings_data.json BTA block reference.

Pushed to live 131664707683. Git: ed3d161.

title: Reamaze widget defer-on-interaction — 212KB blocking on every page
description: cdn.reamaze.com loads 212KB on every mobile pageload. Theme-side gating impossible — Reamaze is an app embed. Per Reamaze docs, defer-on-interaction may be available via admin: load only when user opens chat. Coordinate with marketing/CS team — they own the chat tool.
design: Admin task. Steps: 1) Open Reamaze app admin → Settings → Performance / Loading. 2) Look for 'lazy load' / 'defer until interaction' / 'show on click' option. 3) If supported: enable. 4) If not: contact support requesting feature. 5) Re-run PSI mobile to verify reduction.
acceptance_criteria: Reamaze widget loads only after user clicks chat bubble (or after first scroll if that's the lightest deferral available). cdn.reamaze.com network requests on initial pageload reduce from 212KB → ≤30KB (the chat-launcher icon only).
notes:
2026-05-10 investigation: only theme-code reference is dns-prefetch hint at layout/theme.liquid:84. No SDK script tag, no init code. Reamaze is purely app-embed. Theme-side defer-on-interaction NOT possible without the SDK source. Stays admin-only — coordinate with marketing/CS to enable Reamaze app's own performance settings (Reamaze admin → Settings → Performance) or contact vendor.
SUPERSEDED 2026-05-14 by epic hairmnl-theme-meu. Original conclusion 'theme-side defer impossible' was based on app-embed limitations that we've since worked around via the createElement override pattern (commits cc8d92d VQB + ed3d161 BTA, both app-embeds). The new epic includes a full re-audit (Phase 1: bzx) that re-validates this pattern works for Reamaze before any code change. Closing dgb in favor of the epic's structured rollout.

title: Enhance theme JS error reporter — capture e.error.name for richer error_type categorization
description: Current window.error handler in layout/theme.liquid (line ~625) sends error_type as a literal 'error' or 'rejection' (two categorical buckets). Same-origin errors expose e.error.name (TypeError, ReferenceError, SyntaxError, etc.) which would dramatically improve dashboard diagnostic value. Suggested change: send(e.error?.name || 'error', e.message, src). Cross-origin scripts will still show 'error' (browser redacts e.error). Two-line change. After ship, dashboard JS errors section will group by actual class name for first-party errors. Dependency: hairmnl-theme-l3g must be live first (so GTM tag 776 actually fires). Out-of-scope: capture stack traces (cross-origin redaction makes this low-value).
acceptance_criteria:
- Same-origin errors caught by window.error handler send error_type=<actual JS class name> (TypeError, ReferenceError, SyntaxError, etc.) instead of literal "error".
- Cross-origin errors (where e.error is null due to browser redaction) continue to send error_type="error" (no regression).
- Promise rejections continue to send error_type="rejection" (the unhandledrejection handler is untouched).
- Diff scope: ONLY `layout/theme.liquid` modified. ~3 lines added/changed.
- Live verified: synthetic TypeError throw produces error_type=TypeError in dataLayer.
notes:
Dispatched to OC lead-architect via /hh-orchestrate — sub-orchestrator SOP, free-tier opencode/minimax-m2.5-free. yf3 queued behind this one (file scope overlap on layout/theme.liquid).

Persist per-snapshot dict {origin: total_bytes} on snapshot.psi.mobile.network_by_origin. Render new dashboard section: stacked area chart (Chart.js) over last 30 snapshots + table of latest snapshot bytes by origin, sorted desc. Catches silent bloat from app updates without manual auditing.

title: Parallelize PSI mobile + desktop runs with ThreadPoolExecutor
description: In main() ~line 1529, PSI mobile and desktop runs are currently sequential (~3 min each = ~6 min total). Wrap in concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) to run both concurrently, halving wall-clock time to ~3.5 min.

title: Bump amber/red foreground colors to WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast
description: Current amber #C97A1A on #FBF1E0 and red #C03A3A on #FBE8E8 fail WCAG AA 4.5:1 for normal text. Darken both foregrounds slightly in the CSS block in render_html() until both ratios meet ≥4.5:1. Verify with DevTools or axe.

title: Better empty/loading states for GA4 quota hit, CrUX None, PSI all-runs-failed
description: render_top_pages(), render_rum_cwv_cards(), render_inp_targets(), render_js_errors() currently show raw empty lists or KeyError tracebacks when sources are unavailable. Add informative empty-state HTML: 'No data — GA4 quota exceeded' / 'CrUX not available for this URL' / 'All PSI runs failed — check API key'.

title: Reconcile log filename mismatch across plist, README, and GHA
description: com.hairmnl.daily-perf.plist writes to hairmnl-daily-perf.log but dashboard/README.md says hairmnl-perf.log. Pick one canonical name and update plist, README, and the new GHA workflow to match.

title: Surface GA4 quota/sampling note in UI
description: GA4 Data API applies sampling at high volume; currently silent. In query_ga4_rum() at line 267, detect samplingMetadatas in the response and propagate up. In render_html() at line ~1231, render a small note when the latest snapshot's GA4 query was sampled, so users know data may be approximate.

title: Snapshot rotation — archive older entries when snapshots.jsonl > 1000 lines
description: dashboard/data/snapshots.jsonl is append-only and unbounded. Currently 17 lines but will grow indefinitely. After successful append in main(), check line count: if >1000, move older entries (>365 days) to dashboard/data/archive/YYYY.jsonl. Active file stays scannable.

title: Null-field validation on GA4 response shape
description: query_ga4_rum() at line 267 returns dicts with conditional keys. Downstream rendering assumes presence. Add validation: if required keys (metrics, top_pages, top_inp_targets) are missing or None, log to stderr and return a typed empty-state dict so downstream rendering shows clear empty states instead of KeyError tracebacks.

title: CrUX vs GA4 RUM divergence badge when |delta|>10pp
description: CrUX (28d p75) and GA4 RUM (rolling 7d good/NIP/poor) can diverge meaningfully. In render_snapshot_section() at line ~894, when the same metric shows >10pp difference between CrUX rating and GA4 RUM good%, render a divergence badge prompting the user to investigate sampling differences.

title: Reamaze chat WINDOW body shift — confirm via GA4 + DevTools before broadening existing rule
description:
Existing 7fz fix (css-overrides.liquid:286) covers #reamaze-widget-label. Beads e4a notes flag the chat WINDOW body as a possible remaining CLS source. Before broadening selector to [id^='reamaze-'] (which would also catch #reamaze-frame-host, #reamaze-button, etc.), confirm it's actually shifting:

1. Re-query GA4 layout_shift events with customEvent:debug_target filter — look for any reamaze-* targets beyond reamaze-widget-label.
2. If GA4 shows reamaze-button or reamaze-frame-host events: ship a broader rule.
3. If GA4 shows zero non-label reamaze events: close — current fix is sufficient.

Risk: over-aggressive position:fixed on chat iframe wrapper could break chat usability. Conservative path is to leave the existing label rule and only widen if field data demands it.

Parent of e4a.

The original concern about widening to [id^='reamaze-'] is unnecessary — field data shows no further shifts requiring it. Closing per the conservative-path decision in the original spec.

title: Bundle byte removal: node-html-parser dead library code
description: Task 4 patched the call site (custom-theme.js scripts/domparser.js) to use native textContent — runtime win immediate. Library code (~5,400 lines, 11 sub-modules) remains in bundle as dead but loaded code. Bundle byte removal requires original bundler config / source files which aren't in this repo. Follow-up: identify each // node_modules/node-html-parser/dist/* __commonJS block in custom-theme.js and surgically delete OR rebuild bundle from source if the original esbuild/webpack/parcel config can be located.
close_reason: Dead node-html-parser library + deps (domelementtype, htmlparser2, entities) removed in commit 2f0a02b. 5,473 lines deleted. custom-theme.js 14,598 → 9,125 lines (-37.5%). Verified 0 top-level callers before deletion.

title: Task 2b follow-up: Liquid post-processor for blog body images width/height from URL
description: Task 2 shipped CSS aspect-ratio fallback (16:9 default). Follow-up enhancement: parse '_NNNxNNN' pattern from Shopify CDN URLs in article.content HTML string and inject explicit width/height attrs onto tags. Result = zero CLS on dimensioned images instead of partial reduction. Approach: Liquid string ops (split/replace) on article.content before rendering. Not urgent — current CSS fix gives meaningful improvement.
notes: Closed 2026-05-09 — shipped as snippets/article-content-with-img-dims.liquid (Apr 29 → May 6). 3-pass repair handles dimension injection from Shopify CDN URLs (_NNNxNNN.ext pattern), defensive p Bloggle wrapper rewrite. Routed through both sections/article.liquid AND sections/main-bloggle-article.liquid. Acceptance fully met.

title: Lazy-init .homepage-blog setMaxHeight via IntersectionObserver
description: article-card.js inside custom-theme.js (line ~14238) runs setTimeout(setMaxHeight, 100ms) on .homepage-blog containers at DOMContentLoaded. setMaxHeight does layout reads (clientHeight) + writes (style.height) on each .article__card title element — classic layout thrash. On homepages where the blog section is below fold, this runs as a DCL-time burst for content the user hasn't scrolled to. Solution: same IntersectionObserver pattern as ua7 — observe .homepage-blog elements with rootMargin: '200px' and only run setMaxHeight when the section is about to enter viewport. Estimated 50-150ms TBT reduction (smaller than ua7 because the work is lighter, but still a clean win).
close_reason: IntersectionObserver wrapper around setMaxHeight; 200px rootMargin; preserves 100ms settle delay. Pushed and committed.

title: Re-introduce brand-strip on /collections/<brand>/* via gated strip_brand param
description: Earlier brand-strip fix (commit reverted 2026-04-29) attempted to remove redundant brand prefix on Kérastase / Davines / etc. landing pages. Was applied unconditionally to product-grid-item.liquid and broke ALL product cards site-wide. To re-do safely: add strip_brand:true param to the render calls in collection-branded.liquid, collection-lorealprofessionnel.liquid, collection-discipline.liquid, collection-resistance.liquid, collection-nutritive.liquid, collection-krsymbiose.liquid, collection-krchromaabsolu.liquid, collection-lp-metaldetox.liquid, collection-lp-styling.liquid (~9 branded sections). Then in product-grid-item.liquid, gate the replace chain behind {% if strip_brand %}. Test: brand visible on /, /collections/all, /search; brand stripped on /collections/davines, /collections/kerastase, etc.
close_reason: strip_brand param added to product-grid-item.liquid; collection-branded.liquid + collection-lorealprofessionnel.liquid pass strip_brand: true on the swiper-slide renders. Pushed and committed.

title: Image asset audit: 782 KiB savings on azta-urban gallery JPGs (oversized)
description:
2026-04-28 PSI image-delivery-insight on /pages/azta-urban-salon-service-menu showed 782 KiB of wasted bytes across multiple Azta service-menu gallery JPGs. Top offenders:

  135 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-22_1080x.jpg
   60 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-21_1080x.jpg
   58 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-04_1080x.jpg
   56 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-13_1080x.jpg
   52 KB wasted: 2020_Azta_General_Service_Menu-05_1080x.jpg
  ... (more)

These are 1080x JPGs being delivered to mobile viewports that only need ~720x. Two fixable angles:

1. (theme-side) Add WebP variant via image_url with format: 'webp' filter. WebP is 30-50% smaller than JPG at equivalent quality. We've shipped this pattern for product images via <picture> wrap; needs same treatment in image-fill.liquid.
2. (admin-side) Re-export source JPGs at smaller dimensions / better compression. The Shopify CDN serves whatever was uploaded — if source is huge, even the resized variants are larger than needed.

Worth ~2 hr for theme-side WebP wrap in image-fill.liquid (high impact: every section using image-fill benefits). Plus ~30 min admin task to flag the worst source JPGs for re-export.
close_reason: Theme-side WebP support shipped in commit a2317d1 (2026-04-29). Image-fill.liquid renders azta-urban gallery via <picture> + WebP srcset. Remaining work is admin-side: re-export source JPGs at better compression (non-code task).

title: /account page CLS+LCP+FCP+TTFB all in poor — server-side + app-injection issues
description:
Real-shopper RUM data 2026-04-28 shows /account page failing 4 of 5 Core Web Vitals (only INP passes):
- TTFB: 30% good (Shopify customer-area auth-check + customer.orders DB query — server-side, hard to fix from theme)
- FCP: 36% good
- LCP: 44% good (well below 75% CrUX threshold)
- CLS: 35% good
- INP: 96% good ✓

Template code (templates/customers/account.liquid, 118 lines) is clean — just orders table.

Root causes (theme can't fix all, but can mitigate):
1. Shopify customer-area pages aren't CDN-cached (auth required) → slow TTFB inherent
2. LimeSpot / LoyaltyLion / Klaviyo activate fully on logged-in pages → heavy JS
3. PWA modal injection (separate beads — see #pwa-modal CSS fix)
4. customer.orders query renders inline → LCP delays

Theme-side actions to try:
1. Conditionally skip non-essential apps on /account templates (LimeSpot recs, etc — they don't drive customer-area conversion anyway)
2. Minimize CSS loaded on customer-area templates (per-template CSS already split, but customer-area might pull more than needed)
3. Investigate if Klaviyo signup modal fires on /account (often does) — it shouldn't

Not worth a major code investment until other fixes shipped — /account is low-traffic (216 events / 2 days vs homepage's 1,617). But it's the only page with TTFB <50% so worth a dedicated 1-2 hr session.
close_reason: Theme-side mitigation shipped (commit a8407fc): LimeSpot SDK now skipped on customers/* templates — saves ~300KB JS + DNS + parse on every customer-area page request. The remaining TTFB / LCP issues are server-side (Shopify customer-area auth-check, customer.orders DB query) — unfixable from theme code. Other app-skip candidates (VQB, Klaviyo signup modal, Judge.me) noted in commit message for future iteration. Re-open if RUM after 14d shows /account still failing 4+ metrics.

title: Snippet DRY-up: consolidate 3 product-grid-item variants (~600 lines duplicated)
description:
Three nearly-identical files with significant code duplication:
  - snippets/product-grid-item.liquid (standard, used by most collections)
  - snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid (used by branded collection landing pages)
  - snippets/product-grid-item-branded-subcollection.liquid (used by sub-sections of branded collections)

All three render product cards with: WCProduct_Price logic (Wholesale Club), WebP <picture> wrap, brand-prefix strip from title, sticker (sale/sold/new), price + compare-at price, Judge.me badge.

Differences between the three:
  - branded variants strip brand prefix more aggressively (Davines/Aveda/etc whole word matches)
  - branded-subcollection has narrower image sizes and tighter padding
  - standard has hover-second-image swap; branded does not

Proposed consolidation:
1. Rename product-grid-item.liquid → product-card.liquid (new canonical).
2. Add params: variant ('standard'|'branded'|'branded-subcollection'), strip_brand_prefix (bool), show_hover_image (bool), image_sizes (string).
3. Branch internally based on params.
4. Replace all callers — sweep snippets/sections for {% render 'product-grid-item-*' %} and migrate to {% render 'product-card', variant: '...' %}.
5. Keep the old files as { include 'product-card', variant: 'XXX' } for one release cycle, then delete.

Risk: high regression risk on collection / branded-collection pages. Need to test rendering on:
  - /collections/all (standard)
  - /collections/davines (branded)
  - /collections/loreal-professionnel (branded sub-collections)
  - homepage product grids

Worth 3-4 hours: 1 hr to consolidate, 1 hr to migrate callers, 1-2 hr to test all variants. High maintenance value (any future product card change touches 1 file instead of 3) but no shopper-facing impact.

title: Critical CSS audit + regeneration: ~40KB inline contains 37 Swiper + 10 Flickity selectors, some duplicates
description:
2026-04-28 spot audit of snippets/critical-css.liquid (39,927 bytes, single-line minified, inlined in <head> per layout/theme.liquid:17).

Findings:
  - 37 Swiper selectors (.swiper-button-*, .swiper-slide, etc) — used by homepage banner-slider (above-the-fold on homepage). JUSTIFIED.
  - 10 Flickity selectors (.flickity-*) — used by PDP product slideshow (NOT above-the-fold on homepage). Could potentially be moved out of critical CSS for non-PDP pages.
  - At least 5 duplicate rules detected (e.g., .uppercase, .js-grid[data-grid-small=2], 3x .collection__filters__*).
  - Many template-specific rules (.tousled-header__*) that may not be above-the-fold on every template.

Manual pruning is risky — could remove a rule that IS critical on some template. Better approach: regenerate critical CSS via tooling.

Recommended path:
1. Install  npm package (https://github.com/addyosmani/critical) or  (https://github.com/pocketjoso/penthouse).
2. Run against a representative set of templates (homepage, PDP davines-momo, collection davines, /cart, /pages/about, blog article).
3. Diff output vs current critical-css.liquid — identify rules to remove (or keep).
4. Manually merge: union of all per-template critical CSS becomes the new shared critical CSS.
5. Likely savings: 10-30% reduction (~4-12 KB) based on the Flickity + duplicates findings.

Alternative: ship the current critical CSS as-is and instead split into per-template inline blocks (e.g., critical-css-pdp.liquid, critical-css-collection.liquid) so each page only inlines what it needs. More code change but more savings.

Worth ~3-4 hr for proper audit + tooling setup + regeneration.

title: Image alt audit: 6 branded collection sections + cross-post-blogs missing alts
description: 2026-04-28 alt audit found these images without alt attributes: FIXED in this commit: - snippets/cross-post-blogs.liquid:15 — added alt={article.title} STILL MISSING (filed as follow-up): - sections/collection-krchromaabsolu.liquid (lines 347, 350, 362, 371, 380, 394, 403, 412) - sections/collection-krsymbiose.liquid (similar block patterns) - sections/collection-lp-vitamino.liquid (lines 346, 349) - sections/collection-lp-metaldetox.liquid (lines 347, 350) - sections/collection-lp-serioxyladvanced.liquid (line 346, others) - sections/collection-lorealprofessionnel.liquid (lines 410, 438) These are branded landing page sections rendering imgs from block.settings.left_media_html / middle_media_html / right_media_html / product_image_1/2/3. The img source is a raw URL (settings field), not a Shopify Image object, so there's no .alt attribute to read. Fix options: 1. (Best) Add alt-text fields to each block schema (block.settings.image_alt / left_media_alt / etc), wire into render 2. Use the block's title/heading text as alt where present 3. Add a generic alt like 'L\'Oreal Professionnel Vitamino' for the section's branded products Worth ~1-2 hr per section family, ~6 hrs total for thorough fix. PRE-EXISTING empty alts (NOT bugs — likely intentional decorative): - sections/password.liquid:20, 24 — password page background images, decorative - sections/section-map.liquid:64 — map decorative Leaving those as-is (empty alt is the correct WCAG pattern for decorative images).

title: Cart drawer skeleton-state delay: AJAX cart populate takes several seconds
description:
After opening the cart drawer, the skeleton placeholder (data-cart-loading + cart__items--blankstate) stays visible for several seconds before items populate. User reports 'froze for several seconds'.

Suspected culprits (in approximate order of likely impact):
1. /cart.js (or section_id=api-cart) endpoint slow due to app overhead — BSS B2B, Bold Brain, Smart SEO, Judge.me, Elevar all hook into cart rendering
2. AJAX request not fired until drawer opens (could pre-fetch on page load idle)
3. Cart drawer rerendering pulls a Liquid section that re-runs all the cart-related app injections each time

Diagnostic steps:
1. Open DevTools Network tab, click cart icon, measure /cart.js timing
2. Check what section_id the drawer fetches (likely api-cart or api-product-popdown)
3. Profile JS execution after items arrive (skeleton-to-real swap performance)

Quick wins:
- Pre-fetch cart on requestIdleCallback (drawer is then instant when user clicks)
- Cache the rendered cart HTML in sessionStorage; refresh on add/remove
- Strip out app injections from the drawer-specific Liquid section (drawer doesn't need full /cart page hydration)

Worth ~30-60 min investigation slot.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Header cart icon: defer-load JS race lets click fall through to /cart link before drawer JS binds
description:
On desktop, clicking the cart icon sometimes navigates to /cart standalone page instead of opening the slide-out drawer. On narrower viewports it works correctly.

Root cause: header cart icons (sections/header.liquid lines 134-140 mobile + 189-204 desktop) have BOTH:
  - href={{ routes.cart_url }} (link to /cart)
  - data-drawer-toggle=drawer-cart (JS hook)

The Drawer.connectToggle() handler in theme.js:1980-1994 does e.preventDefault() to suppress link navigation, but only AFTER theme.js loads. theme.js is loaded with defer (theme.liquid:378), so on a fast cold-cache page, the user can click the cart icon BEFORE the deferred script finishes parsing — link navigation wins.

Worsened by 2026-04-27 perf work: jquery, theme.js, storepickup.js all deferred. Gap between paint and JS-ready can be 200-500ms on a fast Mac.

Fix options:
1. Inline a tiny event-delegation script in <head> that listens at document level for clicks on [data-drawer-toggle] and preventDefault until the real handler binds. Hand off after.
2. Remove the href from the desktop cart icon (loses fallback for JS-disabled users; rare).
3. Add a CSS class to <html> when JS is ready, conditionally hide the link's pointer-events until then (jank).
4. Use JS module preload + inline an early data-drawer-toggle stub.

Option 1 is cheapest and safest. ~10 lines of inline JS in <head>.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Cart drawer: clamp 'Spend ₱X more' to ₱0 when cart already qualifies for free shipping
close_reason: Fixed in commit 'fix(cart-drawer): kill 4 residual bugs surfaced by tonight's CSS hotfix'. All four shipped to live theme 131664707683 on 2026-04-28 night. Verified on draft 140785582179 before live push.

title: Split cart.css into cart-shared.css (drawer-shared) + cart-page.css (page-only)
description:
Today (2026-04-27) we discovered that commit 949888a extracted .cart__message + .cart__graph (free-shipping promo + progress dots) from theme.css into cart.css under the assumption they were cart-page-only. They aren't — they're also used by the slide-out cart drawer (snippets/cart-drawer.liquid), which appears on PDP/collection/header/every page. Commit a57b0b8 then gated cart.css to template == 'cart', breaking the drawer's promo banner layout sitewide.

Hotfix tonight: removed the gate so cart.css loads on all pages (~3KB gzipped extra cost on non-cart pages, async, no render blocking).

Proper fix: split cart.css (~9KB / 465 lines) into:
  - cart-shared.css (~5KB, lines ~8-200): .cart__message + .cart__graph and all variants. Used by both /cart page and drawer. Load on all templates.
  - cart-page.css (~4KB, lines ~3, 297-465): .cart__template + .template__cart__footer rules. Only used by /cart page. Gate to template == 'cart'.

Saves ~3KB gzipped on non-cart pages and restores the cart-page-only gating for the cart-page-only chunk.
close_reason: Split shipped 2026-04-28. cart.css trimmed from 465 to 199 lines (shared rules only). New cart-page.css with 262 page-only lines, gated to template == 'cart'. Saves ~1.4KB gzipped on every non-cart page. Verified on draft via Brave inspection — /cart loads both files, PDP loads only cart.css, drawer .cart__message correctly styled display:flex on both.

title: WebP image rollout — biggest remaining mobile bandwidth lever (parked)
description: Lighthouse 'Avoid enormous network payloads — 7,567 KiB' is the next big lever. WebP is 30-50% smaller than JPEG. Pilot on banner-slider failed: image_url filter with format:'webp' silently drops <source type="image/webp"> when applied to block.settings.image_picker (Pipeline 6.1.3 returns URL string, not Image object). Approaches to try in a focused future session: (1) capture image_picker into variable, re-parse as Image object. (2) test if older img_url filter accepts format: param. (3) self-host pre-converted WebP variants. (4) wait for Shopify CDN native Accept-header negotiation. Files using product.featured_image (real Image object) like snippets/product-grid-item-branded.liquid are likely to work — pilot there first.
close_reason: WebP rollout shipped to live 2026-04-27. <picture> + <source type='image/webp'> wrap added to product-grid-item.liquid (active) and product-grid-item-branded.liquid (dormant variant for consistency). Verified on draft /collections/loreal-professionnel: 363 <picture> blocks, 363 image/webp source lines, 1,330 format=webp URL params. Pipeline 6.1.3 image_url filter works after all — earlier attempts failed because they piped .src (URL string) instead of the raw Image object. Estimated 30-50% image bytes saved on collection pages.

title: IA #12: enrich product card alt text (or operator backfill featured_media.alt)
description: Per p8-ia-typography-audit.md finding #12. product-grid-item.liquid:195,231 — alt text falls back to stripped product title. Most products lack featured_media.alt set in Admin, so this is what 99% of cards render. Two paths: (a) operator backfills featured_media.alt with richer descriptions on top-traffic products (manual, high quality), OR (b) snippet appends product.vendor + product.product_type to fallback (cheap, can produce awkward concatenations — test on samples first). Image-search SEO upside. Not P8-blocking.
notes: Code path (vendor append) risks 'Davines Purifying Shampoo by Davines' redundancy for brand-named products. Recommending operator path (a): backfill featured_media.alt on top-traffic products via Shopify Admin bulk editor. Code change won't-do.

title: IA #10: heading class naming sweep (BEM vs utility vs ad-hoc)
description: Per p8-ia-typography-audit.md finding #10. Heading classes mix conventions: BEM (.product__title), utility (.h1--body), ad-hoc (.related_articles_header h5). Not a bug — an architectural inconsistency. Post-cutover, pick one convention (probably BEM matching Pipeline 8 stock) and migrate stragglers. Multi-file sweep. Document the convention in os2-migration/ once complete.

title: IA #9: consolidate 17.14px font-size tokens into a CSS custom property

Future size changes become one edit. Pure refactor — visual identical.

title: 987 Phase 2: #MainContent umbrella CLS (170 events — tracking, no direct fix)
description:
OC's 987 Phase 1 audit (2026-05-13) identified #MainContent as the largest desktop CLS bucket: 170 poor-rated events in the 7-day desktop GA4 RUM window.

#MainContent is the umbrella container (<main id="MainContent">) wrapping all section content. It is NOT directly fixable — its CLS events bubble up from child sections (announcement bar, hero, product grids, cart, FBT, etc.) where the actual shifts originate.

The web-vitals.js attribution algorithm picks the largest-area shifted element. When a deep child shifts, the algorithm sometimes attributes to a higher-up parent if the shift propagated. #MainContent is a frequent fallback when the real shifting element isn't a CSS-targetable selector.

This bd is a TRACKING umbrella — do not attempt to fix #MainContent directly. The count will drop as individual contributor bds close:
- 987 Phase 2 fix #1 shipped today (blog article iframe/video, commit 6abcc1c) — expected to remove some
- 987 Phase 2 cart-template work (separate bd) — expected to remove more
- 987 Phase 2 announcement bar (separate bd) — small drop
- Any LimeSpot / Judge.me / BOGOS regression after the BSS cleanup (commits dyp + gj0) — re-monitor

Close this bd when:
1. Desktop CLS p75 < 0.10 in CrUX (the original 987 acceptance criterion is met), OR
2. #MainContent debug_target events drop below 20 in a 7-day window (95% reduction), OR
3. Phase 2 sub-contributor bds are all closed and CrUX shows desktop CLS < 0.10.

Verification: re-run scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop --days 7 monthly until thresholds met.
design:
This bd is TRACKING-ONLY. There is no code fix dispatch for #MainContent itself.

Monthly cadence task (run by coordinator OR via offload, RO data only):
1. Run `GA4_PROPERTY_ID=248106289 python3 scripts/web-vitals-report.py --metric CLS --by debug_target --rating poor --device desktop --days 7 --top 30` and grep for MainContent.
2. Append a dated note to this bd: { date, MainContent events count, parent contributors most-likely responsible }.
3. If trend is downward as expected (Phase 2 fixes landing) — close noting which sibling bd's drove the drop.
4. If trend is FLAT despite sibling bd's closing — escalate: there's an unaccounted contributor. Investigate (likely a new app injection or recent commit regression).

Reference parent fixes that should cumulatively reduce #MainContent:
- bd 987 Phase 2 fix #1 — blog iframe (commit 6abcc1c, 2026-05-13)
- bd hod — cart-template
- bd 7i0 — announcement bar
- Existing live fixes already in css-overrides.liquid: c8k (LimeSpot), p52 (Judge.me), dzi (BOGOS), 38l (LimeSpot cards), fan (price col — root cause now removed via BSS cleanup, defensive reservation kept)

Out of scope: code changes targeting #MainContent directly. The container itself does not shift; only its children do.
acceptance_criteria:
Close this bd when ANY of:
1. Desktop CLS p75 < 0.10 in CrUX (the original 987 acceptance criterion is met).
2. #MainContent debug_target events drop <20 in a 7-day GA4 window (88% reduction from 170).
3. All listed sibling bds are closed AND CrUX shows desktop CLS < 0.10.

DO NOT close this bd just because a single sibling closes — the umbrella metric needs sustained reduction.

title: sticky-filter-bar.js offsetHeight read — single-shot layout read
description:

title: collection-slider.js layout read — getBoundingClientRect + window.innerHeight

If this is replicating IntersectionObserver behavior via boundingClientRect polling, replace with native IntersectionObserver — same semantics, zero layout reads.

References: 3sr audit findings in bd hairmnl-theme-3sr notes.

title: Slack alert on CWV threshold breach — wire post-script hook
description: When daily run completes, evaluate: (1) any RUM metric drops below 75% good for INP/CLS/LCP, (2) any metric regresses >10pp WoW, (3) GA4 query was sampled. If any condition fires, POST to Slack incoming webhook with a summary. Webhook URL stored as SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL env var (and GHA secret). Threshold values, channel, and message format need decision before implementation.

title: A/B snapshot comparison mode — diff two arbitrary snapshots side-by-side
description: New CLI flag --compare-snapshots <iso1> <iso2> that loads two specific snapshots from snapshots.jsonl and renders a side-by-side comparison HTML showing per-metric deltas with color-coded direction. Output to dashboard/compare-<ts1>-<ts2>.html. Useful for: pre/post-deploy comparison, comparing today vs the same day last month, isolating the impact of a specific commit. Needs spec design before offload — what metrics to compare, layout, whether to support 3+ snapshots.

title: Add @media print rules — hide charts, preserve color cues, readable layout
description: Add an @media print block in the render_html() CSS. Charts (canvas elements) should be hidden or replaced with a text fallback. Color cues (pass/fail indicators) must print in B&W gracefully. Overall layout should be readable when printed.

title: use_custom_accent_font theme setting is vestigial — toggle exists but never gates custom_accent_font
description:
2026-04-28 theme settings audit found: settings_schema.json defines a checkbox 'use_custom_accent_font' but no Liquid template references it. The actual font selector 'custom_accent_font' (a select) IS referenced in snippets/css-variables.liquid:89-90 — it's applied unconditionally when set, not gated by the toggle.

Fix options:
1. Remove use_custom_accent_font from settings_schema.json (clears merchant UI).
2. Or wire it up: change css-variables.liquid:89 to: if settings.use_custom_accent_font and settings.custom_accent_font (gated).

Option 2 is more user-friendly to merchants (toggle on/off without losing the saved font choice). ~10 min change. Worth verifying which merchants currently have the toggle in different states before rolling out (might cause a visual change for some).
design:
## Goal
Wire the existing `use_custom_accent_font` toggle (settings_schema.json:312) so it actually gates the application of `custom_accent_font` in `snippets/css-variables.liquid`. Currently the toggle is defined but never referenced — `custom_accent_font` is applied unconditionally at line 89.

## Target file (single)
- `snippets/css-variables.liquid` — change ONE line at line 89.

## What to do
The other custom-font settings (`use_custom_base_font`, `product_use_custom_heading_font`) ALREADY use the toggle-as-gate pattern. The accent variant is the only one that's broken. Match the existing pattern.

**Find** (line 89):
```liquid
  if settings.custom_accent_font
    assign accent_font_family = settings.custom_accent_font
  else
    assign accent_font_family = accent_font.family
  endif
```

**Replace with:**
```liquid
  if settings.use_custom_accent_font
    assign accent_font_family = settings.custom_accent_font
  else
    assign accent_font_family = accent_font.family
  endif
```

Single character change: `settings.custom_accent_font` → `settings.use_custom_accent_font` on the `if` line. The `assign` line keeps the original (uses the font value). Pattern now matches `use_custom_base_font` (line 83) verbatim.

## Behavioral change

| Toggle state | Custom font set | Before | After |
|---|---|---|---|
| ON | yes | applies custom | applies custom (unchanged) |
| ON | no (default) | applies default (because falsy) | applies default (unchanged) |
| OFF | yes | **applies custom** | **applies default** ← regression risk |
| OFF | no | applies default | applies default (unchanged) |

The 3rd row is the only behavioral diff. If the merchant currently has `custom_accent_font` set WITHOUT `use_custom_accent_font` toggled on, they'd lose their custom font.

## Mitigation
The agent should NOT modify `config/settings_schema.json` or `config/settings_data.json` (admin-managed, per `.opencode_hints` rule 4). Coordinator will check current settings_data.json after the change to verify whether the merchant currently has the toggle on. If OFF + font set, coordinator pre-flips the toggle in Shopify admin BEFORE pushing to live (admin task), so the change is no-op visually.

## Invariants to preserve
- `accent_font.family` fallback (when no custom font set) stays exactly as is.
- The `else` branch is unchanged.
- No other lines modified.
- Don't touch the parallel branches for `base_font` or `product_heading_font` — they already work.

## How to verify
After the agent commits + the coordinator pushes to live:
1. Open Shopify admin → Theme settings → Typography.
2. Toggle "Use custom font for subheadings and button text" OFF — confirm the storefront uses the default accent font (not the previously-saved custom font).
3. Toggle ON — confirm the saved `custom_accent_font` value applies.
4. Visual check: PDP product subtitle, button text, blog post subheadings — they should reflect the gate state.

## Out of scope
- Removing the `use_custom_accent_font` setting entirely (Option 1 in the bd description). We're going with Option 2 (wire the toggle) per the description's recommendation.
- Pre-flipping the merchant's setting — that's a coordinator/admin action.
acceptance_criteria:
- Single line in snippets/css-variables.liquid:89 changed from `if settings.custom_accent_font` to `if settings.use_custom_accent_font`.
- The `assign accent_font_family = settings.custom_accent_font` line on line 90 remains unchanged.
- All other lines in the file unchanged.
- Diff scope: ONLY `snippets/css-variables.liquid`. NO changes to settings_schema.json, settings_data.json, or assets/theme.css.
- After live push: Shopify admin Typography toggle controls accent font application (verified by toggling on/off and inspecting storefront).

title: BOGOS replacement: build custom buy-X-get-Y promo logic (1 day, $420/yr saved)
description: BOGOS app handles buy-one-get-one promotions. Custom build: detect promo-eligible items in cart via Liquid + auto-add free item via Shopify cart AJAX API. ~1 day of theme work. Saves $420/yr in subscription. Lower priority than larger savings; only do if BOGOS promo behavior is well-understood + stable.
notes:
Folded into r8r (STKY replacement) scope per shared codebase. The free-gift threshold meter in r8r Phase 2.2 handles BOGOS use case (auto-add free product when cart > threshold, auto-remove if drops below). Combined replacement saves $720/yr in 5-6 hrs. See memory/stky_replacement_scope.md.
wwni field data 2026-06-10 strengthens this: when the BOGOS promo glider wins mobile /cart LCP (19/211 sessions, 28d) only 31.6% good / 26.3% poor — the app's deferred injection is a measurable UX cost on top of the $420/yr. A custom server-rendered promo bar would paint with HTML.

title: STKY Add to Cart: build custom replacement (3-4 hrs, $300/yr saved)
description: Sticky add-to-cart on PDP currently provided by an app. Custom build: position:fixed bottom bar that appears when product Add-to-Cart button scrolls out of viewport (IntersectionObserver), with current variant + quantity + Add button. ~3-4 hrs of theme work. Saves $300/yr in subscription. Lower priority than the bigger app rationalizations (LimeSpot, AMP) but easy win when capacity allows.
notes:
Comprehensive scope written 2026-04-27 EOD: memory/stky_replacement_scope.md. Scope mirrors the Vertex AI replacement pattern (server-side first, JS-minimal). Phase 1 = STKY parity (~2 hrs). Phase 2 = the 'more features' delta — free-shipping meter, free-gift meter (also covers BOGOS replacement, ur0), quick-add chips, cart-count badge, Shop Pay, low-stock indicator, Elevar event integration (~3-4 hrs). Phase 2.5 = cross-sell via Vertex AI (deferred until Vertex ships). Total active dev: ~5-6 hrs combined STKY+BOGOS replacement → $720/yr saved. Slot suggested: early-to-mid May 2026 after Phase 2 perf work, before Vertex build kicks off. Build prompt embedded in the scope doc for the future Claude Code session.
Superseded by a7av.8 (custom STKY Add to Cart replacement landed 2026-05-19). STKY app uninstall is tracked separately under a7av.7.

title: Custom-theme.js layout-thrash audit (no sourcemaps, deferred)
description: Lighthouse Performance trace showed forced-reflow events in custom-theme.js. No sourcemaps shipped to prod = hard to map back to which source line caused which offsetWidth/offsetHeight read. Approach options: (1) add sourcemaps temporarily for one PSI run. (2) inspect bundled JS by hand and look for forceReflow patterns. (3) use Chrome DevTools Performance tab on a real page interaction. Deferred until other Phase 2 items shipped.
notes:
Resetting status=open after prior dispatch built scripts/layout-thrash-audit.js but did NOT run it / post audit findings to bd. /hh-orchestrate pickup should: (1) verify the tool runs cleanly via 'node scripts/layout-thrash-audit.js assets/custom-theme.js', (2) capture top-N forced-reflow source patterns, (3) append findings here, (4) then mark in_progress for fix dispatches. Tool is untracked in working tree.
## Audit run 2026-05-12 — bounded increment

### Tool verified
- `node scripts/layout-thrash-audit.js assets/custom-theme.js` ran cleanly (exit 0)
- Scanned 9,125 lines across 86 section markers
- Target file: 446 KB minified custom-theme.js (no custom-theme.dev.js paired)

### Findings
- **93 total forced-reflow reads** across 20 distinct patterns
- **20 write→read interleave risks** (true thrash loops)

### Library breakdown
| Library | Reads | Key patterns |
|---|---|---|
| SweetAlert2 | 24 | getComputedStyle×10, scrollTop×4, scrollHeight body-sniffing |
| Sticky.js | 23 | scrollTop×5, offsetHeight×3, layout-rect math |
| Custom scripts | 18 | hover-line.js (offsetLeft×4, clientHeight×4, clientWidth×3, getBoundingClientRect×3), collection-slider.js, sticky-filter-bar.js, article-card.js |
| Swiper (core) | 14 | updateSize, breakpoints, resize/scroll handlers |
| Swiper (dom7) | 13 | DOM traversal layout reads |
| Swiper (ssr-window) | 1 | negligible |

### Actionable (custom scripts, NOT vendor)
1. **hover-line.js L8549-8593**: write setProperty → read offsetLeft/getBoundingClientRect/clientHeight in a tight loop (likely mousemove handler — highest INP impact)
2. **collection-slider.js L8943**: getBoundingClientRect + window.innerHeight (intersection observer proxy)
3. **sticky-filter-bar.js L3256**: offsetHeight read (not kt0 containment — just a layout read)
4. **article-card.js**: clientHeight read

### qa-reviewer verdict
PASS — findings valid, tool reliable, custom scripts contain genuine optimization opportunities. Recommend new bd issue(s) for fix work on owned code.

### Remaining risks
- No sourcemaps = hard to map back to original source lines for custom-theme.dev.js pair
- Sticky.js and SweetAlert2 are vendor bundles — no fix possible in this repo
- tool is untracked: coordinator should decide whether to `git add` and commit
MERGE_GATE_PASS — audit tooling committed in <pending hash>, follow-up fix bds filed:
- hairmnl-theme-87w (or whichever id assigned — see bd list): hover-line.js layout thrash (P3, highest INP impact)
- hairmnl-theme-qku: collection-slider.js getBoundingClientRect (P4)
- hairmnl-theme-6fw: sticky-filter-bar.js offsetHeight (P4)
article-card.js hotspot was the same code path fixed by 987 commit 3b9cf26 (CSS flexbox replaces JS height calc) — no separate bd needed.

3sr closed as audit-complete.

title: jQuery removal — multi-day project, high risk (parked)
description: jQuery still loaded sitewide. Removing it would cut ~80KB but requires rewriting any theme code that uses $.foo, $.ajax, etc. plus auditing all third-party JS that depends on jQuery being available globally (announcement bar, popups, some app scripts). High risk of breaking things mid-flight. Multi-day focused session if attempted.

value: 2026-05-09 perf appraisal: Mobile field CLS 0.30→0.07 (GOOD, May 2 sprint). Mobile field LCP still 3.375s POOR; top friction blog posts at 47-63% poor LCP suggest LCP element is first body image (not hero — hero already preloaded via 9hw). Desktop field CLS still 0.20 POOR (sprint targeted mobile only). 5.7MB+ third-party JS on mobile (VQB 447KB, LL 269KB, Personalizer 248KB, Reamaze 212KB, BookThatApp 171KB). Most third-party gating is admin-track (app embeds, not theme code). Filed: 7tq (LCP blog investigate, P1 CC), kt0 (CLS desktop investigate, P2 CC), 3wt (tab unescape OC-dispatchable, P2), d4c/dgb/xex (admin VQB/Reamaze/BTA gating).

Per-template CWV slicing live as of 2026-05-06 (commit 64a0a6b). Theme snippet (web-vitals-reporter.liquid) pushed to live theme 131664707683. GA4 admin custom dimension 'template' registered by user same day. Per-template friction tables in dashboard will populate from 2026-05-07 onwards once GA4 has 24h of post-push data. Verified rendering across 4 templates: index, product, collection (incl. brand variants), cart.

Reamaze defer-on-interaction epic (bd meu) — CX-critical work to defer the 212KB Reamaze SDK behind user-interaction triggers. CX SAFEGUARDS (non-negotiable): (1) CSS placeholder identical to today's Reamaze bubble so users don't notice, (2) data-reamaze-trigger='open' hot-path attribute for support page/footer CTAs (intent users get chat in <1.5s), (3) auto-disarm at 60s so non-engaging users still get Reamaze passively, (4) 10% canary cookie before ramp, (5) rollback is single-line + <1min deploy (remove cdn.reamaze.com from BLOCKED_ORIGINS), (6) CS team go-signal required between every phase. The 2026-05-10 'theme-side defer impossible' conclusion (old bd dgb, now closed) is SUPERSEDED — proven on VQB + BTA via createElement override (commits cc8d92d + ed3d161). Start at bd bzx (Phase 1 audit, Sonnet 4.6 / Medium).

Shop Sheriff PWA modal CLS root cause (2026-05-01): pwaSlideIn keyframes animate CSS top property (-100% to 0) over 0.4s. Animating top triggers layout reflow every frame even on position:fixed elements. This is why 04-28 position:fixed rule was ineffective. Working fix: display:none on #pwa-modal in css-overrides.liquid:86. PWA install via Chrome address bar still works (manifest preserved). Modal also has displayAlways/timeOnPage triggers besides beforeinstallprompt, so JS preventDefault alone insufficient.

Shopify store: myshopify URL is creations-gdc.myshopify.com (NOT hairmnl.myshopify.com which 404s). Live theme ID 131664707683, draft 140785582179. Push with: shopify theme push --store=creations-gdc.myshopify.com

ujg6.42 CSS chunking left article/blog/page templates without a per-template CSS chunk — they only load theme-core.css + custom-theme-core.css + deferred-templates.css. The strict-purge stripped rules used only by these templates because the analyzer didn't scan them. Confirmed stripped + restored 2026-05-21: .image__fill {position:relative}, .medium-up--one-half/third/sixth/two-sixths. Symptoms: blog listing renders as 1 column, article sidebar collapses below content, .image__fill absolute imgs escape container (look 'blown up'), text appears wrong because layout is so broken. Follow-up audit issue: hairmnl-theme-ncm2.

GHA perf-dashboard.yml fully operational as of 2026-05-05 (commit a01a2df). Failure mode debugged: GA4_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY needs full ~2.3KB JSON file contents (not just a field); PSI_API_KEY is exactly 39 chars starting with AIza (no surrounding quotes/whitespace); GA4_PROPERTY_ID is the numeric 248106289 (NOT the G-prefixed Measurement ID — that's gtag, different system). Workflow has built-in validation that fails loudly with redacted preview if values are wrong.

AI-referral channel (GA4 sessionSource, FLOOR est): ChatGPT dominates HairMNL AI referrals — 12mo-to-2026-04: ChatGPT 2,245 sess / 30 of 31 purchases / ~PHP85k. AI total ~0.22-0.25% of purchases but growing fast (first-3mo 1 purch -> last-3mo 10 = +900%; sessions 90->307/mo). Perplexity/Claude/Copilot send sessions, ~0 purchases yet. AI CVR ~1.3-1.4% (approx organic). Monthly check committed: python3 scripts/ai-referrals.py. Watch llms.txt(5/30) lift on ChatGPT/Claude/Perplexity. Shopify cross-check pending (needs order-scoped Admin token).

Bitdefender + Node.js 25 TLS regression (2026-05-18 BD update): The May 18 Bitdefender update broke TLS 1.3 ClientHello interception for Node.js OpenSSL 3.6.1. Symptom: 'Client network socket disconnected before secure TLS connection was established' on every shopify CLI request. curl works (uses macOS LibreSSL with different TLS 1.3 format). FIX: NODE_OPTIONS="--tls-max-v1.2" forces Node.js to TLS 1.2, which BD's proxy still handles correctly. Confirmed working 2026-05-19 — push succeeded. Use this until BD patches the bug or you disable BD SSL inspection.

CORRECTION to the 2026-06-09 Shopify gotchas memory: (1) article.handle in Liquid is BLOG-QUALIFIED — on article pages it returns 'product-spotlight/15-top-...' not the bare slug; gate with the qualified value or 'contains'. (2) 'request.path contains did not work' was WRONG — it was the render cache; request.path works. (3) Storefront page cache can persist MUCH longer than 30-90s (>30min observed 2026-06-10); for ground-truth verification render via ?preview_theme_id=<LIVE-theme-id> WITH a curl cookie jar (-c/-b) — the preview pipeline bypasses the page cache. Without a cookie jar the preview cookie drops on redirect and you silently get the cached LIVE page (false verification both directions).

CSS containment gotcha (kt0 cart-drawer regression 2026-05-11, commits 4e6ecc2 / ff1ef80): contain:layout on .header__wrapper trapped position:fixed cart drawer inside header bounds. Per CSS Containment spec, contain:layout (and contain:paint, transform, filter, perspective, will-change:transform) establishes a containing block for fixed/absolute descendants. ALWAYS audit descendants before adding these to a parent selector. The kt0 author comment claiming 'contain:layout is no-visual-impact' was actively wrong and propagated the misunderstanding. See .opencode_hints 'CSS containment + position gotchas' section + 'Pre-push smoke checklist' for the prevention playbook.

Epic-audit fix offloading SOP (codified after dashboard-20260515 audit, 2026-05-15):

When ~/Projects/epic-audit/epic-audit.sh produces audit-reports/<label>-<date>.md, the coordinator (CC) classifies each finding by severity and dispatches differently:

CRITICAL — Keep in CC (Opus 4.7 / High). Cross-file architectural bugs need judgment that OC sub-orchestrator misses. Example: dashboard-20260515 XSS via __CHART_DATA__ JSON injection — the fix is 1 line but requires understanding the data source (GA4 values), the escape pattern (</script + <!-- both need backslash escaping), and verifying no current dashboard data breaks. Don't offload.

HIGH — OC /hh-orchestrate on FREE tier (opencode/minimax-m2.5-free). Mechanical hardening with subtle correctness concerns (chmod, scope tightening, escape filters). Pattern-matches the audit's hardened block. Example: dashboard-20260515 H1 chmod 0600 on OAuth tokens — 3 write sites, ~20 LoC, OC's senior-developer + qa-reviewer chain handles cleanly.

MEDIUM — OC FREE tier IF the fix is bounded; promote to CC if it touches config with knock-on effects. Repo hygiene + doc updates + config tightening typically.

LOW — Judgment: inline CC if <30s (e.g., 1-line yaml timeout add), OC dispatch if not. Don't offload for dispatch overhead > value.

PASSED-WITH-CAVEAT — Audit recommendation that conflicts with user intent or known constraints (e.g., dashboard-20260515 recommended drive.file scope, user chose full drive). Inline CC: document override in commit message + bd memory. No dispatch.

Rules for every dispatched fix:
1. File a bd issue per finding before dispatch. Severity → priority (Critical=P0, High=P1, Medium=P2, Low=P3).
2. Description + design + acceptance lifted verbatim from the audit report (audit's hardened block + recommended fix line ARE the spec).
3. Always run merge-gate (CC pulls OC's diff, kt0 lint, draft push, smoke, live, commit, push). OC NEVER live-pushes.
4. Update the audit report with status per finding (✓ DONE <sha> / ⏸ DEFERRED / ✗ WON'T FIX) so subsequent audits know what was applied.

Dashboard cross-workflow trigger pattern (2026-05-05): pages.yml uses on:workflow_run listening to perf-dashboard.yml completion. Do NOT use gh workflow run from one GHA to trigger another — default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot dispatch workflows (HTTP 403). Pre-flight pull-and-diff against the LIVE theme (131664707683) before pushing ANY shared file to live — product.liquid, collection.liquid, brand-collection.liquid, theme.liquid, etc. — not just theme.liquid. Main HEAD's shared files carry stream:b (P8-migration) changes; a stream:a live push of the whole file leaks them to live. bsbi 2026-06-02: za7u.6's product.liquid live-push (a YouTube/stream:a fix) carried 2i8b.37's unguarded {% render 'product-sticky-bar' %} (stream:b/P8-cutover) to live -> 'Could not find asset' Liquid error on EVERY PDP (snippet not on live). MIGRATION-CONTRACT Rule 7 (pre-flight pull) applies to ALL shared-file live pushes. The diff should show ONLY your intended hunk; any stream:b content in the diff is a leak — gate it (show_sticky_purchase_bar-style flag) before pushing.

HairMNL Shopify CLI gotchas (2026-06-09, learned hard): (1) blog-ARTICLE pages cache ~30-90s on the storefront — curl <30s post-push shows stale; wait 60s+ before concluding a schema didn't deploy. (2) article schema gate: use 'article.handle == ...' NOT 'request.path contains ...' (request.path did not match in this P6 theme). (3) --only PULL was fully broken this session (errors on known-live files); --only push false-succeeds for NEW snippet files. Most reliable channel = inline schema in layout/theme.liquid (layout pushes take) gated by page.handle/article.handle. (4) ALWAYS guard a swap-push: if the staged file cp fails, the working-tree theme.liquid (with the a7av sticky-cart-bubble) gets pushed to live — assert the staged file has the intended markers AND no sticky-cart-bubble before pushing.

Judge.me loader.js v490 (fetched 2026-04-27 from cdn.shopify.com/extensions/.../judgeme-490/assets/loader.js, 9926 bytes) maps widget JS files to CSS gating selectors. Critical entry: widget/main.js is gated on '.jdgm-preview-badge, .judgeme-preview-badge' (full word, NOT the short .jdgm-prev-badge). This means renaming Liquid wrapper class from full to short form bypasses badge hydration entirely. Document this if Judge.me ever updates the loader — class names or gating logic could shift and break our static-stars implementation.

Shopify CLI gotcha (hairmnl 2026-06-05): "theme push --only=a.json,b.json,c.json" (comma-separated MULTIPLE files) SILENTLY fails — prints "pushed successfully" but uploads nothing. Single-file --only=a.json works. For multi-file, LOOP single-file pushes. Also "theme pull --only=..." returns false 0s (claims present files absent) — do NOT use pull for pre-flight verification; use git diff HEAD + additive-by-construction.

Variant-picker quick-buy title-covers-price root cause (J25 still broken, verified 2026-06-24): global rule in assets/theme.css:6298-6303 'button,input[type=submit]{white-space:nowrap;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis}' applies to .quick-buy__option (a <button>). white-space is INHERITED, so .quick-buy__option-title <span> inherits nowrap. The J25 css (.quick-buy__option-title: word-break:break-word;overflow-wrap:anywhere) NEVER resets white-space, so the title renders single-line and overflows over the price regardless of box width. Fix: add 'white-space: normal' to .quick-buy__option-title (and optionally to .quick-buy__option) in snippets/css-overrides.liquid:1232. Pure CSS, no JS change needed. Box-width JS (positionQbPopover) is correct and unaffected.
